metadata.note: Europe-focused cultural and music festivals, Jun 2026 - Dec 2027

festivals.name: "Adventures Continue" (Children’s Literature & Arts Festival)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Lithuanian town of Anykščiai fills with stories for a festival made for children and families. Beloved authors and illustrators lead workshops, premieres, and storytelling over two summer days, in a celebration of reading rooted in a town long known for its writers and its love of books. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: "Living Music" Folk Festival (Kokle Playing)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · The Latvian town of Limbaži hosts a folk festival devoted to the kokle, the plucked string instrument at the heart of the country's old music. Concerts and workshops explore its gentle sound and its place in tradition, in a celebration that helps pass this cherished instrument on to a new generation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: "Rustlings of Women" Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March, the town of Saint-Cyr-sur-Loire celebrates women's history and creativity with the 'Rustlings of Women' festival. Concerts, exhibitions, and talks spotlight female artists, historians, and activists, in a celebration of the role of women in culture and society. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: "When Suvalkija Cherries Ripen..." Sung Poetry Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Lithuanian town of Marijampolė gathers singer-songwriters and poets in its Poetry Park for a festival of sung verse. Performers fill the summer evenings with lyrical songs of their native Suvalkija region, in a gentle celebration of storytelling set to music, named for the ripening of the local cherries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: 3 May Constitution Day (Kraków)
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Every May 3, Kraków marks the day Poland adopted its famous 1791 constitution, the first of its kind in Europe. The city holds a Mass at Wawel Cathedral and a patriotic parade that sets off from the royal castle to Plac Matejki, with flags, wreaths, and music along the way. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: 3 May Constitution Day (Poznań)
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · On May 3, Poznań honors Poland's 1791 constitution with a morning Mass at the great Fara church and a midday gathering on Freedom Square. A military band plays as the national flag is raised at noon, in a proud celebration in a city that was an early seat of Polish power. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: 3 May Constitution Day (Warsaw)
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each May 3, Warsaw celebrates the anniversary of Poland's 1791 constitution, the first in Europe. The day begins with a solemn Mass at St John's Archcathedral in the old town, followed by speeches, honors, and a flag-raising on Castle Square, where crowds gather beneath the royal palace. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: A Coruña Night of Saint John Bonfires
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, the Galician city of A Coruña lights up with the great bonfires of San Xoán, blazing along its beaches as crowds leap the flames for luck. Witch figures called meigas parade and an effigy of the year's curses goes up in smoke, in an ancient solstice celebration that turns the whole shoreline to fire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aalst Carnival (Satirical Float Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · For three days before Lent, the Belgian town of Aalst gives itself over to a carnival of giant floats, costumed crews, and sharp, cheeky satire. Centuries old and famous for poking fun at the powerful, it is one of the town's proudest traditions and a real high point of its year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Aarhus Festival Week
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the Danish city of Aarhus throws Festuge, a ten-day cultural festival that fills the whole city with around a thousand events. Music, theatre, art, and talks spill across neighborhoods and squares, much of it free, in one of Scandinavia's largest celebrations of culture, held since 1965. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Abulense Masks and Dances Festival
festivals.description: Mid April 2027 · Each year, the Ávila village of Casavieja gathers the masked winter figures of the region for Mascarávila. Cucurrumachos, Machurreros, and other traditional masqueraders join folk dances from the area's villages, in a celebration of the deep ethnographic heritage and old rites of this corner of Spain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Acireale Carnival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each February, the Sicilian town of Acireale fills with brass bands, masked balls, and parades of huge papier-mâché floats, many of them decorated with thousands of fresh flowers. Often called Sicily's finest carnival, it has been celebrated here for more than four hundred years. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Acquaviva Castle Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the village of Acquaviva gathers beneath its old castle for the Festa del Castello. Live folk music, food stands, and family games fill the day, in a celebration of local identity that draws on the medieval heritage of this San Marino village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Acquaviva, Acquaviva, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Acquaviva, Acquaviva, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Actual Festival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January, the city of Logroño opens Spain's cultural year with Festival Actual, a bold showcase of the country's avant-garde. Music, film, theatre, and art fill the cold first days of the year, in a forward-looking celebration that has put the wine capital of La Rioja on the map of Spanish culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Admiralty Days Dokkum
festivals.description: Early September 2027 · Each September, the historic Frisian town of Dokkum celebrates its days as a naval admiralty city with a free maritime festival. Stately old ships line the canals while music and street theatre fill the streets, in a celebration that brings the town's seafaring past back to life on the water. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Adriatic Festival of Dance
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each summer, the medieval Old Town of Budva fills with movement for the Adriatic Festival of Dance. Ballet, contemporary dance, and musicals play out for weeks on a stage set in the old church square by the sea, in a festival that brings fine performing arts to the heart of the Montenegrin coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aegina Fistiki Fest
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each September, the island of Aegina celebrates the prized pistachio that grows in its volcanic soil with the Fistiki Fest. A week of food stalls, tastings, music, and concerts fills the harbor front, in a cheerful late-summer tribute to the little green nut that is the island's pride and joy. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aeschylia Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the town of Eleusis honors its most famous son, the tragedian Aeschylus, with a festival of theatre, music, and art. Performances unfold among the ancient ruins where the mysterious rites of the Eleusinian Mysteries were once held, blending classical heritage with the living arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Agen Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the city of Agen throws its great end-of-summer festival, once known as the Grand Prune Show after the famous local fruit. Free concerts, brass bandas, and open-air bodegas fill the city center for days, in a warm, festive farewell to summer in this corner of southwest France. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: AgitÁgueda (Umbrella Sky Street Art Festival)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Portuguese town of Águeda becomes an open-air work of art, most famously under a floating sky of hundreds of brightly colored umbrellas strung above its main avenue. Street performances, music, and art fill the weeks of AgitÁgueda, in a festival begun by local artists to brighten the town. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Air’genteuil Kite Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Butte d'Orgemont in Argenteuil fills the sky with color for the Air'genteuil kite festival. Synchronized kite flying, kite-making workshops, and free shows draw families to the hill, in a festive celebration of the simple joy of flying a kite on a windy day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aitoo Kirkastus Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the small Finnish village of Aitoo hosts a rock and pop festival that bills itself as the friendliest in the countryside. Volunteer-run and set in a rural landscape, it grew out of an old church Transfiguration gathering into a beloved music weekend, a warm celebration of small-town community. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Aix-en-Provence Opera Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Aix-en-Provence becomes one of Europe's great stages for opera, with weeks of opera, concerts, and dance. Founded in 1948, the festival is known for bold new stagings of the classics, performed in beautiful old courtyards and theatres around the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Aix-en-Provence, Aix-en-Provence (Provence classical arts community), France
festivals.address: Aix-en-Provence, Aix-en-Provence (Provence classical arts community), France

festivals.name: Al-Mossassa Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the Spanish city of Badajoz looks back to its founding by the Andalusian commander Ibn Marwan in the year 875 with the week-long Al-Mossassa festival. The streets fill with the sounds and crafts of its medieval Moorish past, in a celebration whose name simply means "the foundation." · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Alba International White Truffle Fair
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the Piedmont town of Alba fills with the rich, earthy smell of white truffles, the rare treasure of these hills. At the heart of it is a truffle market where buyers lean in to sniff each knobbly find, alongside tastings of the region's famous food and wine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Albacete Fair
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Every September, the city of Albacete opens its great circular fairground for eleven days of bullfights, concerts, rides, and late-night revelry. What began in 1710 as a market for livestock and goods now draws millions, and remains one of the proudest celebrations in La Mancha. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Albania Flag Day Celebrations (Vlorë)
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · On November 28, the coastal city of Vlora celebrates the day in 1912 when Albania declared its independence and first raised its red-and-black flag right here. The birthplace of Albanian freedom fills with cultural performances and proud community gatherings on the country's national day. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Albania Independence Day & Flag Day
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Every November 28, the capital Tirana celebrates Albania's Independence Day, remembering the 1912 declaration that ended centuries of Ottoman rule. Red-and-black flags fill the boulevards and squares as the country marks the day it claimed its freedom and its flag. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Albania Liberation Day Commemoration
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · On November 29, Albania marks Liberation Day, remembering the end of wartime occupation in 1944. In Tirana, officials lay wreaths at the National Martyrs' Cemetery to honor the partisan fighters who died for the country's freedom, in a solemn day of national memory. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Albanian Poetry Day
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the town of Gostivar honors Albanian poetry with the Dita e Poezisë Shqipe. A poetry contest among young writers and readings fill the day around Kosovo's independence anniversary, in a celebration that honors the poet Sefer Selimi and the living Albanian word. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Gostivar, Albanian Cultural Region, North Macedonia
festivals.address: Gostivar, Albanian Cultural Region, North Macedonia

festivals.name: Alexandria City Days
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the city of Alexandria celebrates its City Days. Concerts and festivity fill the capital of Teleorman County for a week, in a celebration that marks the patronal feast and the civic pride of this southern Romanian city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Alive Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the rural Thy region of northern Jutland hosts the three-day Alive Festival, a blend of live music, art, and outdoor excursions. Begun by local music lovers in 2009, it celebrates the arts and the close community spirit of this quiet corner of Denmark. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · On the first of November, Andorra keeps the old feast of Tots Sants, when churches hold Mass for the saints and the dead. Families climb to the cemeteries to lay flowers and light candles on the graves of those they remember, a gentle, reflective day in the mountains. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day & All Souls' Day (Kraków)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · On the first days of November, Kraków's historic cemeteries like Rakowicki and Salwator turn luminous, with thousands of candles glowing among the graves. Families gather to remember their own relatives and the national heroes buried here, in one of Poland's most heartfelt traditions. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2026-11-01) is a fixed annual date.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day & All Souls' Day (Warsaw)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November, almost all of Poland pauses to honor the dead, and in Warsaw the famous Powązki cemetery becomes a sea of light. Families travel home to lay flowers and light millions of small candles called znicze, gathering at the graves on the year's most quietly moving days. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2026-11-01) is a fixed annual date.

festivals.name: All-Ukrainian Arts Festival "Bessarabian Kolorit"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the town of Khotyn in Ukraine's Chernivtsi region celebrates the folk traditions of northern Bukovina and neighboring Bessarabia. Ensembles bring the songs, dances, and crafts of this richly mixed borderland to the stage, in a colorful gathering of the many peoples who share this land. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: All-Ukrainian Folk Festival "Green Kupalo in Midsummer"
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the city of Rivne marks the midsummer feast of Ivana Kupala with a festival of folk arts first held in 2023. Wreaths, song, and ancient rites fill the celebration, carrying on a Kupala tradition that ran for many years in nearby Korets before finding a new home in the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: All-Ukrainian Folklore Festival 'Kupala Dew'
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · In the Polissya village of Nemyrynci, midsummer brings the old rites of Kupala back to life by the river. Costumed folklore groups weave floral wreaths, sing, and leap the bonfires in search of the fabled fern-flower, in a celebration of fire, water, and the magic the season was long believed to hold. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: All-Ukrainian Youth Art Socio-Ecological Festival "White Lake"
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year since 2001, young people gather on the shore of White Lake, near Ukraine's Rivne Nuclear Power Plant, for an arts festival with an ecological heart. Concerts and talent shows mix with environmental contests and clean-up drives, in a celebration that joins youth culture with care for one of north Polissya's loveliest lakes. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Almabtrieb (Piding, Berchtesgadener Land)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each late September in the Bavarian Alps, the village of Piding welcomes its cattle home from the high summer pastures. Farmers and families lead the cows down decked in flowers and bells, in a joyful autumn homecoming rooted in the close-knit mountain life of the Berchtesgadener Land. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Almond Blossom Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March, as the almond trees burst into white and pink blossom among Agrigento's ancient Greek temples, the town holds its Almond Blossom Festival. Folk dancers and musicians from around the world gather in the Valley of the Temples to welcome spring in one of Sicily's loveliest settings. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Agrigento, Valle dei Templi, Agrigento, Italy
festivals.address: Agrigento, Valle dei Templi, Agrigento, Italy

festivals.name: Alpine Advent Market
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each Advent, the Slovenian Alpine Museum in Mojstrana, at the foot of the Triglav peaks, hosts a Christmas market of mountain crafts. Artisans from across the Julian Alps bring their handmade goods and local treats, in a cozy seasonal gathering set deep in the national park. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Alpine Pasture Festival (Köglalm, Achenkirch)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · High above Achenkirch, at the Köglalm hut, the farmers' wives of the village host a summer pasture festival on the mountain. A Holy Mass and lively folk music join homemade food and cake, in a warm Tyrolean celebration of the grazing season and the alpine life it sustains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Amari Green Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the village of Spili and the green Amari Valley, in the shadow of Crete's Mount Psiloritis, host a four-day celebration of local life. Music, traditions, and the valley's lush natural beauty come together in a festival that has become a cherished fixture of the Cretan calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Amazigh New Year (Yennayer)
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the city of Melilla marks Yennayer, the new year of the ancient Berber calendar, with a week of celebration. Traditional Amazigh music, food, and storytelling fill the program, honoring a centuries-old North African tradition that is part of this city's mixed identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Amel Kirmes (Maypole & Goose-Hitting Fair)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the village of Amel in eastern Belgium throws its four-day Kirmes, a summer fair in the local German-speaking tradition. Music, dancing, and rides fill the days, along with old village contests like goose-hitting, in a gathering that keeps a centuries-old country custom alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Amel, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium
festivals.address: Amel, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium

festivals.name: Ami Fritz Wedding Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Alsatian town of Marlenheim stages a 19th-century village wedding from the pages of local literature, the marriage of "Ami Fritz." Townsfolk in period costume play out the groom's procession, the ceremony, and the feast, in a charming reenactment of old country courtship and celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Andorra la Vella Town Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · At the turn of July into August, the Andorran capital holds its Festa Major, the big town festival in honor of Sant Vicenç. There are processions, packed concerts, and the old local dance called the contrapàs, as Andorra la Vella celebrates summer in the heart of the Pyrenees. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Andorra Livestock Fair and Competition
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, farmers from across Andorra bring their finest cattle, horses, and sheep to the capital for the country's great livestock fair and competition. Animals are judged across dozens of categories, in a tradition that has anchored the mountain economy and brought rural Andorra together for centuries. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Andrijevica Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the town of Andrijevica fills Knjaževac park with music for its festival. Four days of rock tributes, local bands, and mixed lineups join tourism booths and local stalls, in a young celebration begun in 2025 that weds music to the scenic park at the town's heart. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Angra do Heroísmo International Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the UNESCO city of Angra do Heroísmo in the Azores turns its Congress Center into a great jazz club for AngraJazz. Held since 1999, the festival brings acclaimed Portuguese and international musicians to play in an intimate setting, a sophisticated highlight of the island's cultural year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Angra do Heroísmo, Terceira Island, Portugal
festivals.address: Angra do Heroísmo, Terceira Island, Portugal

festivals.name: Anjou Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each June, the Anjou Festival brings theatre, dance, and music to grand old settings around Angers, from castle courtyards to historic stages. One of France's long-running performing-arts festivals, it lets audiences watch fine drama under summer skies in places full of history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Angers, Anjou (Pays de la Loire), France
festivals.address: Angers, Anjou (Pays de la Loire), France

festivals.name: Anniversary of Monaco’s Liberation (Wreath-Laying & Historic Vehicle Parade)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 3, Monaco remembers the day in 1944 when it was freed from wartime occupation. Officials lay wreaths at the war memorials and vintage military vehicles roll through the streets, in a day of remembrance for the resistance fighters and Allied soldiers who brought the war's end. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Anniversary of the 1848/49 Hungarian Revolution and Freedom Fight
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Every March 15, Hungary remembers the 1848 revolution, when the people of Pest rose up, bloodlessly at first, for freedom and independence. Budapest fills with flags and ceremonies, and many pin on a red-white-green cockade, in one of the country's proudest national days. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Annual Reindeer Racing Championship
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · On the last weekend of March, the Lapland village of Inari hosts the finals of Finland's reindeer racing championship. Reindeer pull their drivers at full speed across the snow in the season's top contests, in a thrilling celebration of a sledding skill rooted deep in Sámi herding life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Antonin Under the Stars
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each harvest season, the Marsovin estate in Gozo's Ramla Valley invites guests to celebrate the grape under the open sky. Visitors tour the vineyard and taste the island's wines as the stars come out, in a warm evening tribute to the modern winemaking that has taken root in this Maltese countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Apple Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Madeiran village of Camacha celebrates its apple harvest with the Festa da Maçã. Cooking demonstrations, music, and tastings of local food and wine fill the day, in an intimate country festival rooted in the orchards that have long shaped this hillside parish. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Apricot Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, the village of Sterna celebrates its apricot harvest with a summer festival. Grilled meats, local wine, folk music, and apricot treats fill the central square through the night, in a celebration that gathers the community around a beloved regional crop. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Arad Autumn and Family Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Romanian city of Arad fills its County Council Plaza for a three-day festival of autumn harvest and family. Country traditions, food, and community events mark the close of the growing season, in a warm celebration that brings the rural life of the region into the heart of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Arboga Medieval Days
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Swedish town of Arboga turns back the centuries for its Medieval Days, dressing its streets in the look of the Middle Ages. Knights joust, craftsmen ply old trades, and visitors don period costume, reviving the spirit of a town that was once an important medieval center of trade. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Arctic Chamber Music Festival (Longyearbyen, Svalbard)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the town of Longyearbyen hosts the world's northernmost classical music festival. Chamber concerts fill the polar night of Svalbard, in a celebration begun in 2018 that brings the warmth of music to the harsh beauty of the high Arctic. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ardia of Saint Constantine
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Sardinian town of Sedilo stages S'Ardia, a fierce and ancient horse race in honor of St. Constantine. Hundreds of riders thunder three laps around the country sanctuary in a cloud of dust, in a thrilling blend of religious devotion and daredevil horsemanship that grips the whole town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ardooie Sint-Maartensstoet (St Martin Parade & Giants)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each early November, the town of Ardooie parades for Saint Martin with the Sint-Maartensstoet. Schools, youth clubs, bands, and the town's great giants march by evening in scenes from the saint's life, in a celebration that marks the turn from harvest to winter in Flanders. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Armata Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Greek island of Spetses commemorates its 1822 naval victory over the Ottoman fleet with the Armata festival. A week of cultural and religious events builds to the dramatic burning of a mock enemy ship in the harbor, lit up by fireworks, in a fiery tribute to the island's heroes of the war for independence. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Art Break Svalbard (Polar Night Arts Festival)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · As the long polar night settles over Longyearbyen each November, the town gathers for Art Break Svalbard, a community arts festival. Galleries, halls, and the town square fill with exhibitions, music, film, and readings, in a celebration of the warmth and creativity that carry this remote Arctic community through the dark season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Art-Weekend Festival
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each year, the historic Odesa Film Studio hosts the grassroots Art-Weekend Festival. Local artists, craftspeople, and performers from across the oblast gather to show modern work alongside traditional craft, in a young celebration begun in 2024 to invigorate Odesa's artistic scene. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Arva Three Province Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the Irish village of Arva celebrates its unusual place where the three provinces of Ulster, Leinster, and Connacht all meet. A week of traditional music, dance, historical talks, and community fireworks honors this borderland crossroads, drawing neighbors together from Cavan, Leitrim, and beyond. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Arva, Border region of Ulster, Leinster and Connacht (County Cavan), Ireland
festivals.address: Arva, Border region of Ulster, Leinster and Connacht (County Cavan), Ireland

festivals.name: Ascona Carnival (Lakeside Carnival)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Fat Tuesday, the lakeside town of Ascona keeps its one-day Carnival. Crowds gather on Piazza Motta by the water for music, masquerade, and the risotto of the Carnival king, in a celebration that joins Italian-Swiss carnival spirit to the scenic Lake Maggiore shore. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ashbourne Royal Shrovetide Football
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Every Shrove Tuesday and Ash Wednesday, the Derbyshire town of Ashbourne plays a wild two-day game of street football that engulfs the whole town. Hundreds push and surge through streets and streams to reach goals miles apart, in one of England's last great surviving medieval mass football traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ashura (Bektashi World Headquarters, Tirana)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, Albania's Bektashi community gathers at their world headquarters in Tirana to mark Ashura, remembering the martyrdom of Imam Husayn. After ten days of fasting, believers come together for prayer and shared food, in a deeply felt observance of this Sufi order's calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Assumption Pilgrimage of Fátima
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, pilgrims gather at the great sanctuary of Fátima, where three children said they saw the Virgin Mary in 1917. On the eve of the Assumption they hold a candlelit vigil, thousands of small flames moving across the square as they pray the Rosary together into the night. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Athens Carnival (Apokries)
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · For about three weeks before Lent, Athens slips into Apokries, the Greek carnival season, when the city fills with costumes, music, and street dances. Families and children join the fun, in a tradition that reaches back through Byzantine times to ancient spring rites. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Atlantic Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each June, the island of Madeira opens its summer with the Atlantic Festival, famous for the fireworks that light up Funchal's bay on Saturday nights. Set to music and judged as a competition, the displays draw crowds to the waterfront for some of the finest pyrotechnics anywhere. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Augsburger Plärrer
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Twice a year, around Easter and again in late summer, the Bavarian city of Augsburg throws the Plärrer, one of the region's biggest funfairs. Carnival rides, brass bands, and big beer tents draw over a million visitors, in a noisy, cheerful tradition grown from the city's medieval markets. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Augustiner Bräu Beer Garden Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Augustiner Bräu in Salzburg's Mülln district honors its patron with a three-day beer festival. Brass music, local specialties, and steins of the monastery's own brew fill the days, in a celebration of St. Augustine, patron of brewers, at this 17th-century brewery. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Autumn Farm Festival (Pumpkin Tastings)
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · One day each autumn the Azienda Agricola Ernst in Broglio holds a farm festival of pumpkin displays, tastings, homemade risotto, and an artisan market. The Festa in Fattoria spotlights small-scale rural life in the Ticino mountains, connecting visitors with the rhythm of the Onsernone valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Autumn Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Around the autumn equinox, Latvia celebrates Miķeļi, the old harvest festival of St. Michael's Day. In Riga, a great harvest market fills Dome Square with the season's produce, crafts, and folk music, marking the end of the growing year in a custom that reaches back to the old fertility god Jumis. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Autumn in Lika
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On the first weekend of October, the town of Gospić hosts Autumn in Lika, the region's biggest fair and a celebration of its farming life. Stalls overflow with local food and crafts, and prize Lika sheep are shown off, in a warm gathering that marks the harvest in this rugged mountain county. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Autumn Meetings
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Slovenian town of Prevalje gathers for several days of Autumn Meetings, a long-running community festival. Concerts spanning folk, pop, and comedy join children's activities, sports, and food stalls, in a warm celebration that brings together generations of this Carinthian valley town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aveiro Canals Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the canals of Aveiro, often called the Portuguese Venice, come alive with the Festival dos Canais. Free music, theatre, dance, and circus animate the quays and squares of the historic center, turning the waterways and their traditional boats into the stage for a lively summer celebration of the arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Avenidas Novas Christmas Market
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each December, Lisbon's grand old Campo Pequeno bullring fills with a Christmas market in place of the usual crowd. Stalls of handmade jewelry, ceramics, and Portuguese holiday treats turn the round arena into a cozy indoor market in the heart of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ayer Cow Descent
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the village of Ayer welcomes its cattle down from the high pastures with the Désalpe. The decorated 'queen' cows return from the summer alps amid folk music and food stalls, in a celebration that marks the end of grazing in the Val d'Anniviers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Aínsa Castle International Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the medieval castle of Aínsa, high in the Aragonese Pyrenees, becomes the stage for an international festival of music and the arts. Traditional and contemporary concerts and theatre fill the warm evenings within the old stone walls, bringing culture to a beautiful and remote mountain town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: B Zone Folk International Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the Romanian town of Târgu-Neamț fills its open-air amphitheater with the sounds of folk music for the B Zone Folk Festival. Children's ensembles share the stage with well-known artists performing traditional songs and dances, in a celebration devoted to passing Romania's folk heritage to the next generation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Baba Marta Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March 1, the village of Parkany greets the spring with the Bulgarian custom of Baba Marta. Neighbors craft and exchange the red-and-white martenitsi threads amid traditional dance and music, in a celebration that keeps alive the spring tradition of 'Grandma March' among the local Bulgarians. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Parkany, Bulgarian community of Pridnestrovie, Moldova
festivals.address: Parkany, Bulgarian community of Pridnestrovie, Moldova

festivals.name: Babougera of Kali Vrisi
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 6 to 8, the village of Kali Vrisi fills with the bell-clad Babougera. Men in goat-skin costumes parade and roar through the streets, building to a satirical, almost Dionysian mock wedding, in a centuries-old rite of fertility and renewal that has become the village's emblem. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: Kali Vrisi, Northern Greece (Macedonia & Thrace), Greece
festivals.address: Kali Vrisi, Northern Greece (Macedonia & Thrace), Greece

festivals.name: Babougera of Vamvakofyto
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each Carnival, the village of Vamvakofyto near Serres fills with the masked Babougera. Costumed in rags and cowbells, the figures parade to chase away evil spirits, in a celebration noted as one of Greece's oldest carnivals, with roots in the ancient winter customs of the countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bach-na-Fahrt
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · On Carnival Monday, the Black Forest town of Schramberg holds the riotous Bach-na-Fahrt, a race down a small mountain brook in wooden tubs. Costumed teams paddle and tumble through the chilly water to cheers and battle cries, in a wonderfully silly Swabian carnival tradition unique to the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bad Dürkheim Sausage Market
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the town of Bad Dürkheim throws the Wurstmarkt, which despite its name is billed as the world's largest wine festival. Dozens of wine tents, fairground rides, and parades fill the town to celebrate the grape harvest of the Palatinate, one of Germany's great wine regions. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Bad Homburg Lantern Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each St. Martin's Day, the town of Bad Homburg glows with a lantern procession. Children carry handmade lanterns and sing the old songs through the streets, with a small fair of hot drinks and snacks, in a cozy celebration rooted in the Christian harvest and thanksgiving traditions. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Bad Homburg v. d. Höhe, Bad Homburg, Hesse, Germany
festivals.address: Bad Homburg v. d. Höhe, Bad Homburg, Hesse, Germany

festivals.name: Bad Karlshafen Harbor Festival
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each summer, the spa town of Bad Karlshafen celebrates its historic harbor where the Fulda meets the Diemel. Boat parades, folk music, craft booths, and regional food fill the days, in a celebration of the town's 17th-century founding as a planned Baroque trading harbor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Bad Karlshafen, Bad Karlshafen, Hesse, Germany
festivals.address: Bad Karlshafen, Bad Karlshafen, Hesse, Germany

festivals.name: Bad Tölz Saint Leonhard's Ride
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November 6, the Bavarian spa town of Bad Tölz honors Saint Leonhard, patron of farmers and animals, with a great horse pilgrimage. Hundreds of riders in historical dress drive flower-decked carriages and horses up to the saint's chapel, in a centuries-old blessing of the beasts that draws thousands to line the streets. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Badajoz Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the city of Badajoz throws one of Spain's liveliest carnivals, filling the streets with feathers, sequins, and pounding music before Lent. Costumed troupes called comparsas spend all year preparing their dances for the great parade, the pride of Extremadura. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Baja Fish-Soup Cooking Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Hungarian town of Baja celebrates its world-famous halászlé, the fiery river fish soup, with a great three-day cooking festival. Hundreds of cauldrons simmer on Petőfi Island as cooks compete and concerts play, in a celebration that puts the town's deep fishing heritage right at the center of the table. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Baja, Hungarian Danube (Southern Great Plain), Hungary
festivals.address: Baja, Hungarian Danube (Southern Great Plain), Hungary

festivals.name: Balkan Saze Festival (Laver Bariu)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the Albanian city of Berat celebrates the saze, the string ensembles at the soul of southern Albanian folk music, with a festival named for the legendary player Laver Bariu. Amateur and professional groups from Albania and beyond perform the old tunes, in a warm tribute to a master and the music he loved. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ballinasloe Fair & Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the town of Ballinasloe hosts one of Europe's oldest horse fairs, when dealers and travelers fill the green to buy, sell, and show off their horses. Around the centuries-old fair grows a week-long festival of music, markets, and community, deep in the Irish midlands. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Ballymote Heritage Weekend
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year since 1990, the Irish town of Ballymote celebrates its rich past with a heritage weekend of history, archaeology, and folklore. Talks, walks, and events draw on local landmarks like the old Ballymote Castle, in a warm gathering that keeps the stories and traditions of this corner of Sligo alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Banat Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Czech village of Eibenthal in the Banat celebrates its heritage with Festival Banát. Czech music, theater, lectures, and folklore mingle with Romanian and Serbian influences, in a celebration of one of the Czech-founded villages of the Romanian Banat and its distinct community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Banja Luka Ethno Days
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the city of Banja Luka celebrates the folk traditions of the Krajina region with its Ethno Days. Music, dance, and customs from across Republika Srpska and abroad fill the parades, concerts, and workshops, in a festival that brings old Slavic and Serbian folk heritage proudly to life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Banja Luka, Krajina (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Banja Luka, Krajina (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Bar Olive Festival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each late November, the Old Town of Bar in Montenegro celebrates its olive harvest with the Maslinijada, timed to the town's liberation anniversary. Expert judges crown the finest extra-virgin oils as stalls sell local produce, in a celebration rooted in groves of ancient olive trees, including one said to be over a thousand years old. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Barcelona Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, Barcelona gives itself over to Carnestoltes, a week of costumes, parades, and playful rituals led by a mischievous carnival king. Neighborhoods hold their own parties and processions, in a celebration with roots reaching back through medieval and even Roman spring feasts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Barcelona Saint John’s Eve
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, Barcelona throws itself into the Revetlla de Sant Joan, the wild midsummer party locals call the shortest night of the year. Bonfires burn, fireworks crackle on every corner, and families spill onto beaches and rooftops to eat, drink, and welcome the summer. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Barents Spektakel
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the far-northern Norwegian town of Kirkenes lights up the polar winter with Barents Spektakel, a bold festival of art and culture. Run by a local artist collective, it fills the town with contemporary art, music, and talks that explore life on the meeting point of Norway, Russia, and Finland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Baroque Days of Villafranca
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the town of Villafranca turns back to the 17th century for its Baroque Days. A week of baroque music, historical reenactments, markets, and period gastronomy transforms the town, in a celebration that evokes the prosperous early-1600s heyday of this Navarrese town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Barr Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year around the first weekend of October, the Alsatian wine town of Barr celebrates its grape harvest with the Fête des Vendanges. Flower-covered floats, folk music, a wine queen, and a grand parade of dahlias and autumn produce fill the streets, in a joyful close to the season among the vineyards. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Barranda "Cuadrillas" Folk Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each January, the village of Barranda near Caravaca de la Cruz hosts what is said to be Spain's oldest gathering of folk cuadrillas. Music groups from across the region fill concerts and street corners with jotas and old songs, in a beloved celebration that draws young and old around their shared rural heritage. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Caravaca de la Cruz, Murcia, Spain
festivals.address: Caravaca de la Cruz, Murcia, Spain

festivals.name: Barthelmarkt
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the Upper Bavarian village of Oberstimm holds the Barthelmarkt, a folk fair with roots stretching back to Roman times. A traditional horse and cattle market joins beer tents, fairground rides, and community revelry, in a celebration that has gathered the countryside here for many centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Basel Autumn Fair
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each late October, the Swiss city of Basel fills seven of its squares and halls with the Herbstmesse, an autumn fair that has run for more than 550 years. Carnival rides, market stalls, and stands of sweet Swiss treats turn the whole old town into one of the country's largest and oldest fairs. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Basel Carnival (Morgestraich Lantern Parade)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · At exactly four in the morning, the city of Basel goes dark and the Fasnacht begins, as masked groups march out with glowing lanterns playing fife and drum. For three days the city belongs to these Cliquen, who fill the streets with music, painted lanterns, and sharp satire on the year's events. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Basque Festivals (Donostia)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, San Sebastián celebrates all things Basque with the Euskal Jaiak, two weeks of folk music, dance, rural sports, and food. There is a Cider Day with tastings and song, ox and stone-lifting contests, and dancing in the squares, in a festival the city has kept since 1896. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Battle of Borschts
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the town of Krolevets gathers its cooks for the Battle of Borschts. More than a dozen versions of the soup, traditional and inventive, simmer in the contest, in a celebration that brings the town together around the pride of its cooking heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bauska District Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, a different village in Latvia's Bauska region hosts a folk festival celebrating the traditions of historic Semigallia. Amateur singing and dance ensembles, children's folklore programs, and handicraft fairs fill the day, in a warm gathering that carries the music and customs of Zemgale from village to village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Beached Whale Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer on the shore of Lake Velenje, a wonderfully silly festival celebrates a local tale of a whale that supposedly washed up far from any sea. First held in 2011, the Beached Whale Festival fills the lakeside with music and good humor, in a burst of homegrown creativity that the town has taken to heart. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bear and Carnival Festival of Prats-de-Mollo
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each mid-February, the town of Prats-de-Mollo joins its bear legend to carnival for a week-long festival. Musical processions, street games, and a grand closing parade fill the days, in a celebration rooted in the Vallespir folklore of the bear that comes down from the mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bear Festival of Arles-sur-Tech
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each early February, the town of Arles-sur-Tech revives an ancient legend with its Bear Festival. Costumed figures stage the bear chase and its symbolic 'shaving' to cobla music and sardanes, in a celebration deep in Vallespir folklore, recalling villagers who once captured an injured bear. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bear Festival of Saint-Laurent-de-Cerdans
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each late February, the village of Saint-Laurent-de-Cerdans closes the bear season with its own Bear Festival. The playful bear hunt and ceremonial dances carry on the Vallespir myth, in a celebration that is the last of the region's bear festivals each winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Beer and Flower Festival Laško
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the brewing town of Laško throws Slovenia's biggest outdoor festival, a happy mix of beer and flowers. There is a parade of flower-decked floats, plenty of the local brew, and concerts, in a celebration that started as a brewery party back in 1964. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bektashi Nevruz
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each March 22, Albania's Bektashi community celebrates Sultan Nevruz, marking both the arrival of spring and the birthday of Imam Ali. The faithful gather at the order's tekkes and world headquarters in Tirana for prayer and shared feasting, in a celebration of the Sufi order woven into Albanian life. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Bektashi Pilgrimage to Mount Tomorr
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, thousands of Bektashi believers climb Mount Tomorr, the great peak rising over Berat that has been held sacred since ancient times. They gather at a mountaintop shrine for days of prayer, song, and shared meals, in one of Albania's most storied pilgrimages, where old mountain faith meets Islam. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Belfast International Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each autumn, Belfast fills its theatres, halls, and galleries with three weeks of dance, music, theatre, and art from around the world. Started in 1962, it is Northern Ireland's longest-running arts festival, mixing big international names with the best of local talent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Belfast Maritime Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, Belfast's Titanic Quarter celebrates the city's seafaring past with a maritime festival along the waterfront. Visitors climb aboard historic ships, including a Spanish galleon replica, and explore nautical exhibits amid free entertainment, in a celebration that honors a city once among the world's great shipbuilding ports. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Belfast, Belfast maritime and shipbuilding heritage, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Belfast, Belfast maritime and shipbuilding heritage, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Belfast TradFest
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the UNESCO City of Music that is Belfast fills with fiddles and bodhráns for a week-long celebration of traditional Irish music, song, and dance. Concerts, lively pub sessions, céilís, and workshops gather renowned and local musicians alike, in a festival that keeps the city's rich trad scene thriving. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Belfast, Northern Ireland (Ulster) Irish traditional music community, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Belfast, Northern Ireland (Ulster) Irish traditional music community, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Belgian National Day (Brussels Parade & Fireworks)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Every July 21, Belgium marks its national day, the date in 1831 when its first king swore his oath and the young country was born. Brussels holds a military parade past the royal palace and ends the evening with fireworks, in a day of patriotic ceremony and free public festivities. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Belgrade Manifest (Festival of Events, Destinations, and Tasting)
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year in Belgrade's Kalemegdan Park, more than thirty towns from across Serbia set up along the old fortress promenade to show off their food, crafts, and folk traditions. Families stroll from stall to stall tasting regional specialties, in a festival that turns the whole country into one open-air fair. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Belgrade Summer Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer since 1993, Belgrade has filled its stages and squares with Belef, a festival of theatre, music, dance, and art. It mixes well-known names with up-and-coming Serbian artists, and grew out of the city's drive to bring culture back to life in difficult years. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Beltinci International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Prekmurje town of Beltinci hosts an international folklore festival, with a grand parade of folk ensembles winding through its streets. Groups from across Slovenia and abroad perform in bright costume, led by the local brass band, in one of the country's liveliest celebrations of folk song and dance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Benia Gamonéu Cheese Fair
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each October, the Asturian village of Benia de Onís celebrates its prized Gamonéu, a smoky blue cheese of the high Picos pastures. Cheesemakers compete for honors as visitors taste the cheese with local cider to the sound of bagpipes, in a fair that keeps alive the mountain shepherding behind this rare delicacy. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Berane Cultural Summer
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Montenegrin town of Berane fills a whole month with culture for its Cultural Summer. Theatre, concerts, literary evenings, and art exhibitions draw artists from near and far, in the town's main yearly showcase of the arts and a sign of its ambition to be a creative center. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: BeregCulturFest (Folklore Festival, Berehove)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each year, the town of Berehove holds the charitable BeregCulturFest of folklore and craft. Hungarian, Ukrainian, and Roma ensembles perform beside a craft fair of handmade goods, in a celebration begun in 2023 to support the war effort and honor the town's mixed community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bern Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each mid-September, the city of Bern brings the alpine harvest to its Parliament square for the Sichlete. Mountain cattle, farm products from six regions, and the sound of cowbell-swinging troupes fill the Bundesplatz, in a celebration that carries the pastoral world into the heart of the capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Besançon International Music Festival – Franche-Comté
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September since 1948, the city of Besançon hosts France's oldest classical music festival, famous above all for its conducting competition. Young conductors from around the world come to test themselves before a full orchestra, alongside weeks of concerts in this elegant eastern French town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Beszterce Calling Picnic
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the lakeside at Salgótarján fills with music for the Beszterce Calling Picnic. DJ sets, live bands, food stalls, and family activities gather by the Tó-Strand, in a young celebration begun in 2025 whose name recalls the town's old name of Beszterce. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bielsa Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter before Lent, the Pyrenean village of Bielsa erupts with one of Spain's most striking carnivals. Soot-blackened bear figures called Osos run wild alongside the elegant Madamas and other masked characters, with dances and fireworks, in an ancient rite meant to chase off winter and welcome fertility. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Biike Burning (Spring Bonfire)
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Every February 21, the North Frisian coast of Schleswig-Holstein lights up with great bonfires for Biikebrennen, an old festival welcoming spring. From Husum to the islands, blazing fires drive out winter as communities gather in the cold dark, in a tradition that has long stood as a proud emblem of Frisian identity. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Bilbao Basque Fest
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter, Bilbao fills its streets and squares with the Basque Fest, hundreds of free events celebrating everything Basque. There are concerts, traditional dances, rural sports, food tastings, and comedy and film in the Basque language, in a lively showcase of the region's living culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bile Fest (Herb Festival)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, St. Anastasia Island in Burgas Bay hosts the Bile Fest of herbs. Around Enyovden, the festival celebrates the healing herbs of the season on an island whose old monastery was long tied to healing, in a celebration of the plants and lore of the Bulgarian summer. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Bilozyriya Folk Fest
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the Ukrainian village of Bilozyriya in the Cherkasy region celebrates its folk heritage with a festival of song and tradition. Local ensembles perform national songs in traditional costume alongside craft fairs, in a warm village gathering that keeps the rich folk culture of central Ukraine alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bilçë Olive Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the village of Bilçë near Berat celebrates its olive harvest. Local foods and freshly pressed olive oil fill the festival, in a celebration of a village that has grown olives for generations in this corner of southern Albania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Binduga Wreath Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each Midsummer, the Warta riverbank at Binduga fills with wreaths and bonfires for the Wianki festival. Slavic wreath rituals, fire, folk music, and dance mark the Kupala Night, in a celebration of the ancient Slavic solstice custom of the shortest night in Greater Poland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bingsjö Folk Music Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the tiny Dalarna village of Bingsjö fills with fiddles for one of Sweden's classic folk-music gatherings. Since 1969, fiddlers and folk musicians from across the country have converged here for concerts, dancing, and all-night playing together, in a celebration steeped in the deep folk heritage of Dalarna. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bischberg Saint John’s Fire
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 24, the fishing harbor of Bischberg blazes with a Saint John's fire. The community gathers at the Fischerhafen for the Johannisfeuer with brass band music and food, in a celebration of a Franconian midsummer tradition that reaches back to pre-Christian times. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bistrampolis Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the grand Bistrampolis Manor near Panevėžys hosts a festival of music, crafts, and country pursuits. Lithuanian folk and modern acts share the bill with horse shows and family activities, in a celebration that brings new life to an old estate and shows off the heritage of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bistrița Days
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Transylvanian city of Bistrița celebrates itself with Bistrița Days, a lively municipal festival. Concerts, cultural events, sports, and children's programs fill several days, in a warm city-birthday gathering made for the people who call this old Saxon town home. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: BITEF (Belgrade International Theatre Festival)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Since 1967, Belgrade has hosted BITEF, one of Europe's longest-running theatre festivals and a window onto the boldest new work from around the world. Founded to connect the city with global stages, it still brings daring, experimental theatre to Serbian audiences each autumn. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bleiburg Meadow Market
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Held since 1393, the Bleiburger Wiesenmarkt in Carinthia is one of Austria's oldest folk fairs, gathering each year around the turn from August to September. Once a great pasture and trade market granted by a medieval duke, it lives on as a beloved meadow fair of food, rides, and revelry. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Blessing of the Fishing Fleet
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, near the feast of the fishermen's patron, the pier at Sorobon hosts a blessing of the fishing fleet. Vessels gather to receive a blessing for the safety of boats and crews in the year to come, in a celebration that joins Catholic devotion to the seafaring life of Lac Bay. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Blue Danube International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Danube town of Vidin fills with folk dancers and musicians for the Blue Danube festival. Thousands of performers in bright traditional dress come from Bulgaria and its neighbors along the river, turning the riverbank into a colorful celebration of the region's shared folk heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Blueberry Days
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Montenegrin town of Plav celebrates its wild blueberry harvest with a month-long festival, Dani borovnice. Concerts, food fairs, book launches, and a honey-and-handicraft market fill the weeks, in a warm tribute to the little mountain berry woven into the cooking and life of these highlands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bodo Feast
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Portuguese town of Pombal holds its most emblematic festival, the Festas do Bodo, famous for its communal bread. Folk parades, traditional music, and nightly concerts fill several days, in a celebration whose roots reach back to old tales of royal gifts of bread on feast days. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Boemeldonck Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Dutch village of Prinsenbeek throws itself entirely into the Boemeldonck Carnival. Everyone dons costume to join parades of music and giant floats as the whole village takes on its festive carnival name, in a joyful burst of the pre-Lenten tradition of the Catholic south. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bograč Stew Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the old town of Lendava fills with the smell of simmering bograč, the hearty meat stew of the Prekmurje region. Dozens of teams cook all day in great cauldrons as a jury crowns the finest pot, in a convivial culinary contest celebrating a dish that has fed families here for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Boka Night (Feast of Feasts)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On a summer night in the Bay of Kotor, the water comes alive with Boka Night, a parade of beautifully decorated boats gliding through the harbor under the lights. It began long ago as a sailors' thanksgiving for safe voyages, and still ends in fireworks over one of the Adriatic's loveliest bays. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bolgrad Wine Fest (International Wine Festival)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, the multiethnic town of Bolhrad in Ukraine's Bessarabian south celebrates its winemaking heritage with a two-day wine festival. Vintners from Ukraine and abroad pour their wines for tasting and competition, in a celebration of the Bulgarian, Gagauz, and Ukrainian families who have made wine in this borderland for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bollo Festival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each Easter, the Asturian city of Avilés gathers in its old center to share the bollo, a sweet Easter bread, at long communal tables. Parades with floats and music fill the streets, building to a grand feast on Easter Monday, in a celebration born in the late 1800s to mark the end of Lent and the coming of spring. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bollokume Cookie Festival
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Elbasan celebrates its almond cookie, the bollokume, with a small culinary festival. Local women demonstrate how the spiral cookie is made by hand for Dita e Verës, in a celebration begun in 2023 of a sweet central to the city's Summer Day. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Bonaire Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each February, the Caribbean island of Bonaire bursts into Karnaval, with feather costumes, street dancing, and grand parades through Kralendijk and the village of Rincon. At its heart is the tumba contest, where musicians compete to write the song that will set the whole island moving. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Bonaire Flag Day (Bonaire Day)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Every September 6, the island of Bonaire celebrates Flag Day, its own national holiday honoring the island's identity and symbols. A chosen neighborhood of Kralendijk fills with live music, dancing, and local food, as residents and visitors gather to celebrate everything that makes the island home. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Bonfire of Saint Anthony Abbot
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the Salento town of Novoli builds Italy's largest bonfire to honor Saint Anthony Abbot, protector of farm animals. Volunteers spend much of the year stacking vine branches into a towering pyramid, the focara, more than twenty meters high, then set it ablaze in a spectacular winter fire that lights up the whole town. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Books in the Loop (Literary Festival)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the French city of Besançon fills with readers for Livres dans la Boucle, a three-day literary fair that draws tens of thousands. Author talks, signings, and a great book market spread through the city, in a celebration that has grown into one of the region's liveliest gatherings around the written word. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bor, Buli, Balaton (Boglar Harvest Festival)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August in the lakeside town of Balatonboglár, one of Hungary's oldest grape harvest festivals fills the streets with wine, music, and good company. Its name means roughly "Wine, Party, Balaton," and it celebrates a half-century of shared winemaking tradition along the country's beloved lake. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Borgo Maggiore Castle Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the main piazza of Borgo Maggiore fills with festivity for its castle festival. Folk music, local cuisine, historical reenactments, and children's games gather the community around the old fortress, in a celebration of the medieval past and the pride of this San Marino town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Borgo Maggiore, Borgo Maggiore, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Borgo Maggiore, Borgo Maggiore, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Borsec City Days
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Székely spa town of Borsec celebrates its City Days. Concerts and festivity gather the small mountain town, in a celebration that emphasizes the distinct Székely identity and traditions of this corner of the Carpathians. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bosnian Teferić Community Picnic
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the Bosniak community of Innsbruck gathers outdoors for the Bosanski Teferič. Lamb roasted under the sač, traditional food, and cultural entertainment bring families together, in a celebration that carries the rural Bosnian custom of the communal picnic to the diaspora in Austria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Botarga of Mohernando
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each year on the Sunday nearest January 20, the village of Mohernando celebrates its patron San Sebastián with a botarga and a jester. The costumed masked figures parade and play through the streets in a custom deeply rooted in the folklore of Guadalajara province, where these old characters appear in village after village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Botarga of Tórtola de Henares
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On its feast days, the village of Tórtola de Henares fills its streets with the botarga, a costumed harlequin figure of the Alcarria. Bright and antic, the masked character carries on an old folk tradition tied to Carnival and the turning of the seasons, recovered and cherished as a piece of the village's living heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Tórtola de Henares, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain
festivals.address: Tórtola de Henares, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain

festivals.name: Botarga of Villaseca de Uceda
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each winter, the village of Villaseca de Uceda welcomes its Botarga down from the hills. The colorful masked figure parades through the village with music and dances, in a celebration of the masked-character folklore long kept in the villages of rural Guadalajara. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Villaseca de Uceda, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain
festivals.address: Villaseca de Uceda, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain

festivals.name: Boucard Festival
festivals.description: Late April 2026 · Since 1966, the Festival de Boucard has brought classical music to the Haut-Berry countryside of central France. Violins, clarinets, and fine ensembles fill the summer evenings near the château of Boucard, in a celebration born after the war that helped revive the cultural life of this quiet rural region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Boyle Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Irish town of Boyle fills with music, theatre, and art for its arts festival. A rich program of concerts, plays, dance, and family events plays out across the town, often around the historic King House, in a beloved celebration that has long showcased both local and national talent. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Brabova Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Izvoranu Palace in Brabova hosts a traditional music festival. Regional ensembles perform the classic folk songs and dances of Oltenia over two evenings, in a young celebration created to bring attention to the cultural life of Dolj's small communities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Braderie de Lille
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Every September, the city of Lille becomes one giant flea market for the Braderie, the biggest of its kind in Europe. The whole center fills with stalls and bargain-hunters in a tradition going back to the Middle Ages, when servants could sell off their masters' old things once a year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Braemar Gathering
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · On the first Saturday of September, the Highland village of Braemar holds one of Scotland's most famous gatherings. Kilted athletes toss the caber and put the stone, pipers and dancers compete, and the games carry on a tradition of clan pride that draws visitors from across the world. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Braga Romana (Bracara Augusta Re-enactment)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each May, the Portuguese city of Braga turns back nearly two thousand years to its days as the Roman city of Bracara Augusta. Over five days, costumed parades, gladiator games, workshops, and a Roman market fill the historic center, in a vivid living-history festival celebrating the city's ancient roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Braize Donkey Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year since 1989, the French village of Braize celebrates the gentle Bourbonnais donkey with a fair devoted to the breed. Breeders show their animals, drive them in competition, and parade them before crowds, alongside a farmers' market and country crafts, in a warm tribute to rural life and a cherished local donkey. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Brandenburg State Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Every two years a different city of Brandenburg hosts the Brandenburg-Tag, a state-wide folk festival celebrating the region's culture and community. The rotating celebration is meant as Brandenburg's own state day, letting residents experience the diversity of the region in one place. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Bravio of the Barrels
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Tuscan hill town of Montepulciano holds the Bravìo delle Botti, a fierce uphill race of wooden barrels. Teams from the town's old districts push their heavy barrels through the steep streets to honor the patron saint, preceded by a grand costumed procession, in a centuries-old contest of strength and civic pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bread and Fire Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the town of Prienai joins bread and fire for a festival in Beržeyno Park. A market of bread artisans, fire-dancers, music, and the baking of rye bread in open ovens fill the day, in a young celebration that revives rural bread traditions in southern Lithuania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bread Day and Cider Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · On the last Sunday of July, the Āraiši Windmill near Cēsis celebrates Latvia's new rye harvest with Bread Day. Bakers from across the country grind the year's first rye flour and bake fresh loaves to share, alongside a cider festival, in a warm tribute to the bread that Latvians have long held sacred. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Breminale
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer for five days, the city of Bremen throws Breminale, a free open-air culture festival on the grassy banks of the Weser. Music, theatre, and art fill the riverside as crowds picnic by the water, in a celebration that began in 1987 as a grassroots event and still keeps its easygoing, people-made spirit. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Brotnjo Grape Harvest Days
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the wine country of Brotnjo around Čitluk celebrates its grape harvest with a five-day festival held since the mid-1900s. Wine tastings, folk music, and community gatherings fill the days, in a warm celebration of a Herzegovinian region where the vine has shaped life and land for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Čitluk, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina region, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Čitluk, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina region, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Brouwersdam Seaside Music Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, tens of thousands gather on the Brouwersdam, a great sea barrier in Zeeland, for two days of music with the waves as a backdrop. Started in 2006 by the local band BLØF, Concert at Sea pairs big-name acts with a carefree beach-party feel right at the water's edge. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Brumath Onion Fair
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the Alsatian town of Brumath, which calls itself the capital of the onion, celebrates the humble bulb with its Foire aux Oignons. Stalls and festivities fill the streets in honor of the region's culinary heritage, in a long-cherished fair that has become an unmissable event in this part of Alsace. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Brussels World-Music Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, a park in Brussels fills with the rhythms of the world for Couleur Café, a festival born in 1990 from a love of African and Caribbean music. Now one of the city's biggest, it brings together sounds from across the globe in a warm, multicultural celebration of music and dance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Brăila City Days
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each mid-August, the Romanian port city of Brăila celebrates itself along the banks of the Danube. The famous International Military Music Festival, folk-craft fairs, parades, and fireworks fill the riverfront for days, in a proud celebration that draws on the city's long history as a great trading port on the river. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Brčko Summer Festival “Savski cvijet”
