festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Bosnian district of Brčko celebrates summer along the Sava River with the month-long Savski cvijet festival. Concerts, exhibitions, sports, and folk performances fill squares, galleries, and riverside venues, in a celebration that honors the town's place on the river and its mix of peoples. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Brčko, Brčko District (Bosnia & Herzegovina, multi-ethnic), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Brčko, Brčko District (Bosnia & Herzegovina, multi-ethnic), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Budapest St. Martin's Day Lantern Parade
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each St. Martin's Day, central Budapest glows with a lantern parade. Costumed marchers carry lit lanterns through the streets behind a brass band on Márton-nap, in a celebration that honors the spirit of the saint and his story beyond the season's goose and new wine. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Budapest Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, Hungary's winemakers gather in the grand courtyards of Buda Castle, high above the Danube, for the Budapest Wine Festival. Visitors taste their way through the country's many wine regions with the whole city spread out below, in a harvest celebration set in one of Budapest's loveliest spots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Buffalo Days
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer on the Island of Love, set in the Mura River near Beltinci, Prekmurje celebrates its old buffalo-herding tradition with Buffalo Days. Families gather to grill fish and share regional dishes, in a warm riverside festival that honors the water buffalo husbandry long woven into life on these floodplains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bujanovac Cultural Summer
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the town of Bujanovac fills a month with arts for its Cultural Summer. Concerts, exhibitions, folk dance, and theater showcase the Serbian, Albanian, and Roma traditions of the town, in a celebration that fosters community across this multiethnic corner of the Preševo Valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bukovynske Zustrichi (International Folklore Festival, Chernivtsi)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the region of Bukovina celebrates its many cultures with Bukovynski Zustrichi, an international folklore festival born in 1990. Folk groups with Ukrainian, Romanian, Polish, and other roots come together for parades, music, and dance, reuniting communities once divided by borders. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bulgarian Plum Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the Bulgarian town of Troyan celebrates its plum harvest and its famous plum brandy with a festival of food and folklore. Concerts, craft and food bazaars, and a colorful costume parade fill the town square, in a celebration of the orchards and the rakia that are central to local life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bulgarian Spring Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each spring, the Bulgarian community of Mykolaiv gathers to celebrate its heritage with a festival of music, dance, and song. Young local ensembles perform traditional Bulgarian pieces at the Ternivka House of Culture, in a warm celebration that keeps the customs of this diaspora community alive in southern Ukraine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bull and Horse Run of Segorbe
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September in Segorbe, the bulls run not alone but flanked by riders on galloping horses, who guide and hold the herd as they thunder down the main street. This daily ritual, called the Entrada, is unlike any other bull-run in Spain, and it dates back to medieval festivities for the town's patron Virgin. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Bumbar Feast
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each autumn, the Barban area celebrates its chestnut harvest with the Bumbar Feast. Villagers gather in the square to roast and taste fresh chestnuts over local wine and folk music, in a celebration that revives the old chestnut-gathering customs of this corner of Istria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Buna Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Serbian town of Zaječar gathers its young people in the leafy Kraljevica park for the Buna Festival, a contemporary music and culture event first held in 2024. Its name recalls the 1883 Timok Rebellion, joining youthful creativity with local memory in a wine region rediscovering its cultural spark. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Burgas International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the seaside city of Burgas hosts Bulgaria's oldest folklore festival, gathering folk ensembles from around the world. They parade through the streets and perform at the open-air theatre in the leafy Sea Garden, filling five days with the songs, dances, and costumes of many lands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Burgebrach Saint John’s Fire
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the village of Burgebrach lights a Saint John's fire at its sports ground. The local sports club gathers the community on the weekend after June 24 for the Johannisfeuer, in a celebration that carries the Franconian midsummer bonfire tradition of the Steigerwald. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Burning Brooms Festival
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each December 7, on the eve of the Immaculate Conception, the village of Jarandilla de la Vera blazes with Los Escobazos. Villagers parade by torchlight, striking one another with burning brooms in showers of sparks, in an ancestral Extremaduran festival that joins fiery spectacle with deep devotion. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Jarandilla de la Vera, La Vera, Spain
festivals.address: Jarandilla de la Vera, La Vera, Spain

festivals.name: Burning of the Ribbons
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each spring, the old university city of Coimbra celebrates Queima das Fitas, the students' week-long farewell to the academic year. Graduates burn the colored ribbons of their faculties, and the city fills with parades, concerts, and the haunting sound of Coimbra's own student fado. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Buso Carnival (Mohács Shrovetide Festival)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter, the Hungarian town of Mohács chases away the cold with Busójárás, when men in frightening masks and shaggy fur costumes roam the streets making a glorious racket. Their noisy parade drives out winter and marks the climax of the Farsang carnival season, in one of Hungary's most famous folk traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Butetown Carnival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August bank holiday, the Cardiff Bay district of Butetown, the old Tiger Bay, fills with the color of its carnival. Parades, music, and dance celebrate the African-Caribbean, Welsh, and immigrant heritage of one of Britain's most diverse dockland communities, in a festival rooted in the 1960s. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Cardiff, Tiger Bay/Butetown community, Cardiff, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Cardiff, Tiger Bay/Butetown community, Cardiff, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Buñol Tomato Fight (La Tomatina)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On the last Wednesday of August, the Spanish town of Buñol erupts into the world's biggest food fight, La Tomatina. For one riotous hour, tens of thousands of people pelt each other with overripe tomatoes until the streets run red, in a gloriously messy tradition that has drawn crowds since 1945. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Bócsa Harvest Days
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the village of Bócsa celebrates its harvest with the Sűreti Napok. A cooking contest in memory of a legendary local farmer and a traditional harvest parade fill the days, in a celebration that revives the 19th-century harvest customs of this small farming community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: C.S. Lewis Festival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, East Belfast honors its famous literary son with the C.S. Lewis Festival. C.S. Lewis Square becomes a Narnia-themed winter wonderland of screenings, costumed storytelling, and family activities, in a celebration of the author whose imagined world began in the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Belfast, East Belfast literary community, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Belfast, East Belfast literary community, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Cabbage Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the Serbian village of Mrčajevci near Čačak fills with the smell of simmering cabbage for Kupusijada. Cooks compete for the best sauerkraut and pork stew as thousands gather to taste, in a festival now decades old that celebrates a humble dish at the heart of Serbian country cooking. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cake and Fountain Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the town of Schwäbisch Hall celebrates its old salt-working heritage with the Kuchen- und Brunnenfest, the Cake and Fountain Festival. Locals in period costume parade through the streets amid music and the festival's namesake rituals, in a lively summer tribute to the salt-workers who once made the town rich. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Calvi Polyphony Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Corsican town of Calvi fills its old citadel and cathedral with voices for a festival of polyphonic singing. Corsican choirs share the stage with singers from around the world, celebrating the island's ancient many-voiced song, a tradition recognized by UNESCO. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Calvià Carnival Parade
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each carnival the Mallorcan town of Calvià holds Sa Rua, a colorful parade in which hundreds of costumed participants take to the streets. The parade keeps the island's pre-Lenten carnival tradition, filling the town with costume and celebration before Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Camellia Exhibition
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the lakeside town of Locarno celebrates the camellia, the flower that thrives in its mild climate, with a great exhibition. Thousands of blooms fill one of the world's largest camellia gardens, drawing flower lovers to wander among the celebrated varieties, in a fragrant tribute to a plant that has become the town's emblem. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Campo Maior People’s Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Every few years, the people of Campo Maior bury their entire town under millions of handmade paper flowers, draping the streets in dazzling color overhead. Working in secret for months, each neighborhood competes to decorate its lanes, in a joyful festival rooted in the old celebrations of Saint John. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Campo Maior, Alentejo - Alto Alentejo, Portugal
festivals.address: Campo Maior, Alentejo - Alto Alentejo, Portugal

festivals.name: Canal Street (Arendal Summer Music Festival)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Norwegian harbor town of Arendal fills with music for Canal Street, a festival spanning jazz, rock, and pop. Launched in the 1990s, it offers free concerts on the waterfront and the beloved BarneStreet children's parade, in a celebration that turns the whole city center into a stage by the sea. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Arendal, Arendal and Southern Norway, Norway
festivals.address: Arendal, Arendal and Southern Norway, Norway

festivals.name: Candlemas
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · On the evening of February 2, children across Luxembourg light up the dusk with homemade lanterns, going door to door for the old custom of Liichtmëssdag. They sing a traditional song asking for sweets and treats, in a gentle midwinter tradition with roots reaching back centuries. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Candlemas Botarga of Retiendas
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each February 2, the village of Retiendas celebrates Candlemas with its Botarga. A young man dons the patchwork suit of red, yellow, and green, with mask and bells, to lead the day, in a celebration that blends pre-Christian fertility rites with Catholic devotion in rural Guadalajara. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cannstatter Volksfest
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, Stuttgart throws the Cannstatter Volksfest, one of Europe's biggest folk fairs and a close cousin of Munich's Oktoberfest. For seventeen days, beer tents, carnival rides, and parades fill the fairground, in a celebration that began in 1818 as a harvest fair after years of hardship. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cantabrian Anchovy and Canning Fair
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the town of Santoña celebrates its famous anchovy with a fishing and canning fair. Fish markets, tastings of freshly opened tins, and cooking demonstrations fill the days, in a celebration of the anchovy fleet and the canning factories at the heart of the town's maritime pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cantanhede Agricultural, Commercial, and Industrial Fair (EXPOFACIC)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Portuguese town of Cantanhede hosts EXPOFACIC, a ten-day fair of farming, industry, and trade. Livestock and product displays mingle with concerts and entertainment, drawing big crowds to the heart of the Bairrada wine country, in a celebration grown from the region's old rural markets. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cante Alentejano UNESCO Anniversary Festival (Serpa)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · In the Alentejo town of Serpa, this festival celebrates Cante Alentejano, the slow, soulful group singing of southern Portugal, carried by voices alone with no instruments. Honoring its place on UNESCO's heritage list, the event brings choirs together for concerts and friendly competition in one of the song's heartlands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cardiff Mela
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, Cardiff Bay hosts the Cardiff Mela, a free street festival celebrating the city's many cultures and especially its South Asian community. Music, dance, fashion, and food stalls draw tens of thousands, in a joyful gathering whose name comes from the South Asian word for a coming-together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Cardiff, Cardiff's South Asian and multicultural community, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Cardiff, Cardiff's South Asian and multicultural community, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Carinthian Referendum Day
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Every October 10, Carinthians mark their state holiday in remembrance of the 1920 referendum that kept the region within Austria. In Klagenfurt and beyond, ceremonies recall a vote that decided the borders and shaped the identity of the province, in a solemn celebration of a defining moment in Carinthian history. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Carinthian Tourist Week
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, Slovenia's Koroška region holds its Tourist Week, ten days of festivities that have welcomed summer for nearly seventy years. Towns across the mountainous district fill with music, sport, and local culture, in one of the region's oldest and best-loved gatherings. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Črna na Koroškem, Koroška (Slovenian Carinthia), Slovenia
festivals.address: Črna na Koroškem, Koroška (Slovenian Carinthia), Slovenia

festivals.name: Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the southern city of Maastricht and its neighbors give themselves over to Vastelaovend, a week of costumes, masks, and round-the-clock street parties. A Prince Carnival is crowned to reign over the fun, and float parades and brass bands fill the squares in Limburg's biggest celebration. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Carnival of Binche (Gilles & Orange Throwing)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year, the Belgian town of Binche holds a carnival so old and treasured it is honored by UNESCO. On the big day, the Gilles, costumed in bright wool and padded with straw, dance through the streets and toss oranges to the crowd for good luck, in one of Europe's most singular folk traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carnival of Cádiz
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the seaside city of Cádiz fills with the sharp, funny songs of its carnival, where choirs called chirigotas and comparsas roam the streets poking fun at the year's news. Famous across Spain for its wit, it grew from the city's old trading ties to Venice and Genoa into a riot of music and satire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carnival of King Pitoc
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each Carnival, the lakeside town of Brissago crowns its King Pitoc. Costumed parades wind through the town and end with the burning of a clown effigy that marks the close of carnival, in a celebration that reflects the rural, lakeside carnival spirit of Ticino. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carnival of Lantz
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Carnival, the village of Lantz reenacts the capture and burning of the mythical bandit Miel-Otxin. The whole community joins in wooden masks and costumes for the ancient rite, in a celebration rooted in Basque-Pyrenean folklore and the old struggle of order against chaos. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carnival of Silió
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each first Sunday of January, the Cantabrian village of Silió stages the wild pagan carnival of La Vijanera. Thousands in elaborate masks, the bell-clad Zarramacos and the Oso bear among them, parade in an open-air theater of folklore, in a celebration rooted in ancient winter-solstice rites to drive out the old year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carolers of Chernivtsi
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each Christmas, the Ukrainian city of Chernivtsi fills its Central Square with the sound of ancient carols for "Koliaduiut Chernivtsi." Local folk ensembles in traditional costume revive the old Bukovynian melodies, in a young but heartfelt festival that brings the region's centuries-old caroling tradition out into the heart of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carp Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2027 · Each autumn, the town of Niemodlin celebrates its carp ponds and fishing heritage with a carp festival. A satirical international cartoon contest, children's art, and workshops join traditional gatherings, in a celebration of the centuries-old carp farming of this corner of Silesia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Carpathian Mirror of Cultures Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the Podkarpackie region of southeastern Poland celebrates the many peoples of the Carpathian Mountains with a festival of folk culture. Groups from Poland, Ukraine, Slovakia, Hungary, and beyond bring their music and dance together, joined by ecumenical ceremonies, in a celebration of a borderland where many cultures and faiths meet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Catalonia April Fair (Seville Fair in Barcelona)
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each spring, a corner of Barcelona transforms into a little slice of Seville for the Feria de Abril, brought north by Andalusian families. Rows of decorated booths fill with flamenco guitar and sevillana dancing, women in ruffled dresses, and horses, in a transplanted southern tradition going strong since 1971. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Catherine's Fair and Home Market
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each November 25, on Saint Catherine's Day, the Slovenian town of Lendava holds a traditional fair. Farmers and artisans fill the market with seasonal produce, handicrafts, homemade wine, and honey, marking the end of the harvest year, in one of the old winter fairs long woven into the country's calendar. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cavan Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each spring, the Irish town of Cavan comes alive with four days of theatre, music, comedy, and community arts. A central event of the local calendar since the early 2010s, it spreads performances across the town and into Con Smith Park, in a celebration that mixes homegrown talent with touring acts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ceahlăul International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the region around the great Ceahlău mountain in Romania's Neamț county celebrates folk traditions with a traveling festival. Music and dance ensembles, local and international, perform across the county over several days, in a celebration that carries the rich rural heritage of Moldavia from town to town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Celebration of Dobrogean Traditions and Crafts
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Constanța celebrates the rich folk heritage of Dobrogea with a festival of crafts, food, and custom. Weavers, woodworkers, and potters demonstrate their skills as music plays and local women cook traditional dishes, in a warm tribute to a region where Romanian, Tatar, Turkish, and Lipovan cultures meet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Celje Castle Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the medieval Old Castle above Celje becomes a dramatic stage for a festival of music and theatre. Concerts, musical theatre, and film fill the ancient fortifications high over the Slovenian city, in a celebration that turns its most iconic landmark into a spectacular setting for the arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cento Carnival of Europe
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each year, the town of Cento fills its medieval streets with towering papier-mâché floats for its famous carnival. Whole districts spend months building grand allegorical creations that parade amid costumed performers and revelry, in one of Italy's most celebrated historic carnivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Central Finland Sauna Rally
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Held alongside Finland's great rally race near Jyväskylä, the Saunaralli is a gleeful parody of the sport. Instead of racing for speed, visitors travel at their leisure from sauna to sauna across the region, in a celebration that merges two pillars of Finnish life, motorsport and the sauna, into pure relaxation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Jyväskylä (central meeting point), Western Finland, Finland
festivals.address: Jyväskylä (central meeting point), Western Finland, Finland

festivals.name: Cerknica Carnival (Mask Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the whole town of Cerknica throws itself into one of Slovenia's biggest and most inventive carnivals. Giant homemade masks and figures from local legend parade through the streets, washed down with sweet carnival doughnuts, in a celebration that keeps centuries of mask-making alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cerknica Night
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each year, around its town holiday, the town of Cerknica throws a night festival under the stars. Folk dance, music, majorettes, and fireworks fill the evening, in a celebration that joins modern festivity to municipal pride and a touch of folk tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cetinje Biennale (Contemporary Art)
festivals.description: Mid July 2027 · Every two years, the old royal capital of Cetinje fills with avant-garde art for the Cetinje Biennale. Artists from Montenegro and around the world show works in galleries and public spaces, with performances and installations, in an international forum founded in 1990 to invigorate the country's contemporary art scene. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ceuta Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the North African city of Ceuta fills with the costumes, music, and parades of its carnival. Singing groups compete with funny, satirical songs and the streets turn into open-air parties, in a lively celebration that mirrors the city's mixed, working-class spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ceuta Day
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 2, the Spanish city of Ceuta celebrates its founding and history with a day of civic ceremony. Official events at the Teatro del Revellín honor figures like Pedro de Meneses, the city's first governor, in a celebration of local pride and the milestones that shaped this enclave on the North African coast. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Chalon in the Street Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · For four days each July, the town of Chalon-sur-Saône hands its streets over to performers for one of France's biggest festivals of street theatre. Acrobats, actors, and offbeat spectacles turn squares and alleys into open-air stages, in an event that has championed street arts since 1987. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chambord Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the grand Château de Chambord in the Loire Valley fills its formal gardens with music for a two-week classical festival. Orchestras and chamber ensembles play works from Renaissance to modern, in a celebration that brings live music back to the royal château built by François I, a jewel of the Loire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chambérat Fair (Melons, Donkeys and Cheese)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August, the French village of Chambérat holds a fair celebrating its local cow's cheese, sweet melons, and the skills of horses and donkeys. Agricultural markets, folk performances, and family fun fill the day, in a celebration of country produce and tradition with roots said to reach back to Gaulish times. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Charivari Village (Back-to-School) Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the town of Cergy marks the start of the school year with the Charivari au Village. Artisan markets, children's games, lantern-decorating, folk dance, and a lamp-lit torch procession fill the day, in a celebration whose name recalls the old, joyfully noisy festival parades. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Charles V Renaissance Market
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the city of Melilla turns back to the 16th century with a Renaissance market honoring the era of Emperor Charles V. Costumed vendors, period music, artisan workshops, and a parade of some two hundred performers recreate an old fair, in a celebration of the city's deep historical roots on the North African coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cheese and Wine Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the Slovenian village of Bohinjska Bistrica celebrates its mountain dairy heritage with a festival of cheese and wine. Visitors sample high-altitude alpine cheeses ripened on the nearby pastures alongside Slovenian wines, in a warm late-October tribute to the centuries-old herding life of the Bohinj valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cheese festival in Gruyères
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each year, the medieval town of Gruyères holds a one-day market in honor of the cheese that bears its name. Around twenty stands offer the region's Gruyère AOP and local specialties as cheesemakers work a great pot over a wood fire, keeping alive the old craft at the heart of this storybook Swiss town. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Chernihiv Citywide Ivana Kupala Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each midsummer, the Ukrainian city of Chernihiv gathers in its central park for Ivana Kupala, an ancient night of folk magic. Young people weave flower wreaths and float them on the water, couples leap over bonfires at midnight, and old Polissya songs fill the air, in a celebration whose roots run back to pre-Christian times. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chernivtsi Kupala Fest
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each June 23 and 24, the city of Chernivtsi revives the midsummer rites of Ivan Kupala with Kupala Fest. Folk craft workshops, vyshyvanka fashion shows, and a bonfire ceremony fill the night, in a celebration of the old solstice holiday brought back by local cultural activists. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cherry Festival (Kyustendil)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Bulgarian town of Kyustendil, known as the country's fruit garden, celebrates its cherry harvest with a three-day festival. Cherry-themed exhibits, art, and tasting booths fill the central square and streets, in a warm community tribute to the orchards that have long given this town its sweet renown. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Cherry Festival of Villarcayo
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Villarcayo in mountainous Burgos celebrates its cherry harvest with picking contests, fruit parades, and communal feasts. The crowning of the King Cherry and the tasting of local cherry treats mark the festivities, in a warm celebration of the orchards woven into the life of this northern Spanish town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chervene Vyno Red Wine Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each winter before the Old New Year, the Transcarpathian town of Mukachevo celebrates its red wine with the Chervene Vyno festival. Dozens of local winemakers pour their vintages in a festive market as carolers sing and traditional dishes steam outdoors, in a celebration born of the region's long love of home winemaking and the holidays. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mukachevo, Subcarpathian Rusyn/Hungarian wine culture, Ukraine
festivals.address: Mukachevo, Subcarpathian Rusyn/Hungarian wine culture, Ukraine

festivals.name: Chestnut Fair
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each autumn, the village of Bocognano celebrates the chestnut, long a staple of Corsican life, with its three-day Fiera di a Castagna. Stalls offer everything from roasted chestnuts to chestnut flour, beer, and cakes, alongside local crafts, in a harvest fair that has drawn crowds since 1982. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Chestnut Festival (All Saints)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Around All Saints' Day, Catalonia keeps the cozy autumn tradition of La Castanyada, gathering to remember the dead over seasonal treats. Roasted chestnuts and sweet potatoes warm the chilly evenings, along with little marzipan sweets called panellets, in a gentle family custom found across Barcelona. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Chestnut Festival of Los Corrales de Buelna
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Los Corrales de Buelna roasts its chestnuts for the magosta. Neighbors gather around bonfires for chestnuts, cider, and folk music, often on All Saints' Day, in a cozy celebration of the harvest season common to the valleys of Cantabria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Cantabria, Los Corrales de Buelna, Cantabria, Spain
festivals.address: Cantabria, Los Corrales de Buelna, Cantabria, Spain

festivals.name: Chicharro Festival (Horse Mackerel Festival)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the fishing village of Ribeira Quente celebrates its horse mackerel, the chicharro. Grilled chicharro and seafood, music, and folk performances fill a fair beneath the palms, in a celebration held since 1988 that reinforces the bonds of this Azorean fishing community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ribeira Quente, Ribeira Quente, São Miguel, Portugal
festivals.address: Ribeira Quente, Ribeira Quente, São Miguel, Portugal

festivals.name: Chienbäse Fire Procession
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · On the Sunday after Ash Wednesday, the Swiss town of Liestal blazes to life with the Chienbäse, a fiery night procession. Marchers carry burning bundles of pinewood on their heads and haul flaming carts through the old town, filling the streets with fire and smoke, in a dramatic rite to drive out winter and welcome Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chiesanuova Castle Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each summer, the small village of Chiesanuova gathers near its church and fortress for a castle festival. Food stalls, live music, and community games fill several festive evenings, in a celebration of the rural customs and the close-knit solidarity of this San Marino hamlet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Chiesanuova, Chiesanuova, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Chiesanuova, Chiesanuova, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Children & Youth Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Every couple of years, the Lithuanian town of Telšiai gathers children and young people for an international folklore festival. Young performers share traditional songs, dances, and crafts from Lithuania and abroad, in a celebration rooted in the town's place as a heart of Samogitian culture and devoted to passing the old ways on. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chimney Cake Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, central Buda fills with the scent of kürtőskalács for the Chimney Cake Festival. Bakers roast dozens of the spiral cakes over open flame in many flavors, in a celebration of a sweet pastry deeply tied to Hungarian and Transylvanian-Székely culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chios Music Festival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year, the Greek island of Chios hosts a music festival spanning classical, traditional, and contemporary sounds. Greek and international artists perform at historic and cultural venues across the island, in a young festival that builds on the rich musical heritage of this storied corner of the Aegean. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Choral Arts Festival 'Podillia Sings Leontovych'
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each December, the Ukrainian city of Vinnytsia honors its native composer Mykola Leontovych with a festival of choral music. Choirs raise their voices in his memory, including the famous carol the world knows as "Carol of the Bells," in a celebration of the rich choral tradition of the Podillia region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Choral Folk Song Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, the town of Prevalje gathers its folk-song choirs for a choral festival. Ensembles sing the traditional Slovenian songs, in a celebration mounted by the local cultural society that honors the rich vocal tradition of the Koroška region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Chornomorskyi Steppe Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each winter, the Ukrainian city of Mykolaiv celebrates its many peoples with the Chornomorskyi Steppe festival, first held in 2024. Ethnic performances, talks, workshops, and films explore the traditions of a city long home to Ukrainians, Greeks, Germans, and more, in a celebration built around the theme of unity in diversity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Christ Reigns! Alleluia!
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each year, the village of Rudamina in the Vilnius region fills its church with sacred song for the festival Kristus karaliauja! Aleliuja! Founded in 2010, it gathers local choirs, church musicians, and visitors from Poland and Lithuania to sing liturgical music, in a celebration rooted in the deep Catholic faith of the region's Polish community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Christmas Among Romanians
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · For over three decades, the town of Sfântu Gheorghe has gathered Romanian choirs and folk groups for a festival of Christmas carols and winter customs. Held in a Hungarian-majority county, it brings together performers from across the region, in a heartfelt celebration that keeps Romanian Orthodox caroling traditions alive in this corner of Transylvania. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Christmas Chime (Nativity Plays & Carolers Festival)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each Christmas, the Donbas village of Zvanivka revives the Carpathian holiday traditions of its Lemko and Boyko families. Costumed carolers carry a hand-built Vertep nativity scene from house to house, singing the old highland carols, in a moving celebration of a mountain heritage carried far from home by displaced families. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Christmas Notes (Christmas Eve Concert)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each Christmas Eve, St. Peter's Square hosts a televised concert of sacred and seasonal music, with celebrated singers and dancers performing under the open sky. Held after the evening Mass, it joins the church's solemn ritual with the warmth of popular performance for a worldwide audience. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Chrobry Fest 1025 (Royal Coronation Re-enactment, Opole)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · In Opole, the open-air village museum hosts Chrobry Fest, a journey back to the early Middle Ages marking a thousand years since Poland's first royal coronation. Costumed performers recreate the world of King Bolesław Chrobry, crowned in 1025, in a vivid celebration tied to nationwide festivities for the anniversary. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Château-Gontier Comic Book Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Château-Gontier celebrates the comic strip with a comic book festival. Some fifty artists join drawing sessions, exhibits, and youth programs over a weekend, in a celebration of the French love of the bande dessinée. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cieszanów Rock Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the small Podkarpackie town of Cieszanów fills with guitars and crowds for a three-day rock and punk festival. Major Polish bands and international acts play across multiple stages as thousands camp nearby, in a youthful celebration that has become part of the modern identity of this corner of southern Poland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Citrus Festival (Nadur, Gozo)
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each year, the Gozo village of Nadur celebrates its citrus harvest in the main square. Stalls of citrus liquors, pastries, and savory treats join arts, crafts, and live music, in a young celebration that promotes the fruity orchards long grown around the village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Citywide Free Concerts
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Every June 21, the streets and squares of Monaco fill with free music for the Fête de la Musique. From afternoon into the night, local bands and artists of every genre play in public spaces across the principality, joining the worldwide celebration of music that began in France in 1982. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ciutadella Saint John’s Festivities
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 23, the Menorcan town of Ciutadella holds its ancient Sant Joan festival, where black Menorcan horses rear high on their hind legs in the middle of cheering crowds. Riders from every village lead their adorned horses through the packed streets, in one of the Balearics' most thrilling summer traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Clare Island Folk Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Gaeltacht island of Clare Island hosts a folk festival. Traditional Irish music sessions, sean-nós singing, dancing, and crafts fill the days, in an intimate celebration that spotlights the language and music of this island off the Mayo coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Clare Island, County Mayo (Clare Island), Ireland
festivals.address: Clare Island, County Mayo (Clare Island), Ireland

festivals.name: Clifden Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each spring, the Connemara town of Clifden fills with fiddles and song for its traditional music festival. Run by the local Comhaltas, it puts young musicians, a school marching band, workshops, and a Saturday open-air concert at its heart, in a warm celebration rooted in the Gaelic heritage of this western Irish town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Clonmel Junction Arts Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July since 2001, the Tipperary town of Clonmel hosts the ten-day Junction Arts Festival. Theatre, music, visual art, and dance fill the heart of the town, in a celebration created to bring new life to Clonmel's cultural scene by joining local creativity with national and international talent. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cluj Days
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the Transylvanian city of Cluj-Napoca celebrates itself with Cluj Days, a vibrant Clujiversary for the whole community. Concerts, events, and festivities fill the city as it honors its institutions and its lively cultural diversity, in a colorful celebration of one of Romania's most dynamic cities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cluj-Napoca Folk Music and Folk Dance Meeting
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each year, the city of Cluj-Napoca celebrates Transylvanian-Hungarian folk culture with a meeting of music and dance. Concerts, a children's dance gala, a crafts fair, and nightly participatory táncház sessions fill the days, in a warm celebration rooted in the Hungarian folk-revival movement that keeps these traditions alive. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Coastal Culture Days (Lysøysundet Boat Regatta & Coastal Heritage)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Norwegian fishing village of Lysøysundet celebrates its life by the sea with Coastal Culture Days. Grown from a traditional boat regatta first held in 1985, the festival now brings markets, music, dance, and fresh seafood, in a warm celebration of the maritime heritage that has shaped this coastal community. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Coburg Onion Market
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Bavarian city of Coburg fills its historic Marktplatz with the Zwiebelmarkt, a traditional onion market. Stalls heaped with onions and autumn produce recall a fair that reaches back to the 17th century, when peasants gathered here to sell their harvest, in a cheerful celebration of the season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Coimbra City and Queen Santa Isabel Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the city of Coimbra celebrates itself and its beloved patron, the holy Queen Isabel, with ten days of festivities. Processions in her honor mix with concerts, fairs, and fireworks along the Mondego River, tying the city's deep history to a lively summer party. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cologne Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the city of Cologne erupts into one of Germany's wildest carnivals, with costumes, balls, and packed street parties. The climax is the great Rose Monday parade, when floats and marching bands roll through the city tossing sweets and flowers to the roaring crowds. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cologne Lights
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · On a summer night, the Rhine at Cologne becomes a stage for Kölner Lichter, when fireworks burst from barges in time to music and a parade of lit-up boats glides past. Hundreds of thousands line both banks for the show, a sparkling tradition the city has loved since 2001. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Columbian Festivals
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Canary island of La Gomera celebrates its place in history with a month of festivities marking Christopher Columbus's last stop in Europe. Concerts, theater, exhibitions, and sports fill towns across the island, in a lively celebration recalling the day the explorer took on his final supplies before sailing into the unknown. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: San Sebastián de La Gomera, La Gomera, Spain
festivals.address: San Sebastián de La Gomera, La Gomera, Spain

festivals.name: Columbus Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the streets of Vila Baleira on Porto Santo become a living history book for the Festival Colombo. Over four days, costumed locals reenact Christopher Columbus's stay on the island during the Age of Discovery, in a colorful celebration of the legend that the great explorer once lived on this small Madeiran isle. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Comapedrosa Mountain Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the Andorran village of Arinsal hosts a mountain festival built around the thrill of high-altitude trail running. Runners tackle a series of races, from a steep vertical climb to a full sky marathon, across the rugged Comapedrosa Natural Park, in a celebration of the mountain sports woven into life in the Pyrenees. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Comino Santa Marija Feast (Boat Pilgrimage)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the tiny Maltese island of Comino revives a centuries-old feast for Santa Marija, the Assumption of the Virgin. Her statue is carried by boat in pilgrimage from Gozo, followed by open-air Masses, a feast dinner, and Maltese folk music, in a heartfelt celebration brought back to life on this all-but-uninhabited island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Commerce Workers' Weekend (Iceland)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · On the first weekend of August, nearly all of Iceland heads out of town for the country's biggest holiday weekend, gathering at open-air festivals of music, food, and fun. It started long ago as a simple day off for shop workers, and has grown into the high point of the Icelandic summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Comrat City Day
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Comrat, capital of Gagauzia, celebrates its founding with a day of festivity. A folk-art fair, a parade down the main street, concerts, and evening fireworks fill the city as local artisans show their crafts, in a proud civic celebration of the heart of this Turkic region of Moldova. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Comrie Flambeaux Procession
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each Hogmanay, the village of Comrie carries long birch torches through its streets at midnight. The Flambeaux Procession cleanses the village as the New Year arrives, in a celebration that embodies the Scottish tradition of using fire to greet the turning of the year. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Concert With the Poor and for the Poor
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each Christmas, the Vatican's great audience hall fills with sacred and holiday music for a concert that gives its best seats to the homeless, migrants, and the poor as guests of honor. Begun in 2015, it offers the beauty of fine orchestras and choirs to those most often left out, in a spirit of solidarity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Constitution Day (Denmark)
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Every June 5, Denmark marks Grundlovsdag, the day in 1849 when the country signed its first constitution and stepped away from absolute monarchy. Often treated as the nearest thing to a national day, it is a time for outdoor political speeches and gatherings, with democracy itself the thing being celebrated. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Contemporary Arts & Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Shkodër fills Rozafa Castle and its old venues with the contemporary arts of Zâ Fest. Music, theater, film, and dance by Albanian and international artists fill the days, in a celebration held since 2017 that ties bold new work to Albanian cultural heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Corcrain Tenth Night Bonfire
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · On the night of July 10, the Corcrain neighborhood of Portadown lights a towering bonfire, part of the loyalist community's run-up to the Twelfth of July. The blaze draws crowds together in a long-running local tradition that marks the height of the marching season. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Corfu Carnival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the island of Corfu throws one of Greece's most elegant and playful carnivals, a tradition shaped by four centuries of Venetian rule. Masked balls, satirical floats, and parades fill the old town, ending in a grand procession through streets that still feel half-Italian. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cork Harbour Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the city of Cork celebrates its life by the water with Ireland's largest maritime festival. Over ten days, more than a hundred events fill the city and harbour with boats, music, and seafaring fun, in a celebration that honors Cork's identity as one of the world's great natural deep-water ports. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cork Midsummer Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the city of Cork fills with theatre, music, dance, and street art for its Midsummer Festival. A showcase of the city's famously independent creative spirit, the multi-arts program often draws on local history and stories, in a celebration that lights up Cork at the height of the Irish summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Cork, Cork City, County Cork, Munster Province, Ireland
festivals.address: Cork, Cork City, County Cork, Munster Province, Ireland

festivals.name: Cornouaille Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Quimper celebrates Breton culture with the Cornouaille Festival, days of traditional music, dance, and feasting. A grand parade fills the streets with pipers and dancers in regional costume, in a proud showcase of the songs and customs of this corner of Brittany. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi Festival of Granada
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Andalusian city of Granada celebrates Corpus Christi with a week that joins solemn religious procession and a riotous fair. The dragon-like Tarasca leads the festivities through the streets, in a celebration that captures the baroque faith and the irrepressible festive spirit of Granada. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi Lake Procession
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each Corpus Christi, the Bavarian village of Seehausen carries its celebration out onto Lake Staffelsee. After Mass, parishioners and visitors process by boat across the water to the lake's islands, in an old and exuberant Upper Bavarian tradition that turns one of the region's most joyful feast days into a journey by water. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Cossack Kosh
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October 14, on the feast of the Intercession, the Mamaeva Sloboda open-air complex in Kyiv hosts the Cossack Kosh. Martial displays, music, and folk traditions honor the Zaporozhian Cossacks, who held this holy day sacred, in a celebration of the warrior brotherhood at the heart of Ukrainian history. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cossack Spas Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Ancient Kyiv park near the capital hosts the Cossack Spas, a festival of Ukraine's martial and folk traditions. Demonstrations of Cossack fighting arts, wrestling, and traditional sports fill the grounds, in a celebration rooted in the warrior spirit and communal heritage of the old Cossack and Kievan past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kopachiv (Park Kyivska Rus), Kyiv Oblast (Ukrainian Cossack tradition), Ukraine
festivals.address: Kopachiv (Park Kyivska Rus), Kyiv Oblast (Ukrainian Cossack tradition), Ukraine

festivals.name: Country Bauska Country Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer since 1999, the Latvian town of Bauska fills with twang and steel for its country music festival. Latvian country acts and kindred performers take the stage, in a celebration that has made this small town an unlikely but beloved home for country and folk music in the Baltic. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Covasna City Days
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each first weekend of September, the spa town of Covasna celebrates its City Days. Concerts and festivity gather the community, in a civic celebration begun in the early 2000s that lifts up the regional Székely culture of this mountain town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cowal Highland Gathering
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Scottish town of Dunoon hosts the Cowal Gathering, one of the world's largest Highland Games. Over three days, top pipers, Highland dancers, and athletes compete in caber tossing and stone putting, in a grand celebration of Scottish Highland culture held in Argyll since 1894. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Crafts Alive
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year, Prague's New Town Hall fills with the buzz of saws and the spin of potters' wheels for Crafts Alive. Vocational schools and master artisans lead hands-on workshops in woodworking, ceramics, sewing, and more, in a lively celebration that invites visitors to try the traditional Czech trades for themselves. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Prague, Czech vocational and crafts community, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Prague, Czech vocational and crafts community, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Crafts Festival "In the Malko Tarnovo Way – From the Source of Strandzha"
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the town of Malko Tarnovo gathers its artisans for a Strandzha crafts festival. Weaving, wood carving, pottery, and straw-ornament making fill live workshops and a market of handmade goods, in a celebration that works to preserve the endangered village crafts of the Strandzha. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Craigyhill Eleventh Night Bonfire
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the loyalist community of Craigyhill in Larne builds one of Northern Ireland's largest bonfires, a towering stack of wooden pallets. Lit on the Eleventh Night before the Twelfth of July marches, the great fire blazes into the sky, in a tradition that has become a point of fierce local pride. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Cranger Fair
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Herne hosts the Cranger Kirmes, the largest funfair in North Rhine-Westphalia and one of the biggest in Germany. Hundreds of rides, games, and food stalls line the canal, drawing around four million visitors to a fair that began centuries ago as a humble horse market. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Crayfish Festival (Ao)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the village of Ao celebrates the opening of the crayfish season. Traditional foods and family activities gather the community around the freshwater catch, in a celebration of a long-held village tradition in this corner of Väike-Maarja parish. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cres Sheep Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the village of Orlec celebrates the island's native sheep with the Cres Sheep Festival. Prize animals, shearing demonstrations, local cheese tasting, and folk music gather the shepherd families, in a celebration of the pastoral heritage at the heart of the island of Cres. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cres Summer Carnival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Adriatic island town of Cres does something unusual: it holds a carnival in the warm season rather than in winter. Costumed groups from near and far parade through the old streets in a colorful celebration that, after a quarter-century, has become a high point of the island's summer. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Cross-Border Dance Festival Visavì
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the contemporary dance festival Visavì leaps across the Slovenian-Italian border in the Goriška region. Performances by regional and international troupes play out in venues on both sides of the frontier, in a celebration born of the partnership between the twin cities of Gorizia and Nova Gorica. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Crown of Dniester Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the mighty Khotyn Fortress on the Dniester hosts the Crown of the Dniester, a historical reenactment festival debuting in 2025. Armored knights clash in tournaments, archers compete, and a living-history camp springs up by the river, in a vivid celebration of Ukraine's medieval and Cossack past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Khotyn, Bukovina (Northern Bukovina, Ukraine) / Khotyn Historical Region, Ukraine
festivals.address: Khotyn, Bukovina (Northern Bukovina, Ukraine) / Khotyn Historical Region, Ukraine

festivals.name: Csillagocska Children and Youth Folk Dance Meeting
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the city of Oradea gathers children and young people to learn and perform Hungarian folk dances at the Csillagocska meeting. A weekend of joyful dancing celebrates the heritage of one of Romania's historic Hungarian communities, in a festival devoted to handing the old steps down to the youngest feet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cultura Nova
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Dutch city of Heerlen fills a leafy park with contemporary theatre, music, dance, and art for Cultura Nova. Founded in the 1980s by a local theatre company, the multi-disciplinary festival has grown into one of the region's cultural highlights, in a celebration of bold and inventive performance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Heerlen, Heerlen (South Limburg arts community), Netherlands
festivals.address: Heerlen, Heerlen (South Limburg arts community), Netherlands

festivals.name: Cultural Meeting of Larissa
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Greek city of Larissa gathers dozens of rural dance troupes from across Thessaly for a folk-dance festival. Daily processions, workshops, and performances of traditional dances fill a week of celebration, in an event begun in 2016 to bind the city to the rich folk heritage of its countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Culture and Traditions of Sardinia
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the mining town of Buggerru celebrates Sardinian folk culture over an evening or two. Local traditions take the stage in this corner of the Sulcis, in a young celebration begun around 2024 of the customs of a region better known for its mines. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Culture for Culture (Alba County Traditions and Customs Contest)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the Transylvanian town of Blaj hosts a multi-stage contest celebrating the rural heritage of Alba County. Song, dance, food, and crafts from across the region come together in friendly competition, in a festival created expressly to preserve and show off the living traditions of the Romanian countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Alba Iulia, Transylvanian Romanian (Alba County), Romania
festivals.address: Alba Iulia, Transylvanian Romanian (Alba County), Romania

festivals.name: Curonian Spit Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the seaside resort of Nida on the Curonian Spit fills its historic venues with chamber music. Ensembles and soloists play amid the great dunes and quiet lagoons of Lithuania Minor, in an intimate festival that carries on the spit's long tradition of small, heartfelt musical gatherings. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Customs and Traditions Festival (Capra & Bear Maskers, Târgu Ocna)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each New Year, the town of Târgu Ocna fills with the maskers of its winter customs festival. Costumed players in goat and bear masks dance with drums and bells to bless homes and drive off evil, in a celebration that carries the centuries-old Moldavian winter rituals. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Câmpulung City Days
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the old Romanian town of Câmpulung celebrates itself with City Days, a civic festival of music, crafts, and community cheer. Folk and pop concerts fill the public squares as artisans sell their wares, in a warm gathering that draws on the deep medieval heritage of one of Romania's oldest towns. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Cântecele Oltului National Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the spa town of Călimănești hosts Cântecele Oltului, a folklore festival celebrating the music and costume of the Olt River valley. Singers compete, dancers parade in traditional dress, and concerts fill the central park, in an event that has launched folk talent since 1968. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Căluțul Românesc (Căluș Ritual Dance Festival)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the Romanian town of Caracal celebrates the Căluș, the all-male ritual dance of Oltenia that UNESCO holds as a treasure of human heritage. Dancers leap, stomp, and click their heels in fast, acrobatic figures believed to heal and protect, in a vivid display of an ancient rite of purification and fertility. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Călărași City Days
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each late September, the city of Călărași celebrates its founding of 1593 with City Days. Concerts, parades, exhibitions, and fireworks fill the Danube waterfront, in a celebration that joins the city's patronal feast to free open-air festivity for thousands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: DacFest "Under the Wolf's Sign"
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year near Simeria, the festival DacFest brings the ancient world back to life on the hill of Măgura Uroiului. Reenactors recreate the life and battles of the Dacians and Romans who once held this land, in a vivid celebration of a deep heritage and the modern fascination with Romania's pre-Roman ancestors. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dachauer Volksfest
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Upper Bavarian town of Dachau throws its great Volksfest beneath the old palace. Beer tents, fairground rides, a family day, and a famous fireworks show over the Dachau Palace draw hundreds of thousands, in a beloved folk and marksmen's festival in the classic Bavarian style. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Dacian-Roman Festival "Drubeta"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the city of Drobeta-Turnu Severin, once the Roman gateway into Dacia, becomes an open-air museum of the ancient world. A three-day living-history festival fills the streets with reenacted battles and a grand parade of Dacian warriors and Roman soldiers, on the ground where Trajan's famous bridge once crossed the Danube. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dahlia Flower Parade
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the small village of Loenhout stages Belgium's largest flower parade, with floats covered head to toe in millions of dahlias. Teams of neighbors spend months pinning each bloom into place, then send their giant flowered creations rolling through the streets to music and crowds. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dainava Region Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Lithuanian spa town of Druskininkai fills with folk costume and song for the Dainava Region Folklore Festival. Ensembles perform the music and dances of the Dzūkija region in competitions and a craft fair, in a colorful celebration of the deep ethnographic heritage of this corner of Lithuania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dalane Blues Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each summer, the Norwegian coastal town of Egersund fills its square and venues with blues for the Dalane Bluesfestival. Run largely by volunteers, the three-day festival blends blues and rock with a warm community spirit, in a celebration that has been a cultural institution for the Dalane region since 2003. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dark Season Blues
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each late October, the town of Longyearbyen warms its darkening days with the Dark Season Blues. Blues musicians fill the polar twilight with music the locals simply call 'the blues', in a celebration begun in 2003 to bring warmth and community to the start of the Svalbard night. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Daugavpils International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the Latvian city of Daugavpils gathers ethnographic ensembles for an international folklore festival. Groups from Latgale and abroad fill the city with traditional song, dance, and costume, in a warm celebration of cultural exchange that puts the rich folk heritage of the Latgale region center stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Day of Opatija (Feast of St. James)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July 25, the town of Opatija honors its patron, St. James, on its founding feast day. A Mass at the Church of St. James gives way to a procession of his statue through the streets and along the sea, in a celebration that ties the resort town to the saint at its origins. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Day of Our Lady of Victory
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 8, the city of Melilla honors its patroness, Our Lady of Victory. Special Masses and a procession through the streets gather the faithful and the city's regional societies, in a deeply religious celebration of a Virgin venerated as Melilla's protectress since the 19th century. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Melilla, Catholic community of Melilla, Spain
festivals.address: Melilla, Catholic community of Melilla, Spain

festivals.name: Day of the Azores
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each year, the islands of the Azores celebrate their own day, honoring the self-government they won in 1976 and the distinct identity of this Atlantic archipelago. Official ceremonies, parades, and community feasts bring out island pride, far out in the ocean between Europe and America. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Day of the Indians
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · On Carnival Monday, the streets of Santa Cruz de La Palma fill with white-clad revelers and clouds of talcum powder for the Day of the Indians. In a playful parody of wealthy emigrants returning from Cuba, the Gran Desembarco procession reenacts their grand arrival, in a flamboyant celebration of the island's deep ties to the Caribbean. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Santa Cruz de La Palma, La Palma (Santa Cruz de la Palma), Spain
festivals.address: Santa Cruz de La Palma, La Palma (Santa Cruz de la Palma), Spain

festivals.name: Days of Alka and Our Lady of Sinj
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, the town of Sinj honors the miraculous Madonna who, by local belief, saved it from an Ottoman siege in 1715. At the heart of the celebration is the Alka, a UNESCO-listed contest where horsemen in old uniforms gallop full speed and try to spear a small iron ring with their lances. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Ardino
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August, the mountain town of Ardino celebrates its people with a week-long festival. A Rhodope food fair, folk dance and song, sports, and a great outdoor concert fill the days, in a celebration of a town of mixed Bulgarian and Turkish heritage in the southern hills. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Artichokes
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each spring, the seaside village of Strunjan celebrates the prized Istrian artichoke with its Days of Artichokes. An open-air market, cooking demonstrations, and farm tours invite visitors to taste the vegetable every way, in a warm celebration of a crop grown along this coast since the days of Venice. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Bucharest
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, Bucharest celebrates the anniversary of its first mention in writing, back in 1459, with the Days of Bucharest. Concerts, dance, and art fill the historic center, in a civic party, running since 1994, that toasts the long history of Romania's lively capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Frane Petrić
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the island town of Cres honors its native son, the Renaissance philosopher Frane Petrić, with an international gathering of scholars. Lectures and discussions explore his work and ideas, in a festival begun in 1993 that has become a fixture of Croatian intellectual life on this quiet Adriatic island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Gagauz Culture
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the southern Bessarabian town of Bolhrad celebrates the heritage of the Gagauz, a Turkic Christian people, with a festival of folk culture. Song-and-dance groups from the Gagauz villages perform in traditional costume amid displays of food and craft, in a warm celebration of a community that settled here over two centuries ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Hungarian Culture
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each year around the Hungarian Day of Culture, the town of Berehove celebrates the heritage of its Hungarian community. Two days of art exhibitions, folk music and dance, and historical commemorations honor the community's traditions, in a warm celebration of Hungarian culture in this corner of Ukraine's Zakarpattia. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Berehove, Hungarian minority in Zakarpattia, Ukraine
festivals.address: Berehove, Hungarian minority in Zakarpattia, Ukraine

festivals.name: Days of Mokranjac
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Serbian town of Negotin honors its native son, the composer Stevan Mokranjac, with a week of classical music. Choir competitions, chamber ensembles, and orchestral concerts keep his legacy alive, in a festival founded in 1966 that has become a proud fixture of the cultural life of eastern Serbia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of National Costumes (Kamnik Costume Parade)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Slovenian town of Kamnik fills its old streets with the colors of tradition for the Days of National Costumes. Folk groups parade in the many regional costumes of Slovenia, in a heritage pageant that gathers the country's varied folk dress in one place, a proud celebration of national identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Petržalka
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Petržalka borough of Bratislava celebrates itself with Dni Petržalky. Four days of concerts, sports, family activities, and a folklore night fill the largest community festival of the district, in a celebration of the distinct identity of this vast residential quarter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Poetry & Wine (Filovci)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the Prekmurje village of Filovci hosts an edition of Slovenia's Days of Poetry and Wine. Prominent Slovene poets read their verse at a local wine gallery as music plays and the region's wines are poured, in a celebration that weaves the country's literary tradition together with Prekmurje's love of the vine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Székely Land
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, Covasna County in the heart of the Székely Land devotes ten days to celebrating Székely Hungarian culture. Concerts, exhibitions, theater, and folk events fill the festival, in a wide-ranging celebration of the music, art, and community life of the Székely people of eastern Transylvania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of the Albanian (Albanian Heritage Showcase, Gjakova)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Gjakova celebrates Albanian heritage over two weekends with the festival Ditët e Shqiptarit. Concerts, folk performances, film, poetry, and traditional food honor Albanian traditions, in a young festival held in a city that has long been a center of Albanian culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of the Fortress
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the old Wallachian capital of Târgoviște celebrates its past beneath the medieval Chindia Tower with the Days of the Fortress. Four days of folk tradition, concerts, and family fun unfold in the historic heart of a city that once ruled the principality. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Days of Tramuntana
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer the northern Cres villages of Beli, Dragozetići, and Predošćica welcome home the Tramuntane, the residents who left for abroad in the mid-20th century, with cultural programs and gatherings. The festival keeps alive the memory of these once-depopulated villages and the people who return to them. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Decorated Streets
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each late August, Barcelona's Sants neighborhood dresses its streets in imaginative decorations for its Festa Major. Like its famous neighbor Gràcia, it sets streets competing for the best display amid nightly concerts, in a celebration that grew from the old village feast of Santa Maria de Sants before the town joined the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Decorated Streets
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August around the Feast of the Assumption, Barcelona's Gràcia neighborhood transforms its streets into wild themed wonderlands. Neighbors spend the year crafting elaborate decorations from recycled materials, competing for the finest display, in a beloved week-long festival rooted in Gràcia's days as an independent village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Delsbo Folk Music Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the heritage farm at Delsbo in Hälsingland fills with fiddle music for the Delsbostämman. Folk musicians, dancers, and families from across the region gather to play and dance the old tunes, in a weekend celebration that stands as a cornerstone of the lively folk-music tradition of this Swedish province. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Democritus Festival of Avdira
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Greek town of Avdira honors its most famous ancient son, the philosopher Democritus, with a community festival. Music, dance, sports, and games spread across the municipality, in a warm celebration that ties this northern Greek town to its illustrious past in ancient Abdera. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Derry Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the city of Derry fills its neighborhoods with ten days of music, theatre, storytelling, and art for Féile Derry. Street performances, workshops, and concerts spread from the Bogside to the city center, in a community arts festival that brings together residents of all backgrounds to celebrate their city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Deruny (Potato Pancake) Charity Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Polissya city of Korosten celebrates the humble potato pancake with a charity festival devoted to deruny. Local families cook and donate the crispy pancakes for a good cause, in a warm gathering that ties the simple, beloved cuisine of northern Ukraine to a spirit of community and generosity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Descent of the Candelieri
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Every August 14, the Sardinian city of Sassari fills with the Faradda, when eleven towering wooden "candles," each shouldered by a guild of dancers, sway and spin their way through the old town. The exhausting, joyful procession ends at a centuries-old church, fulfilling a vow of thanks the city has long kept. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Deventer Book Market
