festivals.description: Early August 2026 · On the first Sunday of August, the old Hanseatic city of Deventer turns into Europe's largest book market, with more than five hundred stalls lining the river and squares. Book lovers come from far and wide to browse mile after mile of secondhand treasures, in a tradition going back to 1989. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Deventer Dickens Festival
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Twice each December, the Dutch town of Deventer turns its old Bergkwartier district into a 19th-century English town straight from the novels of Charles Dickens. More than nine hundred costumed characters bring street theatre and carols to life, in a beloved festival, begun in 1991, that draws thousands into a living storybook. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dew Point Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Alentejo town of Montemor-o-Novo hosts the Ponto d'Orvalho, a three-day festival joining art, ecology, and farming. Guided nature walks, communal meals with local chefs, and site-specific music unfold on a country estate and in an old convent, in a celebration created to reconnect people with the land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Diaspora Days (Tutin)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Serbian town of Tutin celebrates its far-flung diaspora and shared heritage with a summer festival. Folk ensembles from Turkey, Bosnia, Montenegro, and home perform music, dance, and crafts, in a warm celebration of the Bosniak culture that binds this community across many borders. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Diekirch Carnival Parade
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Luxembourg town of Diekirch, fondly known as the donkey city, holds one of the country's biggest carnival parades. More than fifty floats roll through the streets amid confetti and music, in a colorful highlight of the local carnival season. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Diligent Hands Festival
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each November, the town of Bujanovac celebrates handicraft and folklore with the Diligent Hands fair. Artisans show traditional crafts and embroidery beside local foods, with Serbian and Albanian folk music and dance, in a celebration of the rural heritage of the Preševo Valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dobroge, Hearth of Folklore National Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Constanța hosts a national folklore festival celebrating the music and dance of Romania, with a special love for the styles of Dobrogea. Children's and youth ensembles join invited performers in authentic costume, in a contest devoted to keeping the region's folk traditions alive and thriving. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dobrogean Recipes and Traditions Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the village of Baia celebrates the cuisine of Dobrogea with a festival of recipes and traditions. Free tastings of classic dishes like tochitură, home-baked pastries, and farm products join folk music, in a celebration that honors the agricultural roots of this corner of Romania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Doina Covurluiului International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the city of Galați hosts the Doina Covurluiului, an international festival of folklore. Hundreds of dancers and musicians from Romania and abroad perform in traditional costume alongside nightly concerts by top Romanian folk artists, in a celebration of the rich heritage of the historic Covurlui region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: DokuFest (Documentary & Short Film Festival)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the old town of Prizren turns into a giant open-air cinema for DokuFest, Kosovo's biggest film festival. Documentaries and short films from around the world screen in courtyards, on rooftops, and below the hilltop fortress, in a festival that has brought movies back to life here since 2002. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dolcinium International Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Montenegrin coastal town of Ulcinj gathers folk ensembles from across the Balkans for the Dolcinium festival. Dancers and musicians in national costume perform on open-air stages near the historic Old Town, in a celebration that reflects the town's long life as a meeting point of many cultures by the sea. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Domagnano Castle Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the village of Domagnano gathers around its old church for a castle festival. Gastronomic stands, music, and folk entertainment fill the square in the evening, in a celebration of the local history and traditions of this San Marino town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Domagnano, Domagnano, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Domagnano, Domagnano, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Domaljevac Sarma Festival
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each late autumn, the village of Domaljevac celebrates its stuffed cabbage rolls with a sarma festival. A cooking contest and tasting fill the cultural hall, hosted by the local women's and cultural associations, in a celebration of a beloved Posavina winter dish. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Domaljevac, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina (Domaljevac), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Domaljevac, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina (Domaljevac), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Don Bosco Palio
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the town of Borgo Maggiore honors Saint John Bosco with the Don Bosco Palio. Neighborhoods compete in friendly races and parades amid communal meals and evening festivity, in a celebration that joins devotion to the 19th-century saint with civic pride. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Borgo Maggiore, Borgo Maggiore, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Borgo Maggiore, Borgo Maggiore, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Donja Kaštela Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Carnival Tuesday, the lower Kaštela towns near Split fill the streets for the Donjokaštelanski Krnjeval, said to be the oldest annual carnival in Croatia. Masked revelers and a great parade carry on a poklade tradition centuries deep, in a celebration that has been the pride of this stretch of the Dalmatian coast for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dormition of the Mother of God (Varna Day)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Every August 15, the Black Sea city of Varna celebrates the feast of the Dormition, which is also its official city day. A liturgy and icon procession at the great Assumption Cathedral give way to open-air concerts and festivities in the seaside park, blending deep faith with civic pride. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Dormition of the Theotokos (Iviron Monastery)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the ancient monastery of Iviron on Mount Athos keeps the Dormition of the Mother of God with full solemnity. Monks and visiting pilgrims gather for Great Vespers and an all-night vigil, marking the holy mountain's greatest feast in a place known as the Garden of the Virgin. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dormition of the Virgin Mary Feast
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August 14 and 15, the Cretan village of Mochos celebrates the Dormition of the Virgin. The great Orthodox feast of the Koimisi fills the village with worship and festivity, in a celebration of one of the holiest days of the Church year in the mountains of Crete. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dragon Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Shrovetide, the city of Ljubljana parades its carnival figures for the Dragon Carnival. Kurenti, laufarji, and other figures from Slovenian folklore wind through the old town on the Saturday before Lent, in a celebration rooted in pre-Christian spring rituals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Druskininkai Saint John’s Night
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the town of Druskininkai keeps Joninės, the festival of St. John's Night. Bonfires, flower wreaths, folk songs, and night-time rituals fill the communal gathering, in a celebration of one of Lithuania's most beloved solstice traditions in the spa town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Dub Camp Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer on the shores of Lac de Vioreau, the Gastineau family hosts Dub Camp, a festival devoted to reggae and its message of unity and love. International sound systems and reggae bands from France and abroad fill the rural lakeside, in a celebration born of one family's deep and abiding passion for the music. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Joué-sur-Erdre, Pays de la Loire, France
festivals.address: Joué-sur-Erdre, Pays de la Loire, France

festivals.name: Dunkirk Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Through the winter, the port of Dunkirk throws itself into a carnival born from its fishing past, peaking in three wild days before Lent. Costumed revelers link arms in great surging crowds, and from the town hall balcony officials fling smoked herring to the masses below, in one of France's most riotous traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Duszniki Wood-Pulp Paper Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the historic paper mill of Duszniki-Zdrój celebrates its centuries-old craft with the Święto Papieru. Two days of hands-on workshops, printing and marbling demonstrations, and museum tours invite visitors to make paper the old way, in a celebration of a trade that has flourished in this Polish town since the 1500s. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Düsseldorf Carnival (Rose Monday Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, Düsseldorf gives itself over to its carnival, the city's cheeky Fifth Season of costumes, satire, and street parties. The climax is the great Rose Monday parade, when floats poking fun at politicians roll through the packed streets amid showers of sweets. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Easter Fountain
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the town of Forchheim dresses its fountain for Easter with the Osterbrunnen. Evergreen garlands, ribbons, and painted eggs crown the Fischerbuben fountain, in a celebration of the Franconian folk custom of adorning wells and fountains at Easter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Easter Riders’ Procession Wittichenau–Ralbitz
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter Sunday, the men of Wittichenau ride out in white shirts, black tailcoats, and top hats on festively decorated horses for the Kreuzreiter procession. Carrying the news of the resurrection in prayer and song, they continue a Sorbian Catholic tradition unbroken since 1541, one of the most striking of Upper Lusatia's Easter rides. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ebeltoft Apple Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Danish town of Ebeltoft celebrates its apple harvest with a festival along its old cobblestone streets. Food, art, crafts, and the fruit of the town's community orchards fill the day, in a warm autumn celebration rooted in Ebeltoft's tradition of shared apple gardens and sustainable farming. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Eco Ethno Gacka
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Croatian town of Otočac in the green Gacka valley celebrates the food and crafts of the Lika region with the Eko Etno Gacka fair. Honey, cheeses, and farm products fill two days of stalls and cultural performances, in a warm celebration of the indigenous flavors and customs of this mountain valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Otočac, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia
festivals.address: Otočac, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia

festivals.name: Educational Bread Festival "With Your Bread You Will Find a Bench Everywhere"
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the manor at Zyplių near Lukšiai celebrates the harvest with an educational festival devoted to bread. Visitors follow the whole journey from threshing the grain and milling the flour to baking rye loaves in a wood-fired oven, in a warm tribute to the grain and bread at the heart of Lithuania's agrarian past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eger Bull’s Blood Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, the Hungarian city of Eger celebrates its famous red wine, the Egri Bikavér or Bull's Blood, with a great autumn festival. Wine tastings, vineyard tours, and cultural events fill the city to mark the wine's "birthday," in a celebration of a centuries-old blend that lies at the heart of Eger's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eguisheim Winegrowers’ Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Every other summer, the Alsatian village of Eguisheim, cradle of the region's vineyards, celebrates its wine with the Fête des Vignerons. Locals in traditional costume fill the illuminated town square for a historical procession, church mass, folklore, and dancing, in a warm two-day tribute to the village's ancient winemaking roots. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: EgyFeszt Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Székely town of Gheorgheni hosts EgyFeszt, a four-day festival of many arts. Rooting itself in Székely culture and the sacred landscapes around the town, it fills stages with music and folklore, in a celebration that has become a lively highlight of the cultural calendar in Harghita County. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ehrhorn Forest Thanksgiving
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the woods of Ehrhorn near Schneverdingen host a forest thanksgiving. An open-air church service and displays of local forest products gather families amid crafts and regional food, in a celebration of the German harvest thanksgiving, the Erntedank, among the trees. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eidsvåg Island Community Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the island of Midsund celebrates the season with a small community festival in the village of Eidsvåg. Live music, a family day, and local food fill the program, in a celebration built by local volunteers that has quickly become a highlight of the municipality's year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: El Dorado Equestrian Sports Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the El-Dorado riding club near Novi Pazar revives the local sport of horse racing. Senior races and an open day of riding fill the festival, in a celebration of an equestrian tradition rooted in the rural life of the Pešter Plateau. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: El Mercaón Cattle Fair of Cabañaquinta
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each year, the village of Cabañaquinta hosts El Mercaón, a great cattle fair of the Aller valley. Auctions of locally bred livestock and a showcase of the pastoral life draw farmers from afar, in a celebration of the importance of cattle to these mountain communities of Asturias. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Elbasan String Orchestra Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year since 2016, the Albanian city of Elbasan gathers string orchestras for a festival of chamber music. Amateur and professional youth ensembles from Albania and neighboring lands meet for competitions and concerts, in a celebration rooted in the region's deep tradition of community string ensembles. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Elbhang Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the hillside villages along the Elbe near Dresden, from Loschwitz to Pillnitz, come alive with the Elbhangfest. Music, art, and church concerts fill the village squares, breweries, and gardens beneath the river slopes, in a warm celebration of the riverside communities and their local traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Emajõgi River Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Tartu celebrates its beloved Emajõgi, the "Mother River," with a one-day river festival. River-themed concerts join a parade of hundreds of decorated boats gliding along the water, in a celebration of the great river that flows through the city and shapes the life of the region around it. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Embroiderers' Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year since 1954, the Breton town of Pont-l'Abbé celebrates the embroidery of the Pays Bigouden with the Festival des Brodeuses. Embroidery workshops, a needlework competition, and parades of dancers and musicians in traditional costume and tall lace coiffes fill the festival, in a proud tribute to a cherished local craft. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Emigrants' Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Azorean village of Lajes das Flores honors its scattered emigrant community with a festival of homecoming. Cultural exhibitions, folk music, and parades bring locals and visiting diaspora together, in a warm celebration that reaffirms the bonds between the people of Flores and the wider world they sailed off to. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lajes das Flores, Lajes das Flores, Flores, Portugal
festivals.address: Lajes das Flores, Lajes das Flores, Flores, Portugal

festivals.name: Emmaus Festival (Luxembourg City)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter Monday, the old town of Luxembourg City fills with the Emaischen, a centuries-old crafts market. Stalls overflow with handmade pottery and folk crafts, drawing crowds through the historic streets in a cheerful springtime tradition that blends Easter faith with local craft heritage. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Emmaus Festival (Nospelt)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter Monday, the village of Nospelt celebrates its pottery heritage with the Émaischen. Artisans make and sell the Päckvillercher, the little clay bird whistles, amid festivity, in a celebration founded in 1957 that ties the Emmaus tradition to the village's potters. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Emperor's Week
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Austrian spa town of Bad Ischl celebrates the birthday of Emperor Franz Josef I, who made it his beloved summer residence. Period city tours, parades, and concerts revive the customs of the 19th century, in a celebration of the town's grand identity as the old imperial Kaiserstadt of the Habsburgs. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Bad Ischl, Bad Ischl (Upper Austria), Austria
festivals.address: Bad Ischl, Bad Ischl (Upper Austria), Austria

festivals.name: Engelberg Cattle Descent
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the village of Engelberg welcomes its cattle home from the high pastures with the Alpabzug. Flower-crowned cows, ringing with bells, parade down into the village to the delight of crowds, in a celebration that locals call the most heartfelt of their traditions, rooted in the alpine farming life of Obwalden. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Enns New Year's Whip-Cracking
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each New Year's Day, the town of Enns cracks its long braided whips on the square. Whip-crackers from local clubs drive away the old year and welcome luck to celebratory music, in a celebration of the Alpine Aper-Schnalzen tradition tied to the turn of the season. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Epiphany (Kings') Festival of Salsas
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each Epiphany, the village of Salsas fills with the tattered, belled caretos. Hundreds of masked figures parade with torches, drums, and bagpipes through the village around January 5 and 6, in a celebration that carries the ancestral Epiphany traditions of the Bragança country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Epiphany Festival of Baçal (Caretos Masks)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each Epiphany, the village of Baçal sends its single young men out as masked caretos. Disguised as goblins, they parade through the village asking for donations, in a celebration within the Bragança winter cycle that enacts the old role-reversal rite of the solstice. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Epiphany Festival of Rebordaínhos
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each Three Kings' Day, the village of Rebordaínhos keeps a daylight singing procession. Carolers and a single careto go house to house singing the cantar dos reis and collecting gifts, in a celebration unique to the village within the winter festival cycle of Bragança. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Epiphany Festival of Rio de Onor
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each Epiphany, the border village of Rio de Onor sends out its caretos and the Filandorra dancer. The masked figures wander to every home the weekend before Kings' Day, in a celebration that ties into the winter-completion rites of the Bragança region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Erasmus Bridge Midnight Fireworks
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each New Year's Eve, the city of Rotterdam rings in midnight with a grand fireworks show over the Erasmus Bridge, set to music. Crowds gather along the river Maas to watch the sky light up above the city's iconic bridge, in a celebration that has become Rotterdam's beloved way to start the year. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Erazm's Days in Podgora
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the village of Podgora near Postojna revives the legend of the knight Erazem with Erazm's Days. Knightly reenactments, medieval games, workshops, and market stalls fill the village, in a celebration that pays homage to the famous lord of nearby Predjama Castle. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Esbjerg Festival Week
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Danish port city of Esbjerg fills a week with free culture for its Festuge. Open-air concerts, street performances, art, and even opera reach out to people of all ages, in a celebration created to share the city's cultural life and draw its residents into the arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Escaldes-Engordany and Andorra la Vella Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the towns of Escaldes-Engordany and Andorra la Vella fill with color for their carnival. Costumed revelers and themed floats parade through the capital area, in a lively celebration of the pre-Lenten tradition that Andorra shares with the wider Catalan world. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Andorra la Vella, Capital region (Andorra la Vella & Escaldes-Eng), Andorra
festivals.address: Andorra la Vella, Capital region (Andorra la Vella & Escaldes-Eng), Andorra

festivals.name: Escaldes-Engordany Town Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Andorran town of Escaldes-Engordany holds its Festa Major, the big summer fiesta of the parish. Concerts, traditional dances, sports, and games for children fill several days, as a town in the Pyrenees comes together to celebrate the heart of summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Esencia (Košice Region Traditional Culture Festival)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the Slovak city of Košice celebrates the traditional culture of its region with Esencia, a three-day festival first held in 2025. Folk music, dance, and craft gather to revive and showcase the heritage of eastern Slovakia, in a young celebration of the deep-rooted customs of the Košice region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eskilstuna Culture Night
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the city of Eskilstuna gathers all its culture into a single evening with Kulturnatt. Concerts, theatre, dance, art, and children's programs fill venues across the city, in a celebration begun in 2023 that reflects the modern cultural life of this historic industrial town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Estonian Bread Day and Autumn Fair
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Estonian Open Air Museum in Tallinn celebrates the country's beloved rye bread with Bread Day. Grain threshing and baking demonstrations join a fair of homemade foods and crafts, in a warm harvest celebration that connects Estonians with the agrarian traditions and the dark, hearty bread at the heart of their table. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ethno Festival Hrastnik
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Each year, the town of Hrastnik celebrates Serbian folk culture with the Etno Festival. Folk dance and music ensembles from Slovenia and the region gather under the local Serbian cultural association, in a celebration of the community born of the historic migration of miners to the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Etnica Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the city of Reșița devotes a week to the many peoples of the mountainous Banat, with each day given to a different community. Romanians, Croats, Czechs, Germans, Hungarians, Serbs, and Roma share their music, food, and tradition, in a celebration, first held in 2021, of a region that has long been a crossroads of cultures. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: EtnoVyr (Vys'pa Island Folk Festival)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the island of Vys'pa in Romaniv fills with music and color for EtnoVyr, a festival of the many peoples of Polissia. Music, crafts, costume displays, and traditional foods celebrate the Ukrainian, Polish, and other heritage of the region, in a young festival first held in 2024 to mark the town's day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eupen Karneval (Rosenmontag Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the town of Eupen throws a Rhineland-style carnival, capped by a big parade on Rose Monday. Dozens of floats and hundreds of costumed club members roll through the streets shouting the carnival cry of "Alaaf!" and tossing sweets, led by a reigning Carnival Prince. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eupen Oberstadt Kirmes (Upper-Town Funfair)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each late June, the Upper Town of Eupen fills with rides and stalls for the Oberstadt Kirmes. Some sixty showmen bring carousels, games, and duck-fishing after an opening parade, in a celebration that carries the customary Volksfest of this German-speaking corner of Belgium. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Eupen Tirolerfest (Tyrolean Music & Charity)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Eupen celebrates Alpine culture with the Tirolerfest. Alpen folk concerts, Schuhplattler dancing, and Tyrolean cuisine fill three days for charity, in a celebration begun in 1981 that honors Eupen's post-war ties with the Alps. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Evening of Wine and Art
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, a city park in Osijek becomes a wine-tasting wonderland for the Evening of Wine and Art. Local wineries pour Slavonian and Danube wines amid live music, art installations, and street food, in a celebration of the region's wine heritage and the city's lively culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Faaschtebounen d’Arlon (Lent Beans & Newlyweds’ Song)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each first Sunday of Lent, the children of Arlon parade to honor the year's newlyweds with the Faaschtebounen. In traditional dress they sing the special bean song beneath the couples' windows and receive treats, in a celebration rooted in Arlon's Luxembourgish heritage since the 19th century. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Arlon, Arlon (Province of Luxembourg, Wallonia), Belgium
festivals.address: Arlon, Arlon (Province of Luxembourg, Wallonia), Belgium

festivals.name: Faetano Castle Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the village of Faetano gathers near its castle ruins for a village fair. Regional cuisine, live music, and folk traditions fill multi-day festivities, in a celebration of the agrarian traditions and communal fellowship of this San Marino town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Faetano, Faetano, San Marino, San Marino
festivals.address: Faetano, Faetano, San Marino, San Marino

festivals.name: Fanø Free Folk Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the village of Sønderho on the island of Fanø hosts a three-day free folk festival. Folk musicians fill the village over the days, in a celebration that grew from the island's own tradition of folk singing and community gathering. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sønderho, Fanø, Fanø (Southwest Denmark, Wadden Sea), Denmark
festivals.address: Sønderho, Fanø, Fanø (Southwest Denmark, Wadden Sea), Denmark

festivals.name: Farmers' Day of Idiazabal
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, the Basque town of Idiazabal, famous for its smoky sheep's cheese, celebrates rural life with Farmers' Day. Agricultural fairs and livestock shows surround the headline event, the Spanish Championship of shepherd's cheese held since 1983, in a celebration that draws thousands to honor the land and its herders. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Fastelavn Carnival (Museum Garden, Copenhagen)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Fastelavn, the garden of the Designmuseum in Copenhagen fills with costumed children. The barrel-beating game crowns a Cat King and Queen amid costume contests, in a celebration of the Nordic pre-Lenten carnival tradition in the museum's historic garden. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feakle Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the small County Clare village of Feakle fills with fiddles and flutes for its traditional music festival. In the heart of one of Ireland's richest trad-music regions, it gathers musicians for concerts, sessions, and workshops, in a celebration founded in the 1980s to keep the local music thriving. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast Day of the Apostle St. James
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the pilgrimage city of Santiago de Compostela honors Saint James, the patron of Galicia and the goal of the famous Camino. The festivities peak on the eve of his feast with a dazzling fireworks and light show over the great cathedral on Obradoiro Square, after weeks of Galician music and dance. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast in Honor of the Holy Christ of Remedies
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, this town celebrates its patron, the Holy Christ of Remedies, with a week that mixes deep devotion and high spirits. Mornings bring bull-runs through the streets, including a thrilling night run, alongside processions, fireworks, and music, in one of the area's most anticipated fiestas. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Spain
festivals.address: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Spain

festivals.name: Feast of Generosity for Dar tal-Providenza
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 1, the village of Siggiewi gathers in support of Dar tal-Providenza with a Feast of Generosity. A solemn Mass and a day of festivities under a great tent raise funds for Malta's main home for persons with disabilities, in a celebration of Maltese charity and community. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Our Lady of Lourdes
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February 11, tens of thousands gather at the sanctuary of Lourdes to mark the day in 1858 when a young girl said the Virgin Mary appeared to her in a grotto. The night fills with a torchlight procession of glowing candles, in one of the most important Marian pilgrimages in the Catholic world. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Our Lady of Mount Carmel
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 16, the Aragonese town of Rubielos de Mora honors its patroness, the Virgin of Carmel, with a fiesta that joins faith and festivity. Religious ceremonies in her honor sit alongside folk spectacle and celebration, in a warm summer tradition at the heart of this mountain town's life. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Feast of Our Lady of Mount Carmel (Virgen del Carmen)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 16, the coastal towns of Ibiza honor the Virgin of Carmel, the protector of sailors and fishermen. After an evening Mass, her statue is carried through the streets and then out onto the water, as decorated boats escort her around the harbor in a moving seaside tradition. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Feast of Our Lady of Viggiano
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each late summer, more than a hundred thousand pilgrims gather in Viggiano to carry the beloved Black Madonna up the mountain. The sacred statue is borne in solemn procession from the town to the high Sanctuary, in a celebration of a Marian devotion that, by legend, reaches back to the discovery of the image centuries ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Panagia Faneromeni
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August 15, the island of Zakynthos honors the Virgin Mary on the feast of the Dormition, centered on the historic Church of Panagia Faneromeni. A solemn vespers on the eve and the Divine Liturgy on the feast day draw islanders together, in devotion to the Lady "Revealed," long revered as the protector of Zakynthos. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Prophet Elias
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 20, the village of Kioni on Ithaca keeps the feast of the Prophet Elias. Residents travel by boat or footpath to the small seaside chapel for the evening liturgy, in a panigyri that brings the saint, usually honored on high hilltops, down to the water's edge. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Agatha
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the Sicilian city of Catania pours into the streets for its patron, Saint Agatha, in one of the world's largest religious festivals. Towering decorated candles are carried through town, and the saint's silver bust is paraded for days among vast, devoted crowds dressed in white. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Anthony the Abbot
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each mid-January, the Mallorcan town of Sa Pobla honors Saint Anthony the Abbot, patron of farmers and animals, with bonfires and revelry. The celebration wakes the town at dawn with noise and folk dancing, in a deeply rural Mallorcan tradition that gathers the community around the fire on the saint's feast. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Daniel and the Martyred Companions
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the city of Ceuta honors its patron, Saint Daniel, remembering seven Franciscan friars martyred here in 1227. A solemn Mass and procession mix with a lively street fair, concerts, and the blessing of animals, in a festival that ties the city's medieval history to its present. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Gerasimos
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Twice a year, the island of Kefalonia honors its patron, Saint Gerasimos, whose preserved body rests in the monastery that bears his name. On the greater feast in October, thousands gather for a liturgy and the procession of his relics, in a celebration at the spiritual heart of the island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Januarius
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Every September 19, Naples holds its breath for Saint Januarius, the city's beloved patron and protector against disaster. Crowds pack the cathedral to watch for the famous miracle, when a vial of the saint's dried blood is said to turn liquid again, a sign the faithful await with hope each year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Panteleimon (Holy Monastery of Rossikon)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the great Russian monastery of Saint Panteleimon on Mount Athos honors its patron saint with solemn ceremony. Monks and male pilgrims gather by the sea below the monastery for vigil and liturgy, in the chief feast of the largest Russian house on the Holy Mountain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Petronius
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October 4, the city of Bologna celebrates its patron, Saint Petronius, the bishop woven deep into the city's identity. A solemn Mass in the vast basilica that bears his name is joined by concerts and games, in a two-day feast that brings the people of Bologna together. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Rosalia
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Every July, the city of Palermo throws the Festino for Santa Rosalia, the hermit saint who, by legend, ended a plague in 1624. The high point is a night procession in which a towering, boat-shaped float carries her silver statue through the old town to roaring crowds and fireworks by the sea. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Saint Sebastian
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the Mallorcan city of Palma celebrates its patron, Saint Sebastian, by lighting bonfires in squares all over town. Neighbors gather to grill sausages over the flames while free concerts fill the night and a fiery correfoc winds through the streets, in the city's biggest winter party. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of Saints Peter & Paul
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the village of Fragkaina honors the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul. A liturgy gives way to a communal celebration in the church courtyard with laïkó music, dance, and shared food, in a panigyri that turns the saints' feast into a great social gathering of the village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of San Marino (Republic Foundation Day)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Every September 3, the little republic of San Marino marks its founding, honoring the stonemason saint who, by legend, settled its mountain long ago. A solemn Mass and relic procession give way to a grand crossbow tournament between the nine townships, in a day full of medieval pageantry and national pride. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of St. James (Kutná Hora)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the silver town of Kutná Hora honors its patron, Saint James, at the great Gothic Church of St. James. An evening concert, a solemn Mass led by the bishop, and a procession of holy icons fill the feast, in a celebration rooted in the town's deep Catholic tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kutná Hora, Kutná Hora (Bohemia), Czech Republic
festivals.address: Kutná Hora, Kutná Hora (Bohemia), Czech Republic

festivals.name: Feast of St. Nicholas
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each December 6, the small monastery of Stavronikita on Mount Athos keeps the feast of Saint Nicholas, the wonder-worker and patron of sailors. Monks and a few pilgrims gather for vespers and liturgy, in a modest but heartfelt celebration at one of the Holy Mountain's tiniest houses. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of St. Tryphon
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the vine-growing village of Kutnje keeps the Orthodox feast of Saint Tryphon. The vineyards are blessed, the slava bread is cut, and prayers are offered, in a celebration of the saint venerated in Serbian tradition as the protector of the vine and the vinegrower. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of the Ascension of the Lord (Esphigmenou Monastery)
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Forty days after Easter, the monastery of Esphigmenou on Mount Athos celebrates the Ascension of Christ with a special liturgy. Monks and Orthodox pilgrims gather in their vestments for one of the church's great feasts, marking the day Christ rose to heaven before his disciples. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mount Athos, Mount Athos monastic community, Greece
festivals.address: Mount Athos, Mount Athos monastic community, Greece

festivals.name: Feast of the Assumption (Campodarsego)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each Ferragosto, the town of Campodarsego celebrates the Assumption with the 'flavors and dances of Ferragosto'. Food stands of polenta and musso line the streets as the town dances and sings, in a celebration that has grown from the Assumption feast into a lively summer street party. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Assumption (Tambre)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August 15, the town of Tambre celebrates the Assumption of Mary. Stalls, communal meals of grilled meats at the 'frasca', and a solemn Mass fill the day at the village playground, in a celebration of summer and community in this corner of the Belluno mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary (Aglona Pilgrimage)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Every August 15, thousands of pilgrims travel to the white basilica of Aglona, Latvia's most important Catholic shrine, to mark the feast of the Assumption of Mary. Open-air Masses and a great procession fill the grounds, in a gathering steeped in centuries of devotion to the Virgin. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Conversion of Saint Paul
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each February, Valletta honors the conversion of Saint Paul, the apostle whose shipwreck on Malta brought the island its Christian faith. A solemn pontifical Mass and grand procession mark this national feast, woven deep into the story Maltese tell of who they are. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of the Entry of the Theotokos (Presentation of the Virgin Mary)
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each year, the Serbian monastery of Hilandar on Mount Athos celebrates the Entry of the Virgin Mary into the Temple, one of the grandest feasts on the Holy Mountain. Pilgrims come from Greece, Serbia, and beyond for the all-night vigil and dawn liturgy, at a place that is a heart of Serbian Orthodoxy. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of the Forty Martyrs of Sebaste
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each year, the monastery of Xeropotamou on Mount Athos honors its patrons, the Forty Martyrs of Sebaste, the Roman soldiers who died for their faith on a frozen lake in the 4th century. The monks gather before the main church for a festive liturgy in memory of their steadfastness. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of the Madonna della Bruna
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Every July 2, the ancient city of Matera honors the Madonna della Bruna with a day that runs from a predawn procession through to a grand night. A decorated float carrying her image is drawn through the old stone town, in a centuries-old festival that mixes deep devotion with high celebration. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of the Minstrels (Ribeauvillé Drympetenspiel)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each first weekend of September, the Alsatian town of Ribeauvillé celebrates its medieval guild of minstrels with the Pfifferdaj. Villagers in elaborate period costume march on ornately decorated floats, telling a new folk story each year, in a centuries-old celebration of the wandering minstrels who once made their home in the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Most Holy Miracle
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each year on the Sunday after Easter, the city of Santarém venerates its "Holy Miracle" relic with the Festas do Santíssimo Milagre. A solemn procession honors a wonder said to date from the 13th century, in a celebration rooted in a medieval Marian devotion that has marked the city's faith for nearly eight hundred years. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Most Holy Savior
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August 6, on Transfiguration Day, the medieval Sicilian town of Cefalù honors its patron, Jesus the Savior, with a week of celebration. Devotion dating back to Norman times, when the great cathedral was consecrated, fills the festival, in one of the most cherished holy days of this beautiful town on the Tyrrhenian coast. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Feast of the Risen Christ (Sliema)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter Sunday, the town of Sliema carries the Risen Christ through its streets. The statue 'breaks into runs' as crowds cheer and throw confetti, in a celebration of the joy of the Resurrection in the Maltese Holy Week tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Risen Christ (Żejtun)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter Sunday, the town of Żejtun carries the Risen Christ from its main church down Victory Street. The crowd cheers and the bearers break into bursts of running amid bells and band music, in a celebration that is a high point of the town's Holy Week. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Feast of the Translation of the Apostle St. James
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each December 30, the city of Santiago de Compostela keeps the feast of the Translation of the Apostle. A solemn pontifical Mass at the cathedral recalls the legendary transfer of St. James's relics from Jerusalem to Galicia, in a celebration that affirms the city's identity as his resting place. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Feast of Venerable Athanasios the Athonite (Great Lavra)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Great Lavra, the oldest and first-ranked monastery on Mount Athos, honors its founder, Saint Athanasios the Athonite. With full Athonite splendor the monks celebrate the man who, in the year 963, began the monastic life that still shapes the entire Holy Mountain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festa del Mare (Portonovo)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each Assumption Day, the bay of Portonovo near Ancona celebrates the Festa del Mare. A morning Mass and procession to the Santa Maria church give way to a blessing of the boats and a wreath cast for the fallen, in a celebration that honors the sea on the feast of Ferragosto. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festa del Redentore
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · On the third weekend of July, Venice gives thanks for the end of the great plague of 1576 with the Festa del Redentore. A long bridge of boats is laid across the water to the Redentore church, and on Saturday night the lagoon erupts in fireworks as Venetians watch from decorated boats. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festicante Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the Alentejo town of Aljustrel celebrates the unlikely kinship of Portugal and Brazil with Festicante. The deep male-voice singing of Cante Alentejano, a UNESCO treasure, meets the rhythms of samba alongside Brazilian food and music, in a warm multicultural festival that bridges two worlds across the ocean. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: FESTIM (Inter-Municipal World Music Festival)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the towns around Águeda fill with the sounds of the world for FESTIM, an inter-municipal festival of world music. Over several weeks, musicians from many lands and traditions perform across the region, in a celebration of cultural diversity that carries global rhythms into the heart of central Portugal. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival "Din Zestrea Neamului"
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each spring, the village of Speia celebrates its Moldovan folk heritage as part of the Mărțișor festivities. Village ensembles, craft fairs, traditional costumes, and old rituals fill the day, in a celebration named "From the Nation's Dowry" that gathers the cultural inheritance of this corner of Transnistria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Speia, Grigoriopol district folk traditions, Moldova
festivals.address: Speia, Grigoriopol district folk traditions, Moldova

festivals.name: Festival 'Mamaliga'
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the village of Nova Cherna holds a cheerful cook-off devoted to mamaliga, the cornmeal porridge at the heart of Dobrudja cooking. Cooks vie to make the finest pot of the humble dish, in a celebration that honors the corn harvest and the agrarian heritage of this corner of northeastern Bulgaria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Nova Cherna, Nova Cherna (Silistra Province), Bulgaria
festivals.address: Nova Cherna, Nova Cherna (Silistra Province), Bulgaria

festivals.name: Festival Bled
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the lakeside resort of Bled fills with music for an international festival of classical chamber music. Founded in 1996 and led by the violinist Jernej Brence, it brings concerts, masterclasses, and a blend of classical, jazz, and folk to one of Slovenia's most beautiful settings, by the famous alpine lake. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival Krumlov (Český Krumlov International Music Festival)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the fairy-tale town of Český Krumlov fills with classical music for a festival set among its castle halls, gardens, and chapels. Running since 1992 across the UNESCO-listed old town, it offers weeks of concerts in some of the most beautiful surroundings any festival could ask for. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: Český Krumlov, South Bohemia, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Český Krumlov, South Bohemia, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Festival of Ancient Heritage 'Eagle of the Danube'
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Each summer, the Bulgarian village of Gigen, on the site of a Roman city, hosts a grand reenactment of the ancient Danube frontier. Local and international history groups recreate battles, ceremonies, and daily life in authentic costume, in a vivid celebration of the Roman heritage that lines the great river. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Gigen, Danubian heritage (Veliko Tarnovo & Pleven), Bulgaria
festivals.address: Gigen, Danubian heritage (Veliko Tarnovo & Pleven), Bulgaria

festivals.name: Festival of Borderlands Heritage
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Lubaczów and its neighbors celebrate the shared culture of the Polish-Ukrainian borderlands, the old Kresy. Concerts, exhibitions, folk markets, and workshops gather Poles and Ukrainians together, in a festival of remembrance and friendship rooted in the heritage of these long-contested eastern lands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Bread and Peasant Civilization
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Cilento hill town of Trentinara, high above the Tyrrhenian coast, celebrates its home-baked bread and old peasant ways. Wood-oven bread, rural crafts, and folk traditions fill the festival, in a warm tribute to the country culture that still shapes life in this beautiful corner of southern Italy. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Bread, Song and Dance
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Bulgarian city of Silistra celebrates its bread, song, and dance with a festival on Svoboda Square. Community centers display handmade breads and pastries while folk ensembles perform, in a celebration of the bread that has long symbolized hospitality and community in Bulgarian life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Brittany
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, a traveling festival carries the culture of Brittany from town to town across the region. Concerts, folk dances, and art exhibitions in communities like Locronan celebrate Breton heritage, in a celebration coordinated to promote the music, language, and traditions shared across the Breton lands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Crete
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the island of Crete celebrates its culture with a region-wide arts festival of concerts, performances, and exhibitions. Founded in 2021, it brings together modern work and the island's deep heritage, carrying music and art to towns across Crete through the summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Fertility
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the Bulgarian town of Kyustendil, blessed with famously fertile land, celebrates its harvest with the Festival of Fertility. The central square becomes a garden of abundance, heaped with fresh fruit, vegetables, and homemade goods, in a celebration of the orchards and fields that have given this region its bounty for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Festival of Festivals (Trebinje Amateur Theatre Showcase)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the Herzegovinian town of Trebinje gathers the finest amateur theatre groups from across the former Yugoslavia for its Festival of Festivals. Running since 1957, daily performances and awards fill the calendar, in a cultural highlight of Eastern Herzegovina that has long celebrated the art of community drama. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Trebinje, Eastern Herzegovina (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Trebinje, Eastern Herzegovina (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Festival of Folk Arts & Crafts
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the courtyards of Buda Castle fill with hundreds of craftspeople for Hungary's great Festival of Folk Arts. Potters, weavers, and woodcarvers demonstrate age-old skills around the country's national holiday, turning the castle hill into a living showcase of folk tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Forests (Compiègne & Laigue)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the forests of Laigue and Compiègne become a concert hall for the Festival des Forêts. Classical music is staged among the trees, blending fine performances with the beauty of the woods, in a celebration that has joined music, nature, and regional heritage for over three decades in the Oise. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Gayant (Douai Giants Festival)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the city of Douai welcomes home its giants, five towering wickerwork figures named Gayant and his family who have paraded through the streets since the 1500s. To music and dancing crowds, the great puppets sway through town, in one of northern France's most beloved folk traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Herbs, Mountain and Tourism (Enyovden)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 24, on the midsummer feast of Enyovden, the Bulgarian town of Troyan celebrates the healing herbs of the Balkan Mountains. Hikers and herb-gatherers climb the green slopes for a festival of nature, folklore, and mountain tourism, in a celebration rooted in the old belief that herbs picked on St. John's Day hold their greatest power. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Festival of Normans
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year around St. Michael's Day, Normandy celebrates its heritage with the grassroots Fête des Normands. For a weekend in late September, communities across the region gather for music, food, and festivity, in a citizen-driven celebration created to foster pride in all things Norman. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Prekmurje Ham and Prekmurje Gibanica
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Prekmurje town of Murska Sobota celebrates two of its most beloved foods: the home-cured šunka ham and the rich layered pastry gibanica. Tastings and competitions draw cooks from across Slovenia, in a warm celebration of two dishes that stand as proud symbols of this region's culinary heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Serbian Traditional Songs and Dances "Izvor"
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the town of Leposavić celebrates Serbian song, dance, and costume with the folk festival "Izvor." Children, youth, and villagers gather to share their heritage through performances and workshops, in a celebration begun by a community linked to the Hilandar monastery to keep the old traditions flowing like a spring. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Leposavić, Kosovo Serb Region (Leposavić Municipality), Kosovo
festivals.address: Leposavić, Kosovo Serb Region (Leposavić Municipality), Kosovo

festivals.name: Festival of St. Mary Magdalene
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Gascon town of Mont-de-Marsan dresses in blue and white for five days of feria in honor of Saint Mary Magdalene. Concerts and parades fill the streets, alongside the region's own daring style of bullfighting, the Course Landaise, where leapers vault clean over charging cows. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of the 100 Voyvodi
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each late summer, the Bulgarian town of Sliven honors its old fair traditions and Karakachan heritage with the Festival of the 100 Voyvodi. Named for a hundred legendary highland warlords, it blends the memory of the Ottoman-era market with the culture of the region's nomadic shepherds, in a colorful celebration of local history. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Festival of the Branch
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each midsummer, the Canary town of Agaete fills with dancers waving green tree branches for the Fiesta de la Rama. Once an indigenous Guanche rite to call down rain, today thousands dance to the waterfront shrine to make offerings to the Virgin of Las Nieves, in a vibrant celebration where ancient and Christian traditions meet. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Festival of the Cooks
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the women cooks of Guadeloupe parade through Pointe-à-Pitre in dazzling madras costume, baskets of food on their arms, for the Fête des Cuisinières. Begun in 1916 as a cooks' mutual-aid society, the day starts with a church blessing and ends in a great feast celebrating the island's kitchens. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of the Istriot Dialect
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the southern Istrian village of Šišan celebrates the Istriot tongue, one of the oldest Romance dialects in the region. Poetry, storytelling, and music in Istriot fill the festival, in a heartfelt effort to keep alive a rare and ancient local language that few still speak. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of the Istrioto Dialect
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, a three-day festival gathers the six small Istrian towns where the endangered Istrioto dialect survives. Literary readings, choirs, and folk music in this ancient Romance tongue fill the program, in a celebration devoted to preserving the fragile "language island" of southern Istria. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ližnjan, Istria Region (southern Istria language island), Croatia
festivals.address: Ližnjan, Istria Region (southern Istria language island), Croatia

festivals.name: Festival of the Istroveneto Dialect
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each year, the Istrian town of Buje celebrates the rare Istro-Venetian dialect, a living remnant of the region's old Venetian communities. Folk songs, poetry, and traditional costume reflect the Venetian influence on Istria, in a celebration that keeps the memory of this fading local speech alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of the Lord Holy Christ of the Caldeira (São Jorge)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · On the first Sunday of September, pilgrims gather in the remote Fajã da Caldeira do Santo Cristo on São Jorge to honor the Lord Holy Christ. A candlelight procession winds from the shrine around the rim of the lagoon, in a centuries-old pilgrimage that fulfills an ancient vow in one of the most beautiful and secluded corners of the Azores. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of the Trays
festivals.description: Early July 2027 · Once every four years, the town of Tomar fills with a procession unlike any other, as hundreds of young women walk through the streets balancing towering trays on their heads. Each tabuleiro is stacked with loaves and crowned with paper flowers, rising as tall as the bearer, in a Pentecost tradition of medieval roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Tomar, Lisbon Metropolitan, Santarém District, Portugal
festivals.address: Tomar, Lisbon Metropolitan, Santarém District, Portugal

festivals.name: Festival of Traditional Dance, Music and Folk Song of Our Land
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each year, the Ibizan town of Sant Antoni gathers its folk groups to keep the island's country traditions alive. In bright costume, dancers perform the old ball pagès and singers raise the island's distinctive songs, offering a window into rural Ibiza far from its beach-resort fame. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sant Antoni de Portmany, Ibiza, Spain
festivals.address: Sant Antoni de Portmany, Ibiza, Spain

festivals.name: Festival of Traditional Dances "Na Armane s Tapane"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Bulgarian town of Razlog gathers more than forty dance ensembles on its central square for "Na Armane s Tapane." Troupes in traditional costume fill a grand gala of authentic regional dances and songs, in a celebration set in the Pirin mountains that carries on the town's long love of great folk gatherings. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Razlog, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Razlog area, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Razlog, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Razlog area, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Festival of Youth Culture of Serbia
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year since 1962, the Serbian town of Knjaževac celebrates the creativity of its young people with a festival of youth culture. Music, poetry, theatre, film, and comics by young Serbs fill the program, including the "Timočka lira" contest, in a celebration that has become a proud symbol of the town's cultural life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festival of Zeeuws-Vlaanderen
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Dutch border region of Zeeuws-Vlaanderen fills its old churches and village halls with classical music. Chamber concerts and recitals unfold in atmospheric venues behind the dikes, in a festival begun in 1988 that brings fine music to the quiet, historic corners of this Flanders-facing land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: FestiValderAa Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the river valley around Schipborg in Drenthe hosts the intimate FestiValderAa. Over three days, music, theatre, and performance unfold among the meadows of the lovely Drentsche Aa, in a festival that weaves contemporary arts together with the quiet natural beauty of one of the Netherlands' most cherished landscapes. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festivalia
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the Bulgarian city of Pazardzhik gathers in Stadion Park for Festivalia, a lively autumn celebration begun in 2024. Concerts, craft workshops, children's activities, food stalls, and fairground rides fill the days, in a young festival created to brighten the city's calendar and bring its people together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Pazardzhik, Upper Thracian Valley community, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Pazardzhik, Upper Thracian Valley community, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Festivals of Lisbon
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each June, Lisbon throws itself into a month-long party in honor of Saint Anthony, the city's patron. The old neighborhoods fill with street parties, music, and the smoke of grilled sardines, peaking on the night of June 12 when couples and crowds dance until dawn under strings of paper lights. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festive Mussel Celebration
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the town of Le Tréport celebrates its mussel harvest with La Moule Festive. Plates of the local mussels draw visitors to the Côte d'Albâtre, in a convivial celebration of a maritime specialty of this Norman coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festivities of Our Lady of La Paloma
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August 15, the old Madrid neighborhoods of La Latina and Lavapiés celebrate the Virgin of La Paloma, the city's much-loved local patroness. The streets fill with people in traditional chulapo dress for a famous open-air mass, dancing, and water-splashing fun, in Madrid's beloved end-of-summer fiesta. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festivities of Our Lady of the Pillar
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the city of Zaragoza honors the Virgin of the Pillar, the patroness of Aragon, with nine days of festivities. The heart of it all is the Flower Offering, when tens of thousands in regional costume build a vast cloak of blooms before her image, beneath nightly fireworks over the Ebro. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Festivities of the Virgin Blanca (Vitoria-Gasteiz)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the city of Vitoria-Gasteiz dresses in white to honor its patroness, the Virgin Blanca. The festival opens with a rocket and the famous descent of Celedón, a figure who glides down on a cord from the church belfry to the cheering square, kicking off days of parades, music, and revelry. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Festivity of the Virgin of Candelaria
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Twice a year, pilgrims stream into the Tenerife town of Candelaria to honor the Virgin of Candelaria, patron saint of all the Canary Islands. On the August feast especially, thousands walk through the night to her great seaside basilica, in the most important religious gathering in the islands. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Festivity Under the Linden Tree
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the village of Podkoren gathers under a great linden tree for a veselica. Live folk music, food, dancing, and games fill the open-air festivity, organized by the village fire department, in a celebration rooted in the Slavic tradition of the communal gathering beneath the linden. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Filigree Children's Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the St. Archangels Monastery near Prizren hosts a children's arts festival in the shadow of Serbian medieval heritage. Music, drama, painting, and crafts gather young people from Kosovo and beyond around the legacy of kings and old churches, in a celebration created to pass that heritage on to the next generation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Prizren, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia
festivals.address: Prizren, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia

festivals.name: First Voice of Zagorje
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the Croatian town of Sveti Križ Začretje gathers choirs and folk ensembles for the First Voice of Zagorje. Singers perform Kajkavian folk songs and new compositions in a community competition and concert, in a celebration of the distinctive musical heritage of the green Hrvatsko Zagorje hills. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sveti Križ Začretje, Hrvatsko Zagorje (Zagorje region), Croatia
festivals.address: Sveti Križ Začretje, Hrvatsko Zagorje (Zagorje region), Croatia

festivals.name: Fishermen's Fest
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the town of Izola honors its fishing heritage with the Fishermen's Fest. Seafood street food, an Istrian market of wine, olive oil, and crafts, music, and a parade by the Brass Band fill the day, in a celebration of this coastal town's fishing identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Fishermen's Festival Breskens
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Dutch port of Breskens fills its harbor with the flavors of the North Sea for its three-day Fishermen's Festival. Fresh mussels, fish, and shrimp join music and entertainment, in a celebration that honors the long fishing heritage of this seafaring town on the Zeeland coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flag Day and Independence Day Celebrations
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each November, the Albanian city of Elbasan joins the nation in celebrating Flag Day and Independence Day. Flag-raising ceremonies and performances by local dance groups fill the central square, in a celebration of Albanian heritage and the proud banner that has united the country through its long history. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Flag of Freedom Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, during Victory Month, the fortress of Knin hosts the Flag of Freedom festival. An evening of patriotic poetry and performance unfolds against the dramatic backdrop of the historic citadel, in a celebration that honors the town's history and its flag ceremonies. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flames Across the Meadows
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · On the eve of the feast of Our Lady of Perpetual Help, the Gozitan village of Kerċem lights up the night sky with Flames Across the Meadows. A themed pyrotechnic show by the village fireworks society fills the July darkness with color, in a dazzling celebration rooted in the festa traditions of the Maltese islands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flan Custard Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each late September, the town of Triel-sur-Seine celebrates the humble custard tart with the Fête du Flan. Giant flan tastings, a flea market, street rides, and family fun fill the day, in a quirky celebration of a classic French pastry and the local culinary heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flavors of Inner Carniola
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the town of Cerknica celebrates the hearty cooking of Inner Carniola with a festival of regional flavors. Dishes like jota, the bean-and-sauerkraut stew, and local wines fill the tastings, in a community celebration of the food traditions tied to the karst landscape of this corner of Slovenia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flavours of Celje Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the Slovenian city of Celje celebrates its regional cooking with the Flavours of Celje festival. Food stalls, tastings of local wines, cheeses, and desserts, cooking demonstrations, and folk music fill the festivities, in a celebration that honors and renews the food culture of the Celje region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Flekkefjord Salmon Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Norwegian town of Flekkefjord celebrates summer with the Laksefestivalen, a community folk festival run by the local sports club. Family concerts, competitions, food stalls, and children's activities fill the festive days, in a warm, long-running gathering that brings the whole town together at the height of the season. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Flekkefjord, Flekkefjord community (Agder, Southern Norway), Norway
festivals.address: Flekkefjord, Flekkefjord community (Agder, Southern Norway), Norway

festivals.name: Folk Maris Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the city of Arad fills the banks of the Mureș with folk music for the Folk Maris festival. Romanian folk bands from the region and beyond play in the riverside recreation area, in a celebration of the deep folk traditions of the Arad country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Folk Music Hit Parade
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each November, the lakeside town of Bled hosts the Narodnozabavna Hit Parada, an evening of Slovenia's beloved folk-pop. Famous ensembles fill the stage with the cheerful narodnozabavni sound, in a celebration that honors a national tradition pioneered by the great Avsenik, himself a son of the Bled valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Folk-Fest Valandovo (New Macedonian Folk Song Festival)
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each May, the Macedonian town of Valandovo gathers songwriters and singers for Folk Fest Valandovo, devoted to newly composed Macedonian songs. Competitions crown the best new compositions and performances, in a festival begun in the late 1980s that has become one of the country's most cherished celebrations of folk music. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Folkart (Maribor International Folklore Festival)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Slovenian city of Maribor gathers folk ensembles from around the world for Folkart. A great opening parade winds through the city before evenings of dance and music from many lands, in a CIOFF festival that turns Maribor into a vibrant crossroads of the world's folk traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: FolkFaro International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Algarve capital of Faro fills with color for FolkFaro, an international folklore festival. Run by the city's own folk group since 2003, it brings dozens of dance and music ensembles from home and abroad, in a celebration of the multicultural heritage of this lively southern Portuguese hub. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Folkiri Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Le Mans area gathers dancers and musicians for the Folkiri festival of regional folk. Two days of bal-folk dances and workshops fill the Saulnières hall, where bands and dance masters lead all ages into the communal steps, in a warm celebration that keeps the region's living folk traditions on their feet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Saint-Georges-du-Bois (Le Mans urban area), Pays de la Loire (Sarthe), France
festivals.address: Saint-Georges-du-Bois (Le Mans urban area), Pays de la Loire (Sarthe), France

festivals.name: Folklore Festival under Poľana
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the town of Detva celebrates the shepherd culture of the Poľana mountains with three days of Slovak folklore. Singers, dancers, and craftspeople in mountain costume fill the open-air stage, in a festival that has gathered the traditions of this proud highland region since 1966. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Detva, Central Slovakia (Banská Bystrica Region, Podpoľanie), Slovakia
festivals.address: Detva, Central Slovakia (Banská Bystrica Region, Podpoľanie), Slovakia

festivals.name: Folklore Parade Weekend
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the village of Amel holds a grand folklore parade for its Summer Festival. Villagers in historical and work costumes march with displays of old farming and crafts, in a celebration that grew from a 1976 music festival into a showcase of Eifel customs. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Folkloric Festival “La Vatra Horelor Bucuriene”
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the southern Moldovan village of Bucuria celebrates its folk heritage at "La Vatra Horelor Bucuriene." A lively parade of traditional costumes, folk performances, and crafts fills the village, with the old Southern Moldovan dances like the hora and sârba, in a celebration now more than thirty years strong. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Folkloric Pastoral Festival "Sheep’s Descent from the Mountain"
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Oltenian village of Baia de Fier celebrates the descent of its flocks from the mountains with a three-day pastoral festival. Shepherds in traditional costume parade alongside folk music, dance, and displays of local craft, in a celebration that honors the age-old transhumance of the Parâng mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Folkowisko: Roots
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Polish-Ukrainian frontier village of Gorajec hosts Folkowisko, an open-air festival of cross-border folk culture and ecology. Multicultural workshops, folk-rock concerts, and craft markets fill the gathering, in a celebration created to foster dialogue and friendship between Polish and Ukrainian communities. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Forgács Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Székely town of Covasna celebrates its woodworking heritage with the Forgács Festival, named for the curls of wood shavings.
