Carving and woodcraft demonstrations join folk music, dance, and displays of old heirlooms, in a grassroots celebration of the rural crafts at the heart of this Transylvanian community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Foxford River Fest
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the town of Foxford gathers along the River Moy for its River Fest. Boating, music, and community events fill the days, in a celebration of the river heritage and community spirit of this Mayo town, organized by local volunteers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Francofolies
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Atlantic port of La Rochelle fills with song for the Francofolies, a festival devoted to music sung in French. Founded in 1985 out of a love for French chanson, it now draws huge crowds and big names, celebrating francophone music in all its modern variety. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Frankfurt Apple Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the city of Frankfurt celebrates its beloved Apfelwein, the tart local cider, with a festival on the Münchener Straße. Long shared tables fill with neighbors over glasses of apple wine, regional dishes, and old drinking songs, in a warm tribute to a drink at the heart of the city's identity. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Frankfurt am Main, Frankfurt am Main, Hesse, Germany
festivals.address: Frankfurt am Main, Frankfurt am Main, Hesse, Germany

festivals.name: Fredrikstad Borderland Literature Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the city of Fredrikstad gathers writers and readers for the literature festival Ord i Grenseland. Talks and readings explore the life and culture of the borderland, in a celebration that draws on Østfold's place on the old Norwegian-Swedish frontier and its love of storytelling. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Free Open-Air Music Stages
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each May, the Belgian town of Eupen fills its center with free open-air music for the Musik Marathon. Stages across the city showcase a wide range of acts in a warm, family-friendly atmosphere, in a celebration that brings out the diverse musical talent of Belgium's German-speaking community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: French Guiana Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · From January to Lent, French Guiana throws itself into a carnival like no other, the territory's biggest celebration. Masked "Touloulou" ladies lead the famous costume balls, parades fill the streets of Cayenne week after week, and it all ends with the burning of King Vaval on Ash Wednesday. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Fresh Wave Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the medieval Kastel Fortress in Banja Luka pulses with the biggest electronic music festival in Bosnia and Herzegovina. DJs and crowds fill the riverside ramparts deep into the night, in a festival that pairs the latest dance music with the old stones of the fortress. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Frisian Balloon Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Frisian town of Joure fills its skies with color for the Friese Ballonfeesten, one of the largest hot-air balloon festivals in the Netherlands. Each evening since 1986, great balloons rise over the countryside as crowds gather below, in a celebration of summer, community, and spectacle in Friesland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Fuschl Harvest Thanksgiving Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the village of Fuschl am See gives thanks for the harvest with the Dorffest zum Erntedank. A church service and a thanksgiving procession fill the Kirchenplatz, in a celebration of the customary Austrian Harvest Thanksgiving and the rural year's bounty. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Fårö Night
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each summer's end, the island of Fårö celebrates the close of the season with Fårönatta. The northern Gotland community gathers for a great finale, in a celebration grown from a single September evening into the island's beloved summer farewell. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Fêra de Barrancos (Touros de Morte Bullfights)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Alentejo border village of Barrancos holds four days of festivity famous for its bullfights. Here alone in Portugal, the traditional touros de morte are still permitted, by a special hard-won exemption, in a celebration of Our Lady of the Conception that lies at the very heart of the village's identity. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Fête de la Musique
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Every June 21, on the summer solstice, all of France turns into a stage for the Fête de la Musique, when free concerts spill into streets, squares, and parks. Launched in Paris in 1982, it invites everyone from star performers to amateurs to play, filling the longest day of the year with music. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Fête de l’Humanité
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, a great field near Paris fills for the Fête de l'Humanité, a three-day mix of big-name concerts, political debate, and festival spirit. Founded in 1930 by the French Communist Party, it has grown into one of the country's largest gatherings, where music and left-wing politics meet. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Le Plessis-Pâté, French Communist community, France
festivals.address: Le Plessis-Pâté, French Communist community, France

festivals.name: Fêtes de Wallonie
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the city of Namur throws the Fêtes de Wallonie, a five-day celebration of the spirit and heritage of French-speaking Wallonia. As the regional capital, Namur fills with folklore, music, and street festivities, in a proud yearly party that draws Walloons from all around. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: G! Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the tiny Faroese village of Syðrugøta hosts G! Festival, with stages set right on the beach beneath dramatic green mountains. For three days, Faroese and international artists play to crowds who can swim between sets, in one of the most striking festival settings anywhere, going since 2002. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gagauz Carpets (Carpet Weaving Festival)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Gagauz village of Gaidar celebrates its weaving heritage with a carpet festival. Weavers gather to show the patterns once made by every bride for her dowry, in a celebration of a home craft handed down from the Gagauz people's nomadic Turkic ancestors and still woven with pride today. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gagauz Folk Song Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Gagauz town of Ceadîr-Lunga gathers musicians and singers for a festival of song in the Gagauz language. Soloists and ensembles from Gagauzia and beyond perform old folk tunes and new compositions, in a celebration that helps keep the language and heritage of this small Turkic people alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gagauz Mărțișor Spring Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each year around March 1, the Gagauz capital of Comrat welcomes spring with the Mărțișor Gagauziada. Ten days of music, color, and traditional motifs fill the cultural center and public squares, in a celebration of the old spring holiday shared across Moldova and given a warm Gagauz spirit all its own. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Gagauz Wine Festival (Gagauz Wine Day)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November 1, the Gagauz capital of Comrat celebrates Wine Day, marking the end of the grape harvest and the start of the new wine. The central square fills with stalls as wineries and families pour their best, in a festival rooted in the deep wine-making traditions of this small southern land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Galanta Fair
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Slovak town of Galanta holds its great fair, a celebration of crafts, food, and music. Traditional Hungarian folk joins contemporary concerts and gastronomy, marking the anniversary of the town's charter, in a gathering that carries on Galanta's long heritage of lively summer fairs. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Galichfest (Eco-Ethno Festival)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each June, the ancient town of Halych hosts Galichfest, an eco-ethnographic festival of Galician culture and care for the land. Permaculture workshops and craft demonstrations in pottery, weaving, and carving fill the day, in a celebration that honors Halych's place as a cradle of Ukrainian statehood and tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Halych, Halych region, Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast, Ukraine
festivals.address: Halych, Halych region, Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast, Ukraine

festivals.name: Galway International Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the city of Galway fills with two weeks of theatre, music, dance, circus, and street spectacle for its International Arts Festival. New Irish work shares the bill with acts from around the world, turning the lively western city into one of Ireland's great stages for the arts. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Galway International Oyster & Seafood Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the city of Galway toasts the start of the native oyster season with one of the world's oldest seafood festivals. Oyster-shucking champions race the clock, and crowds gather over fresh shellfish, music, and pints, in a celebration of Ireland's coast going strong since 1954. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Garage Fest
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia pulses with beats for Garage Fest, a two-day electronic music festival. Quickly grown into a major local event, it blends contemporary club sounds with the energy of the city, in a celebration of the modern urban youth culture of this southern Ukrainian industrial hub. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Zaporizhzhia, Zaporizhzhia region, Ukrainian urban youth culture, Ukraine
festivals.address: Zaporizhzhia, Zaporizhzhia region, Ukrainian urban youth culture, Ukraine

festivals.name: Gdańsk Kashubian Days
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the city of Gdańsk celebrates its Kashubian roots with two weeks of festivities honoring this distinct Pomeranian culture. A Kashubian market, folk concerts on the island of Ołowianka, and an old-time fish market bring the region's language, music, and crafts to life along the waterfront. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Geesala Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each mid-August, the Gaeltacht village of Geesala gathers for its festival. Local music, Gaelic football, and cultural events fill the week, in a celebration that highlights the traditions, sport, and music of this Irish-speaking corner of Mayo. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Genoese Helmet Knight Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the Genoese Fortress above Sudak rings with the clash of swords for the Genoese Helmet festival. Costumed reenactors stage the medieval battles of Tatars, Russians, Genoese, and Ottomans, with armored knights jousting and dueling, in a vivid celebration of the storied medieval past of this Crimean coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: George Russell Easter Festival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each Easter, the town of Lurgan celebrates its native son, the writer and painter George "AE" Russell, a leading light of Ireland's cultural revival. Exhibitions of his art and poetry, literary talks, music, and guided walks fill the festival, in a warm tribute to a remarkable local figure born here in 1867. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Lurgan, Lurgan & North Armagh (Irish literary revival), United Kingdom
festivals.address: Lurgan, Lurgan & North Armagh (Irish literary revival), United Kingdom

festivals.name: German Culture Decade (Mountainous Banat)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the German-speaking communities of the mountainous Banat gather to celebrate their heritage. German folk music, exhibitions, and literary events fill localities across Caraș-Severin, in a celebration that keeps the identity of this Swabian minority alive in their corner of the Romanian highlands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Geyser Nights
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the spa town of Sijarinska Banja celebrates beside its famous hot-water geyser with Geyser Nights. Concerts and festivities blending Albanian and Serbian folk with pop fill the plateau by the spouting spring, in a celebration that grew from the town's long tradition of spa tourism and regional gathering. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ghanafest
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Argotti Gardens in Floriana fill with the sound of għana, the improvised folk song of Malta, for Ghanafest. Local għannejja trade sung poetic verses alongside Mediterranean and world music, in a celebration that honors a centuries-old oral tradition at the heart of Maltese culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gibraltar International Dance Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each year, Gibraltar hosts an international dance festival that gathers troupes from the Rock and far beyond. From ballet to flamenco, dancers compete across many styles before the judges, in a colorful event that reflects the little territory's mix of cultures and its love of the stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gibraltar International Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year, Gibraltar fills dramatic venues like St. Michael's Cave and Casemates Square with the sound of jazz. International and local musicians take the stage for the territory's international jazz festival, begun in 2012, in a celebration of the music and its many influences in one of the Mediterranean's most striking settings. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gibraltar National Day
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Every September 10, Gibraltar turns out in red and white for its National Day, marking the 1967 vote that affirmed its ties to Britain. Street parties, processions, concerts, and fireworks fill the day, in a proud celebration of the Rock's identity and its right to choose its own future. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Gibraltar Pride
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, Gibraltar celebrates diversity and inclusion with its Pride festival and parade. A colorful procession winds along Main Street from Casemates Square to entertainment and family events, in a celebration, established in the 2010s, of the open and welcoming spirit of the Rock. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gibraltar World Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the dramatic cavern of St. Michael's Cave fills with the sounds of the world for the Gibraltar World Music Festival. Fado, samba, Sephardic jazz, and more take the stage, in a celebration, begun in 2012, that mirrors the rich Andalusian, British, Jewish, and Indian threads of Gibraltar's own culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gijón Natural Cider Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Asturian city of Gijón celebrates its beloved natural cider with the Fiesta de la Sidra Natural. A cidermill fair, pouring contests, bagpipe music, and mass attempts at cider-pouring records fill the gardens, in a celebration of the tangy, high-poured drink that Asturians hold as their own. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gjirokastër National Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2027 · Every four years, the stone city of Gjirokastër gathers Albania's folk traditions inside its great hilltop castle. For a week, singers, dancers, and musicians perform from across the country, above all the haunting many-voiced iso-polyphony of the south, a singing style honored by UNESCO. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Glens Storytelling Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each autumn, the village of Cushendall gathers storytellers for a festival of the oral tradition of the Glens of Antrim. Renowned Irish and international tellers spin tales at community story-swaps and on walks through forests and along the coast, in a celebration of the rich storytelling heritage of rural Ulster. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ballygally, Glens of Antrim (Irish and Ulster-Scots storytelling), United Kingdom
festivals.address: Ballygally, Glens of Antrim (Irish and Ulster-Scots storytelling), United Kingdom

festivals.name: Golden Autumn Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Slovenian town of Dravograd welcomes the season with Zlata jesen, the Golden Autumn festival. A rural fair of stalls heaped with the local harvest fills the day, in a warm celebration created to greet autumn and give thanks for the fruits of the fields and orchards of the Koroška region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Golden Belt Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Montenegrin town of Mojkovac gathers folk dance and music ensembles for the Golden Belt festival. Named for a local mountain pass, it brings a parade of performers in traditional costume and stage performances of folk dance, in a celebration that puts Montenegro's deep folklore at its heart. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Golden Fish International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Danube Delta town of Tulcea gathers children and young dancers from Romania and around the world for the Golden Fish folklore festival. Founded in 1993, it has grown into one of the country's largest folk events, in a celebration that ties the heritage of the delta to the wider world through young performers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gombaszög National Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the wooded site of Gombaszög hosts Slovakia's oldest festival of Hungarian culture, two open-air days of folk music, dance, and heritage. Ensembles and crowds gather to celebrate the traditions of the country's Hungarian community, in a gathering that has run since 1955. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Slavec, Hungarian Minority Region - Košický Region (Gemer area), Slovakia
festivals.address: Slavec, Hungarian Minority Region - Košický Region (Gemer area), Slovakia

festivals.name: Good Friday Passion Statues Procession (Qormi)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Good Friday, the parish of St. George in Qormi carries the statues of the Passion through the streets. Devotees bear life-size tableaux, several dating from the 19th century, on wooden floats, in a solemn celebration rooted in the Holy Week traditions of Malta. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Goosebump Festival
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November, Hungary celebrates St. Martin's Day with the Goosebump Festival, a nationwide feast of roast goose and freshly fermented wine. Restaurants and cellars across the country join in, reviving a centuries-old custom of marking this autumn day with the season's goose and its first young wines. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Goral Folklore Festival (Bachledka)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, high in the Bachledka mountains, the village of Ždiar gathers for its Goral folklore festival at some 1,200 meters. Traditional Goral song and dance share the stage with newer performances against a backdrop of Carpathian peaks, in a celebration that keeps the proud highland heritage of the Gorals alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ždiar, Goral community of Ždiar/Pieniny, Slovakia
festivals.address: Ždiar, Goral community of Ždiar/Pieniny, Slovakia

festivals.name: GoroVenture Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each late February, the Slovenian Alpine Museum in Mojstrana hosts the GoroVenture festival. Mountaineering, adventure sports, and literature fill the winter days, in a celebration of the deep influence of mountain culture in the Gorenjska region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gotland Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the island of Gotland fills its medieval churches with classical music for a week-long chamber music festival. Founded in 1986 by the pianist Staffan Scheja, it brings Swedish and international ensembles to atmospheric venues in Visby and the countryside, in a celebration of fine music among the island's ancient stones. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gotland Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the farms of Gotland throw open their gates for the island's harvest festival. Local farmers and producers welcome visitors to taste and buy the bounty of the season, in a celebration that ties into the age-old importance of the harvest on Gotland's rich farmlands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gotland Literature Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each October, the island capital of Visby gathers writers and readers for the Gotland Literature Festival. Author talks, poetry readings, workshops, and a book fair fill a weekend, in a young festival, begun in 2025, created to give the island its own celebration of the written word. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Visby, Literary community of Gotland, Sweden
festivals.address: Visby, Literary community of Gotland, Sweden

festivals.name: Gotland Sheep Week
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the island of Gotland celebrates its centuries-old sheep farming and wool tradition with Sheep Week. Centered on Lövsta, the festival brings lamb, wool products, and farm life to visitors, in a warm celebration of an animal that has shaped the island's economy and landscape for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gotland Taste Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the island of Gotland celebrates its farm-to-table bounty with the Taste Festival. Producers across the island share their food and drink at the height of the autumn harvest, in a celebration that draws on Gotland's rich tradition of local farming and good eating. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gouveia Cultural Fair
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Serra da Estrela town of Gouveia hosts the Romaria Cultural, an outdoor festival of the arts. Free concerts, visual art, and nature workshops unfold in the mountain countryside, in a celebration that brings creativity to the high, green heart of Portugal's tallest range. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Gouveia, Serra da Estrela, Gouveia, Portugal
festivals.address: Gouveia, Serra da Estrela, Gouveia, Portugal

festivals.name: Gozo Ceramics Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the seaside village of Xlendi on Gozo celebrates ceramic art with a festival by the bay. Local and international potters set up stalls and live demonstrations, and visitors can even try the wheel, in a celebration that draws on the island's clay-working heritage and lively creative community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gozo Food Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Gozitan town of Xewkija fills St. John the Baptist Square with the aromas of street food from around the world and home. Cuisines from many lands join traditional Gozitan dishes amid live music and family fun, in a celebration of the island's farming identity and local producers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gradnik Evenings
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, the Brda town of Dobrovo honors the poet Alojz Gradnik, a son of these hills, with the Gradnik Evenings. Literary and musical gatherings bring writers and readers together in the medieval town, in a celebration of a beloved Slovenian poet and the wine-country region that shaped him. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Granada Flamenco Film Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the city of Granada extends its great flamenco tradition onto the screen with a flamenco film festival. Created in 2024 by the historic Peña La Platería as part of the city's flamenco Bienal, it brings the art of the dance and the guitar into the world of cinema, in a celebration of flamenco in a new light. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Grand Carnival of Ter Apel
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each spring, the northern Dutch town of Ter Apel throws a grand carnival of floats and costumes through its streets, followed by parties in great tents. One of the few carnivals in this Protestant-leaning province, it brings the whole community together for the joyful pre-Lenten days in a warmly cherished local tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Grand Crossbow Palio of San Marino
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 3, the republic of San Marino holds its grand crossbow tournament in a stone quarry beneath the old town. Marksmen in medieval dress take aim with heavy crossbows in a contest rooted in the little state's centuries-old martial tradition, complete with flag-throwers and drummers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Granville Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Normandy port of Granville throws one of France's oldest and largest carnivals, born long ago among its fishermen. Giant satirical floats and costumed crowds fill the streets for a week, ending in a wild confetti battle, in a celebration honored by UNESCO. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Grape Festival (Banat, Arad County)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the shore of Imperial Lake near Ghioroc fills with festivity for the Banat grape festival. Grape and must tasting, traditional costumes, folk music, and a Ball of the Grapes fill the day, in a celebration of the viticultural heritage of this Arad County wine country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the Asturian town of Cangas del Narcea celebrates its little-known wine country with the Fiesta de la Vendimia. Growers in traditional dress lead processions through the vineyards, and hundreds of cellars open their doors for tastings and grape-stomping, marking the harvest in these steep green hills. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each autumn, the Kosovar town of Rahovec, heart of the country's wine country, celebrates its grape harvest with Hardh Fest. Festivities honor the work of the growers in this vineyard capital, in a celebration that gathers the community around the vines that give the town its identity and pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Rahovec, Rahovec/Gjakova region, Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia
festivals.address: Rahovec, Rahovec/Gjakova region, Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia

festivals.name: Grape Harvest Festival (Pregrada)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Pregrada celebrates its grape harvest with Branje grožđa. Winegrowers and villagers fill the square with folk music, dance, and a colorful parade of grape-pickers in period costume, in a celebration rooted in the centuries-old viticulture of the green hills of Hrvatsko Zagorje. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Graz Styrian Folk Culture Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Austrian city of Graz turns its squares into one big celebration of Styrian folk culture, with traditional dress, brass music, and regional food at every turn. Known as Aufsteirern, it fills the old town with the songs, crafts, and flavors of the surrounding countryside. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Gračanica Monastery Feast (Holy Dormition of the Virgin)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August 28, on the feast of the Dormition, the medieval Gračanica Monastery celebrates its slava. A solemn Divine Liturgy with bishops draws thousands from Kosovo and Serbia to the church built by King Milutin in 1321, in a celebration at one of the great spiritual centers of Serbian Orthodoxy in Kosovo. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Gračanica, Kosovo Serb Region (Gračanica municipality), Kosovo
festivals.address: Gračanica, Kosovo Serb Region (Gračanica municipality), Kosovo

festivals.name: Great Latgalian Market
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the Latgalian town of Ludza holds a great market at its old castle mound, reviving the spirit of a medieval fair. Vendors from across Latgale sell folk crafts, foods, and textiles amid craft demonstrations and promenades, in a celebration of the agricultural and craft traditions of the Latgale region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Great Laxey Fair
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · In the green of Laxey Valley Gardens, this Victorian-themed fair brings the old Manx mining village together for a costumed parade, live local music, craft stalls, and games. It grew up to honor Laxey's days as a working mining town, and the period dress keeps that heritage close at hand. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Green Man Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, a green valley in the Brecon Beacons hosts Green Man, an independent festival of music and arts set against the Welsh mountains. Rock, folk, and alternative acts share the bill with art, wellness, and family fun, in a four-day gathering known for its non-corporate, community spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Crickhowell, Brecon Beacons (Bannau Brycheiniog) country culture, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Crickhowell, Brecon Beacons (Bannau Brycheiniog) country culture, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Grimstad Ski Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Grimstad turns its central square into a cross-country ski course for a winter weekend of races, children's events, and a cheerful party afterward. Run by local ski and youth groups, it gathers the whole town in the snow to celebrate Norway's deep-rooted love of skiing. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Groningen Market Day
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the city of Groningen hosts the Marktendag, billed as the largest market day in the northern Netherlands. Hundreds of stalls fill the central squares with food, goods, and crafts, drawing crowds from all around in a lively autumn tradition built around the city's old market culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Grovely Woods Rights
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each May 29 the village of Great Wishford keeps Oak Apple Day to proclaim its ancient woodland rights in nearby Grovely Woods. The day begins at dawn as villagers bang pots and pans to wake the parish for an early gathering of wood, in a custom centuries old. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Great Wishford, South West England, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Great Wishford, South West England, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Guadeloupe Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · From early January until Lent, the islands of Guadeloupe throb with carnival, a cornerstone of local life. Week after week, parades fill the streets with elaborate costumes and pounding drums, in a celebration shaped over more than a century by both European and African traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Guadeloupe International Zouk Festival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year, the Guadeloupean city of Pointe-à-Pitre celebrates Zouk, the music born on the island in the 1980s, with an international festival. Concerts, dance, and workshops honor the genre and its Creole roots, in a celebration that puts local artists and a home-grown sound at the heart of the island's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Guadix Cascamorras
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 9, the Andalusian city of Guadix fires a rocket to send the Cascamorras on his legendary journey to neighboring Baza. The mischievous figure sets off in a tradition shared between the two towns, in a celebration of an old rivalry-turned-bond that fills both with festivity and communal pride. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Guardian Angel Church Fair
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Styrian village of Kleinsölk holds its centuries-old Guardian Angel church fair. A morning Holy Mass joins the ceremonial Almabtrieb, the homecoming of flower-decked cattle from the high pastures, in a rural celebration so old that Archduke Johann himself noted it back in 1819. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Guitar Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the Serbian town of Zaječar turns up the volume for Gitarijada, one of the oldest rock festivals in the country, running since 1966. Up-and-coming bands compete alongside big-name headliners over three loud nights, in a festival that earned the town its nickname as Serbia's rock city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Guitars from the World Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the lakeside city of Lugano fills its venues with the sound of strings for the Guitars from the World festival. International virtuosos and folk ensembles bring the guitar's many voices to the stage, in a celebration that reflects Lugano's place as a crossroads of Mediterranean and world music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gusinje Ethno Fest
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · In the mountain town of Gusinje, this cultural festival fills the square with traditional music, dance ensembles, and crafts like filigree and wool weaving. Evening concerts played on old instruments draw out the layered heritage of the Plav-Gusinje region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gusinje Summer
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Montenegrin town of Gusinje fills its main street with music for Gusinje Summer. Mainstream acts and open-air concerts draw visitors from across the region to dance and celebrate, in a lively highlight of the town's summer calendar beneath the peaks of the Prokletije mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gustar Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the natural amphitheater of the Orheiul Vechi reserve hosts the Gustar Music Festival under the open sky. International and local bands play amid the dramatic cliffs and ancient monasteries of this historic Moldovan landscape, in a celebration, begun in 2010, that has become one of the country's great cultural events. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: GuteFår Sheepdog Championship
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, skilled border collies and their handlers from across Sweden gather on Gotland for the GuteFår Sheepdog Championship. Dog-and-handler teams test their craft herding the island's native Gute sheep, in a celebration of the deep partnership between shepherd and dog at the heart of Gotland's farming life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Guča Trumpet Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the small Serbian village of Guča all but explodes as hundreds of thousands gather for the world's wildest trumpet festival. Brass bands roam the streets in fierce, joyful competition, playing the fast Balkan tunes that fuel days of dancing, feasting, and round-the-clock celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Gällivare Minority Languages Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the municipality of Gällivare celebrates its minority languages with a two-day festival. Lule and Northern Sami, Meänkieli, and Finnish fill the music and program, in a celebration of the peoples who have long shared this land of Sápmi and the Torne Valley. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Gällivare, Lule Sami, North Sami, Finnish, Meânåëlæ (Tornedal) communities in Norrbotten, Sweden
festivals.address: Gällivare, Lule Sami, North Sami, Finnish, Meânåëlæ (Tornedal) communities in Norrbotten, Sweden

festivals.name: Haapsalu White Nights Music Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the resort town of Haapsalu celebrates the long, luminous evenings with its White Nights chamber music festival. Classical and chamber concerts fill the castle courtyard, the cathedral, and other historic venues, in a celebration, begun in the early 1990s, that matches fine music to the serene coastal calm. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Haidushka Sofra Festival
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the Bulgarian town of Haskovo hosts the Haidushka Sofra, a great competition of folk dance and the circle dance known as horo. Troupes from across Bulgaria and beyond perform in elaborate costume, in a celebration that honors the legacy of the haiduk rebels and the country's deep love of dance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Halchevskyi Fest
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the Podillia region honors the insurgent commander Yakiv Halchevskyi with a festival of music and history. Historical exhibitions, a commemorative procession, and concerts by Ukrainian rock and folk bands fill the day, in a patriotic celebration of a local hero who fought for Ukraine's independence around 1919. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Handel Procession
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, thousands set out on foot from Valkenswaard before dawn for the Handel Procession, a 40-kilometer pilgrimage to a Marian chapel. Walking through the Brabant countryside to honor the Virgin, they carry on a tradition born in the 17th century in prayer against the plague, now one of the region's great acts of devotion. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hanover Marksmen's Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Hanover hosts what is billed as the world's largest marksmen's festival, a ten-day folk fair rooted in the old shooting guilds. A huge parade of marksmen and bands winds for kilometers through town, alongside fairground rides, beer tents, and the festive spirit of a tradition reaching back to medieval times. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hanselsprung of Schramberg
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · On the Sunday of Fasnacht, Schramberg fills with its Hanselsprung parade, a highlight of the Swabian-Alemannic carnival. The figures trace back to the town's old Hanselmiller craft-guild rituals, and their leaping march carries a symbolic sweeping-out of winter. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hardanger Music Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the orchards and churches of Lofthus in the Hardanger region fill with music for the Hardanger Musikkfest. Founded in 1996, it weaves classical and folk together, often with the keening Hardanger fiddle, amid the fruit trees of Norway's historic orchard country, in a celebration of music and landscape alike. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Haritonenko-Fest (Living Statues Festival)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · For three September days, Sumy hands its streets over to living statues, marionettes, and street performers who freeze and come to life in the open air. Named for a local philanthropist, the festival ties the city's history to a playful, modern kind of public art. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Harlingen Fishermen's Days
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the Frisian port of Harlingen celebrates its seafaring roots with five days of Visserijdagen. Music, dance, markets, and cultural events fill the harbor town, in a maritime festival that honors the long fishing and naval tradition at the heart of this old Dutch port. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Harvest Days (Bread and Grains Festival)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On the Polica fields near Berane each late August, this harvest festival honors the region's wheat and bread. Farmers display their crops and compete for awards as grain producers and bakers, and the day overflows with local food and old-fashioned hospitality. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Harvest Festival (Rumšiškės)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the Open-Air Museum of Lithuania at Rumšiškės celebrates the harvest with Derliaus šventė. Rural traditions of the 18th to 20th centuries come alive among the museum's old farmsteads, in a celebration that brings the rhythms of the country harvest of bygone Lithuania vividly back to life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Harvest Funfair
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September the town of Tubize fills its Grand-Place and nearby streets with a harvest funfair of rides, stalls, and amusements. Like the kermesses of other Brabant Wallon villages, it grew up around the local parish and remains a warm community gathering. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hastings Bonfire Night
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each autumn, the seaside town of Hastings marks Guy Fawkes Night with a torchlit procession down the seafront to a great bonfire. Drummers, costumed marchers, and effigies fill the night, ending in fireworks, in a celebration that carries on the centuries-old Sussex tradition of bonfire societies. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Hazelnut Festival in Infiesto
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On the first Sunday of October, the Asturian town of Infiesto celebrates its hazelnut harvest in the Piloña valley. An artisan market, folk music, woodcraft, and livestock shows fill the day, in a country harvest fair that honors the hazelnut groves which fill the hills around the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Heamiel Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Over four days in late June, Bolsward revives the old hooimaaltijd, the hay-meal that once thanked farmhands for bringing in the harvest. A colorful historical parade of floats, bands, and costumed townsfolk carries that Frisian farming memory through the streets. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Bolsward, City of Bolsward, Friesland, Netherlands
festivals.address: Bolsward, City of Bolsward, Friesland, Netherlands

festivals.name: Heidelberg Summer Day Parade
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Children lead the Heidelberger Sommertagszug through the city, carrying decorated sticks to chase off winter and welcome the spring. The parade belongs to the wider Kurpfalz tradition of driving out the cold season, and it ends with a stage program for the whole community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Helsinki Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each late summer, Helsinki holds its largest cultural festival, a sweep of concerts, dance, theatre, and art across the city. It often opens with the much-loved Night of the Arts, when venues stay open late, in a celebration of Finnish creativity with roots in the Sibelius festivals of 1951. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Helsinki, Helsinki capital arts community, Finland
festivals.address: Helsinki, Helsinki capital arts community, Finland

festivals.name: Henrichenburg Marksmen’s Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · In the Henrichenburg district of Waltrop, the yearly Schützenfest brings out the marksmen's club for parades, a festival tent, and shooting contests. It belongs to a long Westphalian tradition of shooting societies, organized as ever by the local Schützenverein. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Herbs Festival (Mladen)
festivals.description: Late June 2027 · Around Enyovden in late June, the village of Mladen celebrates the healing power of wild herbs gathered at midsummer. Enyovden is one of Bulgaria's oldest folk holidays, marking the turn of the sun, and here it fills the village with the season's medicinal plants. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Herceg Novi Film Festival (Montenegro Film Festival)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the coastal town of Herceg Novi celebrates Montenegrin and regional cinema with an international film festival. Screenings, workshops, and the awarding of the Golden Mimosa fill venues like the old Kanli Kula fortress, in a celebration of the film heritage of this Adriatic town, held since 1987. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Herättäjä Festival (Awakening Festival)
festivals.description: Early July 2027 · Each July, tens of thousands of Finns gather in an open field for the Herättäjäjuhlat, a festival of the old Lutheran Awakening movement. They sit together for hymn singing and quiet worship under the summer sky, in a deeply Finnish gathering that travels to a different town each year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hesse Day
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, a different Hessian town hosts Hessentag, the great state festival of Hesse. A grand parade, concerts, fairs, and folk performances showcase the host town's history alongside the culture of the whole state, in a celebration begun in 1961 to weave the regions of Hesse together. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hidrellez Festival
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each May, the Tatar community of Techirghiol in Dobrogea welcomes summer with the Hidrellez festival. Traditional games like archery and horse races join folk music, dance, and cuisine, in a centuries-old Turkic-Tatar celebration of nature's renewal, marking the day the prophets Khidr and Elias are said to meet. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: High Five Snow & Ski Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the alpine town of Annecy celebrates ski culture with the High Five Snow and Ski Festival. Film screenings, athlete meet-and-greets, and live music gather the skiing community, in a celebration, born in 2010, that grew from the town's deep love of the mountains and the snow. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Highland Festival & Miss Bjeshka Pageant
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the highland village of Lepushë celebrates the traditions of the Kelmend region with Logu i Bjeshkëve. Folk dances, songs, competitions, and the crowning of a "Miss Bjeshka" fill the mountain amphitheater, in a celebration, centuries old and revived in 1998, of the highlander culture of northern Albania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lepushë, Northern Albania (Gheg) - Malësia e Madhe, Shkodër County, Albania
festivals.address: Lepushë, Northern Albania (Gheg) - Malësia e Madhe, Shkodër County, Albania

festivals.name: Hiiu Folk Festival (Hiiumaa)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the quiet island of Hiiumaa hosts the Hiiu Folk festival, a family-friendly gathering of acoustic and traditional music. Set at forest campgrounds and free of alcohol, it puts nature and culture side by side, in a celebration that matches gentle folk song to the peaceful beauty of the Estonian isle. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hiiumaa Fish and Beer Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August on the island of Hiiumaa, Kassari celebrates the fishing trade and local brewing with fish dishes, regional beers, workshops, and music. Fishing has long sustained the island, and the festival brings its fishers and brewers together in a salt-air celebration of coastal life. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hildasholm Folk Music Gathering
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Hildasholm estate in Leksand gathers folk musicians of all ages for a one-day gathering of tunes. Fiddlers and players fill the grounds with the låtar of the region, in a celebration rooted in Dalarna's deep tradition of spelman gatherings, where the old melodies are played and shared. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hildesheim Folk and Marksmen's Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the city of Hildesheim holds its four-day Volks- und Schützenfest, a folk and marksmen's festival. Shooting contests, a great fairground, and festivity fill the old festival grounds, in a celebration deeply rooted in the town's heritage of citizen militias and the old shooting guilds. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Himarë International Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the coastal town of Himarë hosts an international festival of classical music. Pianists, cellists, and ensembles from abroad play works by Beethoven, Chopin, and more alongside local folk musicians in intimate venues, in a celebration that brings world-class music to this corner of the Albanian Riviera. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Himarë, Greek minority in Albania, Albania
festivals.address: Himarë, Greek minority in Albania, Albania

festivals.name: Hinterglemm Farmers' Market
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Salzburg resort village of Hinterglemm holds its farmers' market and folk festival. Dozens of vendors offer homemade foods and crafts amid tractor displays and local music, in a celebration of the Pinzgau alpine farming heritage that has long shaped life in this mountain valley. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Hip Hop Lives
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Slovak capital of Bratislava throws Hip Hop Žije, billed as the largest hip-hop festival in the Czech and Slovak region. Local rap artists and DJs fill the stages, in a celebration, launched in the mid-2010s, of the vibrant contemporary urban music culture of the two countries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hirlatz Devils Krampus Run
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · On St. Nicholas Eve in Hallstatt, the local club known as the Hirlatz Devils takes to the streets in horned Krampus costume to accompany St. Nicholas. Local legend says the Krampus comes out of the Hirlatz cave that night, and the run threads that old Alpine tale into the village Christmas season. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: Hallstatt, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria
festivals.address: Hallstatt, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria

festivals.name: Hirtshals Fish Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Danish port of Hirtshals celebrates the sea and its fishing community with a three-day festival. Live fish auctions, seafood, and harborside festivity fill the days, in a celebration that honors the catch and the long maritime life of a town that has fished these northern waters for over a century. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Hispanicity Day Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October 12, the town of Guadalupe celebrates the Fiesta de la Hispanidad in honor of its Virgin, crowned Queen of all the Hispanic world in 1929. Festivities reaffirm the town's role as a spiritual heart of Hispanic culture, in a celebration that ties this Extremaduran shrine to the lands across the ocean. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Historic Carnival of Ivrea (Battle of the Oranges)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Piedmont town of Ivrea erupts into the Battle of the Oranges, the largest food fight in Italy. Teams on foot pelt costumed fighters riding in carts with tons of oranges, staining the squares red, in a wild three-day spectacle that draws crowds from all over. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Historic Castellers Day (La Mercè Festival)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · During Barcelona's great La Mercè festival each September, the city's main square fills for one of Catalonia's proudest sights: the building of human towers. Teams called colles climb onto one another's shoulders to raise castells many stories high, in a daring tradition that ends with a small child scrambling to the top. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Historic Centre Parishes' Magusto
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November 11, on St. Martin's Day, the parishes of Porto's historic center gather their elders for a communal Magusto. Roasted chestnuts and the year's new wine are shared in the old Portuguese way, in a warm celebration of São Martinho that brings neighbors and generations together against the autumn chill. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Historic Fool's Parade of Oberndorf
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Shrove Tuesday, the town of Oberndorf am Neckar fills with masked fools for its Großer historischer Narrensprung. The centuries-old Fasnet parade blends Alemannic carnival with local folklore and ritual, in one of the Black Forest region's most famous and deeply rooted celebrations of the wild days before Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Historical Music and Dance Festival (FEMUDAHI)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the city of Ceuta celebrates the medieval music of al-Ándalus and the Mediterranean with FEMUDAHI. Concerts, dance workshops, and exhibitions explore the legacy of the Andalusian world, in a festival, begun in 2023, rooted in the city's place as a crossroads of cultures on the Strait of Gibraltar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Historical Tournament of the Boroughs of Susa
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the ancient Italian city of Susa reenacts an 11th-century noble wedding for its historical tournament. Six historic boroughs compete in jousts, archery, and feasts amid costumed processions, recalling the marriage of Countess Adelaide, in a vivid celebration of the town's feudal-era past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hita Medieval Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Castilian town of Hita recreates medieval life with a festival held since the early 1960s. Famed as the setting of the great medieval poem El Libro del Buen Amor, the town fills with jousts, music, and feasting, in a celebration of the literary and historic heritage that gives Hita its lasting fame. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Hita, Castilla-La Mancha (Comarca de La Alcarria), Spain
festivals.address: Hita, Castilla-La Mancha (Comarca de La Alcarria), Spain

festivals.name: Hofer Fair
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the town of Appenzell holds the Hofer Chölbi, the region's largest market fair, on the weekend after St. Maurice's Day. Fairground rides, food stalls of raclette and spiced breads, and lively crowds fill the town, in a celebration with roots in medieval market rights granted centuries ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hohenstein Volksfest (Mountain Festival)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · High on the Pfaffenberg hill above Hohenstein-Ernstthal, this summer Bergfest gathers families for amusement rides, concerts, a beer garden, and a fireworks finale. Run by the town and local community groups, it is one of the high points of the regional summer calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Trinity Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · On Pentecost Sunday, the town of Nafplio honors the Holy Trinity at the church of Agia Triada. A Byzantine liturgy and icon procession give way to a panigyri of Greek music, dancing, grilled meats, and sweets, in a celebration that captures the warm blend of devotion and festivity at the heart of Greek village life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week (Setmana Santa) of Alghero
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the Sardinian city of Alghero marks Holy Week with stately Baroque processions through its medieval streets. Robed members of the old brotherhoods carry sacred statues to the sound of chants sung in Alguerese, the city's own Catalan dialect, in a tradition brought here under Aragonese rule centuries ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week and Drum Festival of Hellín
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · In the town of Hellín, Holy Week means drums, thousands of them. From midnight on Maundy Thursday, drummers dressed in black with red scarves beat their tambours together without pause through Good Friday, building a thunderous roar that takes over the whole town, in one of Spain's great drumming traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week in Valladolid
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Valladolid holds one of Spain's most revered Holy Weeks, a tradition reaching back to the 1400s. Hooded penitents accompany magnificent Baroque sculptures of Christ's Passion through the old streets, in processions famous for the beauty and emotion of their carved religious art. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week in Zamora
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Zamora lives up to its nickname as the city of Holy Week, with processions kept since medieval times. Its rites are famously sober and austere, hooded brotherhoods moving in near-silence through the old streets, in one of the most deeply felt Easter traditions in Spain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week of Braga
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Braga, often called the Portuguese Rome, marks Holy Week with some of the country's most elaborate processions. Robed penitents called farricocos move through the historic center past its many baroque churches, in a week of deep devotion in Portugal's oldest religious city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week of Ceuta
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Ceuta on the North African coast holds its Holy Week, one of its most important celebrations. Robed brotherhoods carry sacred images through the streets in solemn procession, bringing the deep traditions of Spanish Catholic Easter to the far side of the Strait of Gibraltar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week of Cáceres
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Holy Week, the city of Cáceres holds one of Spain's most solemn Easter observances. Candlelit processions wind through the medieval old town as brotherhoods bear their images, in a celebration deeply rooted since the Middle Ages that binds the city together in the days before Easter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Holy Week Processions (Bastia)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Holy Week, the Corsican city of Bastia fills with sacred ritual led by its old confraternities. From the blessing of woven palm crosses on Palm Sunday through the solemn processions to Easter, the week unfolds in deep tradition, in a celebration that reflects the island's Catholic heritage and its medieval brotherhoods. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hop-tu-Naa
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each October 31, the Isle of Man celebrates Hop-tu-Naa, its own ancient version of Halloween rooted in the old Celtic harvest. In Port Erin, families carry carved lanterns and sing traditional Manx songs, in a festival marking the turn toward winter that is far older than the modern Halloween. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Port Erin, Rushen, Isle of Man
festivals.address: Port Erin, Rushen, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Hopping procession of Echternach
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each Pentecost Tuesday, the Luxembourg town of Echternach fills with one of Europe's most unusual processions, in which thousands of pilgrims hop and skip in rhythm to a lively polka tune. Linked arm to arm, they make their way to the tomb of Saint Willibrord, in a dancing pilgrimage honored by UNESCO. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Hora de la Prut (Public Hora Circle Dance, Ungheni)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year in the border town of Ungheni, right on the Prut River, a folk festival fills the main square with Moldovan music, dancing, and song. It ends the way these gatherings should, with a huge open hora, the round dance everyone joins, spinning together hand in hand. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Horbova Malanka
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · On New Year's Day the village of Horbova fills with the Malanka, a winter carnival of costumed villagers, wooden-horse căiuți, and a giant pig figure parading and singing Romanian carols. This ancient midwinter custom blends St. Basil's day with much older pre-Christian rites. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Houlgate Full Wind Festival
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each spring, the Norman beach of Houlgate fills its skies with color for the Plein Vent kite and wind festival. Thousands of kites dance over the sand across a weekend of workshops and acrobatic displays, in a family-friendly celebration of wind and sea on the breezy shore of the Pays d'Auge. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hoyerhagen Marksmen's Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer the village of Hoyerhagen holds its Schützenfest, with the local marksmen's club staging shooting contests, a parade led by the marching band, and days of community festivity. It belongs to the long German tradition of village shooting clubs and the crowning of a new king of the shoot. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hungarian Culture Days
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August, the city of Oradea celebrates the heritage of its Hungarian community with Hungarian Culture Days. Folk dance and music, a craft and farm fair, and a bread-blessing join the much-loved parade of floral floats brought from the city's twin, in a celebration of Oradea's deep Hungarian roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Husum Crab Days
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October the harbor mile of Husum fills with stalls and tasting booths devoted to North Sea brown shrimp, the prized local Krabben. Crab-cooking is part of North Frisian kitchen heritage, and the festival pairs the fresh catch with cooking demonstrations and regional music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hägnan Christmas Market (Gammelstad)
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each Advent, the open-air Hägnan Museum in Gammelstad, a UNESCO-listed church town near Luleå, hosts a traditional Christmas market. Stalls of crafts and seasonal treats fill the historic grounds, in a celebration rooted in Sweden's long love of Christmas markets and the heritage of the old church towns of the north. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Hírös Week Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Hungarian city of Kecskemét celebrates its heritage with Hírös Hét, one of its oldest festivals, running for over ninety years. Concerts, folk dance, crafts, and local food fill the week, building to the August 20 national holiday in a warm celebration of the city and its traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kecskemét, Hungarian (Great Hungarian Plain, local Kecskemét community), Hungary
festivals.address: Kecskemét, Hungarian (Great Hungarian Plain, local Kecskemét community), Hungary

festivals.name: Höfn Lobster Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Icelandic town of Höfn celebrates its title as the nation's lobster capital with a festival of the sea. Dishes of the prized local langoustine, seafood markets, and live music fill the small southeastern town, in a celebration of the unique catch that has long defined this corner of the coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ice Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter, the Norwegian town of Ål hosts the Ice Music Festival, where artists carve and play instruments made entirely of ice. Founded in 2006 by the drummer Terje Isungset, the festival fills the frozen night with fragile, otherworldly sound, in a celebration of Norway's deep bond with its winter landscape. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ijahis Idja Sámi Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Sámi town of Inari gathers for Ijahis Idja, whose name means "Echo of the Ancestors" in Inari Sámi. The festival joins traditional joik with contemporary folk, rap, and collaborations across cultures, in a celebration created by local Sámi to keep their music and languages alive and heard. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: IJzendijke Folklore Day
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer the Zeeland town of IJzendijke holds its Folklore Day, with traditional horse-riding contests like ringrijden and sjezenrijden, crafts, and local dances. Started in the 1970s, the day keeps the rural heritage of West Zeeuws-Vlaanderen alive in the village streets. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ile-de-France Celebrates Theatre
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Île-de-France region brings free theatre, dance, and music to its public parks and leisure grounds. "L'Île-de-France fête le théâtre" fills green spaces near Paris with performances for all, in a celebration funded by the region to make the performing arts a shared and popular joy. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ilindan People’s Gathering
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Around the 2nd of August the mountain town of Plužine gathers for Ilindan, the feast of St. Elijah, with horse races, stone-throwing, long jump, and a cultural fair. A major Balkan holy day, it has grown here into a folk gathering that ties the saint's name to village games and heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ilinden (Kruševo Republic Commemoration)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Every August 2, the mountain town of Kruševo marks Ilinden, honoring the 1903 uprising that briefly created a short-lived republic here. Solemn ceremonies at the memorials and a church service fall on the feast of St. Elijah, in one of the most important days in North Macedonia's national story. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Kruševo, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia
festivals.address: Kruševo, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia

festivals.name: Ilinden (Smilevo Congress Commemoration)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August 2, the village of Smilevo commemorates the 1903 Ilinden Uprising, decided at the historic Smilevo Congress held here. Wreaths are laid at the Dame Gruev monument and folklore fills the day, in a community celebration of a village that stands at the heart of Macedonia's struggle for freedom. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Smilevo, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia
festivals.address: Smilevo, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia

festivals.name: Ilinden Days (Bitola Folk Dance & Music Festival)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the city of Bitola hosts Ilinden Days, a two-week celebration of folk music and dance named for the famous uprising. Macedonian and international folk groups in bright costume fill the streets and stages with traditional song and dance, in one of the country's leading cultural festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ilinden–ASNOM Commemoration
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August 2, the memorial complex at Pelince hosts the official commemoration of Ilinden and ASNOM. State delegations lay wreaths and give speeches honoring the 1903 uprising and the 1944 ASNOM assembly, in a solemn celebration of the two events that lie at the foundation of Macedonian statehood. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Pelince, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia
festivals.address: Pelince, Macedonian Cultural Region, North Macedonia

festivals.name: Ilosaari Rock Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the lakeside city of Joensuu hosts Ilosaarirock, a three-day festival locals proudly call the best weekend of the year. Tens of thousands gather in a leafy park for Finnish rock and pop, in one of the country's most beloved music festivals deep in the forests of North Karelia. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Ilūkste City Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the Latvian town of Ilūkste celebrates its founding with a two-day city festival. An ecumenical service, a brass-band awakening, a great crafts and produce market, and folk performances fill the days, in a warm celebration of a town set where Latgale meets Selonia on the Daugava. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Imnarja (Harvest Festival & Għana, Buskett Gardens)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, Malta's oldest folk festival fills the wooded Buskett Gardens for L-Imnarja, a celebration of the harvest. Crowds gather by firelight as singers trade verses in the island's traditional għana, alongside agricultural shows and horse and donkey races, in a festival of deep rural roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Imnarja (Picnics & Races, Nadur, Gozo)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 29, the Gozo town of Nadur celebrates L-Imnarja, both the feast of Saints Peter and Paul and an old harvest festival marking the start of summer. Families head out for picnics and country races, in a celebration that blends religious devotion with rural customs reaching back to pagan times. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: In the Name of the Red Shirt
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July 7, the town of Bar holds its oldest commemoration, honoring eleven local youth executed in 1944 with a wreath-laying at the Belveder memorial and an evening concert of revolutionary songs. U ime crvene košulje is woven into Bar's identity as a town marked by its wartime history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Inari Weeks
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Lapland municipality of Inari fills ten days with the Inariviikot. Village fairs, gold panning, concerts, markets, and Sámi cultural events blend tradition with modern festivity, in a celebration, begun in 2009, created to honor and share the heritage and life of this far-northern land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Instruments of My Ancestors
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · In Skopje, this international children's festival gathers young performers to play and sing traditional Macedonian folk music on native instruments. Run by the Karpoš Cultural Center, it sets out to pass the old songs and instruments on to a new generation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Inter-Celtic Festival of the West
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Asturian seaside town of Tapia de Casariego fills with bagpipes for the Festival Intercéltico d'Occidente. Nightly concerts of Galician, Asturian, and Irish music draw crowds to celebrate the Celtic roots that this green corner of northern Spain shares with Ireland and Brittany. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Intercultural Festival of Nations
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Riojan city of Logroño fills the Paseo del Espolón with the food, music, and crafts of five continents for the Intercultural Festival of Nations. The multi-week fair gathers the city's many communities, in a celebration of the growing diversity of a town long known for its wine and its welcome. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Interetno Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Vojvodina city of Subotica celebrates its mosaic of peoples with the Interetno Festival. Traditional dance, music, and theatre groups from many ethnic communities take the stage, in a celebration, founded in 2002, of the multilingual, multiethnic life of this city on the Pannonian Plain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Subotica, Multicultural community of Subotica, Vojvodina, Serbia
festivals.address: Subotica, Multicultural community of Subotica, Vojvodina, Serbia

festivals.name: Interkultura (Folklore Ensembles & Traditional Costumes, Žabljak)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the mountain town of Žabljak gathers folk ensembles from across Montenegro for the Interkultura festival. Costumed dancers and musicians perform on a meadow stage beneath the Durmitor peaks, in a celebration that brings the country's varied folk traditions and intangible heritage together for an evening. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International "Golden Snowflake" Children's Song Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the Montenegrin town of Rožaje gathers young singers from across the Balkans and beyond for the Golden Snowflake children's song festival. Competitions of original and folk songs, workshops, and a gala concert fill the event, in a celebration of youthful talent in a region that has always loved music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Children's and Youth Wind Orchestra Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Slovak spa town of Bojnice gathers children's and youth wind orchestras for an international festival. Young brass and wind ensembles from Slovakia and abroad play on an outdoor stage, in a celebration of the strong school music tradition of Bojnice and the surrounding Horná Nitra region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Choral Music Festival "Golden Strand of Šalčia"
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the town of Eišiškės gathers choirs for the international festival "Golden Strand of Šalčia." Singers fill the program with choral music, in a celebration organized by the Šalčininkai district that reflects the rich multiethnic heritage of this corner of southeastern Lithuania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Descent of the Sella River
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, thousands of canoeists race down the Sella River from Arriondas to Ribadesella in one of Europe's great paddling spectacles. Crowds line the banks and a festive train follows the river, in a celebration that has joined world-class sport with Asturian folklore since 1929. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: International Ethno Song Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Gagauz capital of Comrat hosts Bucak Sesleri, an international song contest celebrating the folk music of the Turkic world. Singers from many countries perform a song from their own culture and a global hit, in a warm celebration that bridges communities across a wide family of peoples. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Festival 'Days of Thracian Culture'
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the city of Plovdiv celebrates the ancient Thracians with the Days of Thracian Culture. Traditional music, scholarly lectures, exhibits, and children's events explore the legacy of the people who once filled this land, in a celebration of Plovdiv's place at the very heart of ancient Thrace. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Festival of Arts "Mărțișor" (Spring Festival)
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each spring, the towns of Transnistria celebrate Mărțișor, the ancient festival of renewal, with an international festival of the arts. From March 1 to 10, concerts and performances fill the region, in a celebration of a beloved Romanian and Moldovan tradition that welcomes spring with music and the little red-and-white tokens of the season. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: International Festival of Bulgarian Culture "Balgari da si ostanem"
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year around the Day of Bessarabian Bulgarians, the town of Reni celebrates its Bulgarian heritage with a great cultural forum. Folk music, dance troupes from Ukraine and Bulgaria, and exhibitions of crafts and costume fill the program, in a celebration that helps this Bessarabian Bulgarian community hold proudly to its roots. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: International Festival of Ionian Islands Philharmonic Bands
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the island of Corfu celebrates the great band tradition of the Ionian Islands with a three-day festival. Philharmonic brass and wind orchestras fill the town with the music that has been central to island life since Venetian times, in a celebration of an art that no Ionian town would be without. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Festival of National Cultures "Rudeninės"
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Every other autumn the town of Visaginas celebrates Rudeninės, an Autumn Gatherings festival of the many nationalities who live there. Craft fairs, a dumpling feast, and folk-music hours bring out Russian, Belarusian, Lithuanian, and other traditions side by side. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
