festivals.location: Visaginas, Northeast Lithuania (Russian minority) – Visaginas, Lithuania
festivals.address: Visaginas, Northeast Lithuania (Russian minority) – Visaginas, Lithuania

festivals.name: International Festival of Ukrainian Carols and Winter Customs
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each winter, the town of Sighetu Marmației celebrates Ukrainian Christmas with a festival of carols and customs. Costumed carolers parade from the Greek-Catholic church to the town square for a concert of kolyadky and ethnic dance, in a celebration of the rich Carpathian Ukrainian winter heritage of the Maramureș borderland. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Sighetu Marmației, Ukrainian community of Maramureș, Romania
festivals.address: Sighetu Marmației, Ukrainian community of Maramureș, Romania

festivals.name: International Festival of Winter Customs (Suceava Masked Bears & Goats)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each year between Christmas and New Year, the city of Suceava fills with masked dancers for a festival of Bukovina's winter customs. More than thirty groups parade in elaborate bear, goat, and other costumes, bringing blessings for the new year, in a vivid celebration of the deep midwinter traditions of this Romanian region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Folk Dance Festival of Nea Anchialos
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Greek town of Nea Anchialos gathers folk dance troupes from Greece and abroad for an international festival. Groups perform their regional dances and costumes in the town's halls and open-air stages over several late-June days, in a celebration created to spread folk tradition and cross-cultural friendship. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: Nea Anchialos, Nea Anchialos, Magnesia (Thessaly), Greece
festivals.address: Nea Anchialos, Nea Anchialos, Magnesia (Thessaly), Greece

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival "Dance the Kolo by Timok"
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the town of Svrljig celebrates the circle dances of the Timok region with a lively folklore festival. The kolo and the old music of the valley fill the days, in a celebration of a corner of eastern Serbia long known for the strength and beauty of its folk dance and song. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Svrljig, Eastern Serbia (Timok Valley, Svrljig), Serbia
festivals.address: Svrljig, Eastern Serbia (Timok Valley, Svrljig), Serbia

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival 'Igra kolo kraj Timoka'
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June around Vidovdan, the Serbian town of Svrljig hosts the folklore festival "Igra kolo kraj Timoka." Folk ensembles from the Balkans gather to dance and play, organized by the local Osmeh group, in a celebration of cultural exchange that puts the Serbian traditions of the Timok valley center stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival (Singing from Boats, Klaipėda)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Every two years, the port city of Klaipėda hosts "Parbėg laivelis," an international festival of ethnographic song and dance. Folk ensembles from Lithuania and abroad fill the Old Town with traditional music, including evening concerts, in a celebration whose name, recalling a fisherman's returning boat, ties the city to both land and sea. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival Agrinio
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the town of Agrinio gathers folk dance groups from Greece and abroad for an international folklore festival. Costumed troupes perform traditional dances on the city's stages over several days, in a celebration, held within a European folklore network, that fills Aitoloakarnania with the color of the world's folk traditions. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival in Medveđa
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year the town of Medveđa hosts a folk-dance festival, with ensembles from Serbia and neighboring Balkan countries parading through town and performing at the Cultural Center. Colorful costumes and dances fill the stage in a celebration of the area's mixed heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Folklore Festival Jablonec nad Nisou
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the Bohemian town of Jablonec nad Nisou hosts a three-day international folklore festival at its summer amphitheater. Czech, Polish, and Slovak ensembles perform authentic music, dance, and song in national costume, in a celebration of the folk traditions that have long mingled in this border region. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Jablonec nad Nisou, Jablonec nad Nisou region, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Jablonec nad Nisou, Jablonec nad Nisou region, Czech Republic

festivals.name: International Gwoka Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Guadeloupean town of Sainte-Anne celebrates Gwoka, the drum-and-voice music born among the island's enslaved people, with a great festival. Recognized by UNESCO as world heritage, gwoka fills the days with its powerful rhythms, in Europe's only festival devoted to this living traditional music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Ice Sculpture Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the Latvian city of Jelgava turns Pasta Island into a glittering gallery of ice. International artists carve large-scale statues that gleam in the winter light, in a celebration that brings a modern, dazzling interpretation of the winter arts to the heart of Latvia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Indigenous Arts & Music Festival, Manndalen
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the small Sami community of Manndalen in northern Norway hosts Riddu Riđđu, an international festival of Indigenous arts and music. Performers from Arctic and native peoples around the world gather by the coast, in a celebration that grew from a movement to reclaim Sami language and pride. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: International Intangible Cultural Heritage Festival "Lauksnos"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · In the port city of Klaipėda, Lauksnos gathers performers and craftspeople from around the world around the living traditions that UNESCO works to protect. Its name comes from a Prussian word for lights and stars, and its program runs from polyphonic sutartinės to old dances and crafts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Mediterranean Folklore Festival in Murcia
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the city of Murcia gathers folk groups from around the Mediterranean and beyond for a festival of music, dance, and craft. Hundreds of performers in traditional costume fill the historic squares, celebrating the shared folk heritage that links Murcia to cultures all across the sea. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Poetry Festival "Page Break"
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each year, the Latvian city of Jelgava gathers poets from home and abroad for the four-day festival "Page Break." Readings, poetry walks, and a focus on cultural diversity fill the program, in a celebration, held since 2022, that draws on the city's literary scene to foster exchange and empathy across borders. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Sabor (Kadi Bogaz)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the Kadi Bogaz pass on the Serbian-Bulgarian border hosts a great cross-border sabor. The neighboring municipalities of Knjaževac and Belogradchik gather their people for folk festivity at the frontier, in a celebration of the long friendship between communities who meet freely on the old mountain crossing. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Novo Korito (Serbian side), Eastern Serbia / Bulgaria border (Knjazevac – Belogradchik), Serbia
festivals.address: Novo Korito (Serbian side), Eastern Serbia / Bulgaria border (Knjazevac – Belogradchik), Serbia

festivals.name: International Sigulda Opera Music Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the ruins of Sigulda Castle become an open-air stage for an international opera festival. Latvian and visiting opera singers perform staged productions and gala concerts amid the historic walls, in a celebration that makes one of Latvia's most romantic ruins a highlight of the summer cultural calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: International Slovak Youth Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the village of Valea Cerului near Suplacu de Barcău celebrates its Slovak heritage with a youth folklore festival. Amateur Slovak ensembles from Bihor and beyond perform community songs and dances in costume on the village stage, in a celebration, held since 2004, of the living traditions of this Slovak community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Suplacu de Barcău, Slovak community of Baia Mare Vallage, Romania
festivals.address: Suplacu de Barcău, Slovak community of Baia Mare Vallage, Romania

festivals.name: Investiture Ceremony of the Captains Regent
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Twice a year, on the first of April and October, the republic of San Marino swears in two new heads of state, the Captains Regent, in a stately ceremony in its historic center. Crossbowmen, flag-throwers, and crowds gather for a ritual that has marked the world's oldest republic since the 1200s. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Iron Age Birckala
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the Finnish town of Pirkkala carries visitors back to the Iron Age with the festival Rautakauden Birckala. Viking-era workshops, combat reenactments, crafts, and old music fill the grounds, in a celebration of the deep prehistoric heritage of this lakeside corner of Finland. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Isfjord Radio Music Festival (Svalbard Wilderness Concerts)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Early each September a handful of Norwegian musicians travel to the old Isfjord Radio station on Svalbard for a boutique series of acoustic concerts in the Arctic wild. Guests reach the remote site by boat, and the music plays out among the wide tundra and its wildlife. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Island Solidarity Music & Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the island of Karlsøy in far-northern Norway hosts a music and culture festival born of community and solidarity. Founded in the 1980s by volunteer locals, it gathers people for collective music-making and a shared alternative spirit, in a celebration that reflects the close-knit, do-it-yourself ethos of the island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Islands That Speak Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Gallura region of Sardinia hosts Isole che Parlano, a nine-day festival of "islands that speak." Music, literature, visual art, and folk traditions fill rural palaces, squares, and beaches, in a celebration, born in 1996, created by local cultural activists to preserve and share Gallura's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Isle of Man Festival of Choirs
festivals.description: Early October 2027 · Every two years, the Manx capital of Douglas gathers choirs from across the British Isles for the Isle of Man Festival of Choirs. Male, female, mixed, and youth choirs sing before adjudicators over an October weekend, in a celebration, established in 2010, that has put the island on the map for choral music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Istrian Donkey Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the Istrian village of Svetvinčenat gathers donkey breeders from across the region for the Istrian Donkey Festival. Parades, competitions, and exhibitions celebrate the rare Istrian donkey, in a warm celebration that honors an animal once vital to rural life and raises awareness of its preservation. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Ittiri Folk Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Sardinian town of Ittiri gathers folk dance troupes from around the world for the Ittiri Folk Festa. Now decades old, it fills the town with the dances and costumes of many cultures, in a celebration that makes this corner of the Sassari countryside a meeting place of the world's folk traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ivan Kupala Fair in Shevchenkivskyi Hay
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the open-air museum of Shevchenkivskyi Hai in Lviv holds a fair for Ivan Kupala, the ancient feast of St. John's Eve. Among the old village buildings, Kupala rituals of fire, water, and flower wreaths fill the day, in a celebration of one of the most magical of Ukraine's midsummer traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: James Morrison Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, the Sligo village of Riverstown honors the great fiddler James Morrison with a festival of traditional music. Concerts, sessions, and competitions fill the bank-holiday weekend, in a celebration of Sligo's rich fiddle heritage and a son of the county whose recordings carried its music to America. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Jarramplas Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the village of Piornal stages one of Spain's most extraordinary festivals, the Jarramplas. A figure in a brightly painted costume and horned mask parades the streets, drumming, while the crowd pelts him with thousands of turnips, in a strange and spectacular ritual rooted deep in local folklore. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Jazzinty Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Slovenian town of Novo mesto hosts Jazzinty, an international jazz festival held in its cultural center since the late 1990s. Concerts and workshops draw players and fans from many countries, in a celebration that brings world-class jazz to one of the historic gems of southeastern Slovenia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jokkmokk Winter Market
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the far-north town of Jokkmokk holds a winter market that has gathered the people of Swedish Lapland since 1606. In the deep Arctic cold, stalls overflow with Sámi handicraft, reindeer, music, and food, in a centuries-old meeting that remains a heartland of Sámi culture. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Jorvik Viking Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the city of York travels back to its Norse past for the Jorvik Viking Festival, a week celebrating the Vikings who once ruled here. Costumed warriors fill the streets, with combat displays, longship lore, and a fiery finale, in Europe's largest gathering devoted to Viking culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jotvingian Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the Lithuanian village of Leipalingis honors the Yotvingians, the ancient Baltic tribe from which it takes its name. Historical reenactments, folk music, and crafts connect the modern village to its early medieval roots, in a celebration of a long-vanished people whose legacy still marks this corner of Dzūkija. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Joust of the Saracen
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Twice a year, the Tuscan city of Arezzo fills its sloping Piazza Grande for the Giostra del Saracino, a medieval jousting tournament. Riders from the city's four quarters charge a wooden figure of a Saracen warrior with their lances, in a contest of skill and honor wrapped in centuries of pageantry. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Juan Claudio Cifuentes Jazz Sessions
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February the city of Melilla fills a week with jazz, with concerts in its plazas and auditoriums, jazz-themed dinners, and student showcases. Named for the scholar and broadcaster Juan Claudio Cifuentes, the festival adds a global layer to the city's Mediterranean mix. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Junii Satului Festival (Village Youth Winter Traditions Festival-Contest)
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each winter, the mountain village of Măguri-Răcătău gathers its youth for the Junii Satului festival of village traditions. Groups of young men perform the old colinde and dances of the UNESCO-listed men's caroling rite, in a celebration that keeps the deep midwinter customs of this Transylvanian highland alive. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Jász Sausage Stuffing Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each winter, the Hungarian town of Jászberény celebrates its Jász heritage with a sausage-stuffing festival. Teams of families and friends compete to build and stuff sausages amid pálinka tasting and folk music, in a festive celebration of a proud old family tradition of the Jász people. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jæren Potato Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each late summer, the Norwegian town of Bryne celebrates the potato harvest at the heart of the Jæren farmland. Billed as the Norwegian Potato Festival, it honors the staple crop and the region's farming heritage, in a warm celebration that grew from the community's pride in working this fertile land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jānis Ivanovs Memorial Day
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the Latvian town of Preiļi honors its native composer Jānis Ivanovs with a memorial day. Concerts of his symphonic works join contests for new music and art, organized by the local school of music, in a celebration of one of Latvia's most accomplished composers, born in a nearby village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jēkabpils Shlager Music Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · At the Krustpils open-air stage in Jēkabpils, this one-day festival fills the evening with beloved Latvian šlāgere songs and ends in a night of dancing. Schlager music has spanned generations in Latvia, and the festival gathers its favorite oldies performers in one warm summer crowd. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Jūrmala Jāņi/Līgo Midsummer Celebration
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, the Latvian seaside resort of Jūrmala celebrates Jāņi, the midsummer festival that is the high point of the year. People wear oak-leaf and flower wreaths, light bonfires, and sing the old līgo songs over caraway cheese and beer, staying up all night to greet the returning sun. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kadzidło Sunday (Harvest Festival)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · On the first Sunday of September, the village of Kadzidło opens its harvest festival with a Mass at the Church of the Holy Spirit, then a procession of locals in Kurpie costume carrying grain wreaths and a ceremonial loaf. The day weaves Christian thanksgiving together with the proud folk customs of the Kurpie region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kainuu Music Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Finnish city of Kajaani fills Kajaani Hall with music for the Kainuu Music Festival. Popular Finnish artists across many genres play over a late-summer weekend, in a young celebration, begun in 2022, created to bring a large-scale music event to the Kainuu region. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kalamata International Dance Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the Greek city of Kalamata hosts an international festival of contemporary dance. Local and visiting troupes bring creative choreography to the stage alongside workshops for young dancers, in a celebration, begun by the city, that has made Kalamata a center for modern dance in Greece. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kalitsounia Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer the Cretan village of Kandanos throws a feast for kalitsounia, the sweet cheese pastries that are an island favorite, alongside other local delicacies. Tied to the fig and cheese harvests, the festival puts these iconic treats at the heart of a warm village gathering. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kalix Summer Bazaar
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · For several days in mid-July, the town of Kalix throws its Sommarkalas, a folk and family festival of shows, carnival rides, and children's entertainment. A purely local affair, it draws town and countryside families together along the Kalix River for the heart of summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kalocsa Paprika and Gastro Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Hungarian town of Kalocsa celebrates its world-famous paprika in the archbishop's garden. Paprika cooking contests, folk dance, concerts, and crafts fill the festival, in a celebration that ties together the prized red spice and the colorful folk embroidery for which the town is renowned. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kamianka Music Fest
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the town of Kamianka in central Ukraine hosts an open-air festival of music beneath the sky. Chamber ensembles, organists, jazz bands, and noted artists fill the celebration, set in a town famed for the palace and park that once drew great composers, in a tribute to both classical and contemporary Ukrainian music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kanaria Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the town of Parga celebrates the Kanaria, recalling the return of its people from exile on Corfu and honoring the Dormition of the Virgin. A midnight boat procession crosses to the little island of Panagia for a folk festival, in a celebration that weaves together deep devotion and the memory of the town's hard-won homecoming. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Karaiskakeia International Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Thessalian town of Karditsa fills with folk dancers for the Karaiskakeia, a week-long festival honoring the revolutionary hero Georgios Karaiskakis. Greek and visiting troupes perform traditional dances in the squares, often before the hero's statue, in a celebration of regional heritage held since the 1960s. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Karcag Mutton Stew Cooking Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Hungarian town of Karcag fills with the smell of simmering mutton for its birkapörkölt cooking festival. Hundreds of teams compete over open fires to make the tastiest sheep stew, in a celebration rooted in the deep pastoral traditions of the Kunság, where the dish has fed shepherds for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Karcag, Great Hungarian Plain (Northern Great Plain), Hungary
festivals.address: Karcag, Great Hungarian Plain (Northern Great Plain), Hungary

festivals.name: Kardamyli International Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Mani village of Kardamyli hosts an intimate international jazz festival. Local and visiting musicians play in outdoor venues, often under the stars, in a small celebration that brings warm music nights to a corner of the Peloponnese long cherished for its beauty and its writers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Karlštejn Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the village beneath Karlštejn Castle celebrates the wine harvest with a medieval-themed festival. Wine tastings, folk music, and a historical parade led by an "Emperor Charles IV" fill the day, in a celebration set against the great Gothic castle built by the emperor himself. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Karosta Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · In Liepāja's historic Karosta quarter, this festival fills a former naval district with concerts, art installations, and performances staged even inside the old Karosta prison. Once a Russian naval base, the neighborhood is transformed for a few days into an open stage for its own unusual heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kashubian Fair
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the town of Kartuzy holds the Jarmark Kaszubski, a folklore fair in the heart of Kashubian Switzerland. Artisans and cultural groups fill the park for four days of handicrafts, traditional food, and live performance, in a celebration that embodies the proud identity of the Kashubian highlands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kashubian Meetings with World Folklore
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the village of Wiele hosts a five-day international folklore festival in the heart of Kashubia. Visiting ensembles from around the world dance alongside local Kashubian groups in a swirl of color and costume, in a celebration that brings the world's folk traditions to meet those of these northern Polish hills. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Katrina Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Åland capital of Mariehamn fills a week with classical music for the Katrina Chamber Music Festival. Fine ensembles perform across the islands, in a celebration that reflects the strong tradition of chamber music in Åland's cultural life and the long summer light of the Baltic. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kaunas District Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September the grounds of Raudondvaris Castle host the Kaunas District Festival, a day of free concerts and local art on the banks of the Nemunas. A fairly new creation of the district, it gathers the towns and villages around Kaunas to celebrate a shared sense of place. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kaunas Saint John’s Night
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, the Lithuanian city of Kaunas celebrates Joninės, the midsummer festival of the shortest night. Bonfires blaze along the Nemunas River, young women float flower wreaths on the water, and folk songs mix with concerts, in an ancient solstice celebration of light and fertility. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2026-06-24) is a fixed annual date.

festivals.name: Kayamba Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the beaches of Bandrélé in Mayotte fill with rhythm for the Kayamba festival. Traditional Mahoran maloya and gamelan percussion meet contemporary DJ sets under the open sky, in an intimate, community-minded celebration of an island culture woven from African, Malagasy, and Comorian threads. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kaštel Kambelovac Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Carnival, the Dalmatian town of Kaštel Kambelovac fills its streets with the Veliki Kambelovski krnjeval. Dozens of costumed groups from across Split-Dalmatia parade with satirical floats and performances, in a lively celebration that is part of the wider Dalmatian carnival season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kelmis Kirchweih Funfair
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Ostbelgien town of Kelmis holds its Kirmes, the parish church fair and funfair. Rides, games, food stalls, and nightly dances follow the religious services, in a celebration rooted in the old feast of the patron saint of the town's church, blending devotion with festive fun. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kelmis Pig Parade, 11/11
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November 11, the town of Kelmis opens its carnival season with the Küschespektakel, a pig-themed parade. Revelers gather under a great tent to celebrate the town's carnival mascot, the hog called Küsch, in a lively kickoff that symbolically hands the town over to the spirit of carnival for the season ahead. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: KeNeHeMo Karneval (Kelmis Rosenmontag Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the linked villages around Kelmis throw the KeNeHeMo carnival, a season that runs from November to Lenten Rosenmontag. Costume parties and festivity build to a vibrant Rose Monday parade in Kelmis, in a celebration that joins several neighboring parishes into one joyful carnival community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kerekdomb Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Tokaj wine village of Tállya hosts the three-day Kerekdomb Festival of arts and culture. Live music, theatre, literature, and sport mingle with curated wine tastings among the vineyards, in a celebration that honors both the creative arts and the famous winemaking of this UNESCO-listed region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kharkiv City Day
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Every August 23, the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv celebrates its founding, tracing its roots to a frontier fortress built in 1654. Outdoor concerts, performances, and evening fireworks fill the parks and squares, as one of the country's great centers of science and culture marks its long history. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kharkiv Regional Traditional Dishes Festival-Competition "Smachnoho!" (Bon Appétit!)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the Kharkiv region champions its village cooking with the "Smachnoho!" festival-competition. Cooks vie with traditional recipes as visitors taste rare regional dishes from borscht to old pastries, in a celebration created to rescue and preserve the forgotten culinary heritage of the Kharkiv countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kielegat Carnival (Breda: Farmers’ Costumes & Grand Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Breda becomes Kielegat for four days of carnival, its playful made-up name for the festive season. The streets fill with farmers' costumes, brass music, and a grand parade, in a southern Dutch tradition of merrymaking with roots stretching back centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kihnu Midsummer Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On Midsummer night, the tiny Estonian island of Kihnu gathers around a great bonfire, often a whole old boat set alight, to welcome the longest days. Islanders in their intricate knitted and striped costumes sing ancient runic songs and dance until dawn, in the most important celebration of the year on this storied isle. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kihnu Sea Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · For three days in mid-July the island of Kihnu celebrates its bond with the sea, with concerts, workshops, a market, and traditional boat rides. Grown from the old Fisherman's Day, the Merepidu has widened into a festival of all things maritime and the island's seafaring way of life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kildare Summerfest
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · In the County Kildare town of Kilcullen, this summer festival celebrates Indian culture and the place the Indian community has made in local life. Organized by the KUSHI group, it grew out of a wish to share the heritage of Kildare's Indian diaspora and bring the wider community together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kilkenny Arts Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the medieval city of Kilkenny hosts a ten-day arts festival set against the towers of its great castle. Theatre, opera, dance, music, and visual art fill historic venues across town, in one of Ireland's most cherished cultural gatherings, held since 1974. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Killala Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August the town of Killala in County Mayo holds its community festival, with sports finals, concerts, a fair, and children's events filling several days. Built around Gaelic games and music, it brings the town together in a celebration of local heritage and pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kisecset Pampuska Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Palóc village of Kisecset celebrates its beloved pampuska, a fluffy local pastry like a donut. Baking competitions by the village women, folk music, and festivity fill a summer weekend, in a warm celebration of a treat at the heart of the food traditions of this corner of Nógrád. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kiskunfélegyáza Goose Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Hungarian town of Kiskunfélegyháza celebrates the goose with its Libafesztivál. Goose dishes, gastronomy, and festivity honor a centuries-old livelihood, in a celebration of the goose farming that has long fed and sustained this town and the villages around it. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kiskőrös Harvest and Slovak Ethnic Days
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Hungarian town of Kiskőrös celebrates both its wine harvest and its Slovak heritage. Folk music and dance, harvest parades, and wine and food tastings fill the weekend, in a celebration of a town long home to a Slovak community and the vineyards that have shaped its life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kiskőrös, Hungarian Slovak community (Kiskőrös region), Hungary
festivals.address: Kiskőrös, Hungarian Slovak community (Kiskőrös region), Hungary

festivals.name: Klaipėda Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, around the city's founding date, the port city of Klaipėda hosts a summer arts festival produced by its music theatre. The historic Old Boathouse and other venues become stages for high culture, in a celebration that ties the performing arts to the city's rich maritime heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Klaipėda Light Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the Old Town of Klaipėda glows after dark for the city's Light Festival. Projections on historic buildings, interactive shows, and illuminated artworks fill the evening streets, in a celebration that turns the port city's architecture into a luminous canvas for all to wander. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Klaipėda Saint John’s Night
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June around St. John's Day, the port city of Klaipėda celebrates Joninės, the Baltic midsummer. Bonfires, wreaths of herbs and flowers, and gatherings by the water mark the shortest night, in a celebration of one of the oldest Baltic traditions, tied to nature and the turning of the year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kleine Zeitung Graz Carnival Parade
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Austrian city of Graz throws a big carnival parade through its center, organized by the local newspaper. Dozens of floats, marching bands, and costumed groups wind toward the main square for the prize-giving, in a colorful Fasching tradition rich with Styrian flavor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Klobása Fest (Kulen Festival)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · In the Slovak-rooted town of Bački Petrovac, Klobásafest celebrates kulen, the spicy pork sausage of Vojvodina, around the season of the traditional pig slaughter. First held in 2016, it quickly became one of the area's most popular events, built around good sausage and old country ways. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Bački Petrovac, Slovak and Serbian community of Bački Petrovac, Serbia
festivals.address: Bački Petrovac, Slovak and Serbian community of Bački Petrovac, Serbia

festivals.name: Kobersdorf Castle Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Renaissance courtyard of Kobersdorf Castle becomes an open-air stage for theatre. Since 1972, the Schloss-Spiele have staged classic comedies and dramas in the arcaded setting, in a celebration that reflects the theatrical spirit of Burgenland in a beautiful corner of the Pannonian plain. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kokkola Sea Jazz
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the coastal Finnish city of Kokkola hosts the warm, small-scale Sea Jazz festival. International jazz guitarists and local musicians play in an outdoor waterfront setting, in a celebration founded by enthusiasts that reflects the maritime heritage and easy community spirit of the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kokle Day
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer on Saint Peter's Day, the Latvian Ethnographic Open-Air Museum in Riga fills with the gentle sound of the kokle. More than a hundred players of the national zither gather to perform old tunes, including in the museum's historic village churches, in a celebration of an instrument that symbolizes Latvia itself. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kolyada na Mayzlyakh (Christmas Carol Festival, Ivano-Frankivsk)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each Christmas, the Basilian monastery church in Ivano-Frankivsk fills with carols for the Koliada na Mayzlyakh festival. Choirs and ensembles sing the old Ukrainian Christmas songs, in a celebration of the deeply rooted caroling tradition of Halychyna, where Koliada joins folk custom with the life of the church. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Koprivnica Renaissance Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the old fortified town of Koprivnica steps back into the Renaissance for a grand reenactment festival. Costumed knights, craftsmen, and nobles fill the walls with jousting, archery, and period crafts, in a celebration of the town's rich late-medieval past as a border fortress. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Korsholm Music Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the parish of Korsholm near Vaasa hosts a chamber music festival of classical and baroque works. Concerts fill village churches and open-air venues in the archipelago setting, in a celebration that brings together the Swedish- and Finnish-speaking communities of the region around fine music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kosovo Liberation Army Veterans Day (Prekaz Memorial Ceremonies)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each year, Kosovo honors the veterans of its Liberation Army, gathering at the memorial in Prekaz where the legendary commander Adem Jashari and his family made their stand. The solemn ceremonies recall the resistance that rose in the Drenica region, in a day of memory for those who fought for Kosovo's freedom. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kotka Sea Days
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Finnish port of Kotka throws open its harbor for the Sea Days, turning the town into a hub of concerts, markets, and maritime fun. Tall ships and sailors fill the waterfront, in a much-loved festival that has celebrated the city's seafaring spirit since 1962. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kotor Festival of Theatre for Children
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Montenegrin town of Kotor delights its youngest audiences with a festival of theatre and puppetry for children. Professional troupes from across Europe and beyond perform in a competitive program, in a celebration, part of the KotorArt series, devoted to nurturing young theatre-goers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kotor Summer Carnival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each late July, the waterfront and old streets of Kotor fill with costumes and music for the Summer Carnival. Parades and revelry light up the warm nights of the bay, in a celebration that carries the town's rich carnival tradition into the heart of the tourist season. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Kotor Winter Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter, the old town of Kotor stages a carnival of masks and satire centuries in the making. Costumed parades and mocking performances fill the streets in the sharp Adriatic humor of the region, in a celebration whose roots reach back to old midwinter rites of driving out the dark. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kozara Ethno Festival (Kozara Traditions in Song & Dance)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the city of Banja Luka celebrates the mountain traditions of Kozara with an ethno festival. Some four hundred performers in native costume bring song, dance, and craft to venues in the city and nearby Piskavica, in a celebration of the rich rural folklore of the Potkošarje region beneath Mount Kozara. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Banja Luka, Potkozarje (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Banja Luka, Potkozarje (Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Koziivka Fest
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · At the Singing Terraces amphitheatre near Horodnie, this late-summer festival gathers Kharkiv-region artisans for a craft fair, food and drink stands, and open-air music. Set in the old Slobozhanshchyna country, KoziivkaFEST puts the region's folk crafts and rural spirit on display. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kočani Rice Days
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September, the Macedonian town of Kočani celebrates the rice that grows in its valley with the Days of Kočani Rice. Folk concerts, tastings of rice dishes, and the crowning of a harvest beauty mark the season, in a celebration that honors a deep-rooted farming tradition unusual in this corner of the Balkans. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Krajina Culture & Tradition Day
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each year on International Mother Language Day, Banja Luka celebrates the folk traditions of the Krajina region with a cultural fair. Music, costume, crafts, and food gather from Krajina towns like Prijedor and Čelinac, in a celebration of the heritage and the mother tongue of this storied corner of Bosnia. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Banja Luka, Krajina (northwestern Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Banja Luka, Krajina (northwestern Republika Srpska), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Kraklingbo Market
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September the eastern Gotland village of Kräklingbo holds a country fair that dates back to 1883, with over a hundred stalls of local crafts, produce, and livestock. The parish market keeps the island's rural heritage alive, spread out across a single busy late-summer day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kraków Nativity Scenes Contest
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each December, Kraków's Main Market Square fills with szopki, dazzling little nativity scenes built like fairy-tale towers in the shape of the city's own churches and spires. Craftsmen present their glittering creations in a contest going back to the 1800s, in a UNESCO-listed tradition found nowhere else. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Krampus Run (Salzburg Christmas Market)
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each December 5, on the eve of St. Nicholas, the old town of Salzburg fills with horned, fur-clad Krampuses for a thunderous run. Bells clang and torches flare as the wild figures rampage through the streets, in a celebration that blends Catholic and Alpine folklore at the heart of the city's Christmas market. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Krasnoilsk Malanka
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January 13, on the eve of the Old New Year, the village of Krasnoilsk comes alive with the Malanka carnival. Locals costumed as the King, Queen, Bear, and other figures visit house to house with singing and dancing, in an ancient Slavic celebration of the turning of the year held with rare devotion in Bukovina. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kreuzberg Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year Kreuzbergstraße in Berlin fills with this long-running street festival, established in 1994 as a celebration of the neighborhood's mix of cultures and peaceful coexistence. Set in one of the city's most diverse districts, it has become a yearly affirmation of Kreuzberg's open spirit. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kristiansund Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the coastal Norwegian town of Kristiansund fills with opera and classical music for Festspillene. Local choirs and international performers take harbor-side and indoor stages, in a celebration that brings high culture to the town, joining the salt air of the sea with the grandeur of the concert hall. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Krutiy Zamis Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · In early August the village of Melnyky, in the historic Holodnyi Yar, hosts Krutiy Zamis, a patriot-art festival of folk crafts, music, and volunteer work over two days. Set in a landscape that stands as a symbol of Ukrainian resistance, it weaves that legacy into a gathering of art and shared labor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Krāslava Castle Park Festival "Legends of Krāslava"
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · On a late-October evening the historic castle garden of Krāslava is lit up with lanterns, costumed actors, and a silent disco for a theatrical night of folklore. It belongs to Latvia's tradition of Legends Nights, which fill the country's old castles and manors with story and atmosphere after dark. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Krāslava City Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July the town of Krāslava marks its City Day with music, exhibitions, sports, and family activities across two summer days. Like other Latvian towns, it sets aside this weekend each year to celebrate itself and bring the community together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kuhmo Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the remote eastern Finnish town of Kuhmo fills two weeks with classical music for its chamber music festival. World-class musicians gather year after year in this far-off forest town, in a celebration, founded by local enthusiasts in the 1970s, that has become a beloved haven for the art. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Kukeri Games in Pirin
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each New Year, the city of Blagoevgrad gathers kukeri groups from across the Pirin region for a day of masquerade. Dozens of costumed figures parade through the city ringing great bells and dancing to drive out evil, in a celebration of the old Bulgarian ritual believed to bring health and fertility for the year ahead. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Blagoevgrad, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Blagoevgrad, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Blagoevgrad, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Blagoevgrad, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Kukës Street Artists Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year the central square of Kukës comes alive with the Street Artists Festival, as local and visiting performers bring music, puppetry, acrobatics, and dance into the open air. Long a crossroads town in northern Albania, Kukës turns its streets into a public theater for the days of the festival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kuldīga Old Town Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Latvian town of Kuldīga celebrates its historic heart with the Old Town Festival. Live music, dance, art, and storytelling fill the UNESCO-listed streets of wooden houses and old brick bridges over two days, in a celebration that stages modern arts amid one of Latvia's most beautiful old towns. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kulish Fest
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each July, around the birthday of the writer Panteleimon Kulish, the town of Shostka celebrates his legacy with Kulish-Fest. Events fill the town and the nearby villages where he lived, in a celebration that connects the community with its literary roots and a deep pride in a son of the Sumy region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Shostka, Shostka region, Ukrainian literary heritage, Ukraine
festivals.address: Shostka, Shostka region, Ukrainian literary heritage, Ukraine

festivals.name: Kupala Night at Pyrohiv Museum
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the open-air museum at Pyrohiv near Kyiv celebrates Ivan Kupala among its old village buildings. Participants weave flower wreaths, process with the figures of Ivan and Marena, dance in circles, and leap the bonfires, in a celebration of one of the most magical and pagan-rooted of Ukraine's folk holidays. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kupala Night Festival (Czersk Castle)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each midsummer, the grounds of the Mazovian Dukes' Castle at Czersk fill with bonfires for the Kupalnocka festival. Medieval-themed performances, a craft fair, wreath-making, and folk rituals celebrate Slavic Kupala Night, in a celebration that uses the old castle ruins to bring the pagan customs of midsummer vividly back to life. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Kuressaare Maritime Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the island town of Kuressaare on Saaremaa celebrates its bond with the sea. The harbor fills with traditional boats, races, and concerts, and a maritime fair lines the waterfront, in a warm festival honoring the seafaring life of Estonia's largest island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kuriozum Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, Zabola Castle in Covasna County hosts the Kuriozum chamber music festival. Classical, authentic lăutărească folk, and film music share the same stage in the castle's historic ambiance, in a celebration, convened by the estate's owners, that delights in the unexpected meeting of musical worlds. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kurpie Honey Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the Kurpie region of Poland celebrates its honey at the Miodobranie, a two-day festival set at an open-air folk center. Beekeepers share their golden harvest for tasting while folk musicians and dancers in Kurpie costume perform, in a celebration of a deep local beekeeping heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kurpie Music Competition
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the village of Lelis gathers harmonists and fiddlers for Kurpiowskie Granie, a competition of Kurpie folk music. Performers in traditional costume play the authentic melodies and dances of the region, in a celebration set in the heart of the Kurpie country that keeps its centuries-old musical traditions alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kurultai Intercultural Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the Dobrogea town of Techirghiol celebrates Turkic and Tatar heritage with the great Kurultai. Traditional archery, horse games, and wrestling join folk dance, music, and crafts from dozens of countries, alongside a congress on intangible heritage, in a celebration of the deep nomadic traditions of these peoples. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kuyavian Folk Song Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the spa town of Inowrocław celebrates the folk songs of the Kujawy region with a festival in its salt park. Local choirs and instrumentalists compete in song, traditional cooking, and wreath-making, in a celebration that honors the rural culture of Kuyavia and the poet Jan Kasprowicz, a son of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kuyavian Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the spa town of Ciechocinek becomes the capital of Kuyavian folklore for four days. Folk markets, competitions, and nightly concerts fill the festival, in a celebration deeply rooted in Kujawian tradition that gathers the region's folk arts and community spirit in one place. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Kyparissia Sunshine Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the Peloponnesian town of Kyparissia celebrates its folk traditions with the Sunshine folklore festival. Local dance troupes perform in costume to traditional music, with food booths and old songs, in a celebration created by community associations to keep the heritage of the region alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: L'Aldosa Village Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Over several early-August days, the Andorran mountain village of L'Aldosa keeps its Festa Major, with a solemn Sunday Mass and days of music and community gathering. The village Festa Major lies at the heart of Catalan-Andorran life, built around the patron saint's feast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: La Folle Journée
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each winter, the city of Nantes packs hundreds of short classical concerts into a few intense days for La Folle Journée, the Crazy Day. Created in 1995 to make classical music welcoming to everyone, it lets audiences hop from one bite-sized performance to the next among thousands of fellow music lovers. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Nantes, Nantes (Pays de la Loire), France
festivals.address: Nantes, Nantes (Pays de la Loire), France

festivals.name: La Geria Traditional Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the village of Yaiza celebrates the remarkable vineyards of La Geria, where vines grow in pits dug into Lanzarote's volcanic ash. Wineries open for tastings of malvasía and other volcanic wines amid bodega tours, folk music, and food, in a celebration of the island's resilient agricultural heart. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: La Louvière Laetare Carnival (Mid-Lent Gilles)
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each mid-Lent, the Walloon city of La Louvière fills with the famous Gilles for its Laetare Carnival. Over three days, the costumed Gilles gather before dawn and parade to the town square with their bells, clogs, and ostrich-plume hats, in a celebration over a century old at the heart of Walloon carnival tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: La Mancha International Music Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Toledo town of Quintanar de la Orden fills a month with classical and early music for the La Mancha International Festival. Fine concerts honor the cultural heritage of the region, in a celebration, held since the early 1990s, that has brought historical music to the heart of La Mancha for over three decades. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Quintanar de la Orden, Castilla-La Mancha (La Mancha area), Spain
festivals.address: Quintanar de la Orden, Castilla-La Mancha (La Mancha area), Spain

festivals.name: LaFolk Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · In the coastal city of Vaasa, the LaFolk Festival celebrates the folk music and dance of Ostrobothnia, with concerts, dancing, and cultural workshops. Run by the LaFO folk music center, it brings the region's Swedish- and Finnish-speaking communities together around their shared Nordic folk roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lake Shkodër Festival (Shirokë)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the lakeside village of Shirokë celebrates the beauty of Lake Shkodër with the Festa e Liqenit. Two or three days of festivity honor the natural and cultural heritage of the largest lake in the Balkans, in a celebration that draws the people of Shkodër to the shores of their protected and beloved water. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: LAMPA Discussion Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Over a summer weekend the town of Cēsis fills with LAMPA, a festival of debate and discussion where experts and citizens gather for hundreds of free talks on politics, the environment, culture, and technology. Hosted by an open-society foundation, it is built as a public square for civil dialogue. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Lampegat Carnival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each Carnival, the Dutch city of Eindhoven sheds its name and becomes "Lampegat" for days of pre-Lenten revelry. The whole center turns into a giant festival ground of costumes, music, and parades, in a joyful celebration that joins the great carnival tradition of the Catholic Dutch south. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Landshut Wedding (Festival of 1475)
festivals.description: Late June 2027 · Every four years, the Bavarian town of Landshut throws itself back into the Middle Ages to reenact a royal wedding of 1475, when a Bavarian duke married a Polish princess. Tens of thousands take part in authentic period costume, parading, jousting, and feasting, in one of Europe's grandest historical pageants. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Lanterns, Songs & Street Theatre
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November 11, the city of Utrecht glows with homemade lanterns for the Sint Maarten parade. Thousands of children sing their way from Griftpark to Domplein amid music and street theater, in a celebration inspired by the legend of Saint Martin of Tours. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Las Mondas Festival
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Easter, the town of Talavera de la Reina dresses in blue and white for Las Mondas, a spring festival whose roots reach back before the Romans. Neighborhoods carry offerings to the town's patron Virgin in a tradition once dedicated to the goddess of the harvest, later woven into the Christian calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Talavera de la Reina, Castilla-La Mancha (Talavera de la Reina), Spain
festivals.address: Talavera de la Reina, Castilla-La Mancha (Talavera de la Reina), Spain

festivals.name: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each winter, the city of Las Palmas erupts into one of the Canary Islands' most flamboyant carnivals, a riot of feathers, sequins, and street dancing. Satirical music troupes called murgas poke fun at the year's news, and the famous Drag Queen Gala draws huge crowds, in a celebration that lights up the island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, Gran Canaria, Canary Islands, Spain
festivals.address: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, Gran Canaria, Canary Islands, Spain

festivals.name: Lastovo Festivities
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August the village of Gornja Lastva above Tivat gathers on its old threshing floor for a festival of community singing and dancing. The Napredak Cultural Society leads the traditional Bokeljska kolo, reviving the communal gatherings that once filled this hillside hamlet of the Bay of Kotor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Latgale Senior Song and Dance Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, a town in the Latgale region of eastern Latvia hosts a song and dance festival made for its older generations. Senior choirs and dance groups parade and perform together, keeping the region's folk songs and dances alive and passing their joy on to younger eyes. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Latgale's Hospitality Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the city of Daugavpils celebrates the warmth of Latgale with a festival of hospitality. Folk music and dance, culinary and craft workshops, and farmers' markets fill Unity Square, in a celebration of the food, traditions, and famous welcome of this diverse corner of Latvia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Latvia Independence Day Torchlight Procession
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November 18, on Latvia's Independence Day, thousands fill the streets of Riga carrying torches in a solemn evening march to the Freedom Monument. The river of flame moving through the dark city honors the 1918 declaration of independence, in a striking show of national unity and remembrance. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Latvian Minority Festival "Open Wide the Little Key!"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Latvian city of Liepāja celebrates its ethnic-minority communities with the festival "Open Wide the Little Key!" Traditional songs, dances, and crafts of many national groups fill the central square, in a two-day celebration that honors the rich diversity woven through Latvian life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Liepāja, Latvijas mazākumtautību kopienas (Latvian minority communities), Latvia
festivals.address: Liepāja, Latvijas mazākumtautību kopienas (Latvian minority communities), Latvia

festivals.name: Lauder Common Riding
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August the Border town of Lauder holds its Common Riding, as residents ride horseback along the routes of the town's old burgh boundaries and parade through the streets. Like other Borders towns, Lauder re-asserts its ancient territory each year in this ride around the marches. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lauder, Lauder, Scottish Borders, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Lauder, Lauder, Scottish Borders, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Lauenburg Marksmen’s Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the town of Lauenburg on the Elbe holds its centuries-old marksmen's festival. Parades and shooting competitions by the old Schützengilde culminate in the crowning of a Schützenkönig, in a celebration deeply rooted in the guild traditions of northern Germany. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Laugharne Weekend
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the West Wales village of Laugharne celebrates the arts in honor of the poet Dylan Thomas, who lived and wrote here. Left-field music, comedy, and readings fill intimate local venues, in a celebration set in the coastal town that inspired the world of Under Milk Wood. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Laugharne, Laugharne (Talacharn) community, Wales, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Laugharne, Laugharne (Talacharn) community, Wales, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Lauksnos International Intangible Cultural Heritage Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the village of Laukšno in the Telšiai district celebrates Samogitian intangible heritage. Folk music, crafts like weaving and woodcarving, storytelling, and the local dialect fill the festival, in a celebration that reinforces the deep ethnographic identity of this corner of Lithuania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lazar Vučković Poetry Meetings
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, a literary festival across the Serbian communities of Kosovo honors the memory of the poet Lazar Vučković. Readings and events move among Orthodox sites and towns, culminating in Leposavić, in a celebration that keeps alive the legacy of a beloved Kosovo Serb poet and the culture he wrote from. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Leposavić, Serbian community (Kosovo and Metohija), Serbia
festivals.address: Leposavić, Serbian community (Kosovo and Metohija), Serbia

festivals.name: Lazarim Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Carnival, the Douro village of Lazarim fills its streets with fearsome hand-carved wooden masks. Locals in satirical costume parade in the Entrudo de Lazarim, one of Portugal's most striking rural carnivals, in a celebration tied to old agrarian and pagan rites of winter's renewal. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lazarus Saturday (Krupanj)
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each Lazarus Saturday, a week before Orthodox Palm Sunday, the town of Krupanj fills with children in Serbian costume. They sing the old Lazarev songs and dance the kolo through the streets, in a springtime celebration of an Orthodox tradition that blesses the town with the promise of new life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Le Bourru (Young Wine Tastings, La Côte)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · In autumn the vineyards of La Côte open their cellars for Le Bourru, a tasting of the cloudy, half-fermented young wine fresh from the press. A long-standing Vaud tradition, it lets wine lovers taste the very first stage of the winemaking year, straight from the barrel. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Leganés Festivities of San Juan and San Fortunato
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each summer, the Madrid town of Leganés honors its patron saints San Juan and San Fortunato. Neighborhood fairs, open-air concerts, and traditional bull runs fill the district fiestas, in a celebration that gathers the town around its saints in the warm heart of the year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lendava Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each autumn, the Prekmurje town of Lendava celebrates its grape harvest with a great festival. A procession of costumed cultural groups winds through the old town, followed by folk music, food, and the region's wine, in a celebration marking the end of the picking season in this winegrowing corner of Slovenia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lerwick Up Helly Aa (Viking Fire Festival)
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each year on the last Tuesday of January, the Shetland town of Lerwick blazes with the Up Helly Aa fire festival. Hundreds of guizers in elaborate Viking costume march with torches and set a longship ablaze, in a spectacular celebration that marks the end of Yule and revives the islands' deep Norse heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lesser Poland Carp Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the town of Zator, at the heart of Poland's Carp Valley, celebrates its fish-farming heritage with the Carp Festival. Culinary and cultural events fill the market square, in a celebration of the carp ponds that have shaped this corner of Lesser Poland and the dish at the center of the Polish Christmas table. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lichtenvoorde Flower Parade
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Dutch town of Lichtenvoorde parades floats covered in millions of dahlias for its Bloemencorso. Built by neighborhood teams, the great flower sculptures roll through the Achterhoek town, in a celebration of community pride and craftsmanship dating back to the 1920s. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Liechtenstein National Day (Princely Reception & Fireworks)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Every August 15, the little principality of Liechtenstein celebrates its National Day in Vaduz, gathering at the foot of the prince's castle on the hill. After an official ceremony the whole country joins a great open-air folk festival, ending with fireworks over the valley, in a warm celebration of statehood and crown. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Liechtenstein Winegrowers' Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the town-hall square of Vaduz fills with the new vintage for the Liechtenstein Winegrowers' Festival. Producers from across the principality pour their wines for tasting, in a celebration of the centuries-old viticulture that clings to the rocky slopes above the capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Liepāja International Stars Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March, the Latvian port of Liepāja hosts an international classical music festival that draws world-class soloists and orchestras to the Baltic coast. Chamber, symphonic, and operatic works fill its concerts, in an event run by the city's symphony orchestra that also gives a stage to rising young talent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lilies Festival of Nola
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the town of Nola near Naples honors its patron, San Paolino, by hauling eight towering wooden obelisks, each up to 25 meters tall, through the streets on the shoulders of dancing crews. The famous Festa dei Gigli, honored by UNESCO, recalls the saint's legendary return from captivity long ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Limburg Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the central Dutch region of Limburg fills castles, farms, and riverbanks with open-air theatre for five days. Theatre, dance, music, and street performance unfold in striking local venues, in a celebration that weaves the landscapes and heritage of Limburg into the very staging of the arts. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Limoux Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · From January into March, the southern town of Limoux holds what it calls the world's longest carnival, with masked bands parading every single weekend. Dressed as elegant Pierrots, the Fecos dance through the arcaded main square to traditional music and clouds of confetti, in a celebration steeped in old Occitan custom. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Linden Festival (Luncavița)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the village of Luncavița celebrates its linden forests and beekeeping with a festival of bees and trees. Beekeeper contests, the weighing of the honey harvest, folk dancing, and honey markets fill the day, in a celebration of the apiculture nurtured by the great linden woods of this Dobrogea village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Linz Cathedral Classics Concerts
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the square before Linz's great Mariendom cathedral becomes an open-air concert stage for Klassik am Dom. International stars perform everything from classical to crossover pop beneath the soaring Gothic spire, in a series that has become a highlight of the Austrian city's cultural summer since 2011. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Linz Street Arts Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the center of Linz turns into one big open-air stage for the Pflasterspektakel, a three-day street arts festival. Acrobats, clowns, jugglers, and musicians from around the world perform on every corner, in a free, joyful celebration of street culture the city has loved since 1989. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Lipnica Murowana Easter Palms and Handicrafts Contest (named after Józef Piotrowski)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Palm Sunday, the village of Lipnica Murowana raises towering handmade Easter palms, some over thirty meters tall, to be blessed in the market square. Crafted with deep care and skill, the Lipnica palms are true works of folk art, in a celebration of a beloved local tradition that draws crowds each spring. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Lipnica Murowana, Southern Poland (Lesser Poland/Galicia), Poland
festivals.address: Lipnica Murowana, Southern Poland (Lesser Poland/Galicia), Poland

festivals.name: LiraFest
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, around the feast of the Holy Protectress, the city of Rivne celebrates traditional Ukrainian music with LiraFest. Players of the bandura, kobza, and lyre perform rebel songs, Cossack ballads, and sacred music, in a celebration rooted in the bard tradition of Polissya and its deep ties to the nation's story. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Literary Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Kharkiv gathers writers and readers for its Literary Fair, a festival of author readings, book launches, and talks on local history and culture. Named for a famous magazine of the 1920s, it revives Kharkiv's old role as a great center of Ukrainian publishing and letters. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Liudvinavas Nights
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer the town park of Liudvinavas hosts an intimate live-music festival of folk and soft rock, drawing local families and visitors for evening concerts. Rooted in the strong musical traditions of Suvalkija, it was set up in recent years to revive the town's love of song. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Liv Village Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the Latvian city of Liepāja celebrates the heritage of its historic Liv quarter with the Līvas ciema svētki. Music, craft, and tradition fill the old Livonian district, in a celebration that honors the identity of one of Kurzeme's leading intangible-heritage events and the people who gave the quarter its name. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Liepāja, Kurzeme (Livonian heritage, Liepāja community), Latvia
festivals.address: Liepāja, Kurzeme (Livonian heritage, Liepāja community), Latvia

festivals.name: Liège Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Liège fills with pre-Lenten revelry for the Carnaval de la cité ardente. Costumes, music, and parades bring European Mardi Gras customs to life with a local flavor, in a celebration that lights up the streets of the "fiery city" in the depths of winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ljubljana Biennale of Graphic Arts
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Every two years, the Slovenian capital hosts one of the world's oldest exhibitions of graphic and print art. Founded in 1955, the Ljubljana Biennale gathers leading and experimental printmakers from around the globe, turning the city into a meeting point for this often-overlooked art form. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ljubljana, Ljubljana art and graphic arts community, Slovenia
festivals.address: Ljubljana, Ljubljana art and graphic arts community, Slovenia

festivals.name: Ljubljana Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Slovenian capital swings to one of Europe's oldest jazz festivals, running since 1960. Slovenian and international musicians play across the city's squares, parks, and clubs, in a celebration that first brought jazz to the country and still champions everything from tradition to far-out fusion. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ljubljana, Slovenian and Ljubljana jazz community, Slovenia
festivals.address: Ljubljana, Slovenian and Ljubljana jazz community, Slovenia

festivals.name: Ljubljana Street Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the streets, squares, and parks of Ljubljana fill with performers for Ana Desetnica, an international street theatre festival held since 1998. Circus, dance, and theatre spill into the open air for all to enjoy, bringing art out of the halls and into the heart of the summer city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Llangollen International Musical Eisteddfod
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the small Welsh town of Llangollen welcomes choirs, dancers, and musicians from around the world for a week of music and friendly competition. Founded in 1947 to help heal a world torn by war, this international eisteddfod still turns a quiet border town into a global stage for song. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Locarno Carnival (Stranociada)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the lakeside town of Locarno fills the Piazza Grande with masks and music for the Stranôciada carnival. Street parades, confetti battles, and jester figures bring days of revelry, in a celebration that blends Ticinese folklore with the merrymaking of the Italian carnival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lofotr Viking Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Lofotr Viking Museum on Vestvågøy stages a great Viking festival around a reconstructed chieftain's longhouse. Reenactors fill the days with Viking life, crafts, and combat, in a celebration built on the local archaeology of the Lofoten Islands and the powerful chieftains who once ruled here. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Loire Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2027 · Every two years, the city of Orléans celebrates the great Loire River with a five-day festival. Parades of traditional flat-bottomed boats, concerts on the water, and craft markets fill the quays, in a celebration of the river culture and trade that have shaped this city on one of Europe's last wild rivers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: London Road Eleventh Night Bonfire
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the London Road area of east Belfast raises one of the city's notable Eleventh Night bonfires. The great stack is lit on the eve of the Twelfth, blazing into the night, in a celebration that marks the Protestant and loyalist tradition leading up to the July 12 parades. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Longing for the Doina
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the botanical garden of Bucharest fills with song for Dor de doină, a festival of the doina. Workshops, crafts, storytelling, and exhibits honor the lyrical, improvised folk song that UNESCO holds as a treasure, in a celebration, begun in 2023, of one of the most soulful forms of Romanian music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Longyearbyen Literature Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Arctic town of Longyearbyen hosts the world's northernmost literature festival. Author talks, book launches, film, and concerts fill a free, all-ages "word bath" where schoolchildren and grown-ups read together, in a celebration created to enrich the cultural life of remote Svalbard. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Longyearbyen Sun Return Festival
