festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each year the Arctic town of Longyearbyen celebrates Solfestuka, marking the return of the sun after months of polar night. When the first rays reach the old town again, the community gathers to greet the light in a festival as old as the modern settlement itself. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lontzen Kirmes (Maypole & Village Fair)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August the village of Lontzen holds its three-day Kirmes, opening with a parade and the ceremonial raising of the Kirmes tree by the local shooting clubs, then days of funfair rides, food, and music. The fair carries the Rhineland carnival spirit common to the Eupen region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lontzen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium
festivals.address: Lontzen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium

festivals.name: Lorca Holy Week
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each spring, the town of Lorca stages a Holy Week unlike any other, where two rival brotherhoods, the White and the Blue, compete in dazzling biblical pageants. Richly embroidered banners, Roman costumes, and thundering chariot races fill the streets, in a celebration as much spectacle as solemn devotion. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: LOSINAVA Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the island town of Mali Lošinj celebrates its seafaring past with the LOSINAVA festival. Festivities honor the maritime and shipbuilding heritage of an island long bound to the sea, in a celebration that connects the people of Lošinj to the centuries of sailors and shipwrights who came before. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mali Lošinj, Primorje-Gorski Kotar (Mali Lošinj), Croatia
festivals.address: Mali Lošinj, Primorje-Gorski Kotar (Mali Lošinj), Croatia

festivals.name: Loulé Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Algarve town of Loulé throws the oldest and best-known carnival in the region. Satirical floats, samba dancers, and giant-headed figures fill the streets with color and laughter, in a celebration the town has kept since 1906 as a joyful, welcoming party for all. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lublin Street Arts Carnival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the Polish city of Lublin fills its Old Town with jugglers, acrobats, and fire-eaters for the Carnaval Sztukmistrzów. Held since 2005, this international festival of street arts brings dazzling open-air shows and late-night spectacles to the medieval streets, in a joyful celebration of circus and performance. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Lucerne Carnival (Guggenmusik Street Parades)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Swiss city of Lucerne erupts into Fasnacht, beginning with a wild early-morning start on the Thursday before Ash Wednesday. Masked revelers and brassy Guggenmusik bands fill the lakeside old town with noise and color, in a carnival with its own playful, unmistakable character. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Ludza City Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year Ludza, the oldest city in Latvia, celebrates the anniversary of its founding in 1173 with concerts, sports, markets, and youth events. The City Day brings out the Latgalian culture and long history of a place that has watched the centuries pass since the Middle Ages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lugano Autumn Festival (Harvest Market & Grottini)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the lakeside city of Lugano celebrates the harvest with the Festa d'Autunno. Food stalls, wine cellars, tractor parades, and folk music fill the central squares as visitors sample local cheeses and Merlot, in a celebration of the rural heritage and autumn bounty of Italian-speaking Ticino. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lugano Estival Jazz
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the lakeside city of Lugano fills its main piazza with free open-air jazz for Estival Jazz, a festival running since 1979. Crowds gather under the warm Ticino night for renowned international and local musicians, in one of Switzerland's most beloved music traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Lumière Film Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the city of Lyon, where the Lumière brothers invented cinema, celebrates the movies with a festival devoted to film's heritage. Restored classics screen across the city and a grand lifetime award is given to a great of the screen, in a joyful tribute to where it all began. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: LUV Fest
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Centered on Prešeren Day in early February, Ljubljana's LUV Fest is a festival of love, art, and poetry that runs into early March. Founded to honor the poet France Prešeren, it celebrates the Slovenian language and a deep national pride in literature and culture. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Luxembourg National Day (Torchlight Parade & Fireworks)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 23, Luxembourg celebrates its National Day in honor of the Grand Duke, with two days of festivities across the capital. The eve brings a torchlight parade and great fireworks over the old town, followed by official ceremonies, in the country's proudest and most festive day of the year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Lüûbnitsa Fish and Onion Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · The Seto village of Lüübnitsa holds its Fish and Onion Fair around the golden onions and fresh fish of nearby Lake Peipus. Grown from the old village market, it revives a lively trading day and celebrates the farming and fishing life of these fertile lakeside villages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Madalena Festivities
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Pico town of Madalena throws its great summer festival, the Festas da Madalena. Live music on many stages, a wine expo, folk and philharmonic parades, and a craft market fill several days, in a celebration that showcases the wines of the island's volcanic vineyards and the spirit of the community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Madeira Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the island of Madeira throws its biggest winter party, two weeks of carnival inspired by both Portuguese and Brazilian traditions. Funchal fills with samba, music, and a grand allegorical parade of glittering floats and costumes, in a celebration that lights up the island before Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Madeira Wine Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year during the grape harvest, the Madeiran city of Funchal celebrates its famous wine with the Festa do Vinho. Grape-treading demonstrations in nearby Estreito de Câmara de Lobos and a procession in traditional costume fill the festival, in a celebration of the centuries-old viticulture behind Madeira's storied wine. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Madre City Festival (Urban Weekend)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each early autumn the city of Fuenlabrada hosts Madre City, a youth-focused festival of urban culture spread across public spaces like Huerto del Cura park. First held in 2023 and born from the local youth council, it gives the city's young people a weekend stage for contemporary street arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Madéfalva Massacre Memorial Day
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 7, the Székely community of Siculeni gathers in solemn remembrance of the Madéfalva Massacre of 1764. Villagers come to the memorial chapel to honor the hundreds of Székelys killed by imperial troops, in a grave commemoration, the Siculicidium, that keeps alive the memory of a dark day in Székely history. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Magdalena Festival (Castellón)
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each spring, the city of Castellón celebrates the Magdalena, recalling the day in 1251 when its people came down from the hilltop to settle on the plain. A great pilgrimage climbs back up to the old site, and days of parades, fireworks, and festivity fill the city in its biggest celebration of the year. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Castellón de la Plana, Valencian Community, Spain
festivals.address: Castellón de la Plana, Valencian Community, Spain

festivals.name: Magyarlapád Village Day
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Over several days the Transylvanian Hungarian village of Magyarlapád celebrates its Village Day with folk dances, live music, traditional games, and communal goulash cooking. Begun in the late 2000s, it gathers the village around the rustic rural culture it is proud to keep. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mai que Mai Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each May, the festival Mai que Mai winds through the villages of the Orb and Jaur valleys in celebration of Occitan culture. Concerts, the bal folk dance, storytelling, and workshops fill several days, in a celebration that carries the living language and traditions of Occitania from town to town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mainz Carnival (Rose Monday Parade)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Rhineland city of Mainz throws one of Germany's great carnivals, building to the Rose Monday parade. Floats poking fun at politics and the news roll through the streets behind marching bands and costumed crowds, in a celebration of satire and high spirits the city holds dear. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malanca of Crasna
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · On the night of the Old New Year, the village of Crasna fills with the Malanca, as costumed villagers playing Baba, the Old Man, the Bear, and others parade and bring blessings door to door. This Romanian Bukovinian winter rite blends St. Basil's Eve customs with much older agrarian ritual. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2027-01-13) is a fixed annual date.
festivals.location: Crasna, Bukovina (Northern Bukovina, Ukraine) – Romanian community, Ukraine
festivals.address: Crasna, Bukovina (Northern Bukovina, Ukraine) – Romanian community, Ukraine

festivals.name: Malanka-Fest
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the city of Chernivtsi throws Malanka-Fest, a great winter carnival of the Old New Year. Village groups and folklore ensembles parade in colorful costume and mask, in a celebration of the beloved Bukovinian Malanka, the "Generous Evening" whose roots run deep into the old New Year's rites. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malepère Truffle and Terroir Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer the village square of Roullens fills with producers, artisans, and vintners for a festival of the summer black truffle and the wines of the Malepère hills. Visitors taste truffles, sample local AOC wines, and watch cooking demonstrations in a celebration of this corner of the Aude. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malmedy Cwarmé (Haguète & Longs-Nés)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Walloon town of Malmedy erupts into the Cwarmé, a centuries-old carnival of costumed characters and dances. The long-nosed Longs-Nés roam the crowd and the hook-wielding Haguète playfully snares onlookers, in four days of color that are the pride of the town's heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malta Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Valletta and villages across Malta fill with costumes and floats for the Malta Carnival. With roots in the 16th century, the pre-Lenten festival brings parades, King Carnival, and revelry, in one of the island's most beloved celebrations of indulgence before Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malta Independence Day (Band Concert, Victoria, Gozo)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September 21, the Gozitan city of Victoria marks Malta's independence from Britain in 1964. Official ceremonies fill Independence Square, capped by an evening concert from the renowned Leone Band, in a proud commemoration of the day the islands took charge of their own future. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Malta Independence Day (Official Ceremony, Valletta)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each September 21, the Maltese capital of Valletta hosts the official ceremonies for Independence Day, marking the end of British rule in 1964. Flags, military honors, and state events fill the grand harbour city, in one of the five national days that chart Malta's long road to sovereignty. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Malta International Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the historic city of Valletta hosts ten days of theatre, dance, music, and art across some of its most striking spaces, from grand halls to underground cisterns. The Malta International Arts Festival mixes bold contemporary work with the island's heritage, born of Valletta's turn as a European Capital of Culture. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Malta International Fireworks Festival
festivals.description: Mid April 2027 · Each April, the Grand Harbour of Valletta lights up for the Malta International Fireworks Festival, a dazzling competition of pyrotechnics. Local and visiting teams send their displays bursting over the water, drawing on a Maltese village tradition of fireworks raised here to a grand, international scale. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malta Republic Day (Armed Forces Parade & Investiture, Valletta)
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each December 13, Malta marks Republic Day, the date in 1974 when the islands became a republic. Valletta hosts an armed forces parade and an investiture honoring citizens for their service, in a solemn national holiday celebrating Malta's journey to full self-governance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Malung Dance Band Week
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the small Swedish town of Malung fills with music and dancing for Dansbandsveckan, a whole week devoted to the country's beloved dansband sound. Couples take to open-air floors morning to night as bands play the easy, swinging dance music that has been a Swedish summer staple since the 1950s. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Mamers Carnival
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Norman town of Mamers holds one of the oldest carnivals in France, going back to the 1600s. Schoolchildren and community floats parade through town, ending with a mock trial of Mister Carnival and the burning of his straw effigy to chase away winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mamers, Pays de la Loire (Sarthe), France
festivals.address: Mamers, Pays de la Loire (Sarthe), France

festivals.name: Mamushë Tomato Festival (Harvest Fair & Tomato Cooking)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each harvest the village of Mamushë celebrates its famed tomatoes, as growers display and sell their crop, cook tomato dishes, and share community meals. As Kosovo's only Turkish-majority municipality, Mamushë ties this farming feast to the Ottoman roots of its people. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mamuthones and Issohadores Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the Sardinian village of Mamoiada fills with the slow, hypnotic tread of the Mamuthones, men in dark sheepskins and heavy black wooden masks who move under the weight of clanging bells. The red-clad Issohadores lasso onlookers from the crowd, in one of the island's oldest and most haunting carnivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Manannán International Festivals
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Erin Arts Centre in Port Erin hosts the Manannán International Festivals of the performing arts. Music and performance fill the season, named for Manannán mac Lir, the mythic sea god of Manx legend, in a celebration that reflects the island's deep cultural roots. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.
festivals.location: Port Erin, Rushen, Isle of Man
festivals.address: Port Erin, Rushen, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Manx National Week
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each July, the Isle of Man celebrates everything Manx during National Week, an island-wide burst of music, sport, language, and tradition. Centered on St John's around the great Tynwald gathering, it was revived in 2024 to renew pride in the island's distinctive culture and history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: St John's, National, Isle of Man
festivals.address: St John's, National, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Maramureș Hot Air Balloon Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the skies over Maramureș fill with color for a hot-air balloon festival set among the mountain villages. Dozens of balloons launch over the rural landscape as crafts, food, and folk music fill the ground, in a celebration that joins a modern spectacle with the region's deep communal and natural values. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: March 3 – Liberation Day of Bulgaria
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Every March 3, Bulgaria celebrates its Liberation Day, marking the 1878 treaty that ended five centuries of Ottoman rule. In Sofia, the nation gathers for official ceremonies and wreath-laying, often at the monument to the Tsar Liberator, in the country's most important patriotic holiday. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: March 3 – Liberation Day of Bulgaria
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Every March 3, the Black Sea city of Varna marks Bulgaria's Liberation Day, remembering freedom from Ottoman rule in 1878. A flag-raising and church service give way to a concert and fireworks over the seaside Sea Garden, in a day of patriotic pride and remembrance by the coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: March 3 – Liberation Day of Bulgaria
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Every March 3, the city of Plovdiv honors Bulgaria's Liberation Day with a climb to the Bunarjik hill, where a great monument looks out over the city. After a church service and wreath-laying, a march and evening fireworks mark the freedom won from Ottoman rule in 1878. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Marcoussis Village Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September the town of Marcoussis holds its one-day village fête to mark the start of the school year. Organized by the town hall, the fair is a simple celebration of solidarity and neighborliness, gathering the community for a day of shared fun. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: MareziJazz Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the coastal Slovenian village of Marezige hosts MareziJazz, the country's premier big-band jazz festival. Home-grown and visiting bands fill the village with swing, in a celebration, founded by local youth in 2004, that has grown into a beloved fixture of the Slovenian Istrian summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Maria Prymachenko Amateur Art Festival
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each spring, the city of Kramatorsk honors the great folk painter Maria Prymachenko with an amateur art festival. Painters, artisans, and performers from the Donetsk region show traditional Petrykivka painting, crafts, music, and dance, in a celebration that nurtures the folk-art heritage of Ukraine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kramatorsk, Donetsk region (Ukrainian folk art community), Ukraine
festivals.address: Kramatorsk, Donetsk region (Ukrainian folk art community), Ukraine

festivals.name: Maria Țănase National Folk Song Performers Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the city of Craiova hosts a folk-song festival honoring Maria Țănase, the beloved singer often called the voice of Romania. Since 1969, young folk vocalists from across the country have come to compete and perform, keeping her legacy and the nation's traditional music alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Marijampolė Music Park Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Lithuanian city of Marijampolė fills with concerts for the Music Park festival. Classical, jazz, and contemporary performances spread across the city, in a celebration, now in its second decade, that reflects the town's growing devotion to its cultural life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Maritime Holy Week
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Holy Week, the seaside neighborhoods of Valencia hold the Semana Santa Marinera, a maritime Easter. Brotherhoods process through the old fishing quarters of Canyamelar and Cabanyal by the sea, in a celebration that blends the rituals of Holy Week with the deep port and fishing heritage of these communities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Market Ball (Guinguette Dance Party, Monaco-Ville)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each early September the Place d'Armes in Monaco-Ville is lined with long communal tables for the Bal du Marché, an open-air dance party with a countryside guinguette theme. Market vendors set up stalls of socca and grilled fare, reviving the old spirit of the open-air village feast and dance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Markgröningen Shepherds' Run
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the town of Markgröningen revives a custom centuries old with the Schäferlauf, when young shepherds and shepherdesses race barefoot across a field of stubble to be crowned king and queen. Around the race grows a great folk festival, honoring the town's long history as a center of the wool trade. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Marko’s Days Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer the village of Bukovo near Boljevac gathers for Marko's Days, a small celebration of Vlach folk music and dance. Named for a legendary stone tied to the hero Kraljević Marko, the festival keeps the songs and traditions of the eastern Serbian Vlach community alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Boljevac, Eastern Serbia (Boljevac, Vlach tradition), Serbia
festivals.address: Boljevac, Eastern Serbia (Boljevac, Vlach tradition), Serbia

festivals.name: Marmația Winter Customs Festival (Sighetu Marmației)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each December 27, the Marmația festival fills Sighetu Marmației with masked dancers and performers of the old winter customs. Groups from across Maramureș and beyond parade through town enacting folk rituals and singing carols, in a celebration that has long honored the deep Christmastime heritage of the region. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Marown Parish Day
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year the Marown Memorial Playing Fields in Crosby fill with a village fair and fundraiser, with tombolas, children's sports, bicycle races, arts and crafts, and cake-making. Run by local volunteers and clubs, Marown Parish Day carries the parish's old spirit of looking after its own. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Martinique Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · From January to Lent, the Caribbean island of Martinique gives itself over to Carnival, weeks of parades, music, and costume rooted in Creole tradition. Each day has its own colors and characters, and it all ends with the burning of King Vaval on Ash Wednesday, in a joyful, satirical heart of island life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Martinje (Međimurje Wine Road, Štrigova)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November on St. Martin's Day, the village of Štrigova celebrates the blessing of its new wine along the Međimurje Wine Road. The "baptism" of the young vintage and tastings of the fresh wine fill the day, in a celebration that marks the heart of the harvest year in this Croatian winegrowing region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Marunada
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On October weekends the seaside town of Lovran celebrates Marunada, its festival of the marun chestnut that has become a symbol of the town. Chestnut gathering runs deep in Lovran's heritage, and the festival fills the streets with roasted nuts, chestnut sweets, and a strong sense of community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Maschsee Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the shores of Hanover's Maschsee lake fill with food and music for the Maschseefest. A "culinary world tour" of food stands, hundreds of performances, and water activities draw great crowds, in a celebration of the city's beloved recreational lake and its taste for the wider world. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Maskaraba: Meeting of Rural Carnivals and Botargas of Álava
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the Álava village of Zalduondo gathers the rural carnival figures of the Basque Country for Maskaraba. Costumed botargas and masqueraders from many villages bring their rituals together, in a young celebration, begun in 2024, of the deep masked-carnival heritage of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Bečov nad Teplou)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February the town of Bečov nad Teplou keeps Masopust, the Czech pre-Lenten carnival, with a masked parade through town and a traditional zabijačka pig-feast at the old Stará Pošta. It marks the last days of feasting before Lent in the warm, hearty style of Bohemian winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Bečov nad Teplou, West Bohemia, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Bečov nad Teplou, West Bohemia, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Kroměříž)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year the historic town of Kroměříž fills with Masopust, the Moravian pre-Lenten carnival, as masked and costumed locals dance through the old town to brass bands. A deeply rooted folk custom, it marks the end of the carnival season with food, music, and color before the fasting begins. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Liberec)
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each Shrovetide, the Czech city of Liberec fills with masks for its Masopust carnival. Costumed parades wind through the center past a market of meat pastries, sweets, and crafts, in a celebration of the centuries-old Czech pre-Lenten tradition of masquerade and feasting. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Liberec, North Bohemia (Liberec region), Czech Republic
festivals.address: Liberec, North Bohemia (Liberec region), Czech Republic

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Ostrava-Jih)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · On Shrove Tuesday the Ostrava-Jih district keeps Masopust with homemade masks, a cimbalom band, and street dancing. A neighborhood-rooted version of the Silesian carnival, it gathers residents in costume for a last burst of festivity before Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Prague)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the streets of Prague fill with the Masopust, the old Czech carnival whose name means the farewell to meat. Masked revelers, musicians, and dancers parade through the historic quarters amid feasting on rich foods, in a last burst of indulgence before the lean weeks ahead. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Masopust Carnival (Ústí nad Labem)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Shrovetide, the city of Ústí nad Labem celebrates its Masopust carnival. Costumed revelers and pre-Lenten festivity fill the streets in the town's own version of the tradition, in a celebration of the old Czech folk custom that marks the end of winter and the approach of Lent. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ústí nad Labem, Ústí nad Labem Region, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Ústí nad Labem, Ústí nad Labem Region, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Matarengi Market
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Tornedalen town of Övertorneå hosts the Matarengi Market, run by the local sports club. Launched in 1979, it has grown into one of the region's largest family festivals, in a celebration of community and summer in the far north of Sweden along the Finnish border. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: May Tree Setting & May Festival
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · On May Day the Luxembourg village of Nospelt raises its May Tree together, with brass band music, food, beer stands, and dancing run by the local clubs. The festival keeps the central European spring custom of crowning the season with a tall decorated tree. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Mazovian Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Warsaw celebrates the folk heritage of Mazovia with an open-air festival in its fountain park. Traditional music, dance, crafts, and cuisine fill the day, building to evening concerts, in a celebration that brings the songs, dances, and customs of the Mazovian countryside into the capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Małopolska International Folklore Meetings
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Polish town of Myślenice gathers folk ensembles from around the world for its International Folklore Meetings. The market square becomes a stage for music, dance, and cultural exchange, in a celebration that brings the world's folk traditions to a small town in the heart of Małopolska. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Medieval Castle Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the ruins of Burg Reuland castle come alive with a medieval festival. Costumed reenactors, period music, falconry, and fire performances fill the grounds, while market stalls sell handicrafts and armor, in a celebration of the region's medieval heritage and its castle lore. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Burg-Reuland, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium
festivals.address: Burg-Reuland, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium

festivals.name: Medieval Day (Celje Castle Jousting Tournament)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the castle of Celje turns back to the Middle Ages for a Medieval Day of jousting and pageantry. Costumed reenactors, craft demonstrations, and period music recreate the era when Celje was a powerful ducal seat, in a celebration of the medieval heritage that still shapes the city's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Medieval Days
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the ancient republic of San Marino turns its clifftop old town back into a medieval city for the Giornate Medioevali. Costumed performers, craftsmen, flag-throwers, and archers fill the stone streets, bringing alive the long history of this little state perched high on its mountain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Medieval Festival "Cetățuia Between Worlds"
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Cetățuia hill fortress above Brașov comes alive for the medieval festival "Between Worlds." Three days of knights, fire, music, and craft fill the old stronghold, in a celebration that draws on the heritage of the 14th-century Saxon fortress and the storied past of this Transylvanian city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Brașov, Brașov County (Transylvanian Saxon heritage), Romania
festivals.address: Brașov, Brașov County (Transylvanian Saxon heritage), Romania

festivals.name: Medieval Turku
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the old Finnish city of Turku travels back to the Middle Ages for the country's largest historical festival. The Old Great Square fills with knights, craftsmen, music, and market stalls, bringing to life the medieval days when Turku was Finland's most important town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Medun Fest
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September the Gorski Kotar village of Lič holds Medun Fest in honor of šumski medun, the forest honeydew honey that is a specialty of the region. The festival celebrates local beekeeping with tastings of the dark, woodland honey drawn from these mountain forests. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Melon Festival of Lectoure
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Gascon town of Lectoure celebrates its sweet, fragrant melon with a festival. A tasting contest crowns the best fruit, the melon brotherhood parades in ceremonial robes, and a communal melon feast fills the day, in a celebration of the prized melon grown in the fields of the Gers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Men's Christmas Caroling – Răscăiești
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On the night of Christmas Eve in the village of Răscăiești, bands of young men carry carols from house to house, sprinkling each home with holy water and wishing it abundance. The men's ceată caroling belongs to a cherished collection of Romanian Christmas traditions kept here. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mendrisio Holy Week Processions
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each Holy Week, the Ticinese town of Mendrisio fills its streets with solemn processions of Christ's Passion. Marchers carry great translucent painted transparencies that glow in the night, in a celebration of vivid pageantry that reflects the deep Catholic heritage of this corner of Italian-speaking Switzerland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Menton Lemon Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the Riviera town of Menton parades enormous floats covered in tens of thousands of lemons and oranges for its famous Lemon Festival. More than a hundred tons of citrus build the dazzling sculptures, in a celebration of the lemon orchards that once earned Menton its fame as the jewel of the French coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Meppen Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the old Drenthe village of Meppen celebrates the harvest with the Oogstfeest. Farm produce, foraging traditions, and rural festivity fill a weekend in the rustic esdorp, in a celebration that draws on the deep farming heritage of this corner of the Dutch countryside. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Metis Festival Plaine Commune
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Plaine Commune district north of Paris hosts the free Festival Métis of music and the arts. World, jazz, and classical concerts and workshops fill parks and small halls across the municipalities, in a celebration that reflects the rich multicultural life of these northern suburbs. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Meänkieli Day
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each February 27, the Tornedalen town of Haparanda celebrates Meänkieli Day. Events promote the Meänkieli language and the identity of the Tornedalians, in a celebration that honors one of Sweden's recognized national minority languages and the people who keep it alive along the Torne River. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Midnight Bonfire Tower
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each New Year's Eve, the North Beach of Scheveningen blazes as a towering pyre of wooden pallets, built sky-high through December, is set alight at the stroke of midnight. Thousands gather on the sand to watch the great bonfire roar against the winter dark, in a fiery seaside ritual to welcome the new year. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Midnight Light Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Swedish town of Vilhelmina celebrates the bright nights of the far north with the Midnight Light Festival. Music fills the long, luminous evenings under the midnight sun, in a celebration that draws on the natural wonder of the white nights of Arctic Sweden. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Midnight Sun Island Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the remote island of Træna fills with music under the midnight sun. Eclectic lineups play in an intimate coastal setting at the edge of the Nordland sea, in a celebration that grew from a grassroots arts event into one of Norway's most beloved island festivals. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Midsummer Celebration (Jan Karlsgården, Sund)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · At the Jan Karlsgården open-air museum in Sund, Midsummer is kept among century-old farmhouses with the raising of a flower-decked maypole and traditional dancing. The most important festival of the Ålandic summer, it draws families into the museum's old village world for the longest days of the year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Midsummer Celebration (Jomala Church)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · At Jomala Church on Åland, villagers come together to dress a maypole in fresh greenery and flowers and raise it on Midsummer Eve with music and dance. Each village keeps its own maypole, and this grassroots gathering marks the great Nordic celebration of summer's peak. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Midsummer Dew Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the ancient hill-fort site of Kernavė celebrates the solstice with the Rasos festival. Bonfires, flower wreaths, and old rituals fill the night among the mounds of one of Lithuania's most storied archaeological sites, in a celebration of a deeply rooted Baltic midsummer tradition. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Midsummer Eve at Karl-Erik’s Village Hall (Bjärström)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · In the Åland village of Bjärström, the local byalag keeps Midsummer at the Karl-Erik village hall, leafing the maypole on Thursday evening and raising it on Midsummer Eve. Singing, dancing, and local treats follow, in a deeply rooted celebration of the archipelago's summer. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Midsummer Eve in Åva
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · In the small island village of Åva, the village association keeps Midsummer Eve at the community hall, dressing the maypole with birch leaves and flower wreaths before raising it in the center. Families gather for music and folk singing in a custom shared across the Åland archipelago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Midsummer in Mariehamn
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each Midsummer Eve, the Åland capital of Mariehamn fills with maypoles raised across the town by local associations. Families gather at park and museum grounds to decorate the poles and dance, in a celebration of one of the islands' most cherished Nordic solstice traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Miercurea Ciuc City Days
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, on the first weekend of August, the Transylvanian city of Miercurea Ciuc celebrates its City Days. Concerts, fairs, and festivity fill the streets of Csíkszereda, in a celebration that proudly carries the Hungarian-Székely heritage of this mountain town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Milk, Bread and Honey Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the Latvian city of Jelgava celebrates the staples of country life with the Milk, Bread and Honey Festival. Hundreds of bakers, beekeepers, and cheesemakers fill the city with the tastes of the harvest, in a hearty food fair rooted in the farming traditions of the Zemgale plain. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Mimosa Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each late winter, the seaside town of Herceg Novi celebrates the blooming mimosa with a carnival of golden flowers. Masked balls, street parades, and feasts of mimosa, fish, and wine fill the days, in a celebration begun in 1969 to mark the end of winter on the bay of Kotor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mineral Water Festival "Power of Life in the Carpathian Springs"
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the spa village of Poliana celebrates the mineral springs of the Carpathians with a lively festival. Tastings of the famous local waters, traditional games, and knightly shows fill the day, in a celebration of the spa heritage that flows from this corner of Zakarpattia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Poliana, Zakarpattia mineral water culture, Ukraine
festivals.address: Poliana, Zakarpattia mineral water culture, Ukraine

festivals.name: Mitrovica Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, around St. Vitus Day, North Mitrovica gathers poets, painters, musicians, and folk ensembles from Kosovo and Serbia. Exhibitions, poetry evenings, and concerts fill the program, in a cultural celebration that has run for nearly thirty years in this divided city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mitrovica, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia
festivals.address: Mitrovica, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia

festivals.name: Mladi Levi Festival (Young Lions Festival)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the city of Ljubljana hosts Mladi Levi, an international festival of contemporary performing arts. Avant-garde theater, dance, and sound by emerging and established artists fill the late-summer days, in a celebration of creative experiment running since 1998. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ljubljana, Ljubljana theater and performing arts community, Slovenia
festivals.address: Ljubljana, Ljubljana theater and performing arts community, Slovenia

festivals.name: Mojkovac Stew Festival (Čorbijada)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July the recreation grounds of Mojkovac fill with the Čorbijada, a soup-cooking contest where teams prepare hearty local broths and serve thousands of free portions to the crowd. Soup-making is a communal art in northern Montenegro, and the festival turns it into a day of sharing. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Molde Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the fjord-side town of Molde hosts Moldejazz, one of Norway's oldest and biggest jazz festivals, running since 1961. International stars play the open-air main stage by the water while intimate club gigs fill the town, turning Molde into a jazz capital under the long northern light. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Molde, Western Norway (Møre og Romsdal), Norway
festivals.address: Molde, Western Norway (Møre og Romsdal), Norway

festivals.name: Monaghan Town Country Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the town of Monaghan fills its central Diamond with four days of country music. Irish country stars play as crowds dance in the streets, in a celebration put on by local bars and community groups that shows the town's deep affection for the genre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Monchique Medronho Festival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each late November the hill town of Monchique devotes a festival to medronho, the arbutus berry and the fiery brandy distilled from it. Emblematic of the Serra de Monchique, the fruit and its aguardente take center stage in a celebration of one of the mountain's most treasured products. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mondsee Perchten Run
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · On the evening of November 30, the town of Mondsee opens its Advent market with a Perchtenlauf, as six groups of masked Perchten, around 150 in all, parade through the dark. The run blends old Alpine pagan custom with the Christian season, filling the night with horned figures and noise. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mondsee, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria
festivals.address: Mondsee, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria

festivals.name: Monte-Carlo International Circus Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the greatest circus acts on earth gather under the big top in Monte Carlo for a world-famous competition. Founded in 1974 by a prince who loved the circus, the festival awards its coveted Golden Clown to the finest performers, in a dazzling celebration of acrobats, animals, and old-fashioned wonder. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Montegiardino Castle Festival
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · The smallest of San Marino's castelli, Montegiardino holds its yearly Festa del Castello in honor of its patron saints, Vincent Ferrer and Aloysius Gonzaga. The village festival keeps the close-knit community character of this tiny hilltop place alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Montenegro Statehood Day (Breza Partisan Cemetery Wreath-Laying, Kolašin)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 13, Montenegro marks its Statehood Day, which binds together two milestones: the recognition of its independence in 1878 and the start of its anti-fascist uprising in 1941. In Kolašin, the day opens with a wreath-laying at the Breza partisan cemetery, in a solemn remembrance of the road to freedom. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Montmartre Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the hilltop village of Montmartre celebrates the tiny vineyard that still grows grapes in the middle of Paris. The Fête des Vendanges brings parades, food stalls, and wine to the steep old streets, in a festive harvest party, held since 1934, that keeps a bit of the countryside alive in the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Moors and Christians Festival of Caudete
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the town of Caudete honors its patron, the Virgen de Gracia, with five days of Moors and Christians. Lavishly costumed companies fill the great Entrada parade and the closing Enhorabuena, in a celebration with more than four centuries behind it. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Moors and Christians of Villajoyosa (Santa Marta)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the seaside town of La Vila Joiosa reenacts a dramatic 1538 pirate raid, when, by legend, its patron Santa Marta sent a sudden flood to repel the attackers. Costumed armies parade through the streets and the Moors storm ashore from boats at dawn, in one of the Valencian coast's most thrilling festivals. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Villajoyosa, La Vila Joiosa (Alicante), Valencian Community, Spain
festivals.address: Villajoyosa, La Vila Joiosa (Alicante), Valencian Community, Spain

festivals.name: Moors and Christians Reenactment - Patron Saint Festival of St. Anthony of Padua
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each June, around the feast of San Antonio de Padua, the coastal town of Carboneras stages its Relación de Moros y Cristianos. The drama reenacts a Berber pirate raid and the Christian defense, in a celebration that recalls the town's long history facing the dangers of the Almería coast. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Moros and Christians of Orihuela (Reconquest Festival)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the city of Orihuela relives its medieval Reconquest with grand Moors and Christians parades. Costumed guilds march on foot and horseback, and theatrical skirmishes and embassies play out the struggle for the city in the streets, in a week that blends civic pride with Catholic devotion. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mostar Old Bridge Diving Competition
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, young men leap from the soaring stone arch of Mostar's Old Bridge into the cold green Neretva far below, in a diving contest going back to Ottoman times. Crowds pack the riverbanks to watch the daring jumpers, in a test of nerve and skill that is a point of deep local pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mostar, Herzegovina-Neretva Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Mostar, Herzegovina-Neretva Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Mostar Summer Fest
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Mostar fills with music for the Mostar Summer Fest, three days of concerts spanning rock, pop, and the soulful local sevdah. Held in the heart of a city long known as a crossroads of cultures, it has drawn growing crowds since it began in 2013. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mostar, Herzegovina-Neretva Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Mostar, Herzegovina-Neretva Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Muggia Summer Carnival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each mid-August the Adriatic town of Muggia extends its carnival tradition into summer with a week of costumed floats and festivity. Begun as a warm-weather counterpart to the town's older winter Carnival, it fills the seaside streets with color under the August sun. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Munich Oktoberfest
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · The world's largest folk festival fills Munich's Theresienwiese with beer tents, brass bands, carnival rides, and Bavarian dress. What began as an 1810 royal wedding celebration is now a citywide ritual built around opening parades, local breweries, and the first keg tap. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Munster Fleadh
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the province of Munster celebrates traditional Irish music with the Fleadh Cheoil na Mumhan. Competitions and concerts move among the Munster counties year by year, in a celebration of Gaelic culture and the living craft of trad music, held under Comhaltas Ceoltóirí Éireann. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Murcia Holy Week
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Murcia marks Holy Week in its own baroque style, where robed penitents in great padded tunics hand out sweets to the children lining the route. Brotherhoods carry magnificent religious sculptures through the streets, in a celebration that mixes deep devotion with a warm, generous spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Murcia Moors and Christians Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the city of Murcia stages a week-long pageant of its medieval past, when Moorish and Christian armies took and retook the city. Costumed parades and reenactments lead to the symbolic handing-over of the city keys, in a festival that recalls Murcia's founding and its long, layered history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Murnau Saint Leonhard's Ride
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November 6, the Bavarian town of Murnau honors Saint Leonhard, protector of livestock, with a horse pilgrimage. Costumed locals and musicians form a procession of decorated horses and carriages through the town, in a celebration of an old Catholic devotion of the alpine countryside. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Murnau am Staffelsee, Upper Bavaria, Germany
festivals.address: Murnau am Staffelsee, Upper Bavaria, Germany

festivals.name: Music in the Forest (Mátra Artistic Days)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the villages of the Mátra hills fill with music for the Mátra Artistic Days. Outdoor classical and folk concerts unfold in churches and forest clearings across the countryside, in a celebration begun decades ago that draws on the Palóc folk culture of this northern Hungarian range. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Music Without Borders International Music Camp, Competition and Art Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Balassagyarmat gathers young musicians from many lands for ten days of study under master teachers. Voice, strings, and piano fill the rooms before free public concerts close the camp, in a celebration begun in 1986 to nurture talented young players. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Music, Costume, and Food Fest
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each late July, the town of Darda revives its Ottoman past with the Tarda Festival. Historical reenactments, period costume shows, music, crafts, and traditional food fill two days, in a celebration of the Ottoman-era folklore and architecture of this corner of Baranja. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Musica Sacra Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the city of Maastricht devotes a festival to sacred music. Concerts, film, and performance gather church choirs, oratories, and young musicians around spiritual themes, in a celebration rooted in the cathedral tradition that has long shaped the Catholic culture of Limburg. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Maastricht, Maastricht (Limburg religious music community), Netherlands
festivals.address: Maastricht, Maastricht (Limburg religious music community), Netherlands

festivals.name: Musicalarue Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the pine-forest village of Luxey in the Landes fills with music and street arts for Musicalarue. Three days of live performance bring a wide range of music to the rural setting, in a celebration founded in 1990 by a drummer and local artists that grew from the region's bold creative spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Mussel Festival Middelburg
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year the city center of Middelburg turns into an open-air market and tasting for a festive weekend devoted to Zeeland mussels. The famous local shellfish are served cooked every way alongside live music, celebrating the dish that the province is known for. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: My Kolašin Sings (Choirs & Kolo Dance Review)
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the town of Zubin Potok celebrates Serb folk culture with the review known as My Kolašin Sings. A procession from St. Petka Church to the cultural center opens the day, where choirs, dancers, and bands gather to dance the kolo, in a celebration that has become a symbol of keeping the tradition alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Zubin Potok, Kosovo Serb Region (Zubin Potok Municipality), Kosovo
festivals.address: Zubin Potok, Kosovo Serb Region (Zubin Potok Municipality), Kosovo

festivals.name: Mystery Play of Elche
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the basilica of Elche fills for the Misteri d'Elx, a sacred musical drama performed here since the Middle Ages. Sung in old Valencian and Latin, it tells of the death and crowning of the Virgin Mary, with angels lowered from the great dome on ropes, in a living piece of medieval theatre honored by UNESCO. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Málaga Fair (Feria de Agosto)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the city of Málaga throws its great summer fair, eight days of flamenco, folk music, and fireworks by day and night. Horse-drawn carriages fill the old center while the fairground roars till dawn, in a celebration rooted in the city's history that has become the high point of the Andalusian summer. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Mörbisch Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the lakeside town of Mörbisch am See celebrates its grape harvest with the Weinlesefest. Vine-decked wagons roll through the streets in a traditional procession, with wine tastings at the huts and local music, in a celebration of the long winegrowing life of the shore of the Neusiedler See. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Mörbisch am See, Northern Burgenland (Neusiedlersee), Austria
festivals.address: Mörbisch am See, Northern Burgenland (Neusiedlersee), Austria

festivals.name: Münster City Marksmen's Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the city of Münster gathers its shooting clubs and parish marksmen's brotherhoods for the Stadtschützenfest. The festivities culminate in the crowning of the city marksmen's king, in a celebration that carries the deep-rooted Schützenfest tradition of the Münsterland. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Mărțișor Fair (Botoșani)
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each spring, the city of Botoșani holds a fair for the Mărțișor, the little red-and-white talisman of the Romanian spring. Artisans display handmade mărțișoare and folk crafts, in a celebration of an ancient custom that marks the rebirth of nature and the wish for health as winter gives way. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nadur Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each carnival, the Gozo town of Nadur stages the island's most famous festivities, known for their bold costumes and street parties. An organized parade on Carnival Monday gives way to spontaneous masked gatherings, in a celebration deeply rooted in the town's love of humor and satire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nafpaktos Carnival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each late February, the lakeside town of Nafpaktos celebrates carnival with parades and masquerade. Treasure hunts, children's events, masked dances, and street performances fill the days, in a family-friendly celebration grown from the Greek Orthodox Apokries tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nantes Carnival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each spring, the city of Nantes throws a colorful street carnival of floats, giant papier-mâché heads, and confetti battles. Rooted in medieval revelry and kept alive by local float-builders, it fills the streets with music and crowds, in one of the liveliest celebrations of the western French year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Napoleonic Days
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the Corsican town of Corte celebrates the island's ties to Napoleon Bonaparte with the free Journées Napoléoniennes. Reenactments, talks, and festivity recall the emperor's Corsican roots, in a celebration staged by the city to honor a figure woven into the island's own story. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Narberth Food Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the Welsh town of Narberth fills its center with one of Wales's longest-running food festivals. Dozens of stalls offer artisan cheeses, ciders, meats, and baked goods alongside cooking demonstrations and live music, in a celebration of rural Welsh food begun in the early 2000s. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Barn Theatre Festival "Under Bitė's Wing"
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the village of Smilgiai keeps alive the old 'barn theater' of Aukštaitija with a national folk theater festival. Folk plays, puppet shows, and children's camps fill an ethnographic homestead, in a celebration named for the writer and theater pioneer Gabrielė Petkevičaitė-Bitė, born nearby. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: National Children's Song Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Shkodër gathers young singers for nights of song before the Migjeni Theatre. Children perform original works in an open-air concert series as parents and neighbors fill the square, in a beloved celebration of young Albanian talent held every year since 1962. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Eisteddfod of Wales
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, a different Welsh town hosts the National Eisteddfod, the largest celebration of the Welsh language anywhere, with a week of singing, poetry, and competition. Its roots reach back to the bardic gatherings of the Middle Ages, and the crowning of the year's best poet is still its proudest moment. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Festival "Romanian Căluș"
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the city of Slatina becomes a great stage for the Romanian Căluș, a national festival of the spirited căluș dance. Hundreds of dancers and musicians, many of them children and youth ensembles, perform the leaping ritual dance, in a celebration that lifts this Oltenian tradition onto a national stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Festival of Watermelon Preserve
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the village of Smilets celebrates the old craft of watermelon preserve, the dineni madzhun. Folk singing, dancing, and workshops in preserve-making and watermelon-lantern crafting fill the day, in a celebration that revives a Bulgarian custom of putting food by for the winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Festival of Winter Traditions “Start the Plow, Fine Fellows!”
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each year between Christmas and New Year, the Bucovina town of Vatra Dornei fills with masked dancers and costumed carolers for its winter customs festival. Bands of revelers in bear and goat costumes parade through the snowy streets, keeping alive the old Romanian New Year rituals of this mountain region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Folk Bands & Singers Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the riverside town of Kazimierz Dolny hosts one of Poland's most respected folk music festivals. Rural singing groups and instrumental bands compete and perform the old village songs, in a celebration now past its fifty-ninth year that works to keep authentic peasant music alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kazimierz Dolny, Polish folk tradition (Kazimierz Dolny), Poland
festivals.address: Kazimierz Dolny, Polish folk tradition (Kazimierz Dolny), Poland

festivals.name: National Folklore Festival of the Cartagena Region
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, the La Palma district of Cartagena hosts a week-long national folklore festival. Daily concerts of traditional music and dance join workshops, talks, and exhibitions, in a celebration that shows how deeply this community values its customs and heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Horse Fair (Golegã Fair)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November, the town of Golegã hosts Portugal's premier equestrian fair, the Feira Nacional do Cavalo. Competitions, parades, and exhibitions celebrate the Lusitano horse and Ribatejo riding culture, in a celebration that has earned Golegã its name as the country's horse capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: National Masquerade Festival "Dzhamäla"
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the town of Kyustendil hosts the national masquerade festival known as Dzhamäla. Troupes of masked kukeri stamp and ring their bells to chase away evil, in a young celebration whose name means "great fun" and draws on the old mask games of the Bulgarian winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria
festivals.address: Kyustendil, Western Bulgaria (Shopluk region) - Kyustendil, Bulgaria

festivals.name: Nationwide Shepherd’s Celebration - Ovenálie
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Each year, the highland village of Východná hosts Ovenálie, a nationwide celebration of the shepherd's life. Sheep-cheese making, folk music, and contests in cheese-thread pulling and lamb-stew cooking fill the day, in a celebration of the Walachian pastoral heritage of the central Slovak mountains. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Nativity of Mary Parish Feast
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the village of Negova keeps its patronal feast, the žegnanje, on the Nativity of the Virgin Mary. A solemn Mass gathers villagers and those who grew up here, even from abroad, for communal festivities, in a celebration of the deep-rooted church-dedication tradition of rural Slovenia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nava Cider Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Asturian town of Nava devotes four days to the region's beloved cider. Pouring contests, a sidra market, concerts, and folk dance fill the streets, with small granaries awarded as trophies, in a celebration of Asturian cider honored as a fiesta of national tourist interest. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Neighborhood Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · In the Nikiboko district of Bonaire, the Bario Festival celebrates local community and culture with traditional Papiamentu bari music and dances, food stalls, and local bands. Begun in 2022, it builds on the island's old custom of communal bari gatherings held after the harvest. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Neskaupstaður Town Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each high summer the Eastfjords town of Neskaupstaður throws Neistaflug, a family town festival held over the long Verslunarmannahelgin weekend. Run by local community groups, it has grown into a modern tradition, drawing the whole town and its returning sons and daughters home for the holiday. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Neuhausen Traditional Witches' Dance (Carnival)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Fat Thursday, the town of Neuhausen on the Fildern stages its Hexentanz, the witches' dance. At the stroke of 19:11, costumed witches dance around a bonfire on the castle square, in a celebration that forms a fiery highlight of the local Swabian-Alemannic carnival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Neuhausen on the Fildern, Baden-Württemberg, Germany
festivals.address: Neuhausen on the Fildern, Baden-Württemberg, Germany

festivals.name: Neuneu Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each autumn, a corner of the Bois de Boulogne fills with the lights and music of the Fête à Neuneu, one of Paris's oldest funfairs. Roller coasters, carousels, and games spread across the park, in a traveling carnival that has brought affordable fun to Parisians since the 19th century. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Neustift Church Fair & Heurigen Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August the old winegrowing suburb of Neustift am Walde in Vienna's 19th district holds its multi-day Kirtag, a folk festival rooted in the heritage of its vineyards and Heurigen taverns. Wine, music, and the easy pleasures of the wine-tavern fill the village streets at the edge of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Neuwerk Target Shooting Guild Main Guild Day
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year the Neuwerk Target Shooting Guild in Rendsburg holds its main guild day, with target contests and ceremony. Formed in the 16th century, the guild is the town's oldest free citizens' association, and its guild day carries a tradition that has lasted for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nevesinje Olympics
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August, the meadow of Bratački Lug near Nevesinje hosts the Nevesinjska olimpijada, a festival of sport and culture. Said to be older than the modern Olympics, with more than 140 years behind it, the games grew from the post-harvest gatherings of Herzegovinian horsemen. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Nevesinje, East Herzegovina (Nevesinje), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Nevesinje, East Herzegovina (Nevesinje), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: New Bread Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the town of Pecica blesses its first wheat with the New Bread Festival. A religious ceremony consecrates loaves baked from the new harvest, followed by a week of concerts, folk dance, and choir performances, in a celebration of an old Christian harvest tradition of the Banat. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: New Year Celebration on the Vieux-Port
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each New Year, the city of Marseille celebrates on the Vieux-Port with games, music, and a giant Galette des Rois. Crowds gather around the harbor to share the great kings' cake, in a celebration that carries the French Epiphany tradition into a civic festival by the sea. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: New Year Customs and Traditions Festival (Masked Ritual Parade, Târgu Neamț)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 2, the town of Târgu Neamț parades the winter customs of Moldavia through its streets. Costumed groups of ploughmen, goat and bear dancers, and căiuți riders fill the city, in a celebration that revives the ancient Romanian new-year datini of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: New Year's Day Masquerade
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each New Year's Day, the island of Bonaire comes alive with Maskarada, a masquerade unique to Kralendijk. Silent, anonymous masked groups perform humorous mime-dance skits at the governor's residence and through the neighborhoods, in a celebration of Spanish-influenced roots deeply woven into Bonairean culture. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: New Year's Eve Celebration on the Champs-Élysées
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each December 31, the grand Avenue des Champs-Élysées fills with a vast crowd to ring in the New Year in the heart of Paris. As midnight strikes, fireworks and a glowing light show wrap the Arc de Triomphe, in a free, open-air celebration on one of the world's most famous avenues. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: New Year's Eve Trychling
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On New Year's Eve in the lakeside village of Iseltwald, local bell-ringers parade through the streets sounding great cowbells to bid the old year farewell. The Alpine custom of Trychlen reaches back centuries, its booming bells believed to chase away evil spirits and the gloom of midwinter. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: New Year’s Bell Parade in Habkern
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On New Year's Eve in the mountain village of Habkern, the local Trychler group parades through the square and streets ringing heavy cowbells to see in the new year. Trychlen is a centuries-old Alpine custom, the deep clang of the bells meant to drive off evil spirits and the dark of winter. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: New Year’s Eve in Pamplona
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each New Year's Eve, the Old Town of Pamplona turns into a great costume party known as the Nochevieja. Crowds in inventive disguises fill the streets to ring in the year, in a celebration grown from a grassroots love of communal festivity and creativity. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Nice Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the city of Nice throws France's largest winter carnival, two weeks of giant-headed float parades, marching bands, and flower battles along the seafront. With a fresh theme each year and roots reaching back to medieval times, it fills the Riviera with color in the depths of winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Night of Museums
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each year, the city of Sumy throws open its museums for a single free night of culture. Dozens of exhibitions, performances, and interactive projects let visitors roam the city's art and history after dark, in a celebration that is the local face of the worldwide Night of Museums. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sumy, Sumy Oblast, Ukrainian community, Ukraine
festivals.address: Sumy, Sumy Oblast, Ukrainian community, Ukraine

festivals.name: Night of the City Festival (Sumy)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · On one night each September, the Ukrainian city of Sumy turns its streets into open-air stages for its City Day. Concerts, art exhibitions, street performances, and even medieval reenactments fill the dark with light and music, in a burst of creativity celebrating the city's birthday. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Night of the Full Moon
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer Zadar's waterfront Riva fills with the Night of the Full Moon, a celebration of the city's maritime culture, food, and folk traditions under the moonlit Adriatic. It carries the cherished hour of the Zadar sunset on into the night, when the harbor glows by candle and moonlight. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Night of the Market
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · On the night of December 23, the people of Funchal pour into the Mercado dos Lavradores for the Noite do Mercado, a joyous all-night shop for the Christmas table. The market overflows with fruit, flowers, and song as families stock up and celebrate together, in a Madeiran tradition going back over a century. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Night of the Tarantula
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Salento town of Melpignano hosts La Notte della Taranta, a festival devoted to the pizzica, the fast spinning folk dance once believed to cure a spider's bite. It builds to a huge final concert where hundreds of thousands dance under the stars, in Italy's great celebration of southern folk music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Nin Šokol Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the town of Nin celebrates its prized dried pork, the šokol, with a gastronomic festival. A winter's careful curing culminates in a summer tasting and contest to crown the best šokolar, in a celebration of a salted delicacy honored as intangible cultural heritage of the area. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Njegoš Days (Cetinje)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, the old royal capital of Cetinje honors Njegoš, the poet and prince who shaped Montenegro's language and sense of itself. The festival fills two days with poetry readings, lectures, and tributes to a writer whose verses Montenegrins still know by heart. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Njegoš Days (Nikšić)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each year, around his birthday, the city of Nikšić honors the poet-prince Petar II Petrović Njegoš with Njegoš Days. Poetry readings, lectures, and book launches explore his works, in a literary and spiritual celebration tied to a region long bound up with Montenegrin resistance. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Njegoš Days (Podgorica)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Montenegro's tribute to its great poet-prince Njegoš reaches the capital each November, with readings and talks on his work and his place in the nation's story. A newer companion to the festival in Cetinje, it keeps Njegoš's words alive in Podgorica through poetry and conversation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Noorderzon Performing Arts Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the city of Groningen hands its central park over to Noorderzon, ten days of performing arts under the open sky. International theatre, dance, and music mix with food stalls and a relaxed festival mood among the trees, in one of the Netherlands' leading celebrations of the cutting edge of performance. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: NORDEN - The Nordic Arts Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, the king's meadows of Schleswig host NORDEN, a festival of Northern European culture. Music, literature, and street theatre spread across three weekends and twelve days, in a celebration begun in 2016 that honors the region's deep ties to its Nordic neighbors. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Schleswig, Schleswig (German-Danish border region, Northern European culture), Germany
festivals.address: Schleswig, Schleswig (German-Danish border region, Northern European culture), Germany

festivals.name: Nordic Film Days Lübeck
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each year, the city of Lübeck screens the cinema of the north at the Nordic Film Days. Features, documentaries, and shorts from Scandinavia and the Baltic fill six days, often with the directors present, in a celebration begun in the early 1960s to build cultural exchange across the north. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lübeck, Lübeck, Schleswig-Holstein (Northern European film culture), Germany
festivals.address: Lübeck, Lübeck, Schleswig-Holstein (Northern European film culture), Germany

festivals.name: Nordland Music Festival Week
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August, the northern city of Bodø fills ten days with music for the Nordland Festival Week. Born as a classical celebration, it now reaches across many genres while keeping its regional spirit, in a festival that has become a high point of the Arctic summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: North Africa Pride
festivals.description: Late May 2026 · Each year, the city of Melilla celebrates diversity with North Africa Pride. Colorful parades, concerts, drag shows, and community events fill the center by day and night, in a celebration of LGBT life and rights that mirrors the many-sided makeup of this North African Spanish city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Melilla, LGBT community of Melilla/North Africa, Spain
festivals.address: Melilla, LGBT community of Melilla/North Africa, Spain

festivals.name: North City Jazz & Blues Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the towns of North Mitrovica and Zvečan host the North City Jazz & Blues Festival.
