Acclaimed jazz, blues, and fusion artists play several days of concerts and workshops, in a celebration begun in 2003 that draws on a love of jazz reaching back to Yugoslav times. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: North Mitrovica, Kosovska Mitrovica Serb Community, Kosovo
festivals.address: North Mitrovica, Kosovska Mitrovica Serb Community, Kosovo

festivals.name: Northern Lights and Language Festival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each winter, the Torne Valley town of Pajala celebrates the return of light and its border languages. Meänkieli storytelling, bilingual performances, and traditional music gather under the February skies, in a celebration of the mixed Swedish-Finnish culture of this northern frontier. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Northern Lights Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March, the Arctic town of Alta celebrates the aurora with the Northern Lights Festival. Aurora safaris, concerts, and lectures on the night sky fill the dark season, in a celebration set in the heart of the aurora zone that honors the cultural meaning of the polar lights. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Northern Norway Festival (Arctic Arts Festival)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the northern town of Harstad hosts the Arctic Arts Festival, eight days of music, theatre, and dance held under the midnight sun. Founded in 1965, it draws on the light and landscape of the far north to celebrate the creativity of the Arctic, in northern Norway's flagship cultural event. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Northmavine Up Helly Aa
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter, the Northmavine peninsula of Shetland stages its own Up Helly Aa fire festival. A torchlit procession led by a Viking-clad Guizer Jarl ends with the burning of a handcrafted longship, in a celebration of Shetland's Norse heritage that marks the turning of the dark season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Norwegian Arctic Student Festival (Tromsø Student Arts & Debates)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Arctic city of Tromsø fills with the energy of its students for a festival of knowledge and youth culture. Lectures, debates, workshops, music, and theater are run by and for the university, in a celebration that links academic life to the wider culture of the far north. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Norwick Up Helly Aa Fire Festival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each February, the island of Unst stages the Norwick Up Helly Aa, a community fire festival. Costumed Viking guizers parade by torchlight before the burning of a longship, in a purely local celebration begun in 1985 that draws on Shetland's Norse and Yule traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Norð Festivalurin
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the northernmost Faroese town of Viðareiði fills a full day with music for Norð Festivalurin. Local bands and visiting acts play from morning to late night, with camping and a children's area, in a young celebration begun in 2023 to bring life to the remote Norðoyar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Viðareiði, Faroe Islands (Norðoyar region), Faroe Islands
festivals.address: Viðareiði, Faroe Islands (Norðoyar region), Faroe Islands

festivals.name: Nova Bila Cultural Days
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn the village of Nova Bila near Travnik holds Dani Nove Bile, a festival of folk ensembles and singers performing sevdalinka and other traditional songs. Run by the local cultural society, it sets out to preserve the heritage of central Bosnia and its people. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Travnik, Bosniaks of Central Bosnia (Lašva Valley), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Travnik, Bosniaks of Central Bosnia (Lašva Valley), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Novovolynsk Kupala Lights Ethno-Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Launched in 2025 on the city lake of Novovolynsk, this Kupala Lights ethno-festival revives the rites of Ivana Kupala with bonfire leaping, flower-wreath weaving, and folk singing. A Slavic midsummer holiday of pagan and Christian roots, it returns to the city in authentic village style. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oberstadt Funfair
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Oberstadt quarter of Eupen fills with rides, stalls, and games for the Nikolaus-Kirmes. Carousels and classic funfair attractions draw families for several days, in a celebration that has long been a staple of summer in this German-speaking corner of Belgium. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Eupen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium
festivals.address: Eupen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium

festivals.name: OBWALD Folk Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the forest clearing of Gsang near Giswil fills with folk music for OBWALD. Swiss traditional ensembles share the stage with international guests beneath the stars, in a celebration that honors Swiss folk culture while opening it to the wider world. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Occitan and Catalan Cultural Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, around the summer solstice and the fires of Saint-Jean, Occitanie celebrates its languages with Total Festum. Dozens of events of dance, music, games, and readings honor Occitan and Catalan culture, in a celebration that revives the old solstice rituals and the region's living tongues. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: October in October Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each late October, the city of Zhytomyr gathers around its old Zhovten cinema for ten days of culture. Film screenings, performances, and talks on identity fill the program, in a celebration that has turned the revival of a historic movie theater into one of the city's key arts events. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oda Dibrane Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn the town of Peshkopi hosts the Oda Dibrane folklore festival, drawing rural ensembles from Albania, North Macedonia, and Kosovo to share the costumes, dances, and songs of the Dibra region. Its name evokes the oda, the room where the music and stories of Dibra have always been kept. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Odesa City Day
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 2, the Black Sea city of Odesa celebrates its founding in 1794 with City Day. A ceremonial flag-raising leads into concerts and cultural performances across the famously cosmopolitan port, in a day of pride for a city long known for its wit, its music, and its seaside charm. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oerol Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Frisian island of Terschelling becomes a stage for Oerol, a ten-day festival of site-specific performance. Theatre, music, and art installations spring up across the dunes, beaches, and forests, inviting audiences to wander the island and discover the landscape itself transformed into art. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Oeteldonk Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the city of 's-Hertogenbosch renames itself Oeteldonk and dresses up as a make-believe farming village, with everyone in the smock-and-scarf of country folk. For three days the streets fill with this gleeful playacting, in one of the most beloved and distinctive carnivals of the Dutch south. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: OK Fest (Sutjeska Adventure & Music Festival)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, Sutjeska National Park hosts OK Fest, where adventure meets all-day music. Rafting the Tara canyon and hiking the Maglić and Zelengora trails give way to concerts on open-air stages, in a celebration of the wild beauty and outdoor spirit of this corner of the country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Foča, Foča (Sutjeska NP), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Foča, Foča (Sutjeska NP), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Okra (Ladies' Fingers) Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August the village of Armenioi near Kyparissia celebrates its okra harvest with a festival that goes back decades, centered on a great communal feast. Okra growing was long a mainstay of the local fields, and the festival keeps that tradition and its village table alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Olavsfestdagene (St. Olav’s Day Festival, Trondheim)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the city of Trondheim celebrates Olavsfestdagene around St. Olav's Day, honoring the king who became Norway's patron saint after falling in battle in 1030. Concerts, drama, and historical reenactments fill the city around its great cathedral, in a festival steeped in the country's medieval and Christian roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Old Limburg Marksmen Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, around 150 marksmen's guilds from both Dutch and Belgian Limburg gather for the Oud Limburgs Schuttersfeest, the great festival of the schutterijen. Colorful parades, shooting contests, and Mass fill the day, in a celebration of medieval guild traditions that still bind the towns of the region together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Old Town Festivities
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · In the historic heart of Žilina, this community festival fills the Old Town square with an artisans' market, folk music, and cultural programs. It reconnects the city's people with their medieval past, set among the streets and squares that hold that long history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Old Town Nights
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · At the end of August, Ljubljana turns its old city center into a vast open-air venue for free concerts and cultural events. Nights in Ljubljana Old Town lets the city's historic squares and riverside architecture become the stage, filling warm summer evenings with music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Old Urban Song Festival 'Mara Vrachanka'
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year, the town of Vratsa hosts a competition of Bulgaria's old urban songs, the stara gradska pesen. Singers and ensembles perform the beloved old-town melodies, in a celebration named for a famed local singer that keeps this tender town genre alive across the country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Omiš Dalmatian Klapa Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Dalmatian town of Omiš fills with the rich, layered harmonies of klapa, the region's traditional a cappella singing. Male, female, and mixed groups compete and perform in the town squares, in a festival that has helped keep this UNESCO-honored style of song alive for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ommegang of Brussels (Charles V Pageant)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Grand-Place of Brussels fills with some 1,400 performers in Renaissance dress, plus horses and towering giants, for the Ommegang. The grand pageant reenacts Emperor Charles V's entry into the city in 1549, reviving a procession with medieval roots in one of Europe's most magnificent squares. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Onion Fair
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, the village of Asuaju de Sus celebrates its onion harvest with the Târgul Cepelor. Farmers sell onions in braids and wreaths, visitors sample local foods, and contests fill the day, in a celebration of the distinctive onions long grown in the forested Țara Codrului. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Asuaju de Sus, Ţara Codrului (Maramureş), Romania
festivals.address: Asuaju de Sus, Ţara Codrului (Maramureş), Romania

festivals.name: Onstwedde Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each fourth Saturday of August, the village of Onstwedde recreates the farming life of a century ago for its harvest festival. Volunteers in historic costume work the old crafts and gather the harvest, in a celebration that keeps the early-20th-century rural world of Groningen alive for a day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oostermoer Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Every five years, one of the historic Oostermoer villages of Drenthe hosts the great Oostermoerfeest. A rural-sports vijfkamp, a parade, and days of festivity grow from a fair first held in 1868, in a celebration that has bound the region's farmers together for more than 150 years. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Open Varenyky Festival "Varenyky-Shostka-Fest"
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each year, the town of Shostka gathers teams to cook the Ukrainian dumpling for the Open Varenyky Festival. Cooks compete with creative varenyky recipes as the town shares in the feast, in a celebration begun in 2016 that brings people together around a much-loved dish. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Open-Air Field Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July the Parc du Gué-de-Maulny in Le Mans fills with the Plein Champ festival of street art, as murals, graffiti, and live performances take over the green. A grassroots affair, it sets a younger urban-arts spirit against the racing heritage the city is better known for. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Opera vs Pop Under The Stars
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the restored courtyard of the Gozo Ministry in Victoria hosts an open-air concert that pits opera against pop. The Gozo Youth Orchestra and guest artists perform both under the stars, in a celebration that reflects the island's strong love of music and musical education. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Opera Week Kutná Hora
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the silver-mining town of Kutná Hora devotes a week to opera and vocal music. Concerts fill the historic town through Opera Week, in a celebration founded in 1998 that has become one of the town's major cultural events. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kutná Hora, Central Bohemia (Kutná Hora UNESCO site), Czech Republic
festivals.address: Kutná Hora, Central Bohemia (Kutná Hora UNESCO site), Czech Republic

festivals.name: OperaFest Tulchyn
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the grand Potocki Palace in the Ukrainian town of Tulchyn becomes the backdrop for OperaFest, an open-air festival of opera and ballet. Beneath the Baroque facade, world-class productions play out under the night sky, bringing grand opera to the Podillia countryside since 2017. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oplenac Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the wine town of Topola celebrates the grape harvest at Oplenac. Three days of parades, grape stomping, and tastings honor the local winemaking, in a celebration set in the historic seat of the Serbian royal Karadjordjević dynasty, famed for its vineyards. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oradea FestiFall
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the Romanian city of Oradea celebrates its birthday with FestiFall, its largest festival of the year. Concerts, street performers, and family events fill the elegant Art Nouveau center, in a three-day party that turns the city's anniversary into a season-closing celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oradea Medieval Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the historic district of Oradea comes alive with knights and craft for its Medieval Festival. Several days of reenactment fill the streets of a city that was once an important medieval trading center, in a celebration of the heritage written into Oradea's old quarter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ordino Rosary Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each mid-July, the village of Ordino keeps the feast of Our Lady of the Rosary with El Roser. Esbart folk dances with sheaves of grain, the palos dance, and the giving out of flowers fill the day, in a celebration that ties a church holiday to the old Catalan rhythm of the harvest. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Orthodox Christmas
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 7, the Orthodox Serbs of Banja Luka keep Christmas by the old Julian calendar. On Christmas Eve families light the Badnjak, the yule log, and gather for the midnight liturgy, in a celebration whose warm rituals carry the faith of the region through the heart of winter. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Banja Luka, Republika Srpska, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Banja Luka, Republika Srpska, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Ortigueira International Celtic World Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Galician coastal town of Ortigueira fills with the skirl of bagpipes for one of the world's great Celtic music festivals. Thousands camp on the beaches for four days of concerts by Galician and international folk artists, celebrating the deep Celtic roots this green corner of Spain shares with Ireland and Brittany. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Orujo Festival of Potes
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November, the mountain town of Potes celebrates its grape spirit, the orujo, with a festival in the Liébana valley. Public distillation and tastings of the artisanal liquor draw thousands to the cobbled streets, in a celebration begun in 1984 in the heart of Cantabria's wine country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Oslo Culture Night (Late-Night Museums & Free Performances)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the city of Oslo throws open hundreds of venues for a single late night of culture. Museums, theaters, and galleries fill with free performances and art, in a celebration begun in the mid-2000s to draw the city's people closer to its cultural life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ostrova Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the village of Ostrova in Setomaa hosts a small festival of folk music and culture. Over two days, singers, choirs, and dancers from Setomaa and beyond perform in a woodland setting, in a celebration of Seto and regional traditions held among the trees of the village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Other Sounds Festival (East of Culture, Lublin)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Lublin fills its venues with experimental and world music for the Other Sounds Festival. New sounds, performances, and multimedia art spread through the historic Old Town, in a celebration that underlines Lublin's role as a meeting point of cultures. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Otopeni Folkloric Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the town of Otopeni celebrates Romanian folk music and dance with its folkloric festival. Professional ensembles and local troupes perform authentic folklore, in a celebration now two decades strong that the town holds up as a showcase of Romania's living traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ould Lammas Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the town of Ballycastle hosts the Ould Lammas Fair over two days of harvest festivity. Street trading, horse markets, artisan stalls, music, and dance fill the town, in a celebration whose roots reach back to the ancient Gaelic harvest feast of Lammas. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ballycastle, County Antrim, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Ballycastle, County Antrim, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Our Lady of the Conception Maritime Procession (Ferragudo)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August the Algarve fishing village of Ferragudo honors its patron, Nossa Senhora da Conceição, with a grand maritime procession, a blessing of the fishermen at sea, and evening street dances. The festa carries the village's deep bond with the sea into its most important days of the year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Our Lady’s Island Pilgrimage
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, around the Feast of the Assumption, pilgrims gather at Our Lady's Island in County Wexford. The ancient Marian site fills with devotion and prayer in mid-August, in a celebration that has drawn the faithful to this place of pilgrimage in southeast Ireland for centuries. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Outdoor Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the Funar hills near Elbasan come alive with Funar Fest, a celebration of the region's nature, farming, and culture. Concerts by popular Albanian singers join a fair of local produce, in a festival created to draw visitors to the rural beauty and heritage of this corner of central Albania. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Outhide Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the wooded park of Kraljevica near Zaječar hosts the Outhide Festival of arts and music. Concerts and performances spread across several days in the open air, in a celebration that draws on the rich music and arts heritage of a town long known for its festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Paddy Killoran Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the town of Ballymote honors the fiddler Paddy Killoran with a festival of traditional Irish music. Concerts, pub sessions, workshops, and a memorial Mass for local musicians fill the days, in a celebration of the Sligo-born fiddler who carried the tradition to New York. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pag ART Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the island town of Pag turns to classical music and theater for the Pag ART Festival. Chamber music, recitals, and drama unfold in historic settings like the old cathedral and town squares, in a celebration that offers a quieter, deeper counterpoint to the island's party fame. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pag Sheep and Cheese Exhibition
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the town of Novalja celebrates the island's famous sheep and cheese with an agricultural exhibition. Prize Pag sheep, tastings of the renowned Paški sir, and the work of local shepherds fill the day, in a celebration of a pastoral tradition at the heart of the island's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Novalja, Croatian (Pag island / Pag community), Croatia
festivals.address: Novalja, Croatian (Pag island / Pag community), Croatia

festivals.name: Paide Midsummer Fire
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the eve of Jaanipäev in late June, the town of Paide gathers around a great bonfire for an evening of folk music and live performance. The Jaanituli belongs to Estonia's nationwide midsummer traditions, whose roots run back to pre-Christian times, marking the longest days of the year. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Paimpol Sea Shanty Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the port of Paimpol fills with sea shanties for a festival of maritime music. Breton and international folk artists sing the songs of sailors and fishermen amid concerts and workshops, in a celebration of the long shipbuilding and seafaring heritage of this Breton harbor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pajala Market
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer just after Midsummer, the far-northern Swedish town of Pajala holds one of Norrbotten's largest markets, a fair with roots in the 1700s. Hundreds of stalls fill the square and streets for three days, in a lively gathering that keeps alive the trading traditions and Tornedalian culture of the Torne Valley. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Pakuonis Region Summer Festival "Along the Thyme-Scented Trail…"
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August the village of Pakuonis holds its summer festival around Žolinė, the feast of the Assumption, blending the church holiday with community celebration. Its name evokes a thyme-scented trail, and the day weaves a blessing and old village traditions into the height of summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Palio di Asti
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Piedmont town of Asti holds one of Italy's oldest horse races, a bareback Palio run since at least 1275 in honor of its patron, Saint Secundus. A grand medieval parade in rich costume fills the town before the riders thunder around the central piazza for the coveted banner. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Palio di Parma
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the city of Parma revives a medieval race called the Corsa dello Scarlatto. Costumed pageantry and competition reenact the Scarlet Race once run to mark a noble wedding in 1313, in a celebration that recalls an event tied to the old Correggio family of Parma. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Palio di San Paolino
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July the Tuscan city of Lucca honors its patron, St. Paulinus, with the Palio di San Paolino, a historic crossbow competition. Tied deeply to civic identity, the contest recalls the miracle of the saint and fills the city with the pageantry of its medieval past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Palio di Siena
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Twice each summer, the city of Siena packs its great shell-shaped Piazza del Campo for the Palio, a bareback horse race among the city's historic neighborhoods. Ten riders tear around the square in barely ninety seconds, but the rivalry, pageantry, and passion behind it run through every day of Sienese life. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Palio of Saint James
festivals.description: Late July 2027 · Each summer the Tuscan town of Gallicano honors St. James with the Palio di San Jacopo, as its three districts, Borgo Antico, Bufali, and Monticello, compete with elaborate allegorical floats and choreographed displays. The patronal festival grows from a friendly rivalry among the town's old quarters. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Palma Saint John’s Eve
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, the Mallorcan city of Palma celebrates the Nit de Sant Joan, the great midsummer night of fire. Bonfires blaze along the beaches and fireworks light the sky as crowds gather to leap the flames and swim at midnight, in an ancient solstice celebration the island calls the Night of Fire. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Palotás Fish Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · First held in 2025, the Palotás Fish Festival celebrates the village's traditional fish soup with a halászlé cooking contest, youth sports, and community events. Set near the Ipoly River, where freshwater fishing has long been part of local life, it is a young festival rooted in old waters. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Panadžur Gathering
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the village of Jalovik Izvor gathers for the Panadžur, a fair of the old songs and dances of the Stara Planina. Singers and players keep the original village folk music alive, in a celebration that holds fast to the izvorna sound at the foot of the mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Knjazevac, Eastern Serbia (Stara Mountain, Knjaževac), Serbia
festivals.address: Knjazevac, Eastern Serbia (Stara Mountain, Knjaževac), Serbia

festivals.name: Panche Peshev (International Folklore Festival)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the city of Kumanovo hosts the Panche Peshev International Folklore Festival. Folk ensembles from Macedonia and abroad bring traditional dance and music to the stage, in a celebration named for a local hero that opens the region to cultural exchange. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Panelo Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each last Sunday of January, the village of Seixal in Porto Moniz gathers families to cook and share the panelo. The hearty stew of vegetables and sausages simmers in a custom that is among the village's oldest, in a celebration of Madeira's farming roots and communal spirit. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Papal Christmas Midnight Mass
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On Christmas Eve, the Pope celebrates Midnight Mass in St. Peter's Basilica, one of the most sacred moments of the Catholic year. Beneath Michelangelo's great dome, surrounded by centuries of ritual, he marks the birth of Christ before pilgrims who have gathered from every corner of the world. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Papal Holy Week & Easter Liturgies
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring, the Vatican lives out the most solemn week of the Catholic year, as the Pope leads the great liturgies of Holy Week in St. Peter's. From Palm Sunday through the hushed services of Good Friday to the joy of Easter morning, thousands of pilgrims fill the basilica and square to follow the Passion and Resurrection. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pardubice Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, the city of Pardubice celebrates the close of the grain harvest with a craft and farmers' festival. Traditional crafts, country produce, and festivity fill the town, in a celebration rooted in the old Czech harvest custom of Dožínky. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Parish Day
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · The village of Rakúsy keeps its parish feast, the Farský deň, with a solemn outdoor Mass in the grounds of the Church of St. Martin, followed by traditional Slovak food, folk music, and dancing. The celebration revolves around the parish's patron saint and gathers the whole community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Park Festival (Gostivar Summer Music Festival)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the City Park of Gostivar fills with music for the Park Festival. Pop and electronic acts from the Balkans and Albania draw crowds of young people, in a celebration that grew in the late 2010s into a showcase of youth culture in this Albanian-majority city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Gostivar, Albanian Cultural Region, North Macedonia
festivals.address: Gostivar, Albanian Cultural Region, North Macedonia

festivals.name: Patras Carnival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each winter, the port city of Patras throws Greece's biggest and most flamboyant carnival, weeks of parades, balls, and treasure hunts before Lent. Huge handmade floats and costumed crews fill the streets, in a celebration shaped since the 1800s by Venetian flair and a love of satire and spectacle. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Patron Saint Festivals of Móstoles
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the town of Móstoles honors its patron saints with days of festivity. Religious processions, live music, and public events fill the streets, in a celebration steeped in local pride for a town that was the first to rise in revolt on the second of May, 1808. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Patron Saint Festivities in Honor of the Holy Christ of Remedies
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each early September, the town of Majadahonda honors its patron, the Holy Christ of Remedies, with a week of festivities. Processions, music, and public events fill the days, in a celebration that reflects the close-knit, church-rooted life of this town near Madrid. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Patron Saint Festivities of Our Lady of Val
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Val neighborhood of Alcalá de Henares honors the Virgin of Val, patroness of its district. A procession carries the Virgin from her chapel into town and back amid sports and square dances, in a celebration that blends grassroots Catholic devotion with civic festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Patron Saint Festivities of Saint Ferdinand
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each early summer, the royal town of Aranjuez honors its king-saint patron, San Fernando. Folk customs mingle with the heritage of the great palace and gardens, in a celebration that weaves the World Heritage splendor of Royal Aranjuez into the rhythms of a patronal feast. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Patron Saint Festivities of Saint Sebastian the Martyr
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January, the town of San Sebastián de los Reyes near Madrid honors its patron, Saint Sebastian, with days of festivity. A solemn procession of the saint mixes with traditional dances and famous morning bull-runs through the streets, in a celebration that has earned the town the nickname of the little Pamplona. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Spain
festivals.address: San Sebastián de los Reyes, Spain

festivals.name: Patum of Berga
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each Corpus Christi, the Catalan town of Berga erupts into La Patum, a centuries-old festival of fire, music, and myth honored by UNESCO. Dancers in costumes of devils, giants, and fire-breathing beasts whirl through the square amid showers of sparks, in a hypnotic, smoke-filled celebration unlike anything else in Catalonia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Peebles Beltane Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year the Borders town of Peebles keeps its Beltane Festival, naming festival Principals like the Cornet and the Queen of Love and Beauty, holding a boundary ride, and filling the town with music and dance. An old May fire-and-fertility rite has here merged with the Borders' Common Riding tradition. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: People's Voice Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year the Jesuitenwiese in Vienna's Prater hosts the two-day Volksstimmefest, a free open-air social festival put on by the Austrian Communist Party. Long a cultural fair for left-wing and local groups, it gathers music, debate, and community stalls under the trees of the park. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Perachori Wine Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each last Saturday of July, the village of Perachori on Ithaca celebrates its local wine. Open-air festivities fill the old village schoolyard above Vathy, in a celebration of the native Robola grape that is a true point of island pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pesponedelnik Kukeri Festival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each first Sunday of March, the mountain village of Shiroka Laka fills with masked kukeri. Dozens of mummers in horned masks and sheepskin costumes march to drive away the spirits of winter, in a celebration that carries one of Bulgaria's most ancient carnival traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Shiroka Laka, Rhodope (Shiroka Laka community), Bulgaria
festivals.address: Shiroka Laka, Rhodope (Shiroka Laka community), Bulgaria

festivals.name: Petar Kočić Days
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the city of Banja Luka honors the writer Petar Kočić with Kočićev zbor, a festival held since 1965. Literary evenings, theatre, concerts, and folk contests fill the days in tribute to the beloved author, in a celebration of the region's literary and cultural heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Banja Luka, Banja Luka (Bosanska Krajina), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Banja Luka, Banja Luka (Bosanska Krajina), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Petrovac Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the coastal town of Petrovac fills with jazz and blues for a festival paired with Mediterranean food. Local and visiting artists play concerts and late jam sessions, in a celebration billed as Montenegro's only jazz and blues gastro festival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Petrovac, Montenegrin Adriatic Coast (Budva area), Montenegro
festivals.address: Petrovac, Montenegrin Adriatic Coast (Budva area), Montenegro

festivals.name: Petrovac Night
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each summer, the town of Petrovac marks the end of the tourist season with Petrovačka noć. Sports contests, children's entertainment, and a great communal feast of freshly caught fish fill the night, in a celebration rooted in the old fishermen's feast of this Adriatic town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Peza Congress Commemoration
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the village of Peza near Tirana commemorates the anti-fascist Congress of Peza of 1942. Two days of folk music and festivity honor the historic gathering of the resistance, in a celebration that keeps alive the memory of a key moment in Albania's wartime history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pezinok Wine Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the wine town of Pezinok celebrates its grape harvest with the Vinobranie. Wine tastings, a market, folk music, and a festive parade fill the streets of this historic town in the Little Carpathians, in a celebration of the new vintage and the region's winemaking. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Phallephoria
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · During the carnival season, Athens revives the ancient Dionysian Phallephoria, as participants dressed as satyrs, maenads, and sileni carry the old phallic symbols through Plaka and the historic center. The procession openly reaches back to the festive processions of antiquity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Picokijada Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Croatian town of Đurđevac celebrates the legend of the rooster that, by local tale, saved it from a Turkish siege. An open-air reenactment of the story is the heart of the Picokijada, alongside folk festivities and crafts, in a beloved weekend that turns a charming old legend into a whole town's pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pig and Barter Fair
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each second weekend of November, the town of Champigny-sur-Marne holds its historic Pig and Barter Fair, a trade fair whose roots run back to rights granted by King Charles IX in 1563. The old fair fills the town with barter, food, and the bustle of a centuries-old market tradition. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Pilsen Busking Fest
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each late summer, the historic center of Plzeň fills with street performers for the Busking Fest. Musicians, dancers, jugglers, and circus acts from around the world take the main square for free, in a celebration begun in 2013 to bring the joy of street performance to the city. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Pinkpop Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the Limburg town of Landgraaf hosts Pinkpop, one of the oldest and most famous music festivals in the Netherlands, running since 1970. Big international and Dutch acts play to huge crowds across several days, in a celebration of pop and rock that has been a fixture of the Dutch summer for over half a century. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Piteå Dances and Laughs
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the town of Piteå fills its center with the street festival Dances and Laughs. Local musicians and dancers turn the streets into one long celebration, in a festival whose roots reach back to community dances of the 1970s in this northern Swedish town. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Place du Pin Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each early October the town of Agen holds the Fête du Pin on the Place du Pin, a free community festival built around local green projects, with workshops, a shared organic vegetarian meal, and entertainment. Launched by local social groups, it puts sustainability and neighborliness at its heart. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Planken Rocks
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer the village square of Planken fills with Planken Rockt, a free open-air rock festival of live bands and easy community spirit. Organized by local musicians and young people, it is a modern celebration of the season high in this small mountain village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Podbiel Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the village of Podbiel celebrates the folk culture of Orava on the Bobrova Raľa meadow. Dozens of local ensembles bring traditional music, dance, and craft to the open-air stage, in a celebration that keeps alive the rich singing and tsymbaly playing of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Podilia Colors Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year, the city of Vinnytsia celebrates its many peoples with the Podillski Barvy. Polish, Jewish, Czech, Romani, Indian, Belarusian, and Ukrainian ensembles perform traditional song and dance, in a celebration begun in 1998 that showcases the rich cultural mix of Podillia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Podilska Pectoral Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August 24, on Ukraine's Independence Day, downtown Vinnytsia hosts the Podilska Pectoral festival of contemporary art. Performances and arts events fill the city center, in a celebration that joins the modern creative life of Podillia to the national holiday. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Podkarpackie Products Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, a different town of the Podkarpackie region hosts a festival of its local foods and crafts. Regional specialties and artisan work fill the stalls, in a celebration that showcases a region rich in traditional foods and the makers who keep them alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pokrova & Defenders Day Festival (Kyivska Rus Park)
festivals.description: Mid October 2027 · Each mid-October, the Kyivska Rus park near Kyiv stages a grand celebration of Pokrova and Defenders Day. A reconstructed 'Ancient Kyiv' fills with medieval and Cossack pageantry, in a celebration that ties the country's old heritage to its present-day patriotism. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pokrova Cossack Festival (Khortytsia Island)
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each October, the great river island of Khortytsia, once the heart of the Zaporozhian Cossacks, fills with horsemanship, song, and martial display for the Pokrova Cossack Festival. Held on the old Cossacks' feast day, it celebrates their fierce spirit and freedom, on the very ground where their famous fortress once stood. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Poland National Independence Day
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Every November 11, Poland celebrates its Independence Day, marking the moment in 1918 when the nation rose again after 123 years erased from the map. In Warsaw, flags fill the avenues for official ceremonies, a Mass for the homeland, and patriotic marches, in one of the country's most deeply felt national days. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: PolarJazz (Longyearbyen Polar Night Jazz Festival)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Held in Longyearbyen during the polar night, PolarJazz is the world's northernmost jazz festival, started by local enthusiasts in the late 1990s. In the deep Arctic dark, a small, devoted audience gathers each winter for a few days of music at the top of the world. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Polish Diaspora Children’s Folk Ensemble Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the spa town of Iwonicz-Zdrój gathers the children of the Polish diaspora for a folk ensemble festival. Youth groups from Canada, Lithuania, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, and beyond perform traditional dance and music, in a celebration that ties Polish children abroad back to their roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Polissya Circle (International Folklore Festival, Chernihiv)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the city of Chernihiv hosts the Polissya Circle, a folklore festival of the borderland. Amateur ensembles from Ukrainian villages and abroad perform the songs, dances, and old wedding and harvest rites of Polissya, in a celebration that preserves the shared traditions of this woodland region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Polissya Lady Potato Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · On Ovruch City Day in late September, the Polissya Lady Potato Festival celebrates the region's potato-farming heritage with contests for the best varenyky, pancakes, and potato beer, plus folk music and crafts. Rooted in the agrarian life of Polissya, it even crowns a Potato Lady to reign over the day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Polissya Summer with Folklore
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the city of Lutsk fills its castle and squares with folk music for Polissya Summer with Folklore. Local and visiting ensembles dance and sing at Lubart's Castle and the old cathedral, in an international celebration held since 1994 to keep the heritage of Volhynia and Polissya alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Poltava Dumpling Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the city of Poltava celebrates its famous dumpling, the galushka, with a festival. Cooking, tasting, folk music, and craft fill the day around a dish made famous in Kotlyarevsky's poetry, in a celebration of the deep culinary traditions and hospitality of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pomurje Summer Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August, the village of Velika Polana hosts the largest summer event of the Pomurje region. Music concerts, sports, and festivity fill the days, in a celebration launched in 1996 that showcases the cuisine and cultural heritage of the Prekmurje countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pori Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the Finnish city of Pori hosts one of Europe's oldest jazz festivals, running since 1966. For days, jazz, soul, and pop fill stages along the river and in the city's leafy park, drawing big names and devoted crowds to a town that becomes, each summer, a capital of the music. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Porsanger Festival (Sámi Joik & World Music)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the municipality of Porsanger fills several days with joik and world music. Sami song, Norwegian folk, and music from around the world share the stage, in a celebration that reflects the Sami, Norwegian, and Kven communities living side by side in this northern land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Port Erin Beach Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the village of Port Erin gathers on its bay for a community beach festival. Live bands play on the sand alongside children's games and family activities over a summer weekend, in a celebration born of local efforts to bring the town together and welcome the season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Port Erin, Manx, Isle of Man
festivals.address: Port Erin, Manx, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Porto Flamenco Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the city of Porto fills its stages with the fire of flamenco. Celebrated Andalusian artists bring concerts and dance to venues like the Casa da Música, in a celebration launched in 2019 that carries the flamenco tradition north to Portugal. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Posavina Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, around the Feast of the Assumption, the town of Odžak hosts the Posavsko kolo folklore festival. Ensembles celebrate the heritage of the Posavina region in dance and song, in a celebration tied to Velika Gospa on August 15, the great summer holiday of the area. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Odžak, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina (Odžak), Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Odžak, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatian Herzegovina-Posavina (Odžak), Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Posavje Quintain
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June in Savlje near Ljubljana, unmarried men on horseback charge a wooden wine barrel with iron batons, the winner crowned the quintain rider. Descended from old knightly tournaments, the Posavsko štehvanje has been ridden here for more than a century. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Potato Festival (Kudirkos Naumiestis)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Kudirkos Naumiestis honors the humble potato with a harvest festival. Cooking competitions, notably for cepelinai, and cheerful festivity fill the day, in a celebration rooted in the farming life of Suvalkija, where the potato is central to the table. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Potato Festival (Radom)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the open-air village museum of Radom hosts a festival of the potato harvest. Exhibits of potato varieties, cooking contests, and tastings of traditional dishes fill the grounds, in a celebration of a crop that long sustained the rural communities of Masovia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Poškonys Saint John’s Night
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23 near the village of Poškonys, people gather for Rasos, weaving flower wreaths, lighting a bonfire by the Gauja River, and floating the wreaths on the water as they sing. One of Lithuania's oldest solstice traditions, it blends pagan rite with the feast of St. John. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Šalčininkai District, Dieveniškės historic region, Šalčininkai District, Lithuania
festivals.address: Šalčininkai District, Dieveniškės historic region, Šalčininkai District, Lithuania

festivals.name: Požega Fish Stew Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the town of Požega hosts a contest of the traditional fish stew, the fiš paprikaš. Masters simmer their cauldrons amid music and good company, in a gastronomic celebration that promotes the riverside fish-stew cuisine of Slavonia during the town's Golden Strings festival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Prague Folklore Days
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the city of Prague fills its streets with folk dance and music for Folklore Days. Amateur ensembles from the Czech lands and around the world perform in costume across the city before a colorful parade, in a celebration of the folk traditions at the heart of Czech heritage. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Prague, Czech and Central European folk communities, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Prague, Czech and Central European folk communities, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Prague Pride Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, Prague celebrates its LGBTQ+ community with a week-long Pride festival that ends in a huge Rainbow Parade through the city center. Concerts, exhibitions, and parties draw tens of thousands, in a celebration of equality that has become a symbol of how far the Czech Republic has opened up since the fall of Communism. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Prague Ratha Yatra Chariot Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year Prague's Hare Krishna and Indian community pulls a large decorated wooden chariot bearing the deities through the streets, ending with music and dance at Náměstí Republiky. Drawn from the ancient Jagannath tradition of India, the Ratha Yatra has been held in the city for years. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Prague, Indian/Hindu community in Prague, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Prague, Indian/Hindu community in Prague, Czech Republic

festivals.name: Praia da Vitória Festivities
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Azorean town of Praia da Vitória on Terceira holds its biggest festival around its municipal holiday. Concerts fill the marina-side stages and traditional bull-running spills through the streets, in a warm island celebration honoring local patron saints and the town's deep ties to the sea. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Preiļi City Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August the Latgalian town of Preiļi celebrates its City Festival with live music, folk dances, cultural performances, and an outdoor fair, climaxing in concerts and fireworks. Known for its manor park and heritage, the town turns the weekend into a celebration of its community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Premiers Plans Festival of Angers
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each year, the city of Angers devotes a festival to the first films of new directors. Screenings, competitions, and workshops spotlight emerging European and world talent, in a celebration held since 1989 that has made the city a true champion of fresh voices in cinema. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Prienai Region Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each mid-May the town of Prienai holds its regional festival, a multi-day gathering of cultural exhibits, patriotic ceremonies, family activities, and entertainment. Run by the local culture center, it puts the history and identity of this Suvalkija town at the heart of the celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Primarolia Festival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each early fall, the town of Aigio celebrates the history of its black currant and its sailing past with the Primarolia festival. An international contemporary art exhibition and cultural events fill the program, in a celebration named for the old ships that once carried the town's currant cargoes to Europe. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Primorska Summer Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the towns of the Slovenian coast share an international festival of theatre. Slovenian and foreign productions, often multilingual, fill historic venues in Koper, Izola, Piran, and Ankaran, in a celebration founded in 1993 that became the coast's great platform for the stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: PrizrenFest (Open-Air Theatre Festival)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the historic city of Prizren becomes an open-air stage for an international theatre festival. Squares, cinemas, and landmarks host local and visiting companies over several days, in a celebration that turns a city of deep multicultural heritage into one great theatre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ptuj Carnival (Kurentovanje)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter, the Slovenian town of Ptuj fills with the Kurents, shaggy sheepskin figures hung with cowbells and crowned with feathers or horns, who leap and clang to chase away winter. Their Kurentovanje is the country's biggest carnival and a UNESCO-honored rite, drawing crowds to one of Slovenia's oldest towns. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Puck Fair
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Kerry town of Killorglin holds the Puck Fair, one of Ireland's oldest festivals, when a wild mountain goat is caught, crowned King Puck, and raised on a high stand over the town for three days. Around this strange and ancient harvest rite swirl music, dancing, and a great country horse fair. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2026-08-10) is a fixed annual date.
festivals.location: Killorglin, County Kerry, Munster Province, Ireland
festivals.address: Killorglin, County Kerry, Munster Province, Ireland

festivals.name: Puerta del Sol New Year's Eve Grape Countdown
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each December 31, thousands pack Madrid's Puerta del Sol beneath the famous clock for Spain's great New Year's Eve ritual. As the bells chime midnight, everyone races to eat twelve grapes, one for each stroke, for luck in the year ahead, in a tradition born in Spain over a century ago and now watched across the country. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Pumpkin Days
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Kikinda celebrates the humble pumpkin with its Pumpkin Days. Growers and visitors gather around giant gourds and pumpkin festivity, in a celebration that has become a beloved fixture for pumpkin lovers from across the region of Vojvodina. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Puppet Theatre Festival "Children and Puppets"
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year the town of Kupiškis hosts the Children and Puppets festival, where touring professional puppet troupes perform colorful, educational shows for young audiences. Puppet theatre is a cherished children's art in Lithuania, and the festival brings its best work to the town's stages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Purple Evening Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the town of Anykščiai fills with song and verse for the Purple Evening festival. Bardic singer-songwriters, young performers, and poets gather for open-air concerts and workshops, in a celebration begun in 2008 in a town named a City of Literature for its rich written and musical life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Putignano Carnival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each year, the Puglian town of Putignano holds one of Italy's oldest and longest carnivals, first celebrated in 1394. For weeks before Lent, enormous, often satirical papier-mâché floats roll through the streets amid music and revelry, in a celebration that stretches from the deep of winter to the edge of spring. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pyrofantasia (Kerċem, Gozo)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer the Gozo village of Kerċem stages Pyrofantasia, a fireworks display set in synchrony to music. Begun in the late 2010s, it draws on Gozo's proud fireworks culture, where local kamra tan-nar clubs craft the pyrotechnics that light the island's summer nights. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pärnu Hanseatic Days Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the town of Pärnu returns to its Hanseatic past with the Hansapäevad. A historical market, crafts, medieval music, and archery and axe-throwing tournaments fill the old town, in a celebration of the days when Pärnu was a trading town of the Hanseatic League. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Pützchen Market
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Bonn district of Pützchen hosts one of Germany's oldest folk fairs, a tradition reaching back to 1399. What began as a market for wine and goods now draws over a million visitors to its fairground rides, stalls, and beer tents, in a great autumn celebration that has outlived the centuries. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Quercy Saffron Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the town of Cajarc celebrates its 'red gold', the saffron of Quercy, with a festival. Visits to the saffron fields, harvesting workshops, tastings, and cooking demonstrations fill the program, in a celebration of an age-old crop that has earned the area culinary renown. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Quintana Joust
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the city of Ascoli Piceno stages the Quintana, a great equestrian tournament. Knights of the city's ten neighborhoods charge at a ring with leveled lances before roaring crowds, in a celebration of medieval pageantry at the heart of the city's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Rab Medieval Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the island town of Rab brings its history to life with the Rapska Fjera. Said to be Croatia's oldest and largest medieval summer festival, rooted in a decree of 1364, it fills the streets with crossbowmen and craft, in a celebration of the town's medieval heritage and freedom. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Rab, Croatian (Primorje-Gorski Kotar - Rab community), Croatia
festivals.address: Rab, Croatian (Primorje-Gorski Kotar - Rab community), Croatia

festivals.name: Rabadan - Carnival in Bellinzona
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Swiss town of Bellinzona erupts into Rabadan, one of Ticino's largest and oldest carnivals. Costumed crowds, satirical floats, and noisy bands fill the streets around the medieval castles for four days of music, dancing, and foam battles, in a celebration whose name means joyful racket. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Radovljica Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Radovljica fills its halls with early music for its festival. Baroque and older works, often seldom heard, are performed by fine soloists alongside masterclasses, in a celebration mounted by a local society devoted to the music of centuries past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Randers Festival Week
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · First held in 2024, the Randers Festuge is a people's festival, a week of events dreamed up and run by the town's own citizens, associations, and cultural groups. Created by the community and the municipality, it sets out to put local culture and creativity center stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Raspberry Days
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each mid-July the mountain town of Andrijevica celebrates its raspberry harvest with a mini-fair of raspberry products, cultural programs, music, and games in Knjaževac Park. Raspberries are a mainstay of the local economy, and the festival honors the rural traditions that grow them. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ravne Days
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August the Carinthian town of Ravne na Koroškem holds Ravenski dnevi, a week of rock, folk-pop, and jazz concerts, food stalls, and children's programs. Run by the town, it brings local youth groups, musicians, and families together for the height of summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ravne na Koroškem, Slovenian Carinthia (Koroška), Slovenia
festivals.address: Ravne na Koroškem, Slovenian Carinthia (Koroška), Slovenia

festivals.name: Re:tradition Festival (Jagiellonian Fair, Lublin)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer the city of Lublin holds Re:tradition, a historical market and folk fair that revives its old Jagiellonian Fair, with crafts, regional foods, concerts, and traditional dance. The festival recalls Lublin's heyday as a medieval and Renaissance trading hub. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Red Rose Rusyn Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the Rusyn community of Transcarpathia celebrates its heritage with the Red Rose festival. A parade, a wreath-laying, and poetry and music in the Rusyn language fill the day, in a celebration that affirms the distinct identity of the Carpatho-Rusyn people. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: Mukachevo, Rusyn community of Transcarpathia, Ukraine
festivals.address: Mukachevo, Rusyn community of Transcarpathia, Ukraine

festivals.name: Red Rose Rusyn Culture Festival (Ruski Krstur)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each late June, the village of Ruski Krstur gathers in the Petro Kuzmjak schoolyard for the Red Rose festival of Rusyn culture. Competitions in traditional songs and new compositions fill the weekend, in a long-running celebration of the distinct Slavic language and traditions of Vojvodina's Rusyns. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ruski Krstur, Vojvodina Rusyn community, Serbia
festivals.address: Ruski Krstur, Vojvodina Rusyn community, Serbia

festivals.name: Regiony International Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the city of Hradec Králové hosts Regiony, the largest theatre festival in the Czech Republic. Companies from home and abroad bring contemporary plays and performances to its stages and squares, in a celebration of the stage that has helped earn the city its reputation as a place of fine theatre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Hradec Králové, Czech East Bohemian theater tradition, Czech Republic
festivals.address: Hradec Králové, Czech East Bohemian theater tradition, Czech Republic

festivals.name: RegWine Festival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each year, the wine town of Reguengos de Monsaraz celebrates Alentejo wine with the RegWine festival. Tastings, winery exhibitions, and cultural events center on local grapes and winemaking, in a celebration of a town renowned for the wines of the Alentejo plain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Reguengos de Monsaraz, Reguengos de Monsaraz, Alentejo, Portugal
festivals.address: Reguengos de Monsaraz, Reguengos de Monsaraz, Alentejo, Portugal

festivals.name: Relief of Groningen Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the city of Groningen celebrates the lifting of its 1672 siege with Gronings Ontzet. City-wide festivities recall the day the Spanish were turned back, in a celebration also known as Bommen Berend that has long reinforced the city's pride in standing firm. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Relief of Leiden Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October 3, the Dutch city of Leiden celebrates its deliverance from a Spanish siege in 1574, when relief finally came to its starving people. By tradition the city feasts on herring and white bread and steaming pea soup, with parades and a funfair, in a proud civic holiday that has marked the day for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Requena Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the town of Requena holds what is said to be Spain's oldest grape harvest festival. Tastings, parades, and festivity celebrate the new vintage, in a celebration that flows from medieval wine fairs and the deep tie between this town and its vineyards. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Retranchement Summer Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On the village market of Retranchement, this free two-day summer festival mixes live bands with traditional folk music. Grown from older local events, the Zomerfestival was created to bring life to a small Zeeland border village in the heart of the warm season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Return of Knights (Kolođvar Fortress)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · For an afternoon and evening at Kolođvar Fortress near Osijek, armored reenactors stage sword fights, archery contests, and a night battle for the old stronghold. The Return of Knights revives the medieval heritage of Osijek-Baranja at one of its most storied fortress sites. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Return of the Alpine Pastures
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each second Saturday of October, the city of Annecy welcomes the herds down from the high pastures with Le Retour des Alpages. Cattle, costume, and folk tradition fill the lakeside streets, in a celebration of the rural Alpine and Savoyard heritage of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Retz Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the historic Austrian wine town of Retz celebrates the grape harvest with four days of festivity. Communal wine taverns pour the new vintage, a great harvest market fills the square, and the famous winemaker parade winds through town in costume, in a warm tribute to the vineyards of the Weinviertel. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Retz Wine Week
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the town of Retz fills the Sparkassengarten with hundreds of wines for its Wine Week. Local growers and international sommeliers gather to taste and award the year's best, in a celebration running since 1969 that turns the spring into a festival of the vine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Reykjavík Culture Night
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On the last Saturday of August, the streets of downtown Reykjavík fill from morning till late at night for Culture Night, the city's great free festival of the arts. Music, dance, theatre, and art spill out across the capital, ending in fireworks over the bay, in a joyful celebration of Reykjavík's creative heart. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Rezekne Municipality Days
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July the parishes of Rezekne municipality fill with a week of themed days, Fishermen's Day, Family Day, Sports Day, Crafts Day, and more, with competitions, workshops, concerts, and exhibitions. The municipality runs the series to knit its scattered communities together for a summer of celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Rijeka Carnival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each winter, the Croatian port of Rijeka throws one of Europe's largest carnivals, a whole season of masquerade balls, concerts, and parades from January to Lent. It climaxes in a vast international parade of floats and costumes, in a celebration locals call the Fifth Season and a defining tradition of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Riplianka Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · In the Old Village Museum of Kolochava, the Riplianka Festival celebrates the Zakarpattian potato-and-cornmeal dumpling, with cook-off stalls serving more than twenty versions topped with brynza sauce, eggs, or mushrooms. The humble dish reflects the resourcefulness of the region's mountain peasants. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Risør Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the coastal town of Risør fills with classical music for one of Norway's leading chamber music festivals. Nearly forty concerts gather top performers in the historic seaside town, in a celebration that has long made Risør a meeting place for chamber music. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Robola Wine Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer the village of Fragkata in Kefalonia celebrates Robola, the island's own white wine grape, with a long-running festival of food, dance, and free-flowing wine. Run by local cooperatives, it honors a grape found nowhere else and the mountain vineyards that nurture it. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Rockoff Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the town square of Mariehamn becomes the biggest party in the Åland Islands for Rockoff, a summer music festival started by local youth in the 1980s. Nordic acts and crowds fill the heart of the little Swedish-speaking island capital, in a beloved highlight of the short northern summer. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Roermond Sint-Maarten Lantern Parade (Lantern Blessing & Bonfire)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · On St. Martin's Eve in Roermond, children gather at the Munsterplein to have their lanterns blessed in the Munsterkerk, then walk singing to the Markt, where the legend of St. Martin is retold by a bonfire. The evening keeps the Limburg Catholic tradition of light against the November dark. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romani Gastronomic and Crafts Festival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Around International Roma Day, the town of Giurgiu celebrates Romani culture with a festival of food and crafts, as vendors offer Roma specialties like bulz and goulash and evening concerts feature well-known Romani musicians. Begun by a local Romani alliance, it is a proud celebration of heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romania National Day Military Parade
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Every December 1, Romania celebrates its National Day, marking the Great Union of 1918 that brought Transylvania and other lands into the kingdom. In Bucharest, a wreath-laying at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier leads into a grand military parade beneath the Arc de Triomphe, in the country's proudest national holiday. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Romanian Blouse Day & Sânzienele Rituals (ASTRA Museum, Sibiu)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the ASTRA open-air museum near Sibiu celebrates the Romanian blouse and the rites of the Sânziene. A Saint John's Day liturgy, folk dance and music workshops, and a fair of embroidered blouses fill the grounds, in a celebration of the ia, a UNESCO-listed emblem of Romanian identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romanian Blouse Day (Arad Museum Complex)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On St. John's Day the Arad Museum Complex marks Romanian Blouse Day with an exhibition devoted to the ia, the traditional embroidered folk blouse. Tying local heritage to a worldwide celebration, the day honors the blouse as a spirited emblem of Romanian identity. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Romanian Blouse Day (Village Museum, Bucharest)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On St. John's Day the Dimitrie Gusti Village Museum in Bucharest marks Romanian Blouse Day with a display of hundreds of ia blouses, some more than a century old. The exhibition links Romania's rural heritage to a modern pride in the embroidered shirt recognized by UNESCO. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romanian Blouse Festival: Authentic Romania
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, around the Sânziene solstice, the city of Iași celebrates the Romanian blouse and its folk heritage. Music, craft, and the embroidered ia fill the festival, in a celebration that weaves the Moldavian traditions of the region into a warm midsummer gathering. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Romanian Fecioresc and Bărbătesc Dance Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the city of Cluj-Napoca gathers amateur male dance groups for a festival of the fecioresc and bărbătesc. Costumed dancers compete in the leaping, slapping men's dances and the brâu, in a celebration that preserves a Transylvanian form honored by UNESCO. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romanian Language Day Celebration
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August 31, Moldova celebrates Limba Noastră, its Romanian Language Day, recalling the day in 1989 when the country reclaimed its mother tongue from Soviet rule. Ceremonies and concerts gather around the capital's monuments to the language, in a heartfelt celebration of words, identity, and cultural revival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Romans-sur-Isère Carnival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each year the Drôme town of Romans-sur-Isère fills the Place Jules Nadi with its carnival, a colorful themed parade of music, stilt-walkers, and circus acts. Rooted in the pre-Lenten carnival tradition of France, Romans has grown its own lively, inventive version of the festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: ROMAZURI Roma Arts Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · On Budapest's Margaret Island, the ROMAZURI festival offers a rich immersion into the culture of Hungary's largest minority. Over three days, Roma music, dance, theatre, fashion, and food fill the riverside park, in a vibrant celebration of a community whose artistry has shaped Hungarian culture for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Rosenheim Autumn Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each autumn, the Upper Bavarian town of Rosenheim throws its Herbstfest, often called the likeable little Oktoberfest. Beer tents, rides, music, and parades fill the days, in a celebration begun in 1861 as an agricultural fair that grew into one of the region's great folk festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Roshnik Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Over three days in mid-September, the Berat-region village of Roshnik celebrates its fig and grape harvest, gathering villagers and visitors in the center for communal fruit picking at the orchards. The festival draws on the strong small-farming culture of Roshnik and its neighboring villages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Roskilde Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the fields south of Roskilde fill with one of northern Europe's largest music festivals, a week of rock and pop drawing some 130,000 people. Founded by Danish students in 1971 and run for charity, it has become an icon of Danish summer, as much a vast tent-city community as a concert. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Roskilde, Danish youth of Mid-Zealand, Denmark
festivals.address: Roskilde, Danish youth of Mid-Zealand, Denmark

festivals.name: Rossio Christmas Market
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each winter, Lisbon's grand Rossio square fills with a Christmas market of around seventy stalls offering crafts, seasonal treats, and live music.
