With a Santa's House and twinkling lights, it brings a magical holiday glow to the heart of the city, in a beloved part of the Portuguese capital's festive season. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Rottweil Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Fasnet, the town of Rottweil stages its famous Narrensprung, where costumed jesters 'jump' through the streets. The carved masks and bounding fools make it one of the oldest and most spectacular Alemannic carnivals of southwestern Germany, in a celebration rooted in old winter-banishing rites. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Rouen Impressionnée Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2027 · Each so often, the city of Rouen fills its streets with contemporary art for the Rouen Impressionnée festival. French and international artists create large outdoor works and performances, in a celebration begun in 2010 that answers the Impressionist legacy of Normandy with the art of today. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Royal National Mòd
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year a Scottish town hosts the Royal National Mòd, the country's leading celebration of the Gaelic language. For a week, singers, musicians, and poets compete in Gaelic, carrying on a festival begun in 1891 to keep the old Highland language and its songs alive. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Fort William, Scotland (Highlands and Islands), United Kingdom
festivals.address: Fort William, Scotland (Highlands and Islands), United Kingdom

festivals.name: Røros Winter Market
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the old copper-mining town of Røros, a UNESCO treasure of timber houses, fills with the Rørosmartnan, a winter market held by royal decree since 1854. Horse-drawn sleighs arrive as in the old days, and stalls, music, and crafts crowd the snowy streets, reviving the great trading fairs of the past. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Saaremaa Food Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, the island town of Kuressaare celebrates its food with the Saaremaa Food Festival. Cooking demonstrations, tastings, and farm visits showcase the island's produce and hospitality, in a celebration of the culinary heritage of Saaremaa and the farmers and chefs who keep it. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saaremaa Opera Days
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the castle of Kuressaare becomes a grand stage for the Saaremaa Opera Days. International and local productions fill the castle's opera house with high singing, in a celebration that has grown since 1999 into a beloved island opera festival. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saba Beer, Art and Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · First held in 2024, the Saba Beer, Art and Music Festival gathers craft beer, visual art, and musical talent from Saba and neighboring Sint Maarten. The cross-island event showcases the creativity of tiny Saba, from its new Saba Cloud Top beers to its artists and musicians. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Philipsburg, Saba and Sint Maarten (local craft community), Netherlands
festivals.address: Philipsburg, Saba and Sint Maarten (local craft community), Netherlands

festivals.name: Saba Carnival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the island of Saba celebrates Saban culture with a week-long Carnival. Parades, music, dance, food, and community competitions fill the days, in a celebration whose roots reach back to the early 20th century on this small Caribbean island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sabantuy (Kruszyniany)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the historic Tatar village of Kruszyniany keeps the harvest festival of Sabantuy. Traditional music, dance, tug-of-war, horse racing, and a feast of Tatar dishes fill the day, in a celebration of the Tatar heritage that has lived in this corner of Poland for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sabucedo Wild Horse Shearing Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Galician village of Sabucedo holds the Rapa das Bestas, a centuries-old rite in which villagers drive wild horses down from the mountains into a stone corral. There, bare-handed, they wrestle each animal to cut its mane and tail, in a wild, ancient festival of strength and old pastoral tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saffron Rose Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each late October, the town of Consuegra celebrates the saffron harvest with the Fiesta de la Rosa del Azafrán. Saffron-rose plucking contests and Manchego culture fill the days beneath the famous windmills, in a celebration held since 1963 in the heart of La Mancha's saffron country. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Sagardo Eguna (Cider Day, Astigarraga)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July 26, on the feast of Saint Anne, the town of Astigarraga celebrates Basque cider with Sagardo Eguna. Around twenty local producers pour fresh sidra from the barrel on the town hall square, in a celebration set in the heart of Gipuzkoa's cider country. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sagra del Redentore
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Sardinian city of Nuoro celebrates the Sagra del Redentore, honoring the great bronze statue of Christ atop Monte Ortobene. Pilgrims climb the mountain and one of Sardinia's largest costume parades fills the streets, as folk groups from across the island gather in their finest traditional dress. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Saint Andrew Apostle Parish Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · The San Marino village of Serravalle holds a multi-day parish festival in honor of St. Andrew the Apostle, with a sacred music concert, children's games, family Masses, communal meals, and live music. The festival gathers the community in the warm Catholic tradition of the patron's feast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Anthony Abbot’s Fire Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Every January 16 the Sardinian town of Ottana builds and lights a great bonfire to honor Sant'Antonio Abate, patron of animals and fire. The towering blaze fulfills an old rural vow to the saint, gathering the townsfolk around the flames in the depth of winter. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Saint Bartholomew's Festival of Sitges (Fireworks & Castellers)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the seaside town of Sitges erupts for its Festa Major in honor of Sant Bartomeu, a frantic 36 hours of festivity. Furious fireworks fill the narrow streets as costumed devils run with sparks, and human-tower teams raise their castells, in one of Catalonia's most intense patron-saint celebrations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Demetrius' Fire Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each October 26, the town of Topoloveni runs with torches for the Focul lu' Sumedru, the fire of Saint Demetrius. Lit torches and a ceremonial relay fill the weekly fair, in a celebration that carries an ancient Dacian fire ritual into the customs of this Muntenian land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Devota Day (Torchlight Procession & Boat Burning)
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each January 26 and 27, Monaco honors its patron, Saint Devota, whose relics, by legend, washed ashore here in Roman times. A torchlight procession winds to her chapel and a symbolic boat is set ablaze, lit by the Prince himself, in the principality's oldest and most cherished religious tradition. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Saint Fiacre Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each harvest season the town of La Ville-du-Bois honors St. Fiacre, the patron of gardeners, with folk music, workshops, games, and exhibitions on a horticultural theme. The free, family-friendly festival celebrates the Irish-born saint whom France has long claimed as the gardener's protector. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Ginés Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the Lanzarote town of Arrecife celebrates its patron, San Ginés, with two weeks of festivity by the sea. Nightly concerts, folk parades, and traditional sailing races fill the days, ending in fireworks over the harbor, in the biggest celebration of the year on this volcanic Canary island. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Saint John's Day (Canigou Flame Bonfire & Batafögu)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, Monaco welcomes midsummer with the festival of Saint John, when a flame carried from the distant peak of Canigó lights a great bonfire before the Prince's Palace. Folk groups in Monegasque costume dance around the fire, joining old pagan custom with the principality's Catholic traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint John's Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the Faroese town of Vágur celebrates Saint John's Day with Jóansøka. Rowing regattas, Faroese music, and folk dancing fill the festivities, in a celebration that blends the Christian feast of St. John with the islands' ancient midsummer customs. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Saint Joseph Festival
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each March the shipbuilding town of Astillero honors its patron, St. Joseph, with festivities that open with a proclamation from the Town Hall balcony. Bound up with the town's life of shipyards and fishing, the celebration weaves religious devotion into a week of community festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Joseph’s Festival
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each March 19, the city of Santarém honors its patron, Saint Joseph, with the Festas de São José. Folk dances, bullfighting, and the campino riders of the Ribatejo fill the days, in a celebration where the sacred and the secular meet in this old town on the Tagus. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Matthew's Fair
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year from late summer into autumn, the Portuguese city of Viseu hosts the Feira de São Mateus, one of the oldest fairs in the country, granted by royal charter back in 1392. Fairground rides, craft and food stalls, and weeks of concerts fill the grounds, blending old market tradition with modern festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Matthew’s Fair of Houdan
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · On the last weekend of September the town of Houdan holds its centuries-old Saint-Matthieu fair, with a large funfair, a fireworks finale, and a themed cultural village. Dating to the Middle Ages and now in its ninth century, the agrarian fair is one of the oldest in the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Narcissus Fair (Fira de Sant Narcís)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the Catalan city of Girona throws its great fair for Sant Narcís, its patron saint, around the end of October. Parading giants and big-headed figures, fireworks, castellers, and the circle dance of the sardana fill the city, in a celebration rooted in medieval guild and church festivities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Peter's Fair
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each late June the town of Alcántara honors its patron, San Pedro, with a large feria of live music, games, and festivity. The fair has old roots as a trading event, and it remains the town's high point of the early summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint Peter’s Festival of Afurada
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the fishing village of Afurada honors Saint Peter, patron of fishermen, with a festival on the Douro. A procession carries his image through the streets and blesses the fishing boats on the river, in a celebration at the heart of this old fishing community near Porto. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vila Nova de Gaia, Afurada, Vila Nova de Gaia (fishermen’s community), Portugal
festivals.address: Vila Nova de Gaia, Afurada, Vila Nova de Gaia (fishermen’s community), Portugal

festivals.name: Saint Rose Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the town of Sittard honors Saint Rose of Lima with a three-day festival. The Sunday Rose procession of devotion meets live music, theater, and nostalgic carnival rides, in a celebration that carries the town's centuries-old devotion to its patron. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Saint Tryphon's Day
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year, the town of Kotor honors its patron, Saint Tryphon, who has watched over the city since the 9th century. Solemn services at his cathedral, dances by the Bokeljska Navy, and a procession of his relics fill the day, in a celebration of the saint who guards the town and its seafarers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint-Ours Craft Fair
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each January, the Alpine city of Aosta fills with a thousand stalls of handmade craft for the Foire de Saint-Ours, one of Europe's oldest and largest artisan fairs. Woodcarvers, weavers, and sculptors show off a year's work in the winter cold, in a market that has gathered the makers of the valley for a thousand years. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Saint-Palais Basque Strength Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the town of Saint-Palais celebrates traditional Basque rural sports with a festival of strength. Teams from Lower Navarre and Soule compete in tug-of-war, log lifting, and other classic events, in a celebration of the force basque born from the labors of village and farm. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Saint-Vincent Tournante Wine Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each winter, a different Burgundy village hosts the Saint-Vincent Tournante, a festival honoring the patron saint of winegrowers. A grand procession of costumed wine guilds winds through the vineyards to a mass and a great banquet where thousands of bottles are uncorked, in a celebration of the region's deep wine fellowship. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sajeta Creative Camp Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Sotočje Park in Tolmin fills with experimental sound for the Sajeta festival. Krautrock, jazz, electronic, world, and classical music mingle with multimedia art, in a boutique celebration held since 1998 that pioneered festival culture in this corner of Slovenia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Salesian Living Nativity
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each Christmas season, the Salesian community of Oświęcim performs its living Nativity play, the Jasełka. Local children and adults enact the Christmas story in regional costume with folk music, in a celebration more than forty years old that carries a beloved Polish Catholic tradition. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Saltvik Viking Market
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July the village of Kvarnbo in Saltvik hosts a Viking market and reenactment, held by Åland's Fibula Society for more than twenty-five years. Set in a landscape dotted with Viking Age sites, the market revives the islands' old ties to Nordic seafaring culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Salzkammergut Krampus Run
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each St. Nicholas Eve, the town of Bad Goisern fills its main street with the Salzkammergut Krampus Run. Dozens of Krampus and Perchten groups march in hand-carved masks and fur, in a celebration rooted in pre-Christian Alpine rites meant to banish the evil spirits of winter. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Bad Goisern, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria
festivals.address: Bad Goisern, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria

festivals.name: Samede Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each pre-Lenten season the Galician village of Samede in Paderne keeps its Entroido, with locals in traditional dress dancing the muiñeira around the chapel. A rural carnival with deep folk roots, it joins old folk dances to the ritual customs of the Galician winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Samhuinn Fire Festival (Samhain)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · On the last Friday of October, the streets of Edinburgh blaze with the Samhuinn Fire Festival, a dramatic modern reimagining of the ancient Gaelic Samhain. Fire dancers, drummers, and costumed performers act out the battle of the seasons as summer gives way to winter, in a torchlit spectacle of the old Celtic new year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sami Music Week
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the town of Alta fills the dark winter with Sami Music Week. Concerts range from traditional joik to Sami pop and hip-hop alongside workshops and panels, opening on Sami National Day, in a celebration that ties Alta's Sami community to the wider Sami world. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: San Fausto Festivities (Durango)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each mid-October, the town of Durango honors its patron, Saint Faustus, with ten days of San Fausto festivities. Morning bull-runs, wood-chopping contests, stew competitions, and concerts fill the streets, in a celebration that draws on Durango's farming heritage and Basque community life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: San Mateo and Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the Rioja capital of Logroño celebrates San Mateo with a festival of thanks for the grape harvest. The treading of the season's first grapes is offered to the city's patron, and the streets fill with wine, music, and revelry, in a celebration at the heart of Spain's most famous wine region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: San Sebastián Tamborrada (Drum Parade)
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · At the stroke of midnight on January 20, the Basque city of San Sebastián begins a full day of drumming for the Tamborrada. Thousands dressed as cooks and Napoleonic soldiers beat drums and barrels through the streets without pause for twenty-four hours, in the proud, thunderous signature festival of Donostia. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sancta Barbara Wooden Chapels Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, the area around Velika Gorica celebrates its wooden chapels with the Sancta Barbara festival. A week of events honors the treasured folk monuments and the Baroque heritage of the countryside, in a celebration that gathers the community around these beloved little churches. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Velika Gorica, Central Croatia (Zagreb County), Croatia
festivals.address: Velika Gorica, Central Croatia (Zagreb County), Croatia

festivals.name: Sandzak Art Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Novi Pazar hosts the Sandžak Art Festival of contemporary visual art. Exhibitions and events highlight the interfaith and multiethnic culture of the region, in a young celebration that builds on the city's long role as a crossroads of Bosniak and Balkan life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sanjoaninas (Angra do Heroísmo)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the Azorean city of Angra do Heroísmo on Terceira holds the Sanjoaninas, among the largest and oldest festivals in the islands, in honor of Saint John. Parades of costumed marchas, folk dancing, and island-style bull-running fill days of celebration, in a high point of the Azorean summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Angra do Heroísmo, Terceira Island, Portugal
festivals.address: Angra do Heroísmo, Terceira Island, Portugal

festivals.name: Sankt Hans Eve (Ofelia Plads)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the evening of June 23, the people of Copenhagen gather at the harborside Ofelia Plads to celebrate Sankt Hans, the Danish midsummer. A great bonfire is lit out on the water as crowds sing the traditional summer song, in an old solstice custom, joined to the feast of St. John, that welcomes the lightest nights of the year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sant Francesc Xavier Sant Jaume Festival (Formentera)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the little Formentera capital of Sant Francesc Xavier honors its patron, Saint James, with a week of festivity. A Mass and procession on July 25 sit alongside concerts, folk traditions, and community gatherings, in the biggest celebration of the year on the smallest of the Balearic islands. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sant Julià de Lòria Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year the Andorran town of Sant Julià de Lòria holds its carnival, with processions, floats, and street parties full of music and dancing. A local take on the carnival tradition, it gathers community groups around humorous themes for days of festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sant Julià de Lòria, Sant Julià de Lòria Parish, Andorra, Andorra
festivals.address: Sant Julià de Lòria, Sant Julià de Lòria Parish, Andorra, Andorra

festivals.name: Sant Julià de Lòria Town Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the Andorran town of Sant Julià de Lòria holds its Festa Major in honor of its patron, Saint Vincent. A lively program of concerts, dances, sports, and family events fills several days, bringing every generation of the parish together for one of the high points of the summer in the Pyrenees. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sant Julià de Lòria Transhumance Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each late June, the town of Sant Julià de Lòria celebrates the great seasonal move of the flocks with a transhumance festival. Shepherds lead cows and horses along the old routes to the high pastures, in a celebration that revives a practice once vital to the mountain life of Andorra. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: La Massana, Sant Julià de Lòria, Andorra
festivals.address: La Massana, Sant Julià de Lòria, Andorra

festivals.name: Santa Ana Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the city of Tudela honors its patron, Santa Ana, with a week of festivities. The opening chupinazo rocket, charanga bands, traditional dances, bullfights, and the giants' parade fill the days, in a celebration that blends Catholic devotion with the town's love of festivity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Santa Cruz de Tenerife Carnival
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each winter, the city of Santa Cruz de Tenerife throws one of the most spectacular carnivals in Spain. Dazzling feathered costumes, towering carnival queens, and pounding murga bands fill the streets for weeks of dancing before Lent, in a celebration alive with the color and rhythm of Latin America. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Santa Cruz de Tenerife, Tenerife, Canary Islands, Spain
festivals.address: Santa Cruz de Tenerife, Tenerife, Canary Islands, Spain

festivals.name: Santa Iria Fair
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each autumn, the Algarve fishing town of Olhão holds the Santa Iria Fair, its main religious celebration for over five centuries. Ten days of processions, Masses, and a traditional fairground honor the town's patron saint, in a festival that joins deep local devotion with the easy festivity of an old country fair. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Santa Lucia Kayak Parade
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each December 13, the harbor of Copenhagen glows with the Santa Lucia Kayak Parade. Paddlers carry the light of St. Lucy across the water on the dark winter night, in a celebration that gives a Nordic tradition of light a graceful new form on the sea. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Santa Tecla Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the city of Tarragona honors its patron, Saint Thecla, with twelve days of the richest folklore in Catalonia. Towering human castells, parading giants, fire-breathing beasts, and the old processional dances fill the streets, in a celebration the city has kept since 1321 and prizes above all others. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Santomera Moors and Christians Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the town of Santomera stages a week-long Moors and Christians festival. Camps, mock battles, and ceremonies recall events from the town's past, in a celebration that weaves folklore, historical memory, and devotion into the heart of local identity. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sarajevo Film Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the city of Sarajevo becomes a hub of world cinema for the largest film festival in southeastern Europe. Born during the siege of the 1990s as an act of defiance and hope, it now draws stars and filmmakers from around the globe, in a celebration that turned a city's wartime courage into a cultural beacon. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sarajevo, Bosniak Federation, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Sarajevo, Bosniak Federation, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Sartiglia (Equestrian Joust)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each Carnival in the Sardinian town of Oristano, masked horsemen in eerie, expressionless masks gallop full speed down the course of Sa Sartiglia, trying to spear a small dangling star with their swords. Led by the ceremonial Su Componidori, the daring riders carry on a jousting tradition with Spanish and medieval roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Savonlinna Opera Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the medieval castle of Olavinlinna rises from the waters of Lake Saimaa to host one of the world's most magical opera festivals. Audiences fill the stone courtyard for grand productions beneath the ramparts, in a Finnish tradition founded by a famous soprano in a setting like no other. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Savoy Cheese Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, a different alpine village of Savoie hosts the Fête des Fromages de Savoie. The region's eight protected cheeses, from Beaufort to Reblochon, are celebrated amid the high pastures, in a festival that moves each year to show off the terroir of the Savoyard Alps. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Scalloway Fire Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each January the old Shetland capital of Scalloway holds its Fire Festival, as residents carry blazing torches through the night behind a Guizer Jarl, ending in the burning of a replica Viking galley at the harbor. Rooted in Shetland's Norse heritage, it is among the earliest in the islands' chain of fire festivals. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Schaan Family Festival (Circus & Children’s Music Workshops)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year the town of Schaan holds its Familienfest, a community festival of circus acts, clowns, children's music workshops, and a family concert. A collaboration between the TAK theater and the municipality, it is a beloved way to close the season together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Schifflange Cavalcade
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each Carnival season the town of Schifflange holds its Cavalcade, a parade of costumed groups, floats, and music winding through the streets as part of the local Fuesend. Tied to Luxembourg's pre-Lenten festivities, it draws families and community clubs together in festive color. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Schueberfouer Funfair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer, Luxembourg City hosts the Schueberfouer, the country's largest funfair, a tradition founded by the blind king John back in 1340. Hundreds of rides, games, and stalls of local treats fill the great Glacis field, in a festival grown from a medieval market into the joyful heart of the Luxembourg calendar. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Schwarzenberg Alp Day
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the village of Schwarzenberg in the Bregenzerwald welcomes its cattle down from the summer heights with Alp Day. The whole village turns out as farmers lead the herds to the valley with a blessing, in a celebration rooted in the old three-stage farming of the Vorarlberg Alps. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Schwarzenberg Market Day
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September the village center of Schwarzenberg fills with its historic market day, a fair of craft and farmers' stalls, regional foods, and folk music. Begun centuries ago as a churchyard market, the Schwarzenberger Markt now showcases the artisans and produce of the Bregenzerwald. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Schöngrabern Folk Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year the youth and volunteer clubs of Schöngrabern put on the Brauchtumskirtag, a small-town folk festival of food, drink, and good company. In Lower Austria the Kirtag marks local heritage and community ties, and Schöngrabern's gathers the village in that spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sciacca Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each late winter, the Sicilian town of Sciacca stages one of the island's grand carnivals. Massive papier-mâché floats and costumed masked groups parade through the streets before Lent, in a celebration descended from old fertility festivities that bursts with music and satire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sciacca, Sciacca (Province of Agrigento, Sicily), Italy
festivals.address: Sciacca, Sciacca (Province of Agrigento, Sicily), Italy

festivals.name: Sea of August Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the beach of Praia Formosa on Santa Maria fills with music for the Maré de Agosto. World, jazz, rock, and fado play across stages by the sea, in a celebration begun in 1984 by Azorean artists that turned the island into a creative hub. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vila do Porto, Santa Maria Island, Portugal
festivals.address: Vila do Porto, Santa Maria Island, Portugal

festivals.name: Sea of Rhythms International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the seaside town of Balchik hosts the Sea of Rhythms international folklore festival. More than a thousand performers bring traditional costume, dance, and music to the stage, in a celebration of the rich folklore of the Dobruja Black Sea coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Senhor de Matosinhos Festivals
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each spring, the coastal town of Matosinhos holds its great festival in honor of the Senhor de Matosinhos, a much-revered image of the crucified Christ kept in its old shrine. Deep Catholic devotion mixes with a lively fair of rides and food, in one of the largest and most beloved pilgrimages of northern Portugal. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates. Core date (2027-05-18) is a fixed annual date.

festivals.name: Senj International Summer Carnival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each first week of August, the coastal town of Senj fills its center with a summer carnival. A colorful street parade, music, and dancing brighten the seaside town, in a celebration that carries Senj's long carnival tradition into the height of summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Senj, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia
festivals.address: Senj, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia

festivals.name: Sesimbra Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each carnival, the seaside town of Sesimbra throws one of Portugal's liveliest pre-Lenten festivals. Samba schools, axé groups, and a vast procession of clowns fill the streets, in a celebration deeply rooted in the country's Catholic carnival tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Session Opening of the Cologne Carnival
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · At eleven minutes past eleven on the eleventh of November, the city of Cologne erupts to launch its carnival season. Costumed crowds pack the Old Town squares, shouting "Kölle Alaaf!" as the famous Fifth Season officially begins, months before the great Rose Monday parade, in a joyous citywide rite of welcome. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Seto Dance Festival "Kad’ah kavvõkõsõ’"
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the town of Värska gathers dancers from across Setomaa for the Seto Dance Festival. Troupes from the Seto region and neighboring lands bring their traditional steps to the gathering, in a celebration of dancing that is woven into the seasonal life of the Seto people. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Seto Kingdom Day
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the village of Obinitsa hosts Seto Kingdom Day, the great gathering of the Seto people. Leelo singing, dance, crafts, and contests for the best artisan, brewer, and baker fill the day as a regent is chosen, in a celebration begun in 1994 to reclaim and renew Seto identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Seto Lace Days
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each year, the town of Värska celebrates the delicate craft of Seto lace with Lace Days. Lacemakers gather to show their intricate geometric work and pass the skill on, in a celebration of a traditional craft long woven into the costumes and life of the Seto people. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Seto Song Festival (Leelo Day)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the Seto people of southeastern Estonia gather in Värska for Leelopäev, a great day of their ancient polyphonic singing. Hundreds of singers from the villages join in a massed concert and procession of the leelo, a UNESCO-honored chant in which a lead voice is answered by the whole choir. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Seurasaari Christmas Path
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each early December, the island of Seurasaari in Helsinki lights a Christmas path for families. Walkers follow a glowing, themed trail through the winter woods, in a free celebration begun by volunteers in the 1990s that keeps Finnish Christmas folklore alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: SevEtnoFest (Sevastopol Festival of National Cultures)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Over two days the city of Sevastopol celebrates its many peoples with SevEtnoFest, a festival of folk music and dance, ethnographic exhibitions, and workshops by Russian, Ukrainian, Crimean Tatar, Greek, Armenian, and other groups. The festival reflects the long-mixed population of the port city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sheep Shearing Festival (Koura)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each late spring, the mountain villages above Souda mark the start of summer with the Koura, the sheep-shearing festival. Shepherds gather to shear the flocks for the first time after winter, in a communal celebration that turns an old pastoral task into a Cretan feast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sheep's Wool Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn the Kashubian Ethnographic Park at Wdzydze Kiszewskie celebrates sheep farming and wool-spinning with the Święto Owczej Wełny. Kashubian villages long raised sheep and prized their wool crafts, and the festival keeps the shearing, spinning, and weaving traditions in living view. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Shevchenko Fest
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the village of Morintsi honors Ukraine's national poet Taras Shevchenko in the place of his birth. Art and culture fill She.Fest in the Cherkasy countryside, in a celebration of the poet who stands at the very center of Ukrainian identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Shkodra Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Shkodër fills with masks and parades for its week-long carnival. Daily processions and public performances mark the days before Lent, in a celebration dating to the late 19th century that draws on the city's blended, multi-religious heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Shkodër, Northern Albania (Gheg) - Shkodër County, Albania
festivals.address: Shkodër, Northern Albania (Gheg) - Shkodër County, Albania

festivals.name: Shkodër Carp Casserole Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · First held in 2024, Shkodër's carp casserole festival celebrates tavë krapi, the city's signature dish of carp baked in an earthenware pan, with a culinary competition, music, and crafts. The dish is a symbol of the city's lakeside fishing culture, honored here in a day-long celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Shpirag Village Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each late June, the villages around Shpirag near Berat gather for the Fshatçe n'Shpirag festival. Folk music, a fair of artisanal products, and tours of historic sites fill two days, in a celebration that draws on the Albanian tradition of the village gathering in these scenic mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Shrimp and Fishing Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each year the harbor town of Honfleur celebrates its catch of grey shrimp with the Fête de la Crevette et de la Pêche, a festival of the fishing community. The event underscores Honfleur's long identity as a Norman fishing port, with the fresh catch at the heart of the day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sibelius Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Lahti honors Finland's great composer with the Sibelius Festival. The Lahti Symphony Orchestra fills Sibelius Hall with symphonies and chamber works over three days, in a celebration that ties local audiences to the music of Jean Sibelius. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sigulda Children’s and Youth Literature Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year the town of Sigulda holds a three-day literature festival for children and young people, with workshops, readings, and interactive events that include a school day and a family day. The festival promotes local writing and reflects the town's investment in young readers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Silence Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each summer, the remote village of Kaukonen hosts the Silence Festival of circus and classical music. Performers from Finland and abroad play in an intimate setting by the Ounasjoki River as the Arctic nature blooms, in a celebration that draws on Lapland's wild quiet and its value of silence. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Silver Dance Groups Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each year, the town of Ilirska Bistrica gives the stage to its elders for the Silver Dance Groups festival. Senior folk ensembles perform the traditional dances and songs of the region, in a celebration that keeps older residents active and passes their living tradition on to the young. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Silves Medieval Fair
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Algarve town of Silves turns back the clock beneath its great red sandstone castle for a nine-day medieval fair. Knights, merchants, and musicians fill the old streets in period costume, reviving the days when Silves was a jewel of Moorish and then Christian rule in southern Portugal. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Singing the Janeiras (Epiphany Songs)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · On the first Saturday of January, folklore groups parade through the historic heart of Porto singing the Janeiras, the old Epiphany carols that welcome the new year. The tradition of singing these January songs runs deep in Portugal, filling the downtown streets with voices and music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sint Jansklooster Flower Parade
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each mid-August, the village of Sint Jansklooster parades floats covered in dahlias for its flower corso. Elaborately decorated wagons travel through the village by day and night, in a celebration that carries the beloved Dutch bloemencorso tradition with the village's own community pride. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sintra Medieval Fair
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year, the heart of Sintra becomes a medieval marketplace for the Sintra Medieval Fair. Artisans demonstrate old crafts, merchants sell honey, cheese, and roasted meats, and troubadours play in the square, in a celebration of the town's deep medieval and Moorish heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sintra, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal
festivals.address: Sintra, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal

festivals.name: Sipplingen Corpus Christi Flower-Carpet Procession
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · On Corpus Christi the Upper Swabian village of Sipplingen lays one of the region's largest flower carpets, a floral path running hundreds of meters through the streets, before the procession follows the consecrated Host. Villagers begin before dawn, in a display of baroque Catholic devotion that still runs strong here. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Sitges Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the seaside town of Sitges throws one of Europe's most flamboyant carnivals, a riot of feathers, sequins, and dazzling drag. Two grand parades light up the streets before Lent, in a famously joyful and welcoming celebration that has made this Catalan resort a carnival capital. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Situla Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the town of Novo mesto celebrates its Iron Age past with the Situla Festival. The festival honors the decorated bronze situlae found in local graves, in a celebration of the rich Hallstatt heritage that makes this Slovenian town a treasure house of ancient art. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Siófok Fish Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the lakeside town of Siófok celebrates Balaton's fish with a festival on its main square. Halászlé fish soup and fried fish draw the crowds amid live music and family fun, in a celebration of the freshwater cuisine of Hungary's largest lake. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sjenica Agricultural Fair
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each mid-June, the high plateau town of Sjenica hosts an agricultural fair. Livestock breeders, farmers, and craftspeople gather to show their animals and wares on the Sjeničko-pešterska plateau, in a celebration of the pastoral and farming traditions of this upland heartland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Skagen Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, the harbor town of Skagen hosts Denmark's oldest folk music festival. Singers and bands fill the northern port with song, in a celebration founded in 1971 as a song festival that has gathered music lovers at the country's northern tip ever since. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Skampa International Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the city of Elbasan hosts the Skampa International Theatre Festival at the Teatri Skampa. Albanian and international companies bring contemporary drama to the stage, in a celebration founded in 1999 that has grown into one of Albania's oldest and largest theatre events. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Skvulp Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the harbor town of Holbæk celebrates its waterfront with the Skvulp festival. Music, art, food, and family activities fill the harborfront with free performances, in a celebration that draws on the close bond between this fjord town and the sea at its door. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Skábmagovat Indigenous Peoples’ Film Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each January, the village of Inari hosts Skábmagovat, the world's northernmost indigenous film festival. Sámi and other indigenous cinema fills the dark Arctic winter, in a celebration begun in 1999 to give Sámi film a home and connect indigenous communities across the globe. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Skájdde Sami Culture Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each late July, the Storforsen nature reserve near Älvsbyn hosts the two-day Skájdde festival of Sami culture. Storytelling, music, and communal gathering fill the days by the great rapids, in a celebration that brings the old Sami tradition of meeting and sharing into a modern setting. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sladjan “Slado” Tijanić Memorial (Folklore Dances & Gusle, Plužine)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year the mountain town of Plužine holds a memorial folklore event in honor of the beloved local musician Sladjan Tijanić, with traditional dances and the song of the gusle performed by regional groups. The gathering keeps the cultural legacy of the Rijeka Piva community alive in his memory. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Slaninijada (Bacon Festival)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the Banat village of Kačarevo hosts Slaninijada, Serbia's largest festival of smoked meats. Hundreds of producers and thousands of visitors gather for four days of food, contests, and entertainment, in a celebration of the centuries-old bacon-making of this multiethnic village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Slavonia and Podravina, WINE NOT!?
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the towns of Virovitica-Podravina County celebrate their wines and food with 'Slavonija i Podravina, WINE NOT!?'. Tastings, gourmet pairings, and events at scenic vineyards fill several weeks, in a celebration of the region's age-old winemaking and warm hospitality. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Slinningsodden Bonfire
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each midsummer, the headland of Slinningsodden near Ålesund blazes with one of the world's tallest bonfires. Built ever higher each year, the Slinningsbålet once set a record at over 47 meters, in a celebration of the Norwegian St. Hans rooted in ancient solstice fires. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Slovak Culture Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, around Ukraine's Independence Day, the city of Uzhgorod celebrates Slovak culture with a folklore festival. Choirs, folk dancers, and a heritage exhibition fill the open-air folk museum, in a celebration that strengthens the ties between Ukrainians and the region's Slovak community. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Uzhgorod, Slovak minority of Zakarpattia, Ukraine
festivals.address: Uzhgorod, Slovak minority of Zakarpattia, Ukraine

festivals.name: Slovenian Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the town of Ravne na Koroškem hosts the long-running Festival of Slovene Jazz. Dozens of artists and ensembles play over several days, in a celebration mounted by local jazz societies that has become a driving force in the town's cultural life. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Slovenská Veselica Slovak Folk Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the village of Storozhnytsia celebrates Slovak folk music and arts with the Slovenská Veselica. Cimbalom bands, dancers, and choirs perform amid stalls of Slovak crafts and food, in a celebration of the heritage of the significant Slovak minority of Zakarpattia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Uzhgorod, Slovak community of Zakarpattia, Ukraine
festivals.address: Uzhgorod, Slovak community of Zakarpattia, Ukraine

festivals.name: Smetana Days
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each spring, the city of Plzeň fills its halls with classical music for Smetana Days, honoring the great Czech composer Bedřich Smetana. A month of concerts and lectures, capped by a folk Masopust carnival, brings the romantic music of a national icon to one of Bohemia's leading cultural cities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Smoliansky Kulesh
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the Donetsk countryside celebrates Ukrainian culture with the Smoliansky Kulesh festival. Named for the thick kulish porridge, it brings embroidered costumes, folk music, Cossack games, and craft markets to the village, in a celebration of the revival of rural Ukrainian tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast (Illinivka rural community), Ukraine
festivals.address: Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast (Illinivka rural community), Ukraine

festivals.name: Sneek Week
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Frisian town of Sneek holds Sneekweek, one of the oldest and largest inland sailing festivals in Europe. By day, fleets of sailboats race across the Sneekermeer lake, and by night the old town fills with music and festivity, in a beloved celebration of Friesland's deep bond with the water. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Soboški Days Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each late June, the town of Murska Sobota fills with arts for the Soboški Days. Classical concerts in the Evangelical church, family street performances on the castle platform, and large stage concerts spread across the city, in a celebration of the Hungarian-flavored culture of Prekmurje. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sochos Carnival "Meriou"
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each carnival, the village of Sochos near Thessaloniki fills with the deafening Koudounoforoi. Some three thousand villagers don goatskins and great bells and shake the streets with sound, in a celebration descended from ancient fertility rites and the agrarian past of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Solothurn Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Solothurn goes into a festive 'state of emergency' for its Fasnacht. Masks, music, and parades fill the old town for days as tens of thousands celebrate, in a pre-Lenten carnival that locals call the fifth season of the year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Song of the People Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August, the city of Valletta hosts L-Għanja tal-Poplu, the Song of the People. New Maltese-language songs are performed and judged in a contest and showcase, in a celebration held since 1973 that honors the Maltese tongue and its songwriters. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Song of the Sibyl
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · On Christmas Eve, the churches of Mallorca fall silent for the Cant de la Sibil·la, an ancient chant of the Final Judgment sung by a single robed voice holding a sword. Lit only by candles, the haunting medieval song has echoed through the island's Christmas Mass since the Middle Ages, and UNESCO now guards it. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sopron Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Sopron celebrates its winemaking with a festival crowned by the St. Urban procession. Tastings of local wines, brass bands, crafts, and traditional food fill the days, in a celebration of one of Hungary's oldest wine regions, famed for its red Kékfrankos. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sopron Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Erzsébet-kert garden in Sopron fills with festivity for the wine harvest days. Grape-harvest tradition, folk culture, and local wine come together in the scenic park, in a celebration of the historic Hungarian vintage and the community's wine culture. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: SopronFest
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Sopron fills its squares and venues with music, theater, and art for SopronFest. Local and international performers play across the city in a wide-ranging program, in a celebration launched as the city's first major summer festival to showcase its cultural life. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sorru in Musica Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the mountain villages of Les Deux Sorru in Corsica fill with music for Sorru in Musica. Classical works and Corsican polyphony share the program across the high hamlets, in a celebration created to bring both traditions to the island's rural communities. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vico, Les Deux Sorru (Southwestern Corsica), France
festivals.address: Vico, Les Deux Sorru (Southwestern Corsica), France

festivals.name: Sound Spring Music Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each May, the Burgenland town of Stadtschlaining, known as the city of peace, fills with music for the KLANGfrühling festival. Contemporary classical and world music gather in the historic town, in a celebration whose blend of sounds from many cultures echoes the town's vocation as a center for peace. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sounds of the Trumpet from Timok
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the town of Knjaževac fills its square with the blare of brass for the Sounds of the Trumpet from Timok. Trumpet orchestras play folk and pop sets amid exhibitions and festivity, in a celebration that honors the long tradition of brass bands in the region's celebrations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: SPIDER Festival (Radical Bodies)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, Ljubljana's Tivoli Park hosts the SPIDER festival of contemporary dance and performance. Avant-garde, queer, and feminist works fill the open-air stages, in a celebration founded around 2010 that grew from local dance workshops into a showcase of bold, experimental movement. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Ljubljana, Ljubljana contemporary dance community, Slovenia
festivals.address: Ljubljana, Ljubljana contemporary dance community, Slovenia

festivals.name: Spirit of Mother Jones Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, Cork's Shandon quarter honors the labor leader Mary Harris with the Spirit of Mother Jones festival. Three free days of speeches, films, heritage walks, and music recall her fight for justice, in a celebration of the Cork-born woman who became a famous organizer in America. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Spirit of the Unbroken
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the Volyn agricultural museum at Rokyni hosts the patriotic Spirit of the Unbroken festival. Historical combat, hopak dance battles, archery, and knife-throwing fill two days, in a celebration that revives Cossack and Polissya martial heritage in a time of war. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sprintfest
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · During the Isle of Man TT race week, the town of Ramsey hosts Sprintfest, a multi-night music festival of crowd favorites and new acts. Created by the town to entertain visitors and locals alike during the famous races, it fills the evenings with live music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St David’s Day Parade (Haverfordwest)
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each March 1, the town of Haverfordwest fills its streets with schoolchildren for the St David's Day parade. Pupils from many local schools march waving flags and singing Welsh songs, in a celebration that voices the pride of Pembrokeshire's Welsh-speaking community. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: St. Blaise Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each February 3, the walled city of Dubrovnik honors its patron, Saint Blaise, in a tradition kept since medieval times. White-clad standard-bearers and clergy carry the saint's relics in a grand procession through the old stone streets, in a much-loved celebration at the very heart of the city's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Demetrius Driving-In Festival and Crane Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the Hortobágy puszta celebrates its herding heritage with the Crane and Driving-In Festival. Herdsmen drive cattle and horses from the open pastures to their winter quarters as great flocks of cranes pass overhead, in a celebration of the ancient pastoral life of the Great Plain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Hortobágy, Great Hungarian Plain (Northern Great Plain), Hungary
festivals.address: Hortobágy, Great Hungarian Plain (Northern Great Plain), Hungary

festivals.name: St. George’s Day Parade
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each April the Scouts and Guides of Gibraltar march in uniform down Main Street for St. George's Day, the patron saint of scouting, exercising their Freedom of the City. The parade winds from Casemates Square to The Convent, a long-standing tradition in this British-tied territory. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. George’s Patronal Feast (Värska Orthodox Church)
festivals.description: Early May 2027 · Each May 6, the village of Värska keeps St. George's Day as the patronal feast of its Orthodox church. A special liturgy gives way to an afternoon kirmaski, the village party, in a celebration that joins Orthodox devotion to the folk festivity of the Seto people. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: St. Hans (Gamle Bergen Museum Bonfire Night)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each Midsummer Eve, the Old Bergen Museum by the sea hosts a St. Hans bonfire night. Traditional games, a barrel organ, costumed witches and fortune-tellers, and a great seaside bonfire fill the evening, in a free family celebration of the Norwegian midsummer near the water. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: St. Hans (Hella Midsummer Bonfire Night)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On Midsummer's Eve the Hella beach near Tromsø gathers for St. Hans, with bonfires, rømmegrøt and sausages and waffles, children's games, and music hosted at the Hellastua café. Sankthansaften runs deep in Norwegian tradition, and here it celebrates the bright nights of the far-northern summer. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: St. Johann Dumpling Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the town of St. Johann in Tirol sets a 595-meter table down its main street for the Knödelfest. More than twenty thousand savory and sweet dumplings from dozens of recipes are served along the world's longest dumpling table, in a celebration begun in 1982 of a Tyrolean staple. · Dates are provisional and may change.
festivals.location: St. Johann in Tirol, Tyrolean, Austria
festivals.address: St. Johann in Tirol, Tyrolean, Austria

festivals.name: St. John and St. Peter's Fire Jumping
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, on the feasts of St. John and St. Peter, the people of Sorobon leap over bonfires to Kriyo rhythms. The fire-jumping marks the late-June saint's days with dance and daring, in a celebration that carries the indigenous and African influences woven into the culture of Bonaire. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: St. John's Festival of Porto
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On the night of June 23, the city of Porto erupts into the Festa de São João, one of Portugal's wildest street parties. Neighbors grill sardines, light bonfires, and famously bop each other on the head with soft plastic hammers, dancing till dawn in a midsummer celebration with ancient solstice roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. John's Festivities of Braga
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the city of Braga throws itself into São João, a lively midsummer festival for Saint John the Baptist. The streets fill with folk dancing, concerts, and the cheerful customs of the night, from gifting pots of basil to bopping passersby with little plastic hammers, in a century-old celebration of the solstice. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Luke's Fair of Hoznayo
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Around St. Luke's Day in October, the Cantabrian village of Hoznayo holds its autumn fair, with livestock exhibitions, craft and food stalls, and cultural events over several days. The Feria de San Lucas is an important tradition for the Valles de Toranzo, honoring the rhythms of farm and herd. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Martin's Day Fair (Vas Open-Air Museum)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each St. Martin's Day, the Vas County open-air museum near Szombathely marks the end of the farming year. Artisans, goose dishes, lantern customs, and the new újbor wine fill the festival, in a celebration of the rural traditions of the Vas region as autumn turns to winter. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: St. Martin’s Day Wine Festival (Novo mesto)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Around St. Martin's Day the grounds of Grm Castle near Novo mesto host Martinovo, a one-day autumn festival marking the moment when the new must becomes wine. The festival honors Dolenjska's winemaking heritage, especially its Cviček, in the communal spirit of St. Martin's feast. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: St. Martin’s Lantern Procession
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Around St. Martin's Day, children in Groningen carry homemade lanterns behind St. Martin on horseback and his Roman soldiers, walking from the Hereplein through the old town to the Martinikerk. The evening ends with the saint's story, choir song, and hot chocolate against the November cold. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Martin’s New Wine Festival and Goose Feast (Szentendre Open-Air Museum)
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each St. Martin's weekend, the open-air museum at Szentendre opens its gates for a feast of new wine and goose. Visitors taste the first újbor and gather for the season's roast goose among the old village houses, in a celebration of a Hungarian tradition of marking the year's first wine. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: St. Matthew’s International Organ Music Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Church of St. Matthew in Anykščiai fills with the sound of the organ. International and Lithuanian organists give a series of concerts of sacred and classical music, in a celebration that ties the town's musical life to the worship and heritage of the church. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Nicholas Day (Children’s Ceremony & Blessing, Monaco)
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · On December 6 Monaco honors St. Nicholas, patron of sailors and children, with a children's ceremony at City Hall where schoolchildren join officials and clergy for games and symbolic gifts. First venerated by Monaco's medieval sailors, the saint is now celebrated above all for the young. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: St. Roman's Day (Cathedral Mass & Folk Dancing, Monaco)
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August 9, the principality of Monaco honors Saint Roman with a cathedral Mass and folk festivity. Music and dancing in the Jardins Saint-Martin lead to a solemn Mass attended by the princely family, in a celebration of a martyr venerated in Monaco since 1543. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Rupert's Fair
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the squares around Salzburg's cathedral fill with the Rupertikirtag, a six-day folk fair honoring the city's patron, Saint Rupert. Carousels, market stalls, and beer tents draw crowds in dirndl and lederhosen, in a traditional kirtag tied to the old festival of the cathedral's consecration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: St. Sebastian's Day – The Botarga and the Jester
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each year, the village of Mohernando honors its patron, St. Sebastian, with the botarga and the palace jester. The costumed botarga and the unique 'bufón' bring folk color to the religious feast, in a celebration that joins old masquerade custom to the devotion of this Guadalajara village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Stammersdorf Sturm New Wine Days
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each early October the Viennese wine village of Stammersdorf celebrates its Sturm days along the Kellergasse, the lane of wine cellars, with music, food, and tastings of the first fermenting wine of the year. A traditional wine village in the city's 21st district, Stammersdorf marks the new vintage in cellar-lane style. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Stanisław Moniuszko Polish Song and Dance Festival
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each November the town of Wieliszew holds the Stanisław Moniuszko Polish Song and Dance Festival, gathering local folk ensembles for patriotic performances. The festival showcases regional folk dances and songs from across Poland, reinforcing a shared sense of national tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Statia Carnival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the little Caribbean island of Statia bursts into two weeks of carnival, with road marches, pageants, and music spilling through Oranjestad. Soca and reggae drive the dancing, queens are crowned, and the island celebrates its freedom and heritage, in the high point of the Statian year. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Statia Emancipation Day
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July 1, the island of Statia marks Emancipation Day, remembering the end of slavery in the Dutch empire in 1863. Ceremonies, historical reenactments, and a food fair gather in Wilhelmina Park, honoring the freedom and the Afro-Caribbean heritage of the island's people. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Statia Roots Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2026 · Each year, the island of Sint Eustatius celebrates its Jewish and Afro-Caribbean heritage with the Statia Roots Festival. Weeks of culture and history honor the island's layered past, in a celebration that draws on the deep legacy of a place once a great plantation and trading hub. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Steamboat Arrival & City Parade
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, Amsterdam fills its canals and streets to welcome Sinterklaas, the beloved Dutch gift-bringer who arrives by steamboat as if sailing in from Spain. Children line the route for the cheerful parade that follows, marking the joyful start of the long Dutch holiday season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Steamboat Arrival & City Parade
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November, the Dutch city of Rotterdam welcomes Sinterklaas with a festive steamboat arrival and a great city parade. Crowds of children greet the saint and his helpers as he comes ashore, in a celebration that heralds the gift-giving season, part of the beloved nationwide Sinterklaas tradition. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Steinbach Perchten Demons' Night
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 5, the village of Steinbach an der Steyr fills with fearsome Schiachperchten. Hundreds of masked demons march through the square with bells, torches, and horsehair whips to drive out winter's evil, in a celebration rooted in the Raunächte folklore of the Twelve Nights. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Steinbach an der Steyr, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria
festivals.address: Steinbach an der Steyr, Upper and Lower Austria, Austria

festivals.name: Steinkjer Market Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each early August the town of Steinkjer fills with Steinkjermartnan, a summer market festival of hundreds of vendors, food stalls, folk musicians, and a fairground that draws tens of thousands. The market grew from the old Scandinavian trading fairs, when farmers and traders gathered in the town. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Steyr Music Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the medieval Austrian city of Steyr stages grand open-air musicals in the moat of its hilltop castle. Beneath the old walls, lavish productions draw audiences for weeks, in a festival that has filled the castle grounds with song and spectacle since 1995. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Stockach Fools' Court
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each carnival, the town of Stockach holds its centuries-old Fools' Court. The local jesters put a prominent politician on symbolic trial for folly, in a Swabian-Alemannic celebration said to rest on a medieval promise by a duke, defended with great wit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Stonehaven Fireballs Ceremony
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · At midnight on Hogmanay, the Scottish town of Stonehaven blazes as dozens of locals march down the High Street swinging great balls of fire on chains around their heads. The spinning fireballs light up the New Year's sky in a dramatic old seaport ritual said to burn away the evils of the year gone by. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Stork Day (Haslach)
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each February 22 the town of Haslach keeps Storchentag, as children parade behind the costumed Storchenvater, who wears wooden storks on his hat and carries bread on his back. Likely dating to the 17th century, the Stork Day is tied to the feast of St. Peter and the first stirrings of spring. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Haslach im Kinzigtal, Ortenaukreis (Black Forest), Germany
festivals.address: Haslach im Kinzigtal, Ortenaukreis (Black Forest), Germany

festivals.name: Storming of Tykocin Castle
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter, the town of Tykocin recreates the storming of its castle in the year 1657. Costumed fighters drill and march to revive the Swedish siege broken by Hetman Sapieha, in a reenactment celebration that brings the drama of the Swedish Deluge back to the old stronghold on the Narew. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Storsjö Yacht (Östersund Festival)
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the lakeside town of Östersund throws Storsjöyran, fondly known as Yran, one of the largest city festivals in Scandinavia. Downtown streets and parks fill with concerts and crowds for two days under the long northern light, in a celebration that has been the pride of Jämtland since 1983. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Storsjöyran Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Östersund holds Storsjöyran, a two-day music festival that grew from an old local celebration into one of Sweden's biggest. With tongue in cheek, the festival crowns the region a republic of its own for the weekend, filling the lakeside city with concerts, costumes, and Jämtland pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Straume Hip Hop Music Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year the Zeit Creative Quarter in Līgatne hosts the two-day Straume hip-hop festival, with top Latvian rap artists on a big stage, breakdance battles, and DJ sets. Grown from Riga's hip-hop scene, it has become a summer gathering for rap fans across the Vidzeme region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Straw Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, the mountain fields of Métabief fill with music for the Festival de la Paille. Three days of rock, pop, and folk play in the open air at the foot of the Jura, in a celebration founded by local young people in 2000 that takes its name from its rural straw-field home. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Street Theatre & Acrobatics Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the historic streets of Deventer become open-air stages for Deventer Op Stelten, a festival of street theatre and acrobatics. Performers from around the world fill the squares with daring stunts and surprising shows, turning the city into a playground for the arts since 1993. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Street Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the Austrian city of Graz hands its squares and parks over to La Strada, a festival of street theatre, puppetry, and modern circus. Performers from near and far fill the public spaces with playful, daring shows, turning the whole city into an open-air stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Student Carnival & Workers’ Day
festivals.description: Late April 2027 · Each year on the last evening of April, Finland erupts into Vappu, its great spring carnival, when students don white caps and the whole country takes to the streets. Part Workers' Day and part ancient welcome to spring, it fills Helsinki with picnics, sparkling wine, and revelry, in the biggest party of the Finnish year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Stuöries Njuönies Sami Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · First held in 2025 near Umeå, the Stuöries Njuönies festival is a one-day celebration of Sami culture, with talks on traditional food and health, an open stage for Indigenous performers, and evening concerts. Organized by a local Sami association, it reflects the growing revival of Sami culture in Västerbotten. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Styrian Autumn
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Austrian city of Graz hosts steirischer herbst, one of Europe's pioneering festivals of experimental and contemporary art. Founded in 1968, it fills the city with bold theatre, performance, music, and exhibitions, challenging audiences with the newest and most daring work in the arts. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sudovia’s Dowry Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the city of Marijampolė celebrates the harvest with Sūduvos kraitė, the Dowry of Sudovia. An open-air fair of crafts and produce honors the farmers and artisans of the region, in a celebration of the rich agrarian culture of the fertile Suvalkija country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sult Literature Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the town of Grimstad gathers writers for a four-day literature festival. Once named for Ibsen and Hamsun, both tied to the town, and renamed Sult in 2025, it brings Norwegian and international authors together, in a celebration of the literary heritage that runs through this coastal town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Summar Festivalurin
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the Faroese town of Klaksvík hosts Summar Festivalurin, the largest music festival in the islands. Pop and rock bands from the Faroes and abroad fill the open-air stages, drawing crowds to a celebration of music and long summer light that has grown from a single concert in 2004. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Summer on the Sana (River Festival & Bridge Jumps)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the town of Sanski Most celebrates the season with Ljeto na Sani. Concerts, dance, exhibitions, and daring jumps from the bridge into the Sana River fill the weeks, in a celebration begun in 1997 to gather the community and honor local life along the river. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sanski Most, Bosniak Federation, Una-Sana Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Sanski Most, Bosniak Federation, Una-Sana Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Summer on the Vrbas (River Festival & Bridge Diving)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Banja Luka celebrates its green river with Ljeto na Vrbasu, a festival of concerts, open-air cinema, and dancing along the water. Its famous high point is a daring diving contest from the City Bridge into the Vrbas below, in a tradition the city has loved since 1953. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Summer Oracle (Dodoni)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the ancient sanctuary of Dodona near Ioannina hosts the Summer Oracle. Performances, lectures, and acting workshops revive Greek drama at the oldest oracle of Zeus, in a celebration that links the cultural heritage of Dodona to the living art of the theatre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Summer Scene
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Old Town of Ulcinj hosts the open-air Ljetnja scena of performing arts. Theatre and performance unfold on Slaves' Square beneath the medieval Balšić Tower, in a celebration that draws on the town's old-world ambiance to stage the work of local and visiting artists. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sumy Extreme Style
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Each year, the city of Sumy fills with the energy of youth for the Sumy Extreme Style festival. BMX and rollerblading competitions, breakdance battles, and graffiti jams gather the urban scene, in a celebration founded in 2010 that joins extreme sport with street art. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sun in Glass
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the Purkmistr brewery in the Černice quarter of Plzeň hosts the Slunce ve skle beer festival.
