Microbrewery beers and good company fill the day, in a celebration of craft brewing in the city that gave the world Pilsner. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sunny Hill Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the city of Pristina fills with music for the Sunny Hill Festival, founded by the pop star Dua Lipa and her father. Global headliners share the stage with local talent over several days, in a celebration of Albanian pop culture and the confident, outward-looking spirit of Kosovo's young generation. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Super Sint Maarten (Indische Buurt Lantern Parade & Light Sculptures)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · On St. Martin's Day, Amsterdam's Indische Buurt holds the Super Sint Maarten, a neighborhood lantern parade lit by glowing light sculptures. Run by a local foundation since 2011, it grew from a small community idea into a bright, much-loved walk through the November streets. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Suure-Jaani Music Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the town of Suure-Jaani fills its churches and manor gardens with a week of classical and choral music. Concerts celebrate Estonian composers and the international repertoire, in a celebration rooted in the musical heritage of the town that gave Estonia the composer Artur Kapp. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Swedish-Estonian Song and Dance Celebration
festivals.description: Early July 2027 · Each so often, the town of Haapsalu celebrates the heritage of Estonia's coastal Swedes with a song and dance festival. Choral singing, folk dancing, and Swedish-language song fill the historic Bishop's Castle, in a celebration of the Eestirootslased who have lived on this coast for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Haapsalu, Western Estonia and Islands, Estonia
festivals.address: Haapsalu, Western Estonia and Islands, Estonia

festivals.name: Sânziene Flowers Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August the Banat village of Bârsa holds the Sânziene folk festival, opening with a colorful procession through the village and building to a music and dance show by local and regional ensembles in traditional costume. The midsummer sânziene flowers give the festival its name and spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sânzienele Celebration (Village Museum, Bucharest)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June 24, Bucharest's open-air Village Museum celebrates Sânziene, the Romanian midsummer when, by old belief, the heavens open and fairies bless the fields. Young women weave crowns of golden wildflowers and old customs of love and fortune come alive, in a gentle festival marking the start of the rural summer. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: São Martinho's Magusto (Figueiredo)
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · Each November 11, the parish of Figueiredo in Braga gathers outdoors for a São Martinho magusto. Neighbors roast chestnuts and taste the new wine in the autumn air, in a convivial celebration of Saint Martin's Day that marks the close of the harvest across Portugal. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Sélestat Flower Parade
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Sélestat parades giant floats covered entirely in fresh dahlias for the Corso Fleuri. Ten or more themed floats roll through the streets by day and night before crowds, in a celebration of the dahlia-growing horticultural heritage of Alsace. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sélestat Machores Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Alsatian town of Sélestat throws a carnival famous for its Machores, men dressed up as comical old maidens. Rooted in a medieval legend of a young butcher who saved the town by ringing the alarm bells, the celebration has been a cherished local rite for centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Sălaj SlanăFest (Bacon Fest)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each winter the Sălaj village of Șamșud holds SlanăFest, a one-day festival of cured pork fat with a cooking contest in three categories, tastings, and folk music. The festival celebrates the old village practice of pig slaughter and curing through the cold months. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Șamșud, Romanian community of Sălaj, Romania
festivals.address: Șamșud, Romanian community of Sălaj, Romania

festivals.name: Sēlija Rotā (Selonia Folk Art Festival)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the town of Jaunjelgava celebrates the traditional culture of Selonia with Sēlija Rotā. Folk singing, dancing, crafts, and theater fill the day, this year around a wedding theme, in a celebration that gathers the folk ensembles of this distinct Latvian region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Talsi City Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the town of Talsi celebrates itself with a city festival. Concerts, performances, workshops, and food events spread across the town under a yearly theme, in a celebration that brings together the folk traditions and the future hopes of this Kurzeme town. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Tanneron Mimosa Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each last Sunday of January, the village of Tanneron celebrates its golden mimosa. A morning procession, a market, and a grand floral parade of mimosa-decked floats fill the day, in a celebration set in the largest mimosa forest in Provence, ablaze with winter yellow. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Taras Sycha Field Kitchen Memorial Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, around Constitution Day, the Central Park of Vinnytsia fills with cauldrons for the Taras Sycha field-kitchen festival. Cooks at dozens of stations prepare traditional fire-cooked meals for charity, in a celebration that honors a local cook and pacifist who died in the war. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tarpa Plum Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each late September the village of Tarpa celebrates its famed plums and plum pálinka with folk music, a táncház, a plum-cooking contest, jam competitions, and a parade of illuminated floats. Tarpa claims to be the pálinka capital of Szatmár-Bereg, and the plum is its proud heritage crop. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tarragona Human Tower Competition
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Every two years, the Catalan city of Tarragona hosts the world's greatest human-tower competition, when the best colles gather to raise their castells higher and higher. Teams climb onto one another's shoulders, story upon story, until a small child scrambles to the top, in a breathtaking display of Catalan teamwork and nerve. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tartini Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the seaside town of Piran honors its native son, the composer Giuseppe Tartini, with a chamber music festival. Baroque and classical concerts, sometimes played on Tartini's own violin, fill the town, in a celebration of the musical heritage born on this Adriatic coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Taste Svalbard
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each early October the Arctic town of Longyearbyen holds Taste Svalbard, a multi-day culinary festival where local restaurants, producers, and chefs collaborate. With no farming on the islands, the festival makes the most of reindeer, Arctic char, and the imported flavors of the far north. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tastes and Traditions of Bihor
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September the County Museum in Oradea hosts Gusturi și tradiții de Bihor, a fair of local culinary specialties and traditional crafts. Run by the museum and local cultural groups, it celebrates the food and handwork that give the Bihor county its identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tastes of Vojvodina Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the city of Novi Sad celebrates the cuisine of its region with Ukusi Vojvodine, the Tastes of Vojvodina. Local producers and traditional dishes fill the festival, in a celebration of the rich, many-layered food born from the Serbs, Hungarians, Slovaks, Romanians, and Croats of Vojvodina. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Novi Sad, Multiethnic Vojvodina (gastronomic heritage), Serbia
festivals.address: Novi Sad, Multiethnic Vojvodina (gastronomic heritage), Serbia

festivals.name: Tavrida.ART Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the town of Sudak hosts the Tavrida.ART festival for young artists and cultural workers. Around two thousand participants from the Tavrida art cluster gather for workshops and performance, in a celebration created to nurture a new generation of creators on the Crimean coast. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Templar Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the town of Tomar revives its Templar past with the Festa Templária. Medieval pageantry fills the streets of the town founded by the Knights Templar in 1160, in a celebration that brings the order's storied legacy back to life among Tomar's old stones. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Terezijana Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the city of Bjelovar celebrates its founder, Empress Maria Theresa, with the five-day Terezijana. More than a dozen concerts, food fairs, and family events fill the streets, in a celebration of a city laid out in the 18th century under the empress's patronage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Teuta (Old Civic Songs Festival)
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year, the city of Tetovo celebrates the old Albanian city songs with the Teuta festival. Folklore ensembles from across the Albanian-speaking lands perform the classic këngët qytetare as audiences sing along, in a celebration of a cherished urban folk tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Tetovo, Albanian community of Polog region (Tetovo, North Macedonia), North Macedonia
festivals.address: Tetovo, Albanian community of Polog region (Tetovo, North Macedonia), North Macedonia

festivals.name: The Caballada of Atienza
festivals.description: Mid June 2027 · Each Pentecost, the town of Atienza reenacts a daring rescue from the year 1162 with La Caballada. Costumed locals recall the muleteers who spirited the child King Alfonso VIII to safety, in a celebration of national tourist interest that keeps a medieval legend alive. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: The Carochos
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each New Year's Day, the Zamora village of Riofrío de Aliste stages Los Carochos, a winter masquerade of demons in cork masks. The horned Carocho Grande and Carocho Chiquito and their fellow figures run through the village, in a celebration that belongs to the ancient winter masquerades of this corner of Spain. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Riofrío de Aliste, Castile and León, Spain
festivals.address: Riofrío de Aliste, Castile and León, Spain

festivals.name: The Chíviri Festival
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · At midnight after Easter Sunday, young men of Trujillo in regional dress dance frantically through the main square with heavy tambourines, singing the Canto del Chíviri at the foot of the castle. Once a rite of passage, El Chíviri now marks the joyful end of Lent in the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Dancing Egg
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each Corpus Christi, the churches of Barcelona set an egg dancing atop their fountain jets for L'Ou com Balla. The hollow egg spins on the rising water as if it were alive, in a curious celebration of medieval roots unique to Catalonia and a few nearby places. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: The Fifth Tower Medieval Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Around Moldova's Independence Day, Soroca Fortress is turned into a living-history spectacle of knightly jousts, archery, period crafts, and music for the Fifth Tower festival. A symbol of Moldovan medieval history, the fortress carries visitors back into its own storied past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Gibraltar Fair
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, Gibraltar marks the end of summer with its annual fair, a few days of amusement rides, games, food stalls, and entertainment. Simply known as the Fair, it brings the whole community together in a cheerful tradition that has become a fixture of the Rock's late-summer calendar. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Giunta (Easter Sunday Meeting)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · On Easter Sunday afternoon in Caltagirone, a three-meter papier-mâché statue of St. Peter is carried from his church to mediate the joyful reunion of the risen Christ with his mother. The Giunta is the climax of the town's Easter Week, blending devout procession with Sicilian pageantry. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: The Pasada (Cattle Drive) of Carmona
festivals.description: Early November 2026 · Each year the Cantabrian village of Carmona keeps La Pasada, herding livestock along the old droving routes through town in honor of the transhumant farming life. The ceremonial passage of cattle or horses recalls the seasonal movement of herds between pasture and market. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Passion of Cervera
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each Easter, the Catalan town of Cervera stages La Passió, a Passion play acting out the last days of Christ over several spring afternoons. Performed by local actors in the town's churches and theatre, it has carried on since the 1500s, in one of Catalonia's oldest and most cherished pieces of community theatre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Princely Liechtenstein Tattoo
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the ruined castle of Schellenberg in Liechtenstein hosts the Princely Tattoo, a grand open-air show of military bands and music. Marching bands, pipers, and brass ensembles perform beneath the old walls, in a spectacle inspired by Europe's great military tattoos and made to honor the little principality's heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Secret Garden Festival
festivals.description: Early April 2027 · Each year, the city of Murcia hosts the one-day Secret Garden festival of sustainable art and culture. Interactive installations, themed zones, and six stages of music, theater, and workshops fill the day, in a celebration begun in 2021 that reflects the city's growing care for the environment. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: The Tirage
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the town of Payerne holds the Tirage, a fair of marksmanship and festivity. Four days of target shooting on the old Vernex ranges join carnival rides and music, in a celebration founded by the local shooting society in 1736 in the spirit of skill and fellowship. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Thessalian Poetry Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the region of Thessaly hosts a five-day poetry festival that moves among its cities. Larissa, Volos, Trikala, and Karditsa take turns hosting readings of contemporary poetry, in a celebration founded in 2013 that gathers poets from Greece and beyond. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Thirteenth Day of Christmas Bonfire Night (Gufunes, Reykjavík)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · On the thirteenth day of Christmas, Iceland says goodbye to the season with great bonfires, fireworks, and a parade of elves and trolls. People bring their old Christmas trees to burn, and the last of the Yule Lads heads back to the mountains as midwinter slowly turns toward the light. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Thracian Sea Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each summer, the coastal towns of Greek Thrace celebrate the sea with the Thracian Sea Festival. Concerts and cultural events spotlight the region's Aegean heritage and maritime life, in a celebration begun in 2019 to honor the seafaring spirit of this eastern corner of Greece. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Komotini, Eastern Macedonia and Thrace, Greece
festivals.address: Komotini, Eastern Macedonia and Thrace, Greece

festivals.name: Three Cultures Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the town of Włodawa celebrates its three faiths with the Three Cultures Festival. Folk concerts, synagogue and church services, culinary fairs, and exhibitions honor the Jewish, Catholic, and Orthodox traditions, in a celebration of a town long home to Poles, Jews, and Ukrainians. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Three Kings Cavalcade
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 5, on the eve of Epiphany, Gibraltar parades its Three Kings' Cavalcade down Main Street. As many as twenty floats and walking groups honor the arrival of the Wise Men in the Spanish tradition of the Reyes Magos, in a beloved celebration run by community committees for decades. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Three Kings Parade (Cortege of the Three Wise Men)
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 6, the city of Kraków fills for the Orszak Trzech Króli, a great Epiphany parade reenacting the journey of the Three Wise Men to the Christ child. Families march in costume wearing paper crowns, following the kings through the streets to the Main Market Square, in a tradition that has spread across Poland since 2009. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Tienen Giants & Folk Games
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On the Saturday nearest October 10, the town of Tienen celebrates Kweikersdag with street markets, music, a parade, and a procession of giant puppets known as the Tiense reuzen. The name comes from the locals' own nickname, the Tiense Kweikers, and the day is a proud celebration of the town itself. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Tikveš Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each mid-September, the town of Kavadarci celebrates its grape harvest with the Tikveš Wine Festival. A wine carnival, a king and queen pageant, and the ceremonial first cutting of the grapes fill the days, in a celebration rooted in the ancient Dionysian rites of central Macedonia's wine country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Time in Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the village of Berchidda and the hill towns of Gallura fill with the sound of jazz for Time in Jazz. Concerts unfold in plazas and unexpected venues across the days, in a celebration founded by the trumpeter Paolo Fresu that grew from a local event into a great Sardinian happening. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tiszacsege Catfish Paprika Cooking Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the riverbank at Tiszacsege fills with cauldrons for a catfish paprika cooking festival. Cooks compete over the traditional Tisza-region fish soup amid music and folk fun, in a celebration of the fishing culture that ties this Great Plain community to its river. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Todorica (Todorovden Folk Festival, Sveti Nikole)
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each Todorovden, on the Saturday of Great Lent, the town of Sveti Nikole holds the Todorica folk festival. Villagers and visiting ensembles dance and sing, joining in a great oro on the square, in a celebration that weaves Orthodox tradition together with folk pageantry. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: TODOS Festival (Walk of Cultures)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year the Arroios neighborhood of Lisbon hosts the TODOS Festival, a Walk of Cultures that celebrates the city's diversity through performing arts, music, and food. Founded in 2009 in a city long shaped by immigration, it gathers Lisbon's many communities in one shared celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tokaj Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the Hungarian town of Tokaj celebrates the grape harvest in the heart of one of the world's great wine regions. Harvest processions, folk music, and tastings of the famous sweet golden wine fill the town center, in a festival that honors the deep traditions of the Tokaj-Hegyalja vineyards. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tolosa Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each late February, the town of Tolosa fills with masks and music for its Inauteriak, one of Spain's grandest carnivals. A week of parades and masked dances fills the streets as young and old don the traditional Basque blusas, in a celebration deeply rooted in the pre-Lenten release of Basque culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tornedalen Pride Pajala
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each first weekend of July, the town of Pajala holds Tornedalen Pride. The LGBTQ+ festival has quickly become an important gathering for northern Sweden's community, in a celebration that brings a modern movement for rights and welcome to a traditionally rural region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tornedalians Day
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 15, the town of Haparanda and the Torne Valley celebrate Tornedalians Day. The flag day honors the Tornedalian national minority of Sweden, in a celebration established after their recognition in 2000 that affirms a distinct border culture and language. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Torres Vedras Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the town of Torres Vedras throws what it proudly calls the most Portuguese carnival of all. Sharp, irreverent floats poke fun at politicians, men parade dressed as women, and giant puppets fill the streets, in a cheeky, homegrown celebration free of foreign glamour and full of local wit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Torres Vedras, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal
festivals.address: Torres Vedras, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal

festivals.name: Torres Vedras Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Torres Vedras holds a multi-week festival honoring its local patron saints. Concerts, folk dance, cooking contests, and tasquinhas serving regional food and wine fill the calendar, in a warm community celebration that weaves together old feast days into one festive season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Torres Vedras, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal
festivals.address: Torres Vedras, Lisbon Metropolitan, Lisbon District, Portugal

festivals.name: TOČ Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the ancient town of Nin hosts the TOČ gastro-festival over four days. Local cuisine, wine, and community life take center stage, in a celebration that draws on the identity of one of Croatia's oldest towns, long known for its salt and its storied past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tractors & Village Parade
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the village of Lontzen gathers for its one-day Fest im Dorf. Brass band concerts, an outdoor dance, food courts, and a grand procession of vintage tractors fill the day, in a celebration of rural tradition and the close-knit belonging of the village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Lontzen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium
festivals.address: Lontzen, German-speaking Community of Belgium, Belgium

festivals.name: Traditional Argyrades Carnival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each pre-Lenten season the Corfu village of Argyrades keeps its traditional carnival, with communal feasting and folk dancing run by the village cultural association. A small village with a strong communal spirit, Argyrades celebrates the season in the warm style of the Corfiot countryside. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Carnival of Ioannina
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · Each February, the city of Ioannina fills its streets with masks and music for its traditional carnival. Elaborate costumes, folk music, and dancing mark the pre-Lenten days, in a celebration of Apokries that carries the distinctive local heritage of this lakeside Epirote city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Costume Parade & Village Fair
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the fishing town of Volendam celebrates its heritage with the Volendammer Dagen. Costume parades, folk music, local food, and a funfair fill the days, in a celebration of a village known across the Netherlands for its distinctive fishermen's dress and song. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Food Festival "What Did Our Elders Eat"
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late summer the village of Vrbovac in Zagreb County holds What Did Our Elders Eat, a festival of traditional regional cuisine and old customs. Now decades old, it celebrates the heritage of Turopolje and its villages through the premodern dishes of the past. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vrbovac (Samobor municipality), Central Croatia (Zagreb County), Croatia
festivals.address: Vrbovac (Samobor municipality), Central Croatia (Zagreb County), Croatia

festivals.name: Traditional Lastovo Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each winter the village of Donja Lastva near Tivat holds its carnival, with masked processions and the trial and burning of Krnjo, a straw effigy, to renew hope for the year ahead. Rooted in the carnival traditions of the Bay of Kotor, the celebration is full of Bokelj humor and satire. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Donja Lastva, Tivat (Bay of Kotor), Montenegro
festivals.address: Donja Lastva, Tivat (Bay of Kotor), Montenegro

festivals.name: Traditional Music Festival
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each January, the Temple Bar quarter of Dublin fills with fiddles and song for TradFest, a festival of Irish traditional and folk music. Concerts on grand stages mix with intimate pub sessions and workshops, drawing big names and rising talent to the cobbled cultural heart of the city in the depths of winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Pig Slaughter
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each winter, the parish of Catasós in Lalín reenacts the old pig slaughter, the matanza. Neighbors recreate the full process, from slaughter to butchery and sausage-making, as part of the Lalín Cocido season, in a celebration of a household winter rite once central to rural Galician life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Pig Slaughter Festival of Monesterio
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each year, the town of Monesterio holds a community pig slaughter festival with educational demonstrations. A didactic dissection and tastings of Iberian pork dishes like migas and chorizo fill the day, in a celebration of a rite that long secured the meat supply and the identity of rural Extremadura. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Traditional Trnava Fair
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the city of Trnava revives its medieval market with the Traditional Trnava Fair. Four days of artisans, carousels, traditional foods like lokše and goose, new wine, and music fill the streets, in a celebration tied to Trnava's history as a medieval free royal town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Transhumance Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each mid-June, the village of Jaujac welcomes its flocks down from the high pastures with the Fête de la Transhumance. A parade of sheep and shepherds joins demonstrations of rural life and Ardèche flavors, in a celebration of the seasonal movement of the herds in the Monts d'Ardèche. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Transnistria Independence Day (Military Parade & Flag-Raising, Tiraspol)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 2, the breakaway region of Transnistria marks the day it proclaimed itself a republic in 1990. The capital, Tiraspol, holds a military parade, flag-raisings, and concerts, in a show of statehood and pride for a self-declared land that sits, unrecognized, on the edge of Moldova. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Trawl (Gillnet) Festival of Vasiliki
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August 6, the fishing hamlet of Kato Vasiliki celebrates its sea craft with the Gillnet Festival. Villagers gather to watch the building of a traditional gillnet and share in the festivity, in a celebration that preserves the old fishing skills of this small port on the Gulf of Patras. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Treasures of Podillia Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the city of Vinnytsia gathers the folk arts of its region for the Treasures of Podillia festival. Handicrafts and traditions from across the oblast fill the exhibition, in a celebration mounted by the region to preserve and share the folk heritage of Podillia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tribute of the Three Cows
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July 13, officials of the Roncal valley meet their French neighbors at the Piedra de San Martín to renew an old peace. Three prize cows are handed over as the centuries-old grazing treaty is sealed again, in a celebration that has kept the peace between the valleys since 1375. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Triel-sur-Seine Saint John’s Day Bonfire
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Around June 24 the town of Triel-sur-Seine keeps the Feu de la Saint-Jean, a midsummer celebration with a community bonfire, evening concerts, family activities, and fireworks. Once tied to the feast of St. John the Baptist, the night is now above all a warm gathering of the town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Triesenberg Weeks (Walser Food Festival)
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn the restaurants of Triesenberg serve traditional Walser menus, from Käsknöpfli to venison stew, for the Triesenberger Wochen. The food festival celebrates the Walser heritage of this mountain village and the cooking born of its high-altitude farming life. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Trifon Zarezan Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the city of Asenovgrad celebrates the vine-tending saint Trifon Zarezan with a wine festival. A contest for the best homemade wine and public festivity honor the start of the vine-growing year, in a celebration that ties the old saint's day to the local love of the grape. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tromsø Sami Week
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each February, the city of Tromsø celebrates Sami culture with Sami Week. A market on Storgata, lasso-throwing and reindeer racing, joik concerts, and seminars fill the days around Sami National Day, in a celebration that brings Sami identity and art into the heart of the Arctic city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tropoja Chestnut Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the town of Bajram Curri celebrates the chestnut harvest of Tropoja. Dishes made from the local chestnuts, folk music and dance, and a market of regional goods fill the festival, in a celebration of a nut that has long been a staple of these northern Albanian mountains. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Trout Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the town of Plužine celebrates its beer-marinated trout with the Fiju Fest. A free public tasting and live music gather the community by the Piva River, in a celebration that builds on the rural fishing tradition of the Piva region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tsiknopempti (Smoky Thursday) in Thessaloniki
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · On Smoky Thursday during carnival week, the city of Thessaloniki fills with grill smoke and street parties as everyone takes to the charcoal for one last great feast of meat before Lent. Tsiknopempti reaches back to ancient pre-Lenten feasting, and Thessaloniki keeps it with relish. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tulcea City Days (Tulcea Fest)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August the Danube Delta city of Tulcea marks its founding with Tulcea Fest, several days of cultural shows and concerts on the Piața Civica stage. A civic celebration run by the city, it gathers the community for the height of summer on the edge of the delta. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tullamore Show & FBD National Livestock Show
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Tullamore hosts Ireland's largest one-day agricultural show, drawing huge crowds to the heart of the midlands. Prize livestock, show-jumping, food, crafts, and machinery fill the grounds, in a grand celebration of Irish farming and country life that has grown from old county shows. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Turku Christmas Peace Declaration
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · At noon on Christmas Eve, the people of Turku gather in the old market square to hear the Christmas Peace declared from a balcony, just as it has been for some seven hundred years. The reading calls for a peaceful, reverent holiday, broadcast to the whole nation, in one of Finland's most cherished and oldest traditions. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Tuscan Identity Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year, the region of Tuscany celebrates what makes it Tuscan with an Identity Festival. Art exhibitions, food tastings, and historical displays gather around the theme of regional heritage, in a celebration created to bring the artisans and traditions of Tuscany together. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tutaka Festival (Awakened)
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, an open meadow near Gródek fills with Belarusian music for the Tutaka festival. Concerts by Belarusian and international artists, debates on identity, and workshops gather over several days, in a celebration created by Belarusian activists as a space for the diaspora to express itself. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tynwald Day
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Every July on the Isle of Man, the island's leaders climb a small grassy hill at St John's to read out the year's new laws in the open air. This is Tynwald Day, the island's national day, carrying on a parliament begun by Viking settlers over a thousand years ago and said to be the oldest still running. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: St John's, National, Isle of Man
festivals.address: St John's, National, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Tyrnavos Carnival (Bourani)
festivals.description: Mid March 2027 · On Clean Monday, the town of Tyrnavos throws one of Greece's bawdiest carnivals, the Bourani. Men and women cook a communal soup in giant cauldrons and carry frank phallic symbols through the streets in a frankly ribald fertility rite, with roots in ancient pagan custom, that the town has never been shy about keeping. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Tønder Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the southern Danish town of Tønder fills with folk and roots music from around the world. Since 1974, the festival has brought blues, country, Cajun, and traditional song to its stages and tents, in one of Europe's most respected gatherings for handmade, heartfelt music. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Tønsberg Viking Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the town of Tønsberg revives its Viking past with a free living-history festival. A great market of crafts and reenactors, battle displays, archery, and music fill the days, in a celebration of the heritage of one of Norway's oldest towns, a central Viking-age trading port. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Třeboň Wine Celebrations
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year the spa town of Třeboň holds its Wine Celebrations on the town square, with tastings of South Bohemian and Moravian wines, folk music, and local food. Better known for its chateau and spa, the town turns the square over to a festival of regional winemaking. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: U Sciaratu Summer Carnival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each early July, the Rock of Monaco fills with floats and costumes for the U Sciaratu summer carnival. Music, dancing, and children's activities brighten Le Rocher, in a celebration run by a local club since the mid-1990s that revives the old pre-Lenten carnival in a summer guise. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Udech-Fest (Ivana Kupala, Zhytachiv)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each midsummer, the banks of the Stryi River at Zhytachiv fill with the rites of Ivana Kupala for Udech-Fest. Bonfires, wreaths, and old solstice customs gather the community, in a celebration of the centuries-old Slavic feast of nature, fertility, and purification. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Ukraine Independence Day Celebration (Lviv)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August 24, the city of Lviv marks Ukraine's Independence Day with days of cultural events, from concerts to public lectures. Residents don the embroidered vyshyvanka and fly the blue and yellow, in a heartfelt celebration of statehood and heroes in a city long known for its fierce national spirit. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ulcinj Book Fair
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the seaside town of Ulcinj gathers publishers and readers for its Book Fair. Stalls of new titles from Montenegro and beyond fill the days from late July into August, in a celebration, more than a decade old, that promotes reading and the town's cultural identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ulcinj World Haiku Poetry Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · In the historic old town of Ulcinj, the Kala, this small literary festival gathers poets and enthusiasts to read haiku inspired by nature and human life. Run by the town's cultural center, it has grown around a circle of local poets who have taken the concise Japanese form to heart. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ulm Oath Monday
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the city of Ulm marks Schwörmontag, when the mayor stands on a balcony and swears, as mayors have since 1345, to govern justly. The old oath of this once-free imperial city is followed by lively festivities, in a proud day of local democracy and civic pride reaching back nearly seven centuries. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ulster Fleadh
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the province of Ulster gathers musicians and dancers for the Fleadh Uladh. A week of céilí band concerts, instrument competitions, and pub sessions fills the host town under Comhaltas Ceoltóirí Éireann, in a celebration at the heart of the Gaelic music life of the north. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Warrenpoint, Ulster Irish Gaelic tradition, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Warrenpoint, Ulster Irish Gaelic tradition, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Unity Through Diversity (Ethnic Costumes & Food Fair, Soroca)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the town of Soroca celebrates its many peoples with the Unity Through Diversity fair. Moldovan, Ukrainian, Russian, Polish, Roma, Jewish, and other communities share costume, food, music, and dance, in a celebration of the multi-ethnic heart of this Dniester border region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Unterland Annual Fair & Prize Market
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each October, the village of Eschen hosts the Unterland Annual Fair and Prize Market. Farmers show prize cattle, horses, and produce while visitors browse market stalls and rural festivity, in a celebration that has long been a highlight of the farming calendar of Liechtenstein's lowland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Urach Shepherds' Run
festivals.description: Late July 2027 · Every other July, the town of Bad Urach honors its shepherds with the Schäferlauf. Shepherds and shepherdesses race barefoot across the fields and perform the traditional Schäfertanz, in a celebration rooted in a 1723 ducal decree that honors the herding life of the Swabian Alb. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: URBX Urban Culture Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the Lille metropolitan area celebrates urban culture with the URBX festival. Concerts, breakdance battles, graffiti workshops, and sports shows fill venues across Roubaix and Lille, in a celebration that gives the area's young and creative a platform of their own. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Urkult Folk Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the rapids of Nämforsen near Näsåker fill with music for the Urkult festival. Folk and world music, Sami performances, and an iconic Night of Fire opening gather thousands, in a celebration begun in 1993 that has become one of Sweden's foremost folk festivals. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Näsåker, Folk/world music culture (Ångermanland, Västernorrland), Sweden
festivals.address: Näsåker, Folk/world music culture (Ångermanland, Västernorrland), Sweden

festivals.name: Urnäsch Cattle Descent
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each end of summer, the village of Urnäsch welcomes its cattle down from the high alps with the Alpabfahrt. The cows return decked in flowers and bells in a ceremonial procession, in a celebration deeply rooted in the pastoral life of Appenzell after months of summer grazing. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Uskok Days
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each first weekend of July, the town of Senj revives the age of the Uskoks with Uskočki dani. Costumed reenactment recalls the 16th-century defenders who built and held the Nehaj fortress, in a celebration of the town's famous warriors of the Adriatic coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Senj, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia
festivals.address: Senj, Kvarner and Lika region, Croatia

festivals.name: Uyeasound Up Helly Aa Fire Festival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each early February, the island of Unst holds the Uyeasound Up Helly Aa fire festival. The community gathers for a torchlit procession and the burning of a galley, in a celebration with roots back to 1911 that honors Shetland's Norse heritage and marks the end of Yule. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vadsø Jazz & World Music Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each August, the town of Vadsø in Varanger fills with music for the Varangerfestivalen. Jazz, pop, world, and folk play in halls, on stages, and even in a historic church, in a celebration run by the local jazz club that has become one of the region's oldest festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vadul-lunga Ivan Kupala Night
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each St. John's Eve, the village of Vadul-lunga keeps the midsummer rites of Ivan Kupala. Villagers sing, dance around bonfires, weave flower wreaths, and leap over the flames, in a celebration that carries the ancient Slavic solstice customs through this corner of the Dniester land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vadul-lunga, Pridnestrovskaia Slavic pagan tradition, Moldova
festivals.address: Vadul-lunga, Pridnestrovskaia Slavic pagan tradition, Moldova

festivals.name: Vaduz Gourmet Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, the town of Vaduz celebrates fine food and drink with the Genussfestival. The capital's restaurants and shops serve up their best across the festival days, in a celebration that, though recent, reflects the Liechtensteiners' love of good cuisine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Valea Călugărească Wine Festival
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each autumn, the wine village of Valea Călugărească celebrates its harvest. Grape-harvest customs, tastings of local wines, regional cuisine, and folk music fill the days, in a celebration of a place that has been a center of Romanian viticulture since the time of the Dacians. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Valletta White Night Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On the first Saturday of October, the Maltese capital of Valletta stays up all night for Notte Bianca, a free festival of arts and culture. Museums, palaces, and squares throw open their doors for music, performance, and light, in a sleepless celebration that fills the old fortress city with people till dawn. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Vallorbe Carnival
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · Each spring the Vaud town of Vallorbe holds its carnival, with colorful costumes, brassy Guggenmusik bands, and themed parades organized by local clubs. Following the Swiss pre-Lenten tradition and established in the 1980s, it fills the town hall and streets with festive noise. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Valls Saint John's Town Festival (Castells Season Opening)
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each Saint John's Day, the town of Valls opens the casteller season with its town festival. The two local colles and their guests raise human towers up to nine levels high on the plaça del Blat, in a celebration that mixes traditional dances with the daring art of the castells. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Valmiera City Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year the town of Valmiera, founded in 1283, celebrates its founding with concerts, parades, exhibitions, folk dances, and open-air music in its square and parks. One of Latvia's oldest towns, Valmiera turns its City Festival into a celebration of its long history and identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Valmiera Summer Theatre Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the city of Valmiera stages contemporary theatre in its unlikeliest corners. Original plays unfold in cafes, garages, and parks, often on themes of life and survival, in a celebration begun around 2016 that turns the everyday spaces of the city into a stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vanse Emigration-to-America Parade
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Norwegian village of Vanse celebrates its deep ties to America with a four-day festival of emigrant heritage. An American-style street parade, classic cars, and live music recall the many who once sailed from Lista to the United States, in a proud and unusual local tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Varaždin Courtyards Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the city of Varaždin opens the hidden courtyards of its Old Town to the public. Each historic courtyard hosts artists, craftsmen, musicians, or cooks for a weekend, in a celebration that invites visitors to explore the creative life tucked behind the facades of the baroque town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Varna Summer International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, the port city of Varna hosts an international folklore festival. Dozens of folk dance and music ensembles from Bulgaria and around the world share their traditions, in a celebration begun in 1992 that fills the historic seaside city with the color of folk culture. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Varna Summer International Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each July, the city of Varna hosts an international jazz festival founded by the saxophonist Anatoly Vapirov. Bulgarian and global jazz artists play indoor and open-air venues alongside young local musicians, in a celebration begun in 1992 that grew from the city's progressive jazz scene. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Vaslui Cultural Days
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August the city of Vaslui holds a week of arts and community for its Cultural Days, with concerts, art and book exhibitions, sporting events, and family activities. Begun in the early 2000s to engage citizens and showcase local talent, it has become a yearly stage of creativity for the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vatican Christmas Tree Lighting & Nativity Inauguration
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each Advent, St. Peter's Square unveils a large Nativity scene and lights its Christmas tree in a ceremony presided over by Vatican officials with delegations from the donor communities. The presepe tradition was popularized by St. Francis in 1223, and the square keeps it on the grandest scale. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vatican Museums by Night
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · On select autumn evenings, the Vatican Museums open late for visitors to tour the galleries by moonlight, often with live concerts among the masterworks. Begun after 2010, the night openings let locals and travelers wander the great collections in a quieter, more magical hour. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Vatican City, Vatican City (Roman cultural community), Vatican City
festivals.address: Vatican City, Vatican City (Roman cultural community), Vatican City

festivals.name: Veliko Tarnovo International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each mid-July, the city of Veliko Tarnovo fills its Summer Theater with folk music for an international festival. Ensembles from around the world give nightly concerts in the old Bulgarian capital, in a celebration that has been part of the city's cultural calendar since the 1990s. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Veltenhof Volksfest
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each year the Veltenhof district of Braunschweig holds a three-day Volksfest, with carnival rides, music and dance, fairground food, and the shooting contests and uniformed march of the local Schützenverein. The festival springs from the post-war German tradition of community Volks- und Schützenfeste. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Velyka Pysarivka Borscht Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August around Independence Day, the village of Velyka Pysarivka holds a borscht festival of cooking competitions, where locals prepare the beloved Ukrainian soup and other dishes. Begun in 2021, it turns a national dish into a proud village celebration of food and identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Venetian Carnival of Annecy
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each late February, the city of Annecy fills its lakeside with the masks of a Venetian carnival. Costumed figures in elaborate 18th-century dress parade along the quays and on the water, in a celebration that, though born of Venice, has become part of Annecy's own winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Venice Historical Regatta
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · On the first Sunday of September, the Grand Canal of Venice fills with a parade of richly decorated historic boats rowed by crews in Renaissance dress. After the pageant come the famous gondola races, in a centuries-old water festival that turns the city's great waterway into a stage of color and rivalry. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Venlo Carnival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each pre-Lenten season, the city of Venlo bursts into Vastelaovend, the carnival of 't Jocus Riék, the city of fun. Brass bands, dialect songs, and elaborate handmade costumes fill the streets, in a celebration rooted in the deep carnival tradition of Dutch Limburg. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vesoul International Asian Film Festival
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each year, the small French town of Vesoul hosts the world's oldest festival devoted to Asian cinema, founded in 1995. Around ninety films from across Asia, from the Middle East to the Far East, fill its screens, bringing the continent's storytelling to a corner of eastern France in a celebration of cultural exchange. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vestmannaeyjar National Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, Icelanders sail to the windswept Westman Islands for Þjóðhátíð, the country's most legendary outdoor festival. In a green volcanic valley, tens of thousands camp for a weekend of bonfires, fireworks, and communal singing, in a celebration that began when a storm kept the islanders from the nation's 1874 millennium. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Via Dinarica Kosovo Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each mid-August, the Rugova region near Peja celebrates mountain culture with the Via Dinarica Kosovo Festival. Guided hikes on the Via Dinarica trail join music, crafts, and talks over three days, in a celebration that brings hikers and locals together to honor the beauty of these peaks. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Victory Day (Great Siege Monument Wreath-Laying, Valletta)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 8, Malta marks Victory Day, remembering the lifting of the great Ottoman siege of 1565 that saved the islands. Wreaths are laid at the Great Siege monument in Valletta in a day that joins religious faith with national pride, honoring the forebears who held the line against overwhelming odds. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Victory Day Regatta (Grand Harbour)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September 8, Malta's Grand Harbour fills with the cheers of the Victory Day Regatta, a fierce rowing race between the old harbor towns. Crews from Birgu, Senglea, Cospicua, and beyond pull traditional boats across the water, in a beloved contest of local pride held on the island's day of victory. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vidin Art Festival 'The River'
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each late June, the city of Vidin turns its Danube riverside into a 'city of arts' for the festival 'The River'. Theatre, music, puppetry, and workshops build to a light and 3D mapping show, in a young celebration begun in 2023 that joins modern art to the great river at the city's edge. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vidovdan Kolo Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · On Vidovdan, June 28, the village of Smilovac holds a folklore festival of the traditional Serbian kolo dance and music. Held on the deeply meaningful holy day of St. Vitus, the festival turns the occasion into a celebration of song and the round dance. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vieilles Charrues Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each July, the Breton town of Carhaix-Plouguer hosts Les Vieilles Charrues, one of France's largest music festivals. Over four days, hundreds of thousands gather in the fields for rock, pop, and world music from big international names, in a celebration that grew from a small local party into a giant of the French summer. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vienna Ball of Sciences
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each winter, Vienna's grand City Hall hosts the Ball of Sciences, where the city's research community joins the great Viennese ball tradition. Debutantes open the floor with a polonaise, orchestras play waltzes, and science-themed talks mingle with dancing, in a modern twist on the classic ball season born in 2016. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vienna Danube Canal Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each summer, the banks of the Danube Canal in Vienna fill with the Donaukanaltreiben, a free festival of contemporary urban culture. Live music, DJs, art, and street food line the waterfront over a long weekend, in a laid-back celebration that turned a once-overlooked stretch of the canal into a summer hotspot. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vienna Danube Island Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each June, the long green Danube Island in Vienna hosts the Donauinselfest, billed as the largest free open-air music festival in Europe. Millions stream across the island over three days for dozens of stages of music, in a vast, free celebration that has become a symbol of the city's open, communal spirit. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vienna Philharmonic New Year's Concert
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 1, the Vienna Philharmonic greets the new year with its famous New Year's Concert, a program of waltzes and polkas by the Strauss family and others. Broadcast from the golden Musikverein hall to many millions around the world, it has become a beloved symbol of Austria and a graceful start to the year. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vienna Wiesn Fair (Kaiserwiese)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, the Kaiserwiese in Vienna's Prater fills with folk festivity for the Wiener Wiesn. Folk music, polkas and waltzes, Austrian fare, and beer gather the crowds, in a celebration that lifts up the folk heritage and gastronomy of Austria in the heart of the city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vienna Wine Hiking Day
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · Each year, the city of Vienna sends walkers through its hillside vineyards for the Weinwandertag. Marked trails wind among the vines of Nußdorf, Ottakring, and beyond, with stops at the Heurige wine taverns, in a two-day celebration of Vienna's rare tradition of city winemaking. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Viljandi Folk Music Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each last weekend of July, the town of Viljandi fills its castle ruins and stages with the Viljandi Folk Music Festival. Traditional and contemporary folk artists from around the world play alongside Estonian ensembles, in a celebration of the folk music that lies at the heart of Estonian heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Villach Church Fair
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the city of Villach hosts the Villacher Kirchtag, said to be Austria's largest traditional folk festival. Folk music, dance, and Carinthian food and drink fill the days, in a celebration begun in 1936 that grew from a post-harvest fair into a great gathering of the region. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vilnius Saint John’s Night
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each midsummer, the city of Vilnius celebrates Joninės, the festival of St. John's Night. Bonfires, flower wreaths, folk songs, and dances fill the parks, often by the water at Verkiai, in a celebration that descends from the ancient pagan solstice rites of the Baltic. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Vimoutiers Apple and Cider Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year the Pays d'Auge town of Vimoutiers celebrates its apple harvest and cider-making with the Fête de la Pomme et du Cidre. In the heart of Normandy's cider and cheese country, the festival honors the orchards and presses that have long shaped the region's identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vinarium Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each late summer, the bilingual town of Lendava celebrates its wine and its many peoples with the Vinarium Festival. Themed evenings of Slovenian, Hungarian, Croatian, and Serbian folk music and dance fill the stage, in a celebration of the mixed culture of this town on the Hungarian border. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vinaròs Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each February, the town of Vinaròs throws a flamboyant carnival around a yearly theme. Comparsas design elaborate floats and satirical performances in dazzling costume, in a celebration that carries the Spanish pre-Lenten carnival tradition with the town's own creative flair. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · On the last weekend of August the village of Vauhallan holds its Fête de la Vigne, a small community festival of vineyard tours, wine tastings, live music, and storytelling about local wine heritage. It ties this corner of the Île-de-France to the wider tradition of French harvest festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vinhais Smoked Meat Fair
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each February, the mountain town of Vinhais celebrates its smoked meats with the Feira do Fumeiro. Four days of renowned local sausages and cured pork fill the fair, in a celebration of a curing tradition reaching back to when each farm put up its own pork for the year. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vinnytsia Food Fest
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Timed to the city's birthday, the Vinnytsia Food Fest fills the central park with a multi-day street-food festival. Begun in the late 2010s as part of City Day, it has become a recurring summer gathering, drawing the city out to eat, mingle, and celebrate. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Viru Folk
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the seaside village of Käsmu hosts the two-day Viru Folk festival. Nordic folk and world music fill the Baltic coast under a fresh theme each year, in a celebration that pairs the music with the nature of this traditional captains' village by the sea. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Visaginas City Festival & Spaces Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year the town of Visaginas marks its City Day with music, dance, art exhibitions, a parade, and showcases of local crafts and culture. Built in the Soviet era around a nuclear plant, the multicultural town gathers its many communities in a festival of shared celebration. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Visby Days
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the walled World Heritage town of Visby fills with culture for Visby Days. Heritage walks, concerts, children's events, and a great open-air market fill the medieval streets, in a celebration that gathers all ages to honor the storied history of the Hanseatic town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Visby Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August, the town of Visby hosts a festival of singer-songwriters. Intimate music fills the venues of the old Gotland town, in a celebration begun in the mid-2010s that grew from a small community event into a respected local tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Viva Tremolo International Mandolin Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year the town of Pazardzhik gathers mandolin ensembles from Bulgaria and abroad for the Viva Tremolo festival. Pazardzhik holds a rare tradition of mandolin orchestras in southeastern Europe, and the festival spotlights the bright, trembling sound of the instrument. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Voices and Sounds Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year since 1991, the Corsican village of Pigna in the Balagne hills hosts Festivoce, a five-day festival of voices and sounds. Polyphonic song, instrumental music, and Mediterranean traditions fill the village, in a celebration born of the local Voce association and devoted to the deep Corsican art of part-singing. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Volleyball on Water – Magic Ljubljanica
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the city of Ljubljana floats a glowing volleyball court right on the surface of the Ljubljanica River for a one-of-a-kind sporting festival. Players dive and spike on the water while crowds watch from the banks and bridges, in a playful celebration that turns the city's beloved little river into a stage. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Volodymyr-Volynskyi Ivana Kupala Celebration
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each St. John's Eve, the city of Volodymyr-Volynskyi gathers on the beach by the Luga River for Ivana Kupala. Bonfires, wreaths, and old solstice customs fill the night of July 6 and 7, in a celebration that joins Christian and pagan heritage in the Ukrainian midsummer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Voloshin's September Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Crimean coast around Koktebel hosts a literary and arts festival honoring the poet Maximilian Voloshin, who made his home here. Poetry readings, concerts, and exhibitions gather writers and artists by the sea, in a celebration rooted in the Silver Age culture that once flourished on this storied shore. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Voxbotn Pop Music Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Vágsbotn harbor of Tórshavn fills with pop music for Voxbotn. Faroese and international acts play through a long all-day session into the small hours, in a celebration begun in 2012 that has become a high point of summer for the city's young music lovers. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Vuonislahti Kekri (Harvest Festival)
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each late autumn, the lakeside inn of Herranniemi near Lieksa revives the old Finnish harvest feast of Kekri. Feasting, community rituals, and the remembrance of ancestors mark the end of the farming year by Lake Pielinen, in a celebration that brings back a pre-Christian Finnish tradition. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Vuzenica Days
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Around the municipal holiday in mid-August, the Carinthian town of Vuzenica holds Vuzeniški dnevi, with art exhibitions, concerts, and ceremonies honoring its citizens. The festival grew from the town's anniversary celebrations, including tributes to the local painter Oskar von Pistor. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wachau Folk Fair (Krems)
festivals.description: Late August 2027 · Each late August, the Stadtpark of Krems fills with the Wachauer Volksfest, said to be Lower Austria's largest folk festival. The beer-garden mood of an Austrian Volksfest meets the wine culture of the valley, in a celebration that ties an old village fair to the famous Wachau. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Krems an der Donau, Wachau (Lower Austria), Austria
festivals.address: Krems an der Donau, Wachau (Lower Austria), Austria

festivals.name: Waimes Kermesse (Village Festival Weekend)
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each first weekend of September, the eastern Wallonian village of Waimes holds its Kermesse, a traditional village fair of festivity and good cheer. The fair belongs to the parish and community life of this historic village in the Hautes Fagnes country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wallgau Farmers’ Market
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September the Kurpark of Wallgau hosts a farmers' market, as local farmers, artisans, and musicians gather in traditional Bavarian dress to celebrate the harvest. Part of the Zugspitz-region Farmers' Weeks, it offers local food, crafts, and music in a warm community gathering. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Walnut Festival
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the Quercy village of Thégra celebrates its walnut harvest with a fair. A market of nuts and walnut products, a traditional oil-press demonstration, and wood-fired walnut bread fill the day, in a celebration of the walnut wealth of this corner of southwestern France. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Warm Spring Midsummer Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each first weekend of July, the village of Fuglafjørur gathers around its warm spring for Varmakelda. Rowing competitions, a summer market, and communal festivity fill the days, in a celebration rooted in over four centuries of Faroese rural custom around the warm waters. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Fuglafjørur, Faroe Islands (Fuglafjørður, Eysturoy), Faroe Islands
festivals.address: Fuglafjørur, Faroe Islands (Fuglafjørður, Eysturoy), Faroe Islands

festivals.name: Warsaw Christmas Market
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each December, the Old Town of Warsaw fills with festive Christmas markets. Stalls of crafts, food, and light gather by the Royal Castle from late November into the new year, in a celebration that joins the old Polish fair tradition to the glow of the winter season. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Warsaw Wianki Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the banks of the Vistula in Warsaw fill for Wianki, the midsummer festival of St. John's Night. By the great fountains below the old town, the riverside becomes a "Slavic village" of folk custom and modern concerts, as flower wreaths drift on the water in an ancient welcome to the solstice. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Waterland of Friesland Civic Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each mid-September, the towns of Southwest Friesland launch their cultural season together with a civic festival. Choirs, arts associations, and performers fill several towns at once across the Waterland of Friesland, in a celebration that opens the year of culture across the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Sneek, Súdwest-Fryslân and De Fryske Marren (Waterland van Fryslân), Netherlands
festivals.address: Sneek, Súdwest-Fryslân and De Fryske Marren (Waterland van Fryslân), Netherlands

festivals.name: Way of Light
festivals.description: Mid November 2026 · On November 18, the anniversary of Latvia's independence, the seaside city of Jūrmala lights thousands of candles along its roads to create a continuous Way of Light from Lielupe to Ķemeri. The glowing path honors Latvia's founding in a quiet, moving celebration of the nation's birthday. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Wdzydze Fair
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each summer, the Kashubian Ethnographic Park at Wdzydze hosts the largest Kashubian folk festival. Two days of traditional music, dance, and craft demonstrations gather ensembles and villagers, in a celebration held since 1973 of the rural heritage of Kashubia in northern Poland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Week of Kajkavian Culture
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each early September, the town of Krapina celebrates the Kajkavian dialect and the heritage of Hrvatsko Zagorje. A week of language, folk music, and culture fills the town, in a celebration begun in 1966 to preserve the Kajkavian tongue and the traditions of this Croatian region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: WeitjeRock Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the polder near IJzendijke fills with rock and pop for the two-day WeitjeRock festival. Founded by local volunteers in the late 1990s, lost, and revived in 2013, it now draws thousands, in a celebration of youth culture and alternative music in rural Zeeland. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: West Belfast Festival
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each August, the Falls Road area of Belfast fills with Féile an Phobail, two weeks of music, talks, and street events that have grown into one of Ireland's largest community festivals. Begun in 1988 during the Troubles to lift a struggling community's spirits, it remains a proud celebration of West Belfast's culture and resilience. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Belfast, West Belfast, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Belfast, West Belfast, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom

festivals.name: West Indies Green Fest
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each June, the town of Le Moule hosts the eco-conscious West Indies Green Festival. Music and culture meet a message of environmental care across the days, in a celebration begun in 2022 that reflects Guadeloupe's turn toward eco-responsibility in its festivals. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Westport Festival of Chamber Music
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the town of Westport fills its halls and churches with chamber music. Concerts gather fine players in venues like the Town Hall Theatre, in a celebration that brings high-quality classical music to the heart of this historic town in the west of Ireland. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Wet Monday Water Fight (Dobromierz)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · On Easter Monday the village of Dobromierz gathers in the square for a friendly water battle, run by the local volunteer fire department. The Śmigus-Dyngus soaking is a widespread Polish custom in which water stands for life and purification, here turned into a celebration for all ages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wet Monday Water Fight (Żory)
festivals.description: Late March 2027 · On Easter Monday the town of Żory holds a playful water fight, as families, children, and firemen gather with buckets and water guns in the Śmigus-Dyngus tradition. Run by the local volunteer fire brigade, the soaking marks the spring custom in which water means life renewed. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wexford Festival Opera
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each autumn, the small Irish town of Wexford hosts a world-renowned opera festival famous for staging the forgotten gems of the repertoire. Founded in 1951 to revive the town's opera house, it draws devoted opera-lovers from around the world to a cozy harbor town transformed each year into a stage for rare and beautiful works. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: White Field Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer, the courtyard of the Risto Ratković House in Bijelo Polje fills with the White Field Jazz Festival. Local and regional musicians play two days of contemporary jazz, in a celebration that honors the musical heritage of the town and gives its jazz scene a stage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: White Night of Madrid
festivals.description: Late September 2026 · For one night each September, the museums, galleries, and public spaces of Madrid stay open until dawn for La Noche en Blanco. Free exhibitions, performances, and installations fill the city, drawing crowds out into the late-summer night, in a celebration of contemporary culture open to all. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wieliszew Wreath Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each summer the shore of Lake Wieliszewski fills with the Wreath Festival, a two-day folk celebration of Slavic-inspired décor, music, and crafts. Drawing on the old wianki traditions of midsummer, it sets flower wreaths and folk music against the green of the lakeside park. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wilamowice Smiergust Festival
festivals.description: Late May 2027 · Each year, the town of Wilamowice keeps the Easter water-dousing custom of the Śmiergust over two days. Costumed young men splash the townsfolk in a beloved local rite, in a celebration deeply rooted in the unique Vilamovian culture and language of this small Polish town. · Dates expected based on annual pattern — check for updates.

festivals.name: Wild Flowers Festival (Rhodope Mountains)
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each midsummer, the villages of the upper Arda valley in the Rhodopes celebrate the Wild Flowers Festival. Around Enyovden, the festival honors the mountains' rich flora and its healing herbs over nine days, in a celebration rooted in the deep folklore of the Rhodope highlands. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Wildberg Shepherds' Run
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Every other July, the town of Wildberg holds one of the Black Forest's great folk festivals, the Schäferlauf. A shepherds' run, costumed pageantry, and festivity recall a 1723 decree that made Wildberg a seat of the shepherds' guild, in a celebration of the town's old sheep-farming heritage. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wine & Stories of Berat
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each autumn, the city of Berat celebrates its winemaking with Wine & Stories. A central tasting fair gathers local and regional wines over three days alongside stories of the town, in a celebration of the viticulture that thrives in Berat's favorable climate. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wine Day (Saint Tryphon’s Day)
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each mid-February, on St. Tryphon's Day, northern Mitrovica celebrates the vine with a Wine Day. Local wineries and distilleries pour their award-winning wines and brandies, in a celebration that joins a social gathering of producers to the old Orthodox feast of the vineyards. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Mitrovica, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia
festivals.address: Mitrovica, Serbian community of Kosovo and Metohija, Serbia

festivals.name: Wine Festival of Dafnes
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the village of Dafnes in Crete celebrates its winemaking with a wine festival.
