Local winemakers and villagers present their wines as visitors taste the traditional vintages, in a celebration of one of Crete's historic wine-producing villages. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wine Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August, the town of Jumilla celebrates its grape harvest with a week-long Fiestas de la Vendimia. Grape stomping, a wine fountain, and processions of wine floats fill the days, in a celebration of the centuries-old winemaking tradition of this Murcian town. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Winter Festival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Amid the snow-covered highlands, the town of Pukë holds its three-day winter festival, Puka N'Fest, with open-air concerts of folk and modern music, craft workshops, and winter sports. The festival celebrates the region's natural beauty and the deep seasonal traditions of these mountains. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Winter Music Evenings (Prof. Ivan Spasov Festival)
festivals.description: Late January 2027 · Each late January, the city of Pazardzhik fills its halls with classical music for the Winter Music Evenings. Symphonic and chamber concerts by Bulgarian and international performers honor the composer Ivan Spasov, in a celebration that sustains the city's standing as a center of classical music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Winter Solstice (Bluķis Yule Log Ritual)
festivals.description: Late December 2026 · Each winter solstice, the Latvian Ethnographic Open-Air Museum in Rīga drags and burns the Yule log, the bluķis. Songs, dances, folk masks, and old Ziemassvētki rituals fill the day, in a celebration that revives the pre-Christian seasonal traditions of rural Latvia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: With Selonia in the Heart (Selonia Crafts & Folk Performances)
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August, the town of Jēkabpils celebrates the heritage of Selonia with its 'With Selonia in the Heart' festival. Crafts and folk performances fill the streets, in a celebration that lifts up the distinct culture of Sēlija, the southeastern corner of Latvia. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Women's Literary Creativity Festival
festivals.description: Mid October 2026 · Each year the town of Berane holds a festival devoted to the work of women writers and poets, with public readings, book presentations, and literary discussions. The gathering lifts up the female literary voices of northern Montenegro and the stories of the region. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wonderland Lisbon
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each December, the great Parque Eduardo VII in Lisbon transforms into Wonderland Lisboa, the country's biggest Christmas celebration. A market, an ice rink, a Ferris wheel, and thousands of lights fill the hillside park overlooking the city, in a festive wonderland that has lit up the capital's holidays since 2016. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Woodstock of Brass Music
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Upper Austrian village of Ort im Innkreis hosts the Woodstock of Brass Music, four days celebrating the region's beloved Blasmusik. Bands from around the world play, building to a great Gesamtspiel where hundreds of bands and thousands of musicians play together as one, in a joyful tribute to brass-band culture. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Ort im Innkreis, Upper Austria (Innviertel), Austria
festivals.address: Ort im Innkreis, Upper Austria (Innviertel), Austria

festivals.name: World Day of Peace Papal Mass
festivals.description: Early January 2027 · Each January 1, the Pope celebrates a special Mass in St. Peter's Basilica for the World Day of Peace, which falls on the feast of Mary, Mother of God. Marked since 1968, the day joins an ancient Marian feast with a modern prayer for peace, opening the new year with a call to reconciliation around the world. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: World Festival of Polish Diaspora Folklore Ensembles
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Every three years, the city of Rzeszów welcomes Polish folk dance groups from around the world for a great gathering of the diaspora. Ensembles whose members grew up far from Poland fill the stages with the songs and dances of the homeland, in a moving celebration of the culture that binds Polonia across the globe since 1969. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Roast Potato Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, a Slovenian town celebrates the beloved roast potato, praženi krompir, with a world festival. Twenty-five teams cook their own recipes among more than sixty food stalls, in a cheerful celebration of a humble Carniolan Sunday dish that has deep roots in Slovenian cuisine. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Vyshyvanka Day (Chernivtsi)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each third Thursday of May, the city of Chernivtsi celebrates the holiday it gave the world in 2006. Cultural lectures, exhibitions, and community projects honor the embroidered vyshyvanka, in a celebration especially symbolic in the city where students first dreamed up the day. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Vyshyvanka Day (Kharkiv)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · Each third Thursday of May, the city of Kharkiv fills with embroidered shirts for World Vyshyvanka Day. Students, office workers, and public servants alike wear the vyshyvanka and join organized events, in a celebration of a movement, born in 2006, to keep Ukraine's folk dress alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Vyshyvanka Day (Kyiv)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · On the third Thursday of May, the people of Kyiv pour into the streets in embroidered vyshyvanka shirts to celebrate World Vyshyvanka Day. Offices, schools, and public squares fill with the old patterns, in a day that turns a simple shirt into a proud, shared declaration of Ukrainian identity. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Vyshyvanka Day (Lviv)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · On the third Thursday of May, the city of Lviv joins all of Ukraine in wearing the embroidered vyshyvanka for the day of national unity. Born from a student idea in Chernivtsi in 2006, the grassroots celebration has spread across the country and the diaspora, filling the streets with the colors of tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World Vyshyvanka Day (Odesa)
festivals.description: Mid May 2027 · On the third Thursday of May, the port city of Odesa marks World Vyshyvanka Day with marches and concerts down its grand main streets. People of every age wear the embroidered shirt that stands for Ukrainian identity, in a celebration that has grown from a student idea into a day kept across the country and the world. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: World's Largest Surströmming Feast
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each year, the town of Alfta gathers in its ice rink to open the fermented-herring season. Locals brave and savor the pungent surströmming amid songs and food at what is billed as the world's largest surströmmingsskiva, in a celebration of a century-old Swedish tradition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wreaths on the Warta River
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · On St. John's Night the town of Uniejów holds Wianki nad Wartą, a summer solstice festival on the riverside promenade by the thermal baths. The festival revives the ancient Slavic Noc Świętojańska, with its midsummer customs of fire, flowers, and wreaths set on the water. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Wrocław Christmas Market
festivals.description: Late November 2026 · Each December, the Market Square of Wrocław fills with a great Christmas fair. Wooden stalls of ornaments, crafts, smoked cheeses, and gingerbread glow beneath a canopy of lights from late November into the new year, in a celebration that carries the city's medieval tradition of holiday markets. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Wörthersee Marian Boat Procession
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each August 15, on the Assumption, a flotilla carries a statue of Mary across Lake Wörthersee. The Marienschiffsprozession sails by evening from Velden to Maria Wörth, in a celebration begun in 1954 that honors the feast and the deep Marian devotion of Carinthia. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Velden am Wörther See, Carinthia, Austria
festivals.address: Velden am Wörther See, Carinthia, Austria

festivals.name: Xanthi Carnival
festivals.description: Mid February 2027 · Each pre-Lenten season, the city of Xanthi throws one of Greece's great carnivals. Weeks of parades, masquerades, themed floats, and dances draw thousands, in a celebration of the Thracian festive spirit that the city has kept since at least the 19th century. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Xewkija Feast of St. John the Baptist
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the Gozo village of Xewkija celebrates its patron, Saint John the Baptist, in a classic island festa centered on its great domed rotunda church. Statues are carried in procession, bands play, and fireworks light the night, in the village's proudest celebration of the year beneath one of the largest church domes in the world. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Xino Nero Carnival
festivals.description: Early March 2027 · Each carnival season the small village of Xino Nero keeps its own long-held celebration, with costume-making and street festivities that build to Carnival Monday. Decades old, the village carnival preserves a warm small-community spirit in its own particular style. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Xinzo de Limia Carnival
festivals.description: Mid January 2027 · Each winter, the Galician town of Xinzo de Limia holds what it calls the longest carnival in Spain, weeks of ritual revelry called the Entroido. Masked figures known as pantallas, swinging inflated bladders, chase down anyone they find not in costume, in a wild celebration with roots in ancient pre-Christian rites. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Xinzo de Limia, Galicia (Ourense, Limia), Spain
festivals.address: Xinzo de Limia, Galicia (Ourense, Limia), Spain

festivals.name: Xixëllonjat (Fireflies Festival)
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer the Clock Gate of Berat hosts Xixëllonjat, the Fireflies Festival, with music, folk dancing, outdoor cinema, exhibits, and cultural activities over two evenings. Named for the fireflies, it weaves entertainment together with a gentle environmental theme in the historic city. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Xlendi Sunset Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the seaside village of Xlendi in Munxar fills its bay with free music for the Sunset Festival. Maltese bands and DJs play a family-friendly beachfront, in a celebration run by local youth clubs that captures the close-knit, youthful spirit of the village. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Xwejni Music Fest
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each summer, the picturesque Xwejni Bay near Marsalforn fills with free music for the Xwejni Music Fest. Popular Maltese pop and dance acts and local bands play by the sea, in a celebration that showcases Gozo's young talent and its love of the summer coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Yalta Summer Jam Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the seafront of Yalta fills with beats for the Summer Jam hip-hop festival. DJs, breakdancers, and rap acts turn the embankment into a lively concert venue, in a celebration of the contemporary urban youth culture of the Crimean coast. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Yambol Christmas Carolers Buenek Festival
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each Christmas, the town of Yambol fills with the koledari for its caroling festival. Groups of young men go from house to house singing the old blessings of koleduvane, in a celebration that keeps alive one of Bulgaria's most cherished Christmas traditions. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Yaouank Festival
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · Each autumn, the city of Rennes celebrates Breton music and dance with the Yaouank festival. Established and emerging Celtic musicians play concerts and great festoù-noz, building to a massive closing fest-noz, in a celebration whose name means 'young' and embodies the revival of Breton culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Yell Fire Festival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each late February the village of Cullivoe on the Shetland island of Yell holds its Up Helly Aa, as squads in Norse costume carry burning torches through the night. Grown from Shetland's Norse heritage, the fire festival began as a children's event and now lights the deep northern winter. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Cullivoe, Cullivoe, Yell, Shetland, United Kingdom
festivals.address: Cullivoe, Cullivoe, Yell, Shetland, United Kingdom

festivals.name: Yn Chruinnaght Celtic Gathering
festivals.description: Late July 2026 · Each year, the town of Peel hosts Yn Chruinnaght, an inter-Celtic gathering. Manx, Scottish, Irish, Welsh, Cornish, and Breton music, dance, and culture come together, in a celebration that revives a Manx gathering first held in 1924 and reborn in the modern era. · Dates confirmed.
festivals.location: Peel, Glenfaba and Peel, Isle of Man
festivals.address: Peel, Glenfaba and Peel, Isle of Man

festivals.name: Yogurt Festival (Momchilovtsi)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August, the Rhodope village of Momchilovtsi celebrates its famous yogurt with a three-day festival. Folklore music, dance, and contests honor the dairy long tied in legend to health and long life, in a celebration of a mountain village renowned for its beneficial yogurt. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Ystad Culture Night
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · First held in 2023, Ystad Kulturnatt fills the Skåne town with local music, art, and heritage through open-air events, workshops, and performances. Its highlight is the colorful Kulturparaden, a procession that winds through town in a vivid celebration of the area's culture. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zadar County Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each year, the city of Zadar gathers cultural associations from its islands, coast, and hinterland for a county folklore festival. Three days of traditional dance, song, and authentic costume fill Trg Petra Zoranića, in a celebration that keeps the folk customs of northern Dalmatia alive. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zaječar Guitar Festival (Gitarijada)
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each late August since 1966, the town of Zaječar has rocked to the Gitarijada. Amateur bands compete across the weekend before a final concert by well-known acts, in a celebration that, born in communist Yugoslavia, became one of the world's oldest rock festivals and a rare outlet for the music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zakynthos Carnival
festivals.description: Late February 2027 · Each year before Lent, the Ionian island of Zakynthos throws a carnival rich with the flavor of old Venice. Costumed parades and street dances fill the town, building to a famous mock wedding played out for laughs in the main square, in a celebration shaped by centuries of Venetian rule over the island. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zamość Wine Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each year, the city of Zamość celebrates Polish wine with the Zamojskie Winogranie. Tastings of local wines and regional foods fill the festival, in a celebration begun in 2019 that has grown into one of Poland's largest wine events, lifting up the winemaking of the Roztocze and Lublin country. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zaporizhzhia City Day
festivals.description: Early October 2026 · On the first Saturday of October, the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia celebrates its founding with City Day. Fairs, concerts, and culinary events fill the streets, including the great Pokrovsky Fair, as one of the country's industrial heartlands marks its history with a celebration of unity and pride. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zaporizhzhia International Short Film Festival (ZIFF)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the city of Zaporizhzhia spotlights short films from Ukraine and abroad with its International Short Film Festival. Screenings, workshops, and meetings with filmmakers fill the days, in a celebration first held in 2017 and revived in 2024 that reflects the city's resilience and cultural ambition. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zarasai City Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each mid-August, the lakeside town of Zarasai celebrates its culture with a three-day city festival. Folk and modern concerts, craft fairs, and communal gatherings fill the days around Žolinės, the Assumption, in a celebration that ties local festivity to the high point of the Lithuanian summer. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zaube Wild Food Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each year, the village of Zaube celebrates foraging and the wild table with its Wild Food Festival. Hunters and chefs prepare game, berries, mushrooms, and herbs in open-air cooking, in a celebration that honors the knowledge of hunting and gathering at the heart of rural Vidzeme. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zeeland Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the towns of Middelburg and Terneuzen fill historic venues with the Zeeland Jazz Festival. Local and national artists play jazz and related music across several days, in a celebration that gathers the long-running jazz events of the province under one summer banner. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zeelandic Flanders Carnival (Parades & Costume Balls)
festivals.description: Early February 2027 · Each pre-Lenten season, the Catholic south of Zeeland erupts in four days of carnival. Towns like Hulst hold colorful parades, costume balls, and street parties with elaborate floats, in a celebration rooted in the Catholic and Flemish heritage of Zeeuws-Vlaanderen. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zemplén Festival
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Zemplén Hills of northern Hungary fill with ten days of music for the Zemplén Festival. Classical, jazz, and folk concerts spread across Sárospatak and the small villages of the Tokaj wine country, in a celebration founded around 1992 in this scenic corner of the land. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zemplín Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each summer, the town of Michalovce gathers folk ensembles for the Zemplínske slávnosti. Performers from the Zemplín region and abroad fill the festival with traditional music and dance, in a celebration of the rich folk culture of this region of eastern Slovakia by the Ukrainian border. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Zenica Summer Fest
festivals.description: Early August 2026 · Each summer, the Bosnian city of Zenica throws a ten-day free festival of open-air concerts and culture. Regional music stars and family programs draw the whole city out, including its returning diaspora, in a warm celebration that has become a high point of summer life in this industrial heartland town. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
festivals.location: Zenica, Bosniaks of Zenica-Doboj Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bosnia and Herzegovina
festivals.address: Zenica, Bosniaks of Zenica-Doboj Canton, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bosnia and Herzegovina

festivals.name: Zeta Cakes and Priganica Day
festivals.description: Late October 2026 · Each year, the Zeta region near Golubovci celebrates its sweet table with a day of cakes and priganice. Cooking contests, tastings, and live music gather around the fried dough and cakes long baked for celebrations, in a celebration of the dessert traditions of the Zeta plain. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zielona Góra Grape Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each year, the city of Zielona Góra celebrates its wine harvest with the week-long Winobranie. The crowning of a Wine Queen, an international folklore parade, open-air tastings, and concerts fill the streets, in a celebration more than half a century old of vineyards planted here centuries ago. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Zundert Flower Parade
festivals.description: Early September 2026 · Each September, the Dutch town of Zundert stages the largest flower parade in the world, when colossal floats covered entirely in dahlias roll through the streets. Neighborhood teams spend the whole year designing their giants and pinning on millions of blooms by hand, in a dazzling feat of community held since 1936. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Zurich Knabenschiessen (Rifle Festival)
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each September, the city of Zurich throws its largest folk festival around an old tradition: a target-shooting contest for teenagers, held since 1656. While the young marksmen and markswomen compete for the title of king or queen, a huge funfair fills the grounds, in a celebration that mixes a centuries-old custom with a great autumn fair. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Zvolen Castle Games
festivals.description: Early June 2027 · Each year, the medieval Zvolen Castle hosts an open-air theatre festival. Slovak and international classical plays are staged in the picturesque historic courtyard, in a celebration that ties the dramatic arts to the storied heritage of the castle, a staple of Slovak summer theatre. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Åland Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Mid September 2026 · Each autumn, farms across the Åland Islands throw open their gates for Skördefesten, the harvest festival. Visitors drive from farm to farm to taste fresh local food, meet the growers, and get a feel for island country life, in a celebration of the islands' deep farming roots. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Åland Opera Festival
festivals.description: Mid August 2026 · Each summer, the Åland Islands stage opera in some of their most beautiful historic settings, such as Mozart's "The Magic Flute" at the medieval Kastelholm Castle. The festival brings the grandeur of European opera to this quiet Baltic archipelago, pairing great music with the islands' own history. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Åland Organ Festival
festivals.description: Late June 2026 · Each June, the churches of the Åland Islands fill with the sound of their historic pipe organs for a classical music festival. Concerts move through several old island churches, celebrating a Nordic musical heritage that the islands have nurtured since the festival began in 1975. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Åland Pride Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each year, the Åland Islands celebrate their LGBTQ+ community with a Pride festival that has grown from a small gathering into a weeklong event. Drawing people from across the islands and beyond, it reflects the warm, open spirit of this small Baltic community. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Åland’s Lucia
festivals.description: Mid December 2026 · Each December 13, a young woman is crowned Lucia in St. Göran's Church in Mariehamn, carrying light into the darkest days of the Åland winter. Celebrated in the Swedish style with candles and song, it is the islands' own gentle observance of Saint Lucia's Day. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Évron Sacred Arts Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each July, the great pilgrimage basilica of Évron fills with sacred music for a festival spanning a thousand years of religious song. Local and international ensembles perform Gregorian chant, Baroque masses, and contemporary works beneath its medieval vaults, in a celebration of spirituality and music in one of the region's historic holy places. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Öblarn Krampus Play
festivals.description: Early December 2026 · Each early December, the market square of Öblarn comes alive with its Krampus play. Costumed Krampus figures act out an Advent drama before the crowd, in a celebration recognized as cultural heritage that preserves a centuries-old Alpine custom of the Christmas season. · Dates are provisional and may change.

festivals.name: Östbjörka Folk Music Gathering
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · The weekend after the great Bingsjö gathering, the small village of Östbjörka near Rättvik holds its own folk-music stämma, full of polska tunes and group dances. Part of Dalarna's week of folk gatherings, it keeps the region's fiddle tradition alive in a convivial, low-key evening of music. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Čakčariemut Autumn Festival
festivals.description: Late August 2026 · Each August, the Sámi cultural center at Jukkasjärvi hosts Čakčariemut, a one-day celebration of the autumn harvest. Visitors gather at the Nutti Sámi Siida museum for traditional food, crafts, and the rhythms of Sámi life, in a warm festival that bridges old artisan traditions and the turning of the northern seasons. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Čegran International Folklore Festival
festivals.description: Mid July 2026 · Each year, the village of Čegran near Gostivar hosts the Rudina international folklore festival. Folk dance, music, and costume from groups around the world fill the program, in a celebration that helps preserve and share the traditions of the Pollog region and beyond. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Đakovo Embroideries Festival
festivals.description: Early July 2026 · Each summer, the Slavonian town of Đakovo celebrates its rich folk heritage with the Đakovo Embroideries, a festival named for the region's famous needlework. Dozens of folk groups in elaborate costume perform the songs and dances of the plains, in a colorful gathering that has run for generations. · Dates are approximate and may shift.

festivals.name: Šariš Song and Dance Festival
festivals.description: Mid June 2026 · Each year, the village of Raslavice gathers folk groups for the Šariš Song and Dance Festival. Days of stage shows and festivity showcase the dialect, music, and customs of Šariš, in a celebration that reinforces the distinct folk identity of this historic Slovak region. · Dates confirmed.

festivals.name: Šibenik International Children's Festival
festivals.description: Early June 2026 · Each summer, the Dalmatian city of Šibenik turns itself into a playground of the arts for its International Children's Festival. Puppet shows, theatre, music, and creative workshops fill the old stone streets for weeks, in a celebration made just for children that has delighted the young since 1958. · Dates are approximate and may shift.
